title:
Subject
VS Code Explorer Not Showing Files Inside Folder
description:
Hello Support Team,

I am experiencing an issue in Visual Studio Code where files inside a folder are not visible in the Explorer panel.

In the screen recording I attached, I open my project directory ("week 11") which contains folders like "backend" and "demo". However, when I select or view these folders in VS Code, the files inside them are not displayed.

The video shows that:

- The folder structure is visible
- But the files inside the folders are missing in the Explorer view
- The same folders exist in File Explorer

I have already tried restarting VS Code and reopening the folder, but the issue still persists.

Could you please help me understand why the files are not appearing and how to fix this?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
balakrishnankayan999@gmail.com
Balakrishnan m

<!-- Error while uploading Recording 2026-04-19 111709.mp4 -->
<!-- Error while uploading Recording 2026-04-19 111709.mp4 -->

title: Copilot weekly rate limit triggered unexpectedly on Claude Opus 4.6, 4.7 (Pro account)
description:
Hello GitHub Support,
I am being blocked by a weekly rate-limit message in Copilot Chat, even though I am a paying user and this started unexpectedly.

Observed behavior:

Copilot returns HTTP 429 with code user_weekly_rate_limited.
Requests to Claude Opus 4.6 are explicitly marked as rateLimited.
Requests to Claude Opus 4.7 are also marked as rateLimited.
Relevant log evidence (from VS Code Copilot Chat logs on 2026-04-18):

2026-04-18 21:36:41.759 [error] Server error: 429 {"error":{"message":"...","code":"user_weekly_rate_limited"}}
2026-04-18 21:42:39.758 [info] ccreq:4b94bc95.copilotmd | rateLimited | claude-opus-4.6 | 261ms | [panel/editAgent]
2026-04-18 21:30:09.156 [info] ccreq:ffe3e383.copilotmd | rateLimited | claude-opus-4.7 | 230ms | [panel/editAgent]
Please verify:

My Copilot entitlement and premium quota status.
Whether my account is incorrectly classified or capped.
Whether there is a backend issue affecting model access for my plan.
Thank you.
comments: This issue is a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/253124. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

Now I have to wait 2 DAMN DAYS!

You can do better but you won't.

DO BETTER.

title: GitHub Copilot summarization now with no warning and not reversible in v1.116
description:
It's exhausting filing these summarization/compaction issues (refer to e.g. #289220). This new ticket a degradation of #303549.

Now in v1.116 GitHub Copilot will summarize even with it's only around 76%, even when the remaining space on the meter (even with the reserved space accounted for!) has sufficient room for what I was requesting. At least under #303549 I could see that it was starting to summarize, and cancel the summarization, back up, and perform some other operation that needed the context. Now out of the blue GitHub Copilot will say "summarized" and immediately I lose all context.

Even worse, if I go back in the history, I don't get that summarized context back!! In previous versions, I could cancel compaction and resubmit the previous request, and the resubmission would use the pre-compaction context (i.e. as if compaction had not happened). Now there is no warning and summarization happens instantly. Instant summarization would be fine if I could resend the request with the pre-summarized conversation, but now even if I resubmit the prompt, the pre-compaction context is gone—the context stays compacted.

It's as if every new version comes out with a further degredation of summarization/compaction.

Look, let me try to break this down in simple terms. Ideally you would provide a hook and let me do my own summarization. If you can't do that, at least do this:

* Have the context full meter level _include_ the reserved space (as it seemed to do before #303549).
* Don't compact the conversation until the meter reaches at least 95%.
* If you compact the conversation, allow me to resubmit the last prompt before compaction using the context that that prompt had before compaction. (i.e. Do not "alter history" by imputing a compacted context backwards in time to a previous prompt's state.)

Those are extremely simple principles! Developers would know what to expect! We could work with the LLM and make informed decision! But now _all three of these simple principles_ are broken!! Why does the behavior have to be so convoluted to deal with, and against user expectations?

```
Version: 1.116.0 (user setup)
Commit: 560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679
Date: 2026-04-15T00:28:13Z
Electron: 39.8.7
ElectronBuildId: 13797146
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
```
comments:
One hypothesis of what is going on in v1.116 is that GitHub Copilot realizes that it is running out of space and preemptively compacts the conversation but _does not tell the user about it_ until the subsequent prompt. For example, after I submit Prompt 1, perhaps GitHub Copilot will compact the conversation in the middle of Response 1, but _not tell the user about it_. Thus after Response 1 is finished, the meter still shows space left, but I suspect that the conversation has already been compacted.

This would explain why that when I submit Prompt 2, GitHub Copilot _immediately_ says "Compacted conversation" with no pause or warning —because the conversation has already been summarized in Response 1. It would also explain why resubmitting Prompt 2 does _not_ get the full conversation context back—again because the conversation has already been summarized in Response 1.

So maybe this ticket indicates a new bug in which GitHub Copilot no longer informs the user when conversation is starting to occur, or even immediately after conversation has occurred; and instead still shows a not-yet-full meter and only informs the user of compaction _in the next turn_. Such a bug would explain this new, further degraded behavior.

In pre-v1.116, if GitHub Copilot decided it needed to compact in the middle of a long response, at least it would inform me of that immediately, so that I could cancel summarization, back up, and adjust the prompt or switch prompts altogether (to use a higher-value request that needs the context before it is compacted, for example).

Something is really broken in v1.116. I see this sequence of events frequently—over and over. It's not just a fluke happening every once in a while.

1. The meter will be on 60% or so.
2. I'll have the LLM do some work (such as updating a couple of documents), and the meter will go up to like 73%.
3. Then I'll request that the LLM update a single, small document. I even use the same prompt for updating that document, which I have used hundreds of times in the past, and which should not use more than 1% or 2% of the context window.

**Expected:** The meter will go up to 75%.

**Actual:** The LLM will _immediately_ say "Compacted conversation", and the meter will drop to 20% or so.

This really makes me suspicious that the LLM actually compacted the conversation in Step 2 above, but neglected to tell me compaction was starting, and afterwards showed me a stale meter.

title: Vertical Center on Each "Search for Next Match"
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
If you search for a keyword using VSCode built-in search, when there're multiple matches in the current view of a file (like the window width is very long), pressing F4 to search for the next match will simply change the highlighted block to the next match, but won't scroll the screen, unless the next match is outside of the current view.
However, in GitLens Inspect, to compare a file between two commits, once you press Alt+F5 to jump to the next diff block, the screen will immediately scroll so that the next diff block is at the vertical center of the VSCode window, in terms of width.
Now here's a problem: what if a file is too big for GitLens Inspect to load? In that case, I have to use `git diff` command in Git Bash to export the diff to a file, open that file in VSCode, and then search for the regular expression "^[^-+].*\n[+-]" in this diff file to quickly jump between diff blocks. However, the pain is, once I press F4 to jump to the next match of this regular expression, the VSCode window doesn't set the next diff block to be at the vertical center, but instead just change the highlighted block to the next match. Because this action is different from that of GitLens Inspect, I have to adjust myself to this habit.
So, I'm wondering if you could add an option, like "Center on Search", so that when this option is checked, each time I search for the next match, the screen will always be scrolled so that the next match will be at the vertical center of the window? I'd appreciate it if you could take this into consideration.
comments:
Supplement: I just found that by pressing Ctrl+G, one can jump to a specific line. That specific line is always put in the vertical center of the VSCode window. So if you plan to add this feature, you can just use the logic from "Go To Line" function.
And on top of this, would you please enable users to adjust the location where the line would end? I've been stressing vertical center (which means 50% from the top or bottom) above, but is that possible to implement 40%, 30% from the top or bottom, or even -10%, +110%, etc., so that the user can decide?

Clicking this badge removes the dev extension from EDH. Steps to reproduce: 1. Install extension "foo" from marketplace (e.g. "clangd"), disable it. 2. Open extension "foo" source code in VS Code. 3. Launch Extension Development Host (Run > Start Debugging). Current behavior: - In EDH, the disabled marketplace extension shows "Restart Extensions". title: Subject: Codex session handling in VS Code is broken and unacceptable
description:
Type: Bug I am using the OpenAI Codex extension in VS Code under WSL Ubuntu. There is a serious workflow and product issue: Codex sessions are being tied to the GitHub Copilot chat/storage layer, and individual Codex sessions cannot be deleted. That is broken behavior. It destroys basic workspace hygiene and makes serious iterative work unnecessarily messy. A single aborted, mistaken, or half-executed Codex run remains stuck in the session history, with no granular way to remove it. The only apparent fallback is deleting broader chat storage, which is not an acceptable solution when other active conversations still need to be preserved. This creates multiple concrete problems: * no per-session cleanup * no clean separation between Codex and Copilot session handling * broken or accidental runs permanently clutter the workspace * users are forced into “delete everything or keep the mess” behavior That is not a minor UX inconvenience. It is a fundamental session management failure. Expected behavior: * every Codex session must be individually deletable * Codex session handling must be clearly separated from Copilot chat/session storage * users must have transparent control over session lifecycle: create, archive, delete Current behavior: * Codex appears entangled with Copilot chat infrastructure * individual threads cannot be removed * the user cannot maintain a clean working environment without risking unrelated chat history This needs to be fixed. The current state is not acceptable for a coding workflow. Environment: * VS Code * WSL Ubuntu * OpenAI Codex extension * multi-project workspace with active ongoing chats that must not be lost I expect a concrete response on how this will be addressed asap, because otherwise I will have to look for a proper alternative tool. title: Dragging a file into one terminal editor while holding Shift inserts the path into other terminal editors as well
description:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- OS Version: Windows 10 Home China 26200

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code on Windows.
2. Create two integrated terminals in the editor area (not in the panel). In my case both use Git Bash.
3. Make sure they are separate shell sessions, for example `echo $$` returns different PIDs in terminal A and terminal B.
4. Leave both terminals idle.
5. From the Explorer, drag a file into terminal A while holding Shift so VS Code inserts the file path into the terminal instead of opening the file in the editor.
6. Observe that terminal B also receives the same file path even though the file was only dropped on terminal A.

Expected:
Only terminal A should receive the dropped file path.

Actual:
Both terminal editor instances receive the same file path.

Notes:
- Reproduces with `code --disable-extensions`.
- This is specifically about terminals opened in the editor area.
- If I do not hold Shift, the file opens in the editor, which is expected.
- The two terminals are not the same shell session. Example PIDs from `echo $$`: A=8554, B=8599.
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.116.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

I updated to VS Code 1.116.0 (560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679) on Windows 10 Home China 26200 and the issue still reproduces with the same steps.

It also still reproduces with extensions disabled.

The two editor-area terminals are still separate shell sessions (different `echo $$` PIDs).

title: Copilot Chat: Is anyone using Auto and having success with it?
description:
In my testing, Auto choose models I would never choose myself. That said, I use Opus 4.6 for almost everything, as I don't think any other model really cuts it.

It's probably a case of me not being in the target group for Auto. I think I want more control over model selection, not less. E.g. I love that I can select reasoning level from the chat interface. 

But my question remains. Are people having success with Auto, and I am just holding things wrong?
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! We figured it's missing some basic information or in some other way doesn't follow our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting). Please take the time to review these and update the issue.

For Copilot Issues, be sure to visit our [Copilot-specific guidelines](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Copilot-Issues) page for details on the necessary information.

Happy Coding!

title: No free models available and now quota reached even it was yesterday 60%
description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:0.41 (2026-03-25)
- VS Code Version: 1.116.0
- OS Version:26.3 (25D125) Tahoe MAC
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode):all
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet):all
- Logs: chat quota reached and no free models, system is useless

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Just open chat ant try to use copilot
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! We figured it's missing some basic information or in some other way doesn't follow our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting). Please take the time to review these and update the issue.

For Copilot Issues, be sure to visit our [Copilot-specific guidelines](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Copilot-Issues) page for details on the necessary information.

Happy Coding!

@markkusiermala-sketch Approximately how many chat messages did you use between the time it was 60% yesterday and when you last checked? (This also includes Copilot commit message summaries, these are part of your quota.)

Not many chat messages. 
This started suddenly (about 50% requests left) it start to say that "models is not available". Then next day it works again, I tried to sign out, reinstall plugin. Yesterday acting was also strange it works suddenly and then it start to say that no premium requests available. But any way there must be those free models available and I cannot see them.

@markkusiermala-sketch There are no models that are _completely_ free; unfortunately, a "quota" system has been implemented for non-subscribers. I would suggest using a model from an official website such as [Gemini](https://www.gemini.google.com) or [Claude](https://www.claude.ai); while these don't work as well for analyzing codebases, they can still analyze open-source codebases extremely effectively. Another alternative is subscribing to Claude Pro and using Claude Code from the terminal or inside VSCode.

I’m a paying Copilot user (10 USD/month), and my subscription has been active and working normally until a couple of days ago. At that time, my usage was nowhere near 100%, and I was using models that should not consume premium request credits.
Despite this, Copilot suddenly started showing “quota full” errors and hiding the non‑premium models. This behavior does not match my actual usage, and it has never happened before. It looks like the quota system is either miscalculating my usage or failing to switch to the models that are supposed to remain available without consuming premium credits.
Could you please check my account’s quota state and correct it if it is stuck or incorrectly depleted?
Thanks.
@markkusiermala-sketch I am not a maintainer, but I believe there's a support team to contact if this issue arises. Could someone like @mjbvz weight in on this?

title: Quota limit 100% event it is not and and free models not available!

title: PostToolUse hook additionalContext not injected into same-turn model request
description:
## Bug

A `PostToolUse` hook that returns `additionalContext` successfully executes and returns the context, but the context is **not injected into the tool result** for the model request on the same turn. It only becomes visible in subsequent turns when conversation history is replayed.

This means a hook designed to influence the model's immediate next action (e.g., "before presenting this plan, run a review skill") has no effect — the model never sees the instruction on the turn it matters.

## Reproduction

1. Configure a `PostToolUse` hook that matches the `memory` tool and returns `additionalContext`:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "memory",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "node ${PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/remind-plan-review.mts"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

2. The hook script outputs:
```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "PostToolUse",
    "additionalContext": "Before presenting the plan to the user, you MUST use the 'review-plan' skill"
  }
}
```

3. Trigger the hook by having the agent call the `memory` tool to create a `plan.md` file.

## Evidence from debug logs

| Timestamp (ms) | Event | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| 604432 | `tool_call` (memory) | Completed successfully |
| 604457 | `hook` (PostToolUse) | Ran in 118ms, `resultKind: "success"`, returned `additionalContext` |
| 604467 | `llm_request` | Sent to model — **tool result contains only** `"File created successfully at: /memories/session/plan.md"` |

- The `additionalContext` text ("Before presenting the plan...") appears **0 times** in the `inputMessages` of the same-turn `llm_request` (line 33 of `main.jsonl`).
- The same text appears in **every subsequent** `llm_request` (line 44+), because the conversation history now includes the `PostToolUse-context` annotation from the transcript.

## Expected behavior

The `additionalContext` from a `PostToolUse` hook should be appended to the tool result **before** the next model request on the same turn, so the model can act on it immediately.

## Actual behavior

The `additionalContext` is recorded in the transcript but not injected into the tool result for the same-turn model call. It only becomes visible on the next user message turn, making it too late to influence the model's behavior.

## Version

- VS Code Insiders (version from session log)
- Copilot Chat extension
comments: Hooks for testing: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-team-kit/pull/14

title: disable the ask_questions tool (or fix it!)
description: Type: Feature Request The ask_questions tool is terrible UX, IMO. It makes it easy to click, but not easy to inject nuance. The AI thinks it knows what my answer should be, and it's often wrong. Sometimes it gives me an option for a free-form answer, but even that option is bad UX because it requires me to give it a short-form answer as part of a larger set, without allowing me to stop it part way through the questions and redirect it. Any preliminary thinking or partial response is thrown away if I cancel. And worse, I can't put settings in ~/.config/vscode/settings.json that are reliably injected to disable ask_questions; I've tried creating one for tool use, one for code generation, and one for general chat. All are not scanned reliably. The models just use ask_questions anyway. The design of this UX is encouraging sloppy coding, not careful, thoughtful interactions with nuance. It is making the work product more careless, not more precise, because it is encouraging easy answers that are approximately right, but not really. Either fix the tool so I have better options, or fix the override settings so they're actually honored. Or give me a new setting that lets me disable the tool that is super frustrating. Extension version: 0.37.5 VS Code version: Code 1.109.2 (591199df409fbf59b4b52d5ad4ee0470152a9b31, 2026-02-10T20:18:23.520Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2

title: MCP tool results for large files are silently truncated with a vscode-chat-response-resource:// hint that does not work
description:
### Description

When a GitHub MCP `get_file_contents` tool call returns a large file (50KB–999KB, under the MCP server's 1MB hard limit), VS Code's context window manager truncates the tool result before it reaches the LLM and marks the response `isTruncated=true`.

VS Code then displays the following hint to the agent/user:

> "you can read the full contents of any truncated resources by passing their URIs as the absolutePath to the read_file tool"

The hint provides a `vscode-chat-response-resource://` URI. However, passing this URI to `read_file` (VS Code's built-in file reading tool) **does not work**. It returns:

> "File is outside of the workspace, and not open in an editor, and can't be read."

The hint instructs a recovery action that is impossible to perform. This causes AI agents to enter a confused state where they have been told a recovery path exists, attempt it, fail, and have no clear way forward.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code with the GitHub MCP server configured
2. In Copilot Chat, use the `get_file_contents` tool to fetch a file between 50KB and 999KB from a GitHub repository (e.g., a large C# utility file ~120KB)
3. Observe that VS Code truncates the result and marks it `isTruncated=true`
4. Observe the hint displayed: "you can read the full contents of any truncated resources by passing their URIs as the absolutePath to the read_file tool" along with a `vscode-chat-response-resource://...` URI
5. Attempt to call `read_file` with that URI as the `absolutePath` parameter
6. Observe the error: "File is outside of the workspace, and not open in an editor, and can't be read."

**Reproduction files used:**
- `PQA_Training_Services.cs` (~120KB) from `PremierInc/pqa-browser-base-framework`
- `Browser.Base.Framework.xml` (~233KB) from same repo

Both triggered the truncation + broken hint on the same session.

### Expected Behavior

Either:
- (a) The `vscode-chat-response-resource://` URI is readable via `read_file`, allowing the agent to retrieve the full content as the hint promises, **OR**
- (b) The hint is removed entirely and replaced with an honest message such as: "This file is too large to include in full. Use `search_code` with specific search terms to find the content you need."

### Actual Behavior

The hint is displayed but the suggested action is impossible. `read_file` with the URI returns an error. The agent has no actionable recovery path from the hint.

### Additional Context

- This appears to be an incomplete or abandoned feature — the URI scheme (`vscode-chat-response-resource://`) exists in VS Code internals but is not exposed as a readable file path to tools
- Related community discussion: https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/164353 (even 14.4KB files truncated)
- Related community discussion: https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/169224 ("Copilot's context pipeline isn't currently designed to automatically ingest arbitrary file contents")
- Separate but related: copilot-cli has a distinct 10KB truncation bug (https://github.com/github/copilot-cli/issues/1732) — that is a different issue, not this one

### Copilot Chat Extension Version
- VS Code Version: 1.116.0
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent/ask
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Opus
- Logs:
comments:
Thanks for raising this! A few questions that might help narrow it down:

1. Does this work on a fresh install?
2. What does the full error trace show?
3. Have you tried the workaround mentioned in similar issues?

Let me know what you find!

Hi, thanks for the questions!

1. Does this work on a fresh install?
It does not work on a fresh install. The vscode-chat-response-resource:// URI scheme fails deterministically every time read_file is called with it — it's not flaky or environment-dependent. The behavior is reproducible across sessions.

2. What does the full error trace show?
There is no stack trace — the failure is a tool-level response, not an exception. The complete output returned to the agent when calling read_file with a vscode-chat-response-resource:// URI is exactly:

"File is outside of the workspace, and not open in an editor, and can't be read."

That is the entire response. No additional detail is surfaced to the agent.

3. Have you tried the workaround mentioned in similar issues?
Could you point me to the specific issue? I wasn't able to find a prior report for this exact scenario. The hint itself is the intended recovery path — there is no other workaround built into the flow. Our actual workaround is to ignore the hint entirely and use search_code with multiple targeted queries to reconstruct the file content from fragments, which is not ideal but functional.

title: Provide a built-in way to route arbitrary external links to the integrated browser
description:
### Problem

VS Code has an integrated browser, but there does not appear to be a documented built-in way for arbitrary external `http/https` links to open there when they come through normal external-link flows.

As far as I can tell today:
- `workbench.browser.openLocalhostLinks` only covers localhost links
- `workbench.externalBrowser` is documented for choosing an external browser, not the integrated browser
- extensions using `vscode.env.openExternal(...)` still send normal external links to the system browser unless they implement extra opener logic themselves

### Feature request

Please add a documented built-in way for users and extensions to route arbitrary external `http/https` links to the integrated browser.

Possible shapes:
- allow `workbench.externalBrowser` to target the integrated browser
- add a separate setting for opening external links in the integrated browser
- add a first-class API/option so extensions can request "open this external URL in the integrated browser"

### Why this would help

Extensions such as OpenAI Codex surface documentation and reference links inside the editor UI. Being able to keep those links inside VS Code would reduce context switching and make the integrated browser useful beyond localhost and preview flows.

### Related issues / why I think this is broader

I found #300003, which asks for markdown links to open in the integrated browser. That request is close, but narrower than what I'm asking for here.

This issue is about a broader built-in capability for arbitrary external links, including extension-driven flows such as `openExternal(...)`, not only markdown editor behavior.

Related background:
- #300003
- #109277
- #114962

### Environment

- VS Code: `1.116.0`
- Platform: Linux x64
comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->
This feature request is now a candidate for our backlog. The community has 60 days to [upvote](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging#up-voting-a-feature-request) the issue. If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

More than that, there's no way to route an arbitrary `file:///` link to the browser.

I want to view the sonarqube report without going through the loops of copypaste and command fiddling. Especially not when I'm developing remotely. And the file://// link points inside the remote host connected through SSH, or inside a dev container.

> * add a first-class API/option so extensions can request "open this external URL in the integrated browser"

This is how my extension achieves this;

```
vscode.commands.executeCommand("workbench.action.browser.open", uri.toString(true)):
```

title: gpt-5.3-codex returns HTTP 500 in VS Code stable (Copilot Chat)
description:
# gpt-5.3-codex HTTP 500 in VS Code Stable

## Summary
Selecting `gpt-5.3-codex` in VS Code stable fails with HTTP 500. The same account and machine can use `gpt-5.2-codex` successfully, and `gpt-5.3-codex` works in VS Code Insiders. The failure appears specific to the 5.3 deployment on stable.

## Environment
- OS: Linux
- VS Code (stable): 1.116.0
- GitHub Copilot Chat extension: 0.44.1
- Account SKU: free_educational_quota
- Date/time: 2026-04-18 00:17 UTC (approx)

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open VS Code stable 1.116.0 with Copilot Chat 0.44.1.
2. Select model `gpt-5.3-codex` in Copilot Chat.
3. Send any prompt.
4. Observe failure.
5. Switch to `gpt-5.2-codex` or use VS Code Insiders and it works.

## Expected Result
Copilot Chat returns a response using `gpt-5.3-codex`.

## Actual Result
Copilot Chat fails with an error message: "Sorry, your request failed. Please try again." The backend reports HTTP 500.

## Request IDs
- Copilot Request id: dc5b6e92-c73a-4964-8bc4-1ae6092fc170
- GitHub Request Id: D6DA:3EBB3:1D8A6C9:2040C16:69E27955

## Relevant Logs (Copilot Chat)
```
2026-04-18 00:17:55.841 [info] Request ID for failed request: dc5b6e92-c73a-4964-8bc4-1ae6092fc170
2026-04-18 00:17:55.842 [error] Server error: 500 Internal Server Error
2026-04-18 00:17:55.843 [info] Retrying chat request with default fetcher after: Server error: 500
2026-04-18 00:17:57.124 [info] CAPI ping successful, proceeding with chat request retry...
2026-04-18 00:18:04.060 [error] Server error: 500 Internal Server Error
2026-04-18 00:18:04.064 [info] ccreq:cc4399f2.copilotmd | failed | gpt-5.3-codex | 6936ms | [retry-server-error-panel/editAgent]
2026-04-18 00:18:04.064 [info] ccreq:b8958a45.copilotmd | failed | gpt-5.3-codex | 19164ms | [panel/editAgent]
2026-04-18 00:19:24.179 [info] ccreq:e48e2003.copilotmd | success | gpt-5.2-codex | 9596ms | [panel/editAgent]
```

## Additional Observations
- CAPI ping succeeds right before the retry, suggesting network connectivity is fine.
- The issue appears specific to `gpt-5.3-codex` on the stable channel.
comments: This issue is a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/253136. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

title: Inline chat does not produce code while code is running in jupyter notebooks.
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version:   1.116.0
- OS Version: vscode on the web
- copilot chat version: 0.44.1

Steps to Reproduce:

1. In a jupyter notebook run a cell (that takes some time to run)
2. Using the inline chat (ctrl + I), ask to modify a different cell. This results in waiting for all cells to finish running. 

This is not desirable since it is valid to change different parts of the notebook while a cell is running.

title: `send_to_terminal` auto approval handling improvements
description:
The `send_to_terminal` tool is almost exclusively invoked after the `ask_questions` tool has been used to seek user's confirmation. In that case, the command has already been approved, so doesn't need to go through the usual auto approval rules. 

However, if it gets invoked directly (not via ask questions), auto approval rules are not applied, but should be.
comments: I had yolo mode enabled

title: Agents: Enable side by side file viewing
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Participants had significant difficulty setting things up so that they could view the content of a file at the same time as their conversation with the agent. The modal viewer blocked the chat, forcing most participants into a back and forth between the chat and the modal. Is there a better way that we can support viewing file contents and chat at the same time?
comments: Hi @stevencl. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

Thanks for filing! I believe the updated default behavior of the Changes panel addresses this, and we're discussing further potential updates here: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310853. What do you think about continuing discussion in the linked issue?

title: Agents: Session scoping
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
In the recent study one participant started a local workspace session. Once they were done with that they started working on a cloud repo but reused the session. As a result, while the agent was able to read and write to the cloud repo, the file list was not updated with the new context. This meant that it was difficult for the participant to see new files that were created or browse the files that had been modified. Also, the agent committed directly to main, rather than creating a PR.
comments: Hi @stevencl. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.
Thanks for filing this! Do you have more details (i.e. a screenshot(s) or example steps) on how they started work on a cloud repo but reused a different session?

title: Agents: Increase visibility of branch/worktree
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Display the active branch and worktree prominently at all times, and clearly show where merges will land before the user approves them. Participants in the study could not easily tell which branch they were in after their initial prompt. The fact that a worktree was created (in the local workspace context) was not easily observed as it is shown in the chat conversation but quickly scrolls out of view.
comments: Hi @stevencl. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

Thanks for filing - I've been thinking about this too (and especially interested in it in scenarios like https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309537). Looping in @lszomoru and @hawkticehurst for thoughts.

Looping in @justschen as we were just discussing this.

My current suggestion: persisting worktree and branch name at the top of the chat output, between the first user message and first agent response (so that it's always visible there, but doesn't take up extra room at the bottom with dropdowns that aren't interactive anymore once the session is ongoing / complete).

title: Agents: Surface the handoff between Agent app and the PR
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
When the agent creates a PR, show an explicit in-app banner with next steps and a direct link. Participants in the study did not realise that they could only continue interacting with the agent/PR on GH.com. They assumed that the fact that their subsequent prompts were not being accepted was because they had run out of quota, had typed a prompt that was too long, or some other mistake they had made. Don't rely on email notifications that participants may not associate with the Agents app.
comments: Hi @stevencl. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

title: Agents: Make the cloud/local choice transparent
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
On first launch, when selecting a workspace, clearly explain that cloud repos trigger a PR-based workflow on GitHub.com, not in-app iteration. Users need to understand what they're choosing before they choose it, including the sharing implications of a draft PR.
comments: Hi @stevencl. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.
Thanks for filing. I opened https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310789 yesterday - I wonder if we could combine these two issues. What do you think?

comments:
## Stable Release Anomaly Details

- **Anomaly type**: `new`
- **Previous stable release**: [1.115.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/85812010-a12a-2d00-8874-3c0b4cc4bdbb?product=VSCode&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) — not present
- **Current stable release**: [1.116.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/85812010-a12a-2d00-8874-3c0b4cc4bdbb?product=VSCode&commit=560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679) — 11.3K users
- **Last release without this error**: [1.116.0-insider (47add6df)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/85812010-a12a-2d00-8874-3c0b4cc4bdbb?product=VSCode&commit=47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0)
- **First release with this error**: [1.116.0-insider (1dfb3b9d)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/85812010-a12a-2d00-8874-3c0b4cc4bdbb?product=VSCode&commit=1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681)
- **Commit range**: [47add6df...1dfb3b9d](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0...1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681)

### Analysis

This is a `popular` listener leak in the **notebook cell output rendering** path: `event.ts` → `menuService.ts:437` → `toolbar.ts:377` → `cellOutput.ts._attachToolbar` → `codeCell.ts` → `cellRenderer.ts`. The leak was introduced between the Apr 10 and Apr 13 insider builds, and is now affecting 11,327 stable users.

The deminified stack shows `CellOutputElement._attachToolbar()` in `cellOutput.ts:349` subscribes to a menu's `onDidChange`, which chains through `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` → `MenuImpl` → `PersistedMenuHideState._onDidChange` singleton emitter. Each notebook cell output creates a new `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` (and thus a new `MenuImpl` listener) when rendering output toolbars. With many notebook cells, this pushes the global leak threshold of 175.

This is the same `PersistedMenuHideState._onDidChange` threshold-crossing pattern as #308255 (search results view), but through the notebook cell output path instead.

> Generated by [errors-stable-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24573108998) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo:microsoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id:+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-stable-scan%22&type=issues)

> Generated by [errors-stable-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24573108998/agentic_workflow) · ● 7.6M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-stable-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-stable-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24573108998, workflow_id: errors-stable-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24573108998 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-stable-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `potential listener LEAK detected, popular` fires on the singleton `PersistedMenuHideState._onDidChange` emitter in `menuService.ts`. Every `MenuImpl` instance that has an active `onDidChange` listener subscribes to this shared emitter via a lazy listener pattern. The notebook cell output rendering path creates a `MenuImpl` per cell output toolbar (`CellOutputElement._attachToolbar()` at `cellOutput.ts:335`), and when combined with other menu subscribers across the application, the total listener count on the singleton exceeds the global leak threshold of **175**. This is the same root cause as #308255 (search results view leak) — a threshold-crossing event, not an actual resource leak. Currently affecting **11.3K stable users** across all platforms. ### Culprit Commit No single commit in the range [`47add6df...1dfb3b9d`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0...1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681) directly modifies the affected files (`cellOutput.ts`, `menuService.ts`, `event.ts`). This is a **threshold-crossing event**: the ~100 commits in the range include multiple Sessions/Agents UI additions that cumulatively add new menus and toolbars to the application, each subscribing to the shared `PersistedMenuHideState._onDidChange` singleton. The notebook cell output toolbar path is the "straw that broke the camel's back" — it creates a `MenuImpl` per visible cell output, and the total active `MenuImpl` count across all views now exceeds 175. This matches the analysis from #308255, where `@bryanchen-d` confirmed: > this is more a false alarm: the global leak threshold is set to 175 [...] It just wasn't tuned for a scenario where [...] toolbars [are created] across hundreds of [...] rows. This is a false positive from too many legitimate subscribers, not an actual leak. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Scroll as NotebookCellListView
onScroll → render participant Renderer as CodeCellRenderer
renderElement() participant CodeCell as CodeCell
constructor participant Output as CellOutputElement
._attachToolbar() participant Menu as MenuImpl
(per cell output) participant Singleton as PersistedMenuHideState
._onDidChange (singleton) participant LeakMon as LeakageMonitor Scroll->>Renderer: insertItemInDOM → renderElement Renderer->>CodeCell: createInstance(CodeCell) CodeCell->>Output: _outputContainerRenderer.render() Output->>Menu: menuService.createMenu(NotebookOutputToolbar) Output->>Menu: menu.onDidChange(updateMenuToolbar) Note over Menu: First listener →
onWillAddFirstListener Menu->>Singleton: hiddenStates.onDidChange(callback) Note over Singleton: ⚠️ Root cause:
Singleton emitter with default
threshold (175), receives 1
listener per active MenuImpl Singleton->>LeakMon: check(stack, listenerCount) Note over LeakMon: 💥 listenerCount ≥ 175
Error: "potential listener
LEAK detected, popular" ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts:59-60` | **Root cause** — `PersistedMenuHideState._onDidChange` singleton `Emitter` with no custom leak threshold (uses global 175) | | `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts:427-440` | Each `MenuImpl.startLazyListener()` subscribes to `hiddenStates.onDidChange` when its own `onDidChange` gets first listener | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/cellOutput.ts:335` | `CellOutputElement._attachToolbar()` creates a `MenuImpl` per cell output — **leak trigger** | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/cellOutput.ts:349` | Subscribes to `menu.onDidChange`, activating the lazy listener chain | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/cellOutput.ts:211-212` | First cell output (index 0) always attaches toolbar eagerly; others lazy-attach on visibility | | `src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1008-1034` | `LeakageMonitor.check()` — fires leak error when `listenerCount ≥ threshold` | | `src/vs/workbench/browser/workbench.ts:135` | Sets `_globalLeakWarningThreshold` to 175 | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code 1.116.0 (stable) 2. Open a Jupyter notebook with **175+ code cells** that each have at least one output 3. Scroll through the notebook to render many cells 4. The leak error fires when the total count of `MenuImpl` instances subscribing to `hiddenStates.onDidChange` exceeds 175 5. The error triggers more easily when VS Code has other menus active (search results, editor toolbars, sidebar panels) in addition to the notebook Note: The threshold is **global** — notebook cell outputs are not the only subscribers. The error can also trigger with fewer notebook cells if the user has other views open that create many `MenuImpl` instances (e.g., search results with many results, as in #308255). ### Fix Proposal **Option A (quick fix — shared with #308255)** — Increase the leak threshold on the singleton emitter: **Fix location**: `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` → `PersistedMenuHideState` class, line 59 **Approach**: Change `new Emitter()` to `new Emitter({ leakWarningThreshold: 500 })`. This emitter is a singleton that legitimately receives one listener per active `MenuImpl` across the entire application. With many views creating menus (notebook cells, search results, editor toolbars, agents panels), 175 is too low. This is the same fix proposed in #308255. **Option B (notebook-specific optimization)** — Reduce menu instances in the notebook cell output path: **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/cellOutput.ts` → `CellOutputElement._attachToolbar()` **Approach**: Instead of creating a new `MenuImpl` per cell output, use a shared menu instance across cell outputs (similar to how cell toolbar parts are shared per template). Alternatively, defer menu creation until the user hovers over the output area, since the toolbar is primarily for secondary actions. This would reduce the notebook's contribution to the singleton listener count from O(cells) to O(1) or O(templates). **Option C (hybrid)** — Apply both: raise the threshold to prevent false positives, and optimize the notebook path for efficiency. ### Related Issues - #308255 — Same root cause (`PersistedMenuHideState._onDidChange` threshold crossing) through the search results view path. Confirmed as a false alarm. ### Recommended Owner `@amunger` — Notebook core/view area owner (per `working-areas.md`). The structural fix (Option B) requires notebook-specific changes to reduce menu instance creation. For Option A (menuService threshold), coordination with `@jrieken` (platform owner) may be needed. Note that #308255 tracks the same underlying issue from the search results view perspective. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24573545632/agentic_workflow) · ● 13M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## Stable Release Anomaly Details

- **Anomaly type**: `new`
- **Previous stable release**: [1.115.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/6bdf9085-0616-14b3-0521-faed3435f305?product=VSCode&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) — not present
- **Current stable release**: [1.116.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/6bdf9085-0616-14b3-0521-faed3435f305?product=VSCode&commit=560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679) — 21.6K users
- **Last release without this error**: [1.116.0-insider (ae130017)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/6bdf9085-0616-14b3-0521-faed3435f305?product=VSCode&commit=ae130017f8afe532557dbb8539a6ef3bdaec6389)
- **First release with this error**: [1.116.0 (560a9dba)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/6bdf9085-0616-14b3-0521-faed3435f305?product=VSCode&commit=560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679)
- **Commit range**: [ae130017...560a9dba](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/ae130017f8afe532557dbb8539a6ef3bdaec6389...560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679)

### Analysis

This error is **stable-only** — it does not appear in any Insider build. The ENOENT occurs when the extension host tries to resolve the path for `github.copilot-chat-0.44.1` during `_createExtensionPathIndex`. The error affects 21,603 users (bucket `6bdf9085`) plus an additional 17,761 users in a related bucket (`1aa14df0`) with the same error through `_realPathExtensionUri`.

The stack trace shows `pfs.ts` → `extHostExtensionService.ts._createExtensionPathIndex`, indicating the extension directory path is expected but does not exist on disk. Since this error only appears in the stable channel and is tied to a specific Copilot Chat extension version (0.44.1), this is likely a packaging or deployment timing issue where the extension path is referenced before the extension files are fully available.

title: Make `diffEditor.ignoreTrimWhitespace` false by default
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

The option `diffEditor.ignoreTrimWhitespace`, which is set to `true` by default, hides changes in the diff editor when a line remains the same in content but the indentation (leading whitespaces) change.

This is dangerous if you're restructuring logic, most notably with early returns because VS Code does not show it as a diff at all, making me think it was broken the first time it happened, and I accidentally committed code with wrong indentation.

I would like to request for this option to be false by default unless users explicitly disable it, since it is pretty important and makes no sense to be enabled by default.
comments:
it should already be false by default for YAML

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/8dac5997541f1232b54bb8b453ce9a76af73da8f/extensions/yaml/package.json#L92-L97

why does it need to be false for JSON?
Isn't this an editor-wide setting?

> Isn't this an editor-wide setting?

Settings can be language specific

https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/settings#_language-specific-editor-settings

title: Copilot Chat crashes when repo remote has only pushurl (TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim'))
description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.44.1
- VS Code Version: 1.116.0
- OS Version: Windows 11 25H2
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): I tried both Ask and Agent mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Tested several, seems to be all of them, and unrelated to model selection.
- Logs:
Key lines:
  - CodeSearchRepoTracker.openRepo(...). Error during remote resolution: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')
  - Stack frames point at c:\Users\….vscode\extensions\github.copilot-chat-0.44.1\dist\extension.js (multiple occurrences)

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Create or open a git repo whose `.git/config` has an `origin` remote with only a `pushurl` and no `url`. Example minimal problematic config:
```
   [remote "origin"]
       pushurl = no-push
```
2. Open the repo in VS Code with GitHub Copilot Chat installed and signed in.
3. Send any message via Copilot Chat (Ask or Agent). The extension throws the TypeError and the chat request fails immediately.


Hey!
So when I send a message of any kind in the copilot chat extension, seemingly with any agent type and/or model selected, it will instantly respond with "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')" error, the error is shown in a similar style as a reply in the chat itself.
I made sure everything is up to date and have restarted things.

If I remove the
```
   [remote "origin"]
       pushurl = no-push
```
from the .git/config file where the full config is like this
```
[core]
	repositoryformatversion = 0
	filemode = false
	bare = false
	logallrefupdates = true
	symlinks = false
	ignorecase = true
[remote "origin"]
    pushurl = no-push
```
then it works again, intentionally configured this way to make sure a git push would fail so that the project would not be pushed by accident, but seems like the extension does not check if url exists or not before it tries to trim it and fails, so I assume a guard on that trim function would solve the problem? (Might be more stuff that is not checking if the url exists or not, but thats the obvious one I can spot)
Claude Code extension does not produce this error on the same project, so likely a Copilot Chat bug with this particular type of git config set up.
Copilot Chat works fine in projects without the git config, or where it has a url and/or a pushurl = no-push, but if its missing the url, but has pushurl = no-push then it triggers the error.

Hope this helps! I am just leaving the origin block out of the config for now, so its not preventing/blocking use of the tool.
comments:
Thanks for raising this! A few questions that might help narrow it down:

1. Does this work on a fresh install?
2. What does the full error trace show?
3. Have you tried the workaround mentioned in similar issues?

Let me know what you find!

Disable GitHub Copilot Chat extension 2. Try to enable GitHub Copilot Chat extension for workspace only.
comments: Looks like #310143

title: VS Code Copilot Chat on macOS arm64 fails with Language model unavailable and ByteString/65279 errors before model fetch
description:
Environment:
- VS Code `1.116.0`
- macOS Apple Silicon
- GitHub Copilot Chat `0.44.0` / `0.44.1`
- GitHub Copilot Pro account
- Reproduced on April 17, 2026

Symptom:
- Copilot Chat shows `Language model unavailable`
- Model picker is stuck on `Auto`
- Premium models sometimes show `Upgrade` incorrectly even though the account is Pro
- Chat cannot start at all

Key log signature:
- Copilot fails before model resolution/fetch completes
- Repeated low-level error:

```text
TypeError: Cannot convert argument to a ByteString because the character at index 0 has a value of 65279 which is greater than 255.
```

Representative log excerpt:

```text
2026-04-17 17:32:06.113 [info] Logged in as apricot-peoplewiz
2026-04-17 17:32:06.113 [info] Using the Electron fetcher.
2026-04-17 17:32:06.113 [error] TypeError: Cannot convert argument to a ByteString because the character at index 0 has a value of 65279 which is greater than 255.
    at webidl.converters.ByteString (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:4005:17)
    at Object.record<ByteString, ByteString> (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:3912:32)
    at webidl.converters.HeadersInit (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:9210:67)
    at Object.RequestInit (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:3968:21)
    at new Request (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:9767:34)
    at fetchWithSession (node:electron/js2c/utility_init:2:358)
    at t.fetch [as _fetchImpl] (...)
    ...
2026-04-17 17:32:06.528 [error] ... Failed to fetch models
2026-04-17 17:32:11.302 [error] Error: Language model unavailable
```

What I already tried:
- signed out / signed back in
- upgraded from Copilot Free to Copilot Pro
- reinstalled VS Code
- downgraded VS Code to older version
- used a fresh `--user-data-dir`
- wiped `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User`
- removed custom language model config
- removed custom Copilot-related settings
- uninstalled marketplace `github.copilot-chat`
- removed `~/.vscode/extensions/github.copilot-chat-0.44.1`
- removed stale extension registry entries
- disabled Settings Sync
- verified network connectivity:
  - `curl https://api.githubcopilot.com/_ping` returns `OK`
- issue still reproduces

Important observation:
- Earlier investigation suggested a BOM-like `65279` value may be getting into request headers / extension state.
- Even after wiping local user data, the same `ByteString` failure reappears on startup before model fetch completes.

Expected:
- Copilot Chat should load models normally and allow `Auto` / Pro models to work.

Actual:
- Model fetch fails immediately with `ByteString`/`65279`
- chat always ends in `Language model unavailable`

Relevant files:
- `~/Library/Application Support/Code/logs/20260417T173203/window1/exthost/GitHub.copilot-chat/GitHub Copilot Chat.log`
- `~/Library/Application Support/Code/logs/20260417T173203/window1/exthost/exthost.log`

If you want, I can also turn this into a shorter GitHub-issue version and a separate one for GitHub Support.
comments: This issue is a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/253137. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

title: Copilot ==Waist of money. It's full of bug
description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates --> 
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->
Copilot Chat Extension Version: 109.5 / 116
VS Code Version:  0.37.9
OS Version: Linux x64 6.17.0-20-generic
Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Chat / Edit
Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 4.5/4.6 Sonnet/Opus): Claude, Gemini, Others
Logs: (attach if available)

Steps to Reproduce:
- Open any project in VS Code
- Open Copilot Chat and select model (Claude / Gemini / others)
- Ask to modify or refactor a component (including sub-components)
- Observe behavior based on the version

Issues by Version:
**Version 109.5**

Claude Model:

Very slow response
Skips tasks after clarification
Breaks already correct code

Gemini Model:

Partially completes tasks
Modifies only main component, ignores sub-components


**Version 116**

Claude Model:

Invalid string length → request not executed
mgt.clearMarks is not a function
File mention issue (mentioned files not showing on top)

Unable to edit code, shows tool error:

"I'm encountering a technical issue with the file manipulation tools..."

Other Models:

Says files are modified but no actual changes happen
Expected Result:
Stable and fast response
Complete task execution
Modify all related components/files
No breaking of existing working code
No tool or execution errors
Additional Notes:

Issues persist even after frequent updates.
Recommended to test using Claude model with a multi-file refactor task to reproduce easily.
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.116.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

title: agent chat exposed my sudo password!!!!!!!!!!!!
description: Type: Bug ran a project that had 'npx playwright install-deps' as part of the process, this caused the agent to prompt me to put in my sudo password saying "Q: Please provide your sudo password for `npx playwright install-deps` (input is sent to terminal, not echoed)." it fully echoed my sudo password both while I was typing it and in the chat history!!!!!! this seems really extra bad, please fix :) VS Code version: Code 1.116.0 (560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679, 2026-04-15T00:28:13Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2 A/B Experiments ``` vsliv368:30146709 vswsl492cf:30256860 binariesv615:30325510 nativeloc1:31344060 dwcopilot:31170013 dwoutputs:31242946 copilot_t_ci:31333650 e5gg6876:31282496 pythonrdcb7:31342333 6518g693:31463988 aj953862:31281341 6abeh943:31336334 envsdeactivate2:31464701 cloudbuttont:31379625 3efgi100_wstrepl:31403338 use-responses-api:31390855 je187915:31401257 ec5jj548:31422691 cp_cls_c_966_ss:31454199 4je02754:31466945 c3h7c220:31478652 ge8j1254_inline_auto_hint_haiku:31490510 38bie571_auto:31478678 cp_cls_c_1081:31454833 conptydll_false:31489735 ia-use-proxy-models-svc:31452481 e9c30283:31461165 test_treatment2:31471001 ts_provider_enabled:31458190 control_6dc23131:31495068 idci7584:31464702 e3e4d672:31494082 534a6447_copy:31478748 nes-extended-on:31455476 chat:31457767 8hig5102:31480529 67jbj424:31495046 cpptoolsoff-v2:31475362 i2gc6536:31494041 30450953:31494756 ghj88844:31492074 23c7c724:31491644 client_tst_t:31495907 po_split_t:31488386 ddid_c:31478207 getcmakediagnosticson:31489824 g_20_d1382936:31495671 nes-perm-reb-1:31490410 pro_conc_t:31492543 cp_cls_c_1082:31491634 55165474_sum_c:31495625 nes-async-ew-check-off:31491821 logging_enabled_new:31493367 j0d79568:31494311 output_2x:31496635 ```
comments: A pic, worth 1000 words:
Don't worry, the agent just sees `******`

Sorry, the agent doesn't actually know how things are displayed. We have something new coming that would in theory allow you to take over the terminal and type the password directly, but in this case it looks like it just used the ask_questions tool before it even ran a terminal command.

That's right ^. In Insider's already, you can instead focus the terminal and typing your input directly in the terminal will cancel the questions part.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3268dc09-7b1a-442b-97a3-28836ac45bdb
That's great to hear, thanks for the rapid reply!

title: Inability to select xHigh reasoning level for some models when the Session Target is set to Copilot CLI
description: Type: Bug 1. Select Copilot CLI as the Session Target. 2. Select a model that is at the bottom of the list, like GPT-5.3-Codex or GPT-5.4 mini. 3. Select Reasoning level for the model. 4. Xhigh cannot be selected because the dropdown exits the visible window despite the reasoning level being selectable in the dropdown when the session target is set to Local. Session Target is set to Copilot CLI: Session Target is set to Local: VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.117.0-insider (294552bebf81954a3d25842468ad199d9e8a6239, 2026-04-16T20:21:51+02:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.29565 Modes:
comments: This is the issue in Copilot CLI that originates this: https://github.com/github/copilot-cli/issues/2725. Extra High (xhigh) reasoning effort is now limited in Copilot CLI for some tiers, but it is not limited in Visual Studio Code. I'd like to see an official confirmation about what is included on each tier, and to offer the same reasoning efforts in both applications.

title: Claude Opus 4.7 can be selected in Copilot Chat Agent mode, but requests fail with "Language model unavailable" on Copilot Pro+
description:
- VS Code Extension: GitHub Copilot 1.388.0
- VS Code Version: Version: 1.116.0 Commit: 560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679 Date: 2026-04-15T00:28:13Z (2 days ago)
- OS Version: macOS 26.4.1 (25E253)


Claude Opus 4.7 appears as a selectable model in VS Code GitHub Copilot Chat (Agent mode), but every request fails with `Language model unavailable`.

I am on an active GitHub Copilot Pro+ subscription, so I would expect the model to work if it is shown as selectable.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Open VS Code.
2. Open Copilot Chat.
3. Switch to Agent mode.
4. Select Claude Opus 4.7.
5. Send any prompt.

Expected:
The request is processed with Claude Opus 4.7.

Actual:
The request fails immediately with `Language model unavailable`.
comments: This issue is a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/253137. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

[solution] 
For anyone else who ran into this issue: 
After restarting to install the VS Code update, I had to completely close VS Code and restart it, and then it worked.

title: GPT-5.2 in Copilot CLI session target missing xHigh option
description: Type: Bug 1. Select Copilot CLI as the Session Target. 2. Select GPT-5.2 as the model. 3. Select Reasoning level for the model. 4. Xhigh is missing despite the option being supported by GPT-5.2 when the session target is set to Local. Session Target is set to Copilot CLI: Session Target is set to Local: VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.117.0-insider (294552bebf81954a3d25842468ad199d9e8a6239, 2026-04-16T20:21:51+02:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.29565 Modes:
comments: This also happens when you try to select the reasoning level while you are using Copilot CLI on the console, so it seems that it is an issue within Copilot CLI, at least for me.

This is the issue in Copilot CLI that originates this: https://github.com/github/copilot-cli/issues/2725. Extra High (xhigh) reasoning effort is now limited in Copilot CLI for some tiers, but it is not limited in Visual Studio Code. I'd like to see an official confirmation about what is included on each tier, and to offer the same reasoning efforts in both applications.

title: Stabilize `vscode.proposed.tunnels` API: enabling named portless tunnel aliases for AI-assisted devcontainer workflows
description:
## Summary

`vscode.proposed.tunnels` has been a proposed API since August 2022 (~3.5 years). This issue requests its graduation to the stable API surface, or at minimum a formal plan for stabilization. The ability to programmatically create named portless tunnel aliases is a key building block for modern AI-assisted development workflows.

## Why This Matters Now

### The AI-Assisted Dev Workflow

Effective AI agent-assisted development increasingly requires:

1. **Git worktrees** — one branch per worktree for parallel, isolated development
2. **Dev containers** — per-worktree hermetic environments via `devcontainer.json`
3. **Stable, predictable service endpoints** — so AI agents, CI pipelines, and reviewers can consistently reach the running application without hunting for ephemeral `localhost:<random_port>` assignments

The `workspace.tunnels` proposed API is the *only* mechanism by which a VS Code extension can programmatically create **named, portless tunnel aliases** — converting `localhost:3000` inside a container into a stable `my-app-feature-x.devtunnels.ms` URL.

Without this, the verification step of an AI coding loop is non-deterministic: the URL changes every session, breaking automated reviewers, agents, and integration tests that need a stable endpoint.

### The Devcontainer Standardization Gap

Critically, using `vscode.proposed.tunnels` requires `--enable-proposed-api` to be set on the VS Code Server process. This flag is only configurable via the **local client's** `argv.json`, making it impossible to distribute as part of a `devcontainer.json`. This undermines the core promise of devcontainers — a fully reproducible, shareable environment definition.

(A separate feature request for `devcontainer.json` server arg support has been filed: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-remote-release/issues/11595)

## API Status

- **Created**: August 2, 2022 (PR #155835, by @alexr00)
- **Last modified**: October 2025 (readonly Event property cleanup only)
- **Stable API surface**: No `workspace.tunnels` or `openTunnel` in `vscode.d.ts` today
- **Known "forever proposed" concern**: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/131165

## What Graduation Would Enable

```typescript
// Stable API usage (today requires proposed):
const tunnel = await vscode.workspace.openTunnel({
  remoteAddress: { port: 3000, host: 'localhost' },
  localAddressPort: 3000,
  label: 'my-app-feature-x'  // portless named alias
});
console.log(tunnel.localAddress); // https://my-app-feature-x.devtunnels.ms
```

Combined with a worktree-aware extension reading `devcontainer.json`'s `forwardPorts`, this enables fully automatic, stable endpoint provisioning per feature branch — no manual steps, no port collisions, no ephemeral URLs.

## Request

1. **Stabilize `vscode.proposed.tunnels`** — or a subset of it (e.g. `openTunnel` + `TunnelOptions.label`) that enables named portless aliases
2. If full stabilization is not feasible, **document the path forward** — what concerns remain, what changes are needed?
3. As an interim, consider **allowing extensions published to the Marketplace to opt-in** to this specific proposed API (like the Remote extensions already do internally)

## Related

- Proposed API file: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.tunnels.d.ts
- Portless tunnel extension discussion: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-remote-release/issues/9981
- Forever proposed APIs discussion: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/131165
- devcontainer.json server args feature request: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-remote-release/issues/11595

cc @alexr00 @chrmarti @connor4312
comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->
This feature request is now a candidate for our backlog. The community has 60 days to [upvote](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging#up-voting-a-feature-request) the issue. If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

title: Custom Chat Participants (Agents) cannot invoke MCP tool servers — only built-in participants can
description:
Type: Bug VS Code: latest stable (April 2026) GitHub Copilot Chat extension: latest OS: Windows Description Custom chat participants defined via chat.registerChatParticipant (custom agents configured in copilot-instructions.md or workspace agent definitions) are unable to invoke MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool servers. The MCP tools are only accessible to the built-in default participant (@workspace, etc.) but not to any user-defined custom agents. Steps to Reproduce Configure an MCP tool server in VS Code settings (e.g., via mcp.servers in settings.json). Verify the MCP tools work correctly when used with the default built-in chat participant. Define custom agents/participants (e.g., via workspace-level agent configuration with specialized roles). Invoke one of the custom agents and request it to use an MCP tool. Expected Behavior Custom agents should be able to discover and invoke MCP tool servers the same way the built-in participant does, since they share the same underlying model and tool infrastructure. Actual Behavior MCP tools are not available to custom agents. The agent either ignores the MCP tools entirely or behaves as if they do not exist. Only the default built-in participant can successfully call MCP services. Impact This limitation severely reduces the usefulness of custom agents in multi-role workflows (e.g., tech-lead → architect → backend-dev delegation patterns), where specialized agents need access to external tools (database clients, API testers, code generators) provided via MCP servers. Workaround Currently the only workaround is to fall back to the default participant for any task requiring MCP tool access, which defeats the purpose of role-specialized custom agents. title: Start implement does not exit plan mode
description:
Type: Bug Description I used a session in plan mode to write a feature requirements document. After finishing the document, I clicked Start implement, expecting the session to leave plan mode and begin modifying code. Instead, it kept telling me that it was still in plan mode and could not edit files or start implementation. Expected behavior After clicking Start implement, the session should exit plan mode and switch to implementation mode so it can modify code and continue the task in the same session. Actual behavior The session remains stuck in plan mode. Even after clicking Start implement, it repeatedly says that it is still in plan mode and cannot make code changes. Steps to reproduce Start a chat session in plan mode. Use the session to write a feature requirements or implementation plan document. Click Start implement. Ask the agent to begin editing code. The agent still says it is in plan mode and refuses to modify files. Impact This blocks the normal handoff from planning to implementation in the same session. I have to start a new session or manually hand off the plan instead of continuing directly. title: Restart to Update not working on Mac
description:
Bug: "Restart to apply the latest update" does not restart or apply update on macOS
Environment:

OS: macOS (Darwin arm64 25.2.0) — Apple Silicon
VS Code Version: 1.113.0 (Universal)
Commit: cfbea10c5ffb233ea9177d34726e6056e89913dc
Date: 2026-03-24
Electron: 39.8.3
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
Installation method: Direct download

Steps to Reproduce:

VS Code displays the notification: "Restart Visual Studio Code to apply the latest update."
Click "Update Now" — VS Code closes but does not reopen.
Manually reopen VS Code.
The same update notification appears again. The update was not applied.

Expected Behavior:
Clicking "Update Now" should close VS Code, apply the update, and automatically relaunch. The notification should not reappear.
Actual Behavior:
Clicking "Update Now" simply closes VS Code. It does not relaunch, and the update is not applied. Manually reopening VS Code shows the same notification again, with the version unchanged.
Additional Context:
The only current workaround is to manually download the latest build from code.visualstudio.com and replace the app in /Applications. This suggests VS Code may lack the necessary permissions to overwrite itself and relaunch during the in-app update process on macOS.
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.116.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

title: Slow startup / Open Recent command response even all the extensions disabled
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.116.0 (560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679)
- OS Version: darwin(25.4.0)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VSCode with all the extensions disabled by `code . --disable-extensions`
2. Run `File: Open Recent...` command
3. Sometimes it takes about 10 seconds or more, sometimes it responds quickly

Some notes:

- Possibly related to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/307206
- Not all startups are slow. Sometimes it starts quickly even after killing the process, while other times it is slow even when just opening a new window.
- This started about three to four weeks ago and had never happened before.
- When a slow startup occurs, File: Open Recent does not respond for 10–20 seconds.
- During a slow startup, my GitHub account (for Copilot) and Microsoft account (for sync) are not shown in the account menu at the bottom-left of the window.
- A clean reinstall of VS Code did not resolve the issue.
- VSCode freezes with "discarding backups is taking a bit longer" message if I close the window during the slow startup.

I’d be happy to run any experiments if that would help.

Here is a startup performance report when the issue happened with all the extensions disabled.

[startup_perf.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26807789/startup_perf.txt)
comments:
Observed same issue, but with command palette in general. It took a second or more to draw the menu after I pressed Cmd+Shift+P. Next calls appear to work without noticeable delay.

In earlier releases the reaction was always instant.

I added another clue to the description.

> VSCode freezes with "discarding backups is taking a bit longer" message if I close the window during the slow startup.
