title: Remote SSH + Remote Tunnel = Failed tunnel on wrong machine
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: N/A

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.116.0
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19045

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Start a linux server VM, install dev tools, connect to it with vscode via ssh on 192.168.*.* address.
2. Start a remote tunnel with Github authentication.

The tunnel fails to launch.

Remote Tunnel Service Log
```
2026-04-16 21:17:52.492 [info] Session updated: trbot86 (github) (service=true)
2026-04-16 21:17:52.493 [info] Session token updated: trbot86 (github)

2026-04-16 21:18:25.262 [info] tunnel Spawning: c:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code-tunnel.exe tunnel --accept-server-license-terms --log info --name DESKTOP-QIMB4F5 --parent-process-id 42816

2026-04-16 21:18:25.278 [info] *

2026-04-16 21:18:57.890 [info] [2026-04-16 21:18:57] warn error access singleton, retrying: could not connect to socket/pipe: Os { code: 5, kind: PermissionDenied, message: "Access is denied." }

2026-04-16 21:19:32.500 [info] [2026-04-16 21:19:32] warn error access singleton, retrying: could not connect to socket/pipe: Os { code: 5, kind: PermissionDenied, message: "Access is denied." }

2026-04-16 21:20:05.980 [info] tunnel terminating(60512)

2026-04-16 21:20:38.604 [info] [2026-04-16 21:20:38] warn The tunnel service has been unregistered, but we couldn't find a running tunnel process. You may need to restart or log out and back in to fully stop the tunnel.

2026-04-16 21:20:38.606 [info] uninstallService exit(53660): + 0

2026-04-16 21:26:11.315 [info] tunnel Spawning: c:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code-tunnel.exe tunnel --accept-server-license-terms --log info --name DESKTOP-QIMB4F5 --parent-process-id 42816

2026-04-16 21:26:11.331 [info] *

2026-04-16 21:26:11.331 [info] * By using the software, you agree to

2026-04-16 21:26:11.331 [info] * the Visual Studio Code Server License Terms (https://aka.ms/vscode-server-license) and

2026-04-16 21:26:11.331 [info] * the Microsoft Privacy Statement (https://privacy.microsoft.com/en-US/privacystatement).

2026-04-16 21:26:11.331 [info] *

2026-04-16 21:26:43.934 [info] [2026-04-16 21:26:43] warn error access singleton, retrying: could not connect to socket/pipe: Os { code: 5, kind: PermissionDenied, message: "Access is denied." }

2026-04-16 21:27:18.542 [info] [2026-04-16 21:27:18] warn error access singleton, retrying: could not connect to socket/pipe: Os { code: 5, kind: PermissionDenied, message: "Access is denied." }
```

Asking Codex to fix was successful. It appears remote tunnel is trying to open the tunnel on the local machine, and not the VM I'm SSH'd to, and is failing to accomplish anything meaningful as a result. Codex output:

```
I found two useful things:

The server-side VS Code tunnel service is not installed right now. code tunnel status reports:

{"tunnel":null,"service_installed":false}
Also, your pasted error is almost certainly from the local VS Code desktop side, because it says Windows error code 5 (Access is denied). On Linux the permission code would normally be 13. So the quickest workaround is to start/install the tunnel from this server directly, bypassing the desktop singleton issue.

I started the official install command and it reached the auth picker successfully, then I stopped it so it wasn’t left hanging:

~/.vscode-server/code-560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679 tunnel service install --name qwen1 --accept-server-license-terms --log debug
It asked:

How would you like to log in to Visual Studio Code?
> Microsoft Account
  GitHub Account
Reply with Microsoft or GitHub and I’ll continue the install. After the browser/device-code login, I’ll verify code tunnel status and the user service logs.

9:28 PM
github
9:29 PM

15 previous messages
Done. The GitHub-authenticated tunnel service is installed and running.

Verified status:

{"tunnel":{"name":"qwen1","tunnel":"Connected","last_fail_reason":null},"service_installed":true}
The user service is active as code-tunnel.service, and I also enabled linger for trbot, so the tunnel should keep running after you disconnect from the server.

Useful commands:

~/.vscode-server/code-560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679 tunnel status
~/.vscode-server/code-560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679 tunnel service log
systemctl --user status code-tunnel.service
You should now be able to connect to the tunnel named qwen1 from VS Code / vscode.dev. Next useful thing: try connecting from your local VS Code Remote Tunnels view; if it still shows the Windows-side singleton error, restart the local VS Code app once, because the server tunnel itself is healthy now.
```
comments: Note: I'd also be happy with tunneling to the local machine, not the remote machine, if that actually worked. It just gives these socket errors.

Also, if I don't connect via ssh, and just run vscode locally, and setup a tunnel, I can connect to the local tunnel (e.g., via vscode.dev/tunnel/...). But, when I then try to connect to the VM from there (via connect to machine), I get repeated auth errors.

Full tunnel-service.log:

[tunnel-service.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26806779/tunnel-service.log)

Solved my issue:

- the first tunnel setup failed (not sure why, but _perhaps_ the root cause of that first failed setup was an authentication issue trying to use an SSO Microsoft account to open a service level tunnel, and SSO failing to render on the phone browser, followed by me trying to retry the tunnel, and the tunnel setup crashing / degrading non-gracefully)
- this left a tunnel-stable.lock file in %USERPROFILE%\.vscode\cli even after i shut down vscode completely
- deleting that let me open a tunnel properly (github account this time)

This got me a tunnel to the local machine, not the remote, but that's fine, since a follow-up remote connection worked once I started the tunnel cleanly after killing the lock file. Note the lock file in question was on the local machine, not the remote (despite being on the remote when I started the tunnel).

You might consider hardening the tunnel launch and removing the lock file if there are no running tunnel instances (other than the instance trying to launch and doing this check).

title: Local agent plugin configured via `chat.pluginLocations` is not discovered in remote VS Code
description:
Type: Bug ## Summary A local agent plugin does not get discovered by VS Code/Copilot Chat even though the plugin structure appears valid, `chat.plugins.enabled` is set to `true`, and `chat.pluginLocations` points to the exact plugin directory. This is happening in a remote VS Code server environment. ## Reproduction 1. Create a local agent plugin directory with this structure: ```text plugin.json skills//SKILL.md agents/.agent.md ``` 2. Use a plugin manifest like this: ```json { "name": "my-copilot-plugin", "description": "My Custom skills and agents.", "version": "1.0.0", "author": { "name": "Developer Name", }, "skills": "skills/", "agents": "agents/" } ``` 3. In remote user settings, enable plugins and point `chat.pluginLocations` to the exact plugin directory: ```json { "chat.plugins.enabled": true, "chat.pluginLocations": { "path/to/my-copilot-plugin": true } } ``` 4. Reload the VS Code window. 5. Open Extensions or Copilot Chat and try to discover the plugin. ## Expected Result The local plugin should be discovered and its skills and agents should become available after reload. ## Actual Result The plugin is not discovered. Its skills and agents do not appear in the UI after reload. ## Environment - VS Code version: 1.116.0 - Commit: 560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679 - Copilot Chat extension observed on server: `github.copilot-chat-0.44.1` - OS: Linux - Remote VS Code server environment ## Additional Notes - The plugin repo structure was validated manually. - The skill names are kebab-case and the `SKILL.md` files already contain frontmatter. - The agent file already contains frontmatter and follows the expected naming pattern. - I also added compatibility manifests at `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` and `.plugin/plugin.json` during debugging, but discovery still did not occur. - The original Install Plugin From Source flow also failed earlier with `spawn git ENOENT`, even though `git` exists in the shell environment, which may indicate an extension-host PATH issue in remote environments. - During debugging, no obvious local plugin cache or plugin-manager activity was observed under the remote VS Code user data path, which suggests the local plugin sync/load path may not be running. Extension version: 0.44.1 VS Code version: Code 1.116.0 (560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679, 2026-04-15T00:28:13Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100 Modes: Connection to 'SSH: zsc9' could not be established Connection to 'SSH: zsc9' could not be established Remote OS version: Linux x64 5.14.21-150400.24.158-default Remote OS version: Linux x64 5.14.21-150400.24.158-default Remote OS version: Linux x64 5.14.21-150400.24.158-default Remote OS version: Linux x64 5.14.21-150400.24.158-default

## Regression Details

| Spike factor | **14.3x** increase (3 → 43 users) |
| Baseline release | [1.116.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/b53a37a1-2c29-a9ec-6262-2fda9bd408d2?product=VSCode&commit=560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679) — 3 users |

| Commits between | [560a9dba...98b4d3e2](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679...98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62) |

This error saw a **14.3x spike** in affected users between stable 1.116.0 and insider 1.117.0. The error occurs during gallery installation of `ms-vscode.vscode-websearchforcopilot`, suggesting a potential issue with this extension's packaging or marketplace availability on the latest insider builds.

> Generated by [errors-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24534890504/agentic_workflow) · ● 15.7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24534890504, workflow_id: errors-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24534890504 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Unknown error while installing extension ms-vscode.vscode-websearchforcopilot` is thrown at [`abstractExtensionManagementService.ts:184`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62/src/vs/platform/extensionManagement/common/abstractExtensionManagementService.ts#L184) when `installFromGallery()` receives empty results from `installGalleryExtensions()`. This is caused by a race condition in `installExtensions()`: when a concurrent install for the same extension is already in progress, the result of the awaited concurrent task is silently discarded, causing the caller to see empty results and throw the generic "Unknown error". The 14.3× spike (3→43 users) from 1.116.0→1.117.0-insider suggests concurrent installation of this Copilot web-search extension is happening more frequently in insider builds. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit was identified. The underlying race condition is a **pre-existing bug** in `installExtensions()` (present before the commit range). The spike in 1.117.0-insider is likely triggered by increased concurrent installation of `ms-vscode.vscode-websearchforcopilot` — probably driven by changes in the Copilot extension ecosystem that auto-install this extension from multiple code paths simultaneously. PR [#303888](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/303888) by `@sandy081` (merged 2026-03-22) added redirect-fallback logic to `installFromGallery` but did not address the empty-results case from concurrent installs. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Caller as Extension Installer
(e.g. Copilot) participant IFG as installFromGallery() participant IGE as installGalleryExtensions() participant IE as installExtensions() participant Cache as this.installingExtensions
(global cache) Caller->>IFG: installFromGallery(ext) IFG->>IGE: installGalleryExtensions([{ext}]) IGE->>IE: installExtensions(installableExts) IE->>Cache: get(extensionKey) Note over Cache: ⚠️ Root cause:
Extension already installing
(concurrent request) Cache-->>IE: existingTask found IE->>IE: alreadyRequestedInstallations.push(
existingTask.waitUntilTaskIsFinished()) Note over IE: installingExtensionsMap is EMPTY
for this call IE->>IE: await joinAllSettled(
alreadyRequestedInstallations) Note over IE: ⚠️ Result discarded!
installExtensionResultsMap is empty IE-->>IGE: returns [] (empty) IGE-->>IFG: results = [] Note over IFG: results.find() → undefined
results[0] → undefined IFG->>IFG: 💥 throw ExtensionManagementError
"Unknown error while installing
extension ..." ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/platform/extensionManagement/common/abstractExtensionManagementService.ts` | **Crash site** (line 184) and **root cause** (lines 349-352, 449-451) | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders with the Copilot extension active. 2. Trigger a scenario that causes `ms-vscode.vscode-websearchforcopilot` to be installed (e.g., invoking Copilot web search, or having it auto-installed on startup). 3. While the first install request is in progress, trigger a second concurrent `installFromGallery` call for the same extension (this likely happens automatically via multiple install paths — recommendation notifications, auto-install, or user action). 4. The second call detects the extension is already being installed (line 349), waits for it (line 449-451), but discards the result and returns empty results. 5. `installFromGallery` receives empty results and throws "Unknown error while installing extension ms-vscode.vscode-websearchforcopilot". > **Note**: This is a race condition. Reproduction depends on concurrent install timing. It's more likely when the extension is triggered for installation from multiple code paths during startup. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/platform/extensionManagement/common/abstractExtensionManagementService.ts` → `installExtensions()` **Approach**: When the extension is already being installed and the concurrent install completes successfully, the result should be captured in `installExtensionResultsMap` so the caller receives proper results. Specifically: 1. At line 349-352, when `existingInstallExtensionTask` is found, record the extension's identifier/source/options alongside the promise so results can be mapped back. 2. At lines 449-451, capture the return value of `joinAllSettled(alreadyRequestedInstallations)` and add the resolved `ILocalExtension` results to `installExtensionResultsMap` with the appropriate keys. Alternatively, `installFromGallery` could be modified to handle the empty-results case more gracefully — e.g., if `installGalleryExtensions` returns empty results and the extension is found in `this.installingExtensions`, wait for the existing task directly rather than going through `installGalleryExtensions`. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Extension management maintainer and author of the `installFromGallery`/`installExtensions` code, including the recent redirect-fallback changes in PR #303888. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24535401758/agentic_workflow) · ● 15.9M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

title: Agents: Changes rendering defaults and flexibility
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
The latest side-by-side diff rendering is really neat!

I think I'm finding that depending on my monitor setup, I sometimes want the side-by-side, and other times want the modal(s).

If I'm only using my laptop and not a second larger monitor, I'd love the option to still use a modal for review, since I'm low on horizontal space.

A few thoughts:

- If I click on the `View All Changes` button, I might expect the side-by-side rendering
- If I click on an individual file, I might expect either 1) just one diff to open side-by-side, or 2) open up a modal with that file's diff
- It could also be interesting to provide users an option in the `...` menu of the Changes panel (so default to side-by-side, but provide a setting so that they could use modals instead if they'd prefer - similar to the filtering options we have for the sessions list on the left)

@lostintangent shared great insights too:

- Making the panel responsive, so that it opens a modal when the available horizontal space is below a threshold
- @lszomoru is planning to allow maximizing the panel
- There's a setting to enable the modal, and it's probably worth adding to the `...` menu (since it feels very much like a user decision, like flat vs tree view already is)
comments: As part of @DonJayamanne's work on plan mode, I'd also like the option for the generated plan to open as a modal rather than only side-by-side.

Feedback from @Yoyokrazy: 

- I want the files/diff sidebar to collapse if I open a diff or plan artifact. I will never need both of these, same thinking as why we don't mind the modal. 
- I have never wanted a markdown preview to open to the side of the code editor, I want it to open in the same editor group
- I don't really ever want to open a md file in the code editor. these are typically just specs, so I always want to leave comments on a md preview. I want a simple way to be able to set these defaults, ideally without a dig through the vscode settings to be able to remember how to set default editors. Right now the most "intuitive" approach is to open the artifact, right click the tab, click reopen editor with, then configure the default. This last bit is probably a bit more of a nit, but I'm not sure what the typical experiences are. 
- I would ideally have artifacts stored in a top level tab with files and diff. It feels really out of place to have them float above the input box. For me, I want all spec files, tmp files, plan files, etc in one neat section, just like we keep the diff
- Adding comments to the artifacts is my biggest ask. The whole point of the sessions app to me is to look at less code, so I almost never use the diff to actually review code. I'm only opening it to amend the copilot feedback I might've gotten which landed in there. Specs on the other hand, I iterate on a ton

Feedback from @joshspicer also highlighted the simplicity of the modal-based interface.

Related issues:
- https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310859
- https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309656

title: Opening large files causes crash
description:
Type: Performance Issue I opened a jsonl file that is 495mb large, and 700k lines long, and vscode crashed. Now I understand that vscode would have trouble handling a file this large, and may not be able to provide all IDE features, or even edit the file at all. But I would rather it give me a warning that it can't handle files over a certain size, and not crash the entire window. This is especially annoying when I drag a very large file into the project, and vscode auto opens the file then crashes. Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.116.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!
Updated to latest version, still crashes.

Thanks for reporting this. I tried to reproduce with a ~495 MB JSONL file on the latest Insiders build but was unable to get a crash.

VS Code already has several protections for large files:
- A confirmation dialog before opening files above a configurable size threshold (`workbench.editorLargeFileConfirmation`)
- Automatic disabling of tokenization, folding, word highlighting, etc. for files > 20 MB or > 300K lines
- A hard limit that blocks full-buffer operations for files > 256M characters

Could you share the crash log or any error output you see? You can find it via **Help > Open Log Folder**. Also, does this happen with extensions disabled (`--disable-extensions`)?

Yeah disabling extensions works. What I tried to do then, was uninstall all my extensions one by one (I didn't have that many to start with), to see if I could pinpoint which extension was causing the crash. I'd disable an extension, open the project with a large file, and see if it would crash, then repeat. But eventually I got to the point where all extensions were disabled, and the crash is still occurring when I open my project that has a very large file in it. So I tried just to uninstall all extensions, but still the same. So even with no extensions it still crashes. But with extensions explicitly disabled with `--disable-extensions`, vscode works. So I'm pretty confused at what's happening. Here are some logs from one of the crashes, with having no extensions installed. [20260418T105602.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26852348/20260418T105602.zip) One thing I'll add: When I first started up the project, I wasn't having any issues manipulating large files. But I left vscode open overnight because I was training an LLM locally. But then when I came back in the morning and tried to open the large file, it crashed.

title: Agent mode can access other Agent files through vscode-userdata uris, other modes or sub-agents can't
description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: v0.44.1 
- VS Code Version: 1.115.0 (user setup)
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19045
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent + plan sub-agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): GPT-5-3-Codex - Medium
- Logs: where do I get logs?

## Intro
I have a custom agent file in my User profile `~/.copilot/agents/Plan2.agent.md `

This agent is intended to EXTEND upon the default built-in Plan.agent.md by NOT invoking it as a sub-agent, but reading it's file directly into its context instead.

It was my xx-th unsuccessful attempt and this time I mistakenly didn't choose the Plan2 mode agent from the options and left the default Agent mode on. This was also the first time when the desired file was actually accessed.
`vscode-userdata:/c:/Users/Qwerty/AppData/Roaming/Code/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat/plan-agent/Plan.agent.md`. It then explained to me why I couldn't access the file before, although I don't know if I can trust it, because I was not able to reproduce it again as it always searches in the extension folder and not globalStorage - being unable to find the correct `Plan.agent.md` file.

I also tried to tweak the prompt several times to specifically say "vscode-userdata globalStorage" with no success.

> - 👉 How can I achieve the desired functionality of "extending" on existing Agent modes by letting an Agent read the other Agent's file directly without invoking it as a sub-agent? - what instruction to give?
> - Are the Agents files (visible from Chat Customizations popup) readily available through the VSCode Chat API?


<details>
  <summary>user: plan2.agent.md</summary>

~~~
---
name: Plan2
description: Researches and outlines multi-step plans
argument-hint: Outline the goal or problem to research
model: GPT-5.3-Codex (copilot)
target: vscode
disable-model-invocation: true
tools: ['search', 'read', 'web', 'vscode/memory', 'github/issue_read', 'github.vscode-pull-request-github/issue_fetch', 'github.vscode-pull-request-github/activePullRequest', 'execute/getTerminalOutput', 'execute/testFailure', 'agent', 'vscode/askQuestions']
agents: []
handoffs:
  - label: Start Implementation
    agent: agent
    prompt: 'Start implementation'
    send: true
  - label: Open in Editor
    agent: agent
    prompt: '#createFile the plan as is into an untitled file (`untitled:plan-${camelCaseName}.prompt.md` without frontmatter) for further refinement.'
    send: true
    showContinueOn: false
---
You are a PLANNING AGENT, pairing with the user to create a detailed, actionable plan.

Always read other available Plan agents' files (workspace-provided, default built-in Plan agent) to discover environment specific guidance and tools not covered in your current workflow. Do not spawn the Plan agent as a subagent; instead, directly apply any discovered guidance and tools from that file in your own workflow.

However, these additional instructions are top priority:

## Workspace guidance discovery requirements:
- Discover workspace documentation and contributor instructions (for example README.md files and any linked instruction documents).
- Discover workspace AI-agent guidance (for example AGENTS.md files and .agents/ instructions/skills/ folders).
- If workspace guidance explicitly defines planning behavior, PLANS format, or a PLANS instruction source, follow it exactly as the primary authority.
- Use useful guidance from the default Plan agent only as a fallback when workspace guidance is missing, silent, or ambiguous on a specific planning behavior (including VS Code-specific planning tools/workflow support).

## Conflict resolution rule:
- If workspace guidance conflicts with the default Plan agent guidance, prefer workspace guidance.
~~~
</details>



## Steps to Reproduce:
<details>
  <summary>Chat transcript</summary>

---
---

## User:

I want to verify the workflow. Use the chosen Plan agent and give me a step by step report of the planning phase, what sub-agents you invoked, what files you read and what instructions you follow WITHOUT actually going into the ExecPlan creation task itself. If any, each sub-agent must report the same including reporting its name and file-path to avoid ambiguity.

## GitHub Copilot:

Need verify workflow only, no ExecPlan creation. First step: collect source-of-truth docs and locate plan agent file, then run that agent in read-only planning mode and capture full trace.

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/AGENTS.md

- Read file:///c%3A/Users/Qwerty/.copilot/agents

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/.agents

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/README.md#1-1), lines 1 to 26

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/.agents/PLANS.md

Docs loaded. Next step: inspect the Plan agent definition itself, then invoke it in strict read-only planning-report mode so we can produce exact trace.

- Read file:///c%3A/Users/Qwerty/.copilot/agents/Plan2.agent.md

Now I will discover all available Plan agent files (workspace and default) because Plan2 requires reading them before planning behavior can be reported.

Searched for files matching `mnd-call-dashboard` · `**/*.agent.md`, no matches

Searched for files matching `c:\Users\Qwerty\.copilot\agents\*.agent.md`, no matches

Searched for regex `name:\s*Plan|PLANS.md|ExecPlan` (`mnd-call-dashboard` · `**/*.md`), 46 results

Found Plan agents: workspace has none; user-scoped has Plan2; default built-in Plan exists at vscode userdata path. Next I will read default Plan file and then run Plan2 sub-agent with explicit “report-only/no ExecPlan creation” constraints.

- Read vscode-userdata:/c%3A/Users/Qwerty/AppData/Roaming/Code/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat/plan-agent/Plan.agent.md

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/.agents/archive/README.md

I have enough guidance loaded. Next I will invoke Plan2 in report-only mode and request a full planning-phase trace, including any nested sub-agent usage with names and file paths.

Planning workflow trace

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/.agents

- Read file:///c%3A/Users/Qwerty/.copilot/agents

- Read file:///c%3A/Users/Qwerty/.copilot/agents/Plan2.agent.md

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/AGENTS.md

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/README.md#1-1), lines 1 to 26

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/.agents/PLANS.md

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/.agents/skills

Searched for files matching `**/*Plan*.agent*`, no matches

- Read file:///c%3A/repos/techmates/mnd-call-dashboard/ARCHITECTURE.md

Searched for files matching `c:\Users\Qwerty\**\*Plan*.agent.md`, no matches

Searched for files matching `/c:/Users/Qwerty/**/*.agent.md`, no matches

- Read file:///c%3A/Users/Qwerty

- Read file:///c%3A/Users/Qwerty/.copilot

- Read file:///c%3A/Users/Qwerty/.copilot/instructions

Chosen Plan agent used: Plan2  
Agent file path: Plan2.agent.md

Step-by-step planning-phase report (no ExecPlan work):

1. Loaded mandatory workspace guidance first.
Read README.md, AGENTS.md, and PLANS.md to satisfy repo rules before any planning flow work.

2. Loaded Plan-agent guidance sources.
Read Plan2 file at c:\Users\Qwerty\.copilot\agents\Plan2.agent.md.
Read default Plan file at vscode-userdata:/c%3A/Users/Qwerty/AppData/Roaming/Code/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat/plan-agent/Plan.agent.md to satisfy Plan2 rule “read other Plan agents”.

3. Collected planning-context support docs.
Read ARCHITECTURE.md.
Also read README.md during discovery sweep.

4. Resolved instruction precedence.
Applied this order:
System/developer safety/tooling rules.
Plan2 agent rules.
Workspace rules from AGENTS.md and PLANS.md.
Default Plan guidance only as fallback.
No conflict required override for this verification task.

5. Invoked chosen Plan agent for audit.
Sub-agent invoked by me:
Name: Plan2
File path: Plan2.agent.md
Purpose: planning-phase trace only, read-only, explicitly skip ExecPlan creation.

6. Sub-agent invocation chain result.
Inside Plan2 run, additional sub-agents invoked: none.
So there is no deeper sub-agent file-path list.

7. File-read report from Plan2 run (self-reported).
Plan2 reported reading:
Plan2.agent.md
AGENTS.md
README.md
PLANS.md
ARCHITECTURE.md

8. ExecPlan scope guard enforced.
No ExecPlan file created.
No ExecPlan file modified.
No memory plan persisted for execution.
Planning workflow verification only, as requested.

If you want, next pass can include raw tool-call transcript grouped by actor:
1. Main agent calls
2. Plan2 self-reported internal actions
3. Final compliance checklist against AGENTS.md and PLANS.md.

##  User:

Did the Plan2 agent also read the vscode-built-in Plan file as specified in the Plan2.agent.md?

## GitHub Copilot:

No.  
Plan2 self-report says it did not read VS Code built-in Plan file; it only said it tried to locate it and could not access readable standalone file.  
Built-in Plan file was read by main run, not by Plan2 sub-agent run.

## User: 

Why could you read it but the Plan2 agent could not?

## GitHub Copilot:

Tool scope mismatch.

Main agent run used direct file-read call with explicit vscode-userdata URI. That path resolved in my session, so read worked.

Plan2 sub-agent run had its own execution context and tool routing. In that context, its discovery/read flow did not successfully open the same vscode-userdata source, so it reported “not accessible.”

Short version: same machine, different agent sandbox/tool path visibility.

## User: 

how can I make the Plan2 agent access the file?
dDoes this change anything? agents: [Plan]"

## GitHub Copilot:

Short answer: no, agents: [Plan] alone does not fix access.

What that field does:
- It only declares a callable sub-agent named Plan is available.
- It does not auto-read the built-in Plan file.
- It does not grant extra filesystem visibility.

Why Plan2 missed it:
- Plan2 ran in a different tool context/sandbox than main agent run.
- In that context, vscode-userdata path resolution was not reliable.

How to make it reliable:
1. Add explicit fallback rule in Plan2 instructions:
- First try built-in Plan URI.
- If read fails, read a workspace mirror copy.
- If both fail, report failure explicitly.

2. Put a workspace mirror of built-in Plan text in repo, then read that path (most reliable in sub-agents).
- Example location: skills sibling like .agents/plan-default/Plan.agent.md

3. Pass the built-in Plan content directly in the sub-agent prompt when invoking Plan2 (bypasses path access issues entirely).

4. Keep read tool enabled (already enabled), and require “evidence of read” in output:
- “Read source: <path>”
- “First 1-2 unique lines from source”

Practical verdict on your question:
- agents: [Plan] changes capability discovery only.
- It does not change file-access permissions or path resolution behavior.

---
---

</details>

Happy Coding!

title: Copilot Pro+ – Weekly Rate Limit Not Resetting After Expected Reset (Copilot)
description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->
I'm experiencing an issue with my Copilot Pro+ subscription.

At approximately 14:33, I attempted to use Copilot Chat and received the following error:

"Sorry, you have been rate-limited. Please wait 2 minutes before trying again or consider switching to Auto.
Server Error: Sorry, you've exceeded your weekly rate limit. Please review our Terms of Service.
Error Code: user_weekly_rate_limited"

I waited over an hour before trying again, but the same error persisted.

I initially reached the weekly rate limit a few days ago, which was expected. However, the limit should have reset by now, but I am still receiving the same error today.

title: Arabic text not rendered in RTL in Copilot input
description:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.44.1
- VS Code Version: 1.116.0
- OS Version: Windows 10 (Build 10.0.26200)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Chat input (all mode)
- Selected model: all
- Logs: N/A

Description:
The Copilot Chat input field does not correctly support right-to-left (RTL) languages such as Arabic.

When typing Arabic text, the input remains left-to-right (LTR), which breaks readability and writing flow.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open Copilot Chat in VS Code
2. Type Arabic text (e.g. "مرحبا كيف الحال")
3. Observe the text direction in the input box

Expected Behavior:
The input field should automatically switch to RTL when Arabic (or other RTL languages) are detected.
Standard approaches include:
- Using `dir="auto"` on the input element
- Applying dynamic direction based on detected script

Actual Behavior:
Text remains LTR regardless of the language being typed.

Impact:
This significantly degrades usability for RTL language users and affects accessibility and internationalization (i18n) support.

Suggested Fix:
Update the input component to support automatic direction detection:
- Add `dir="auto"` to the textarea/input
OR
- Implement script detection and dynamically set `direction: rtl` when appropriate

Additional Context:
Many modern editors and input components already support automatic RTL handling, so this appears to be a missing feature in Copilot Chat.
comments: As shown in the image, Arabic text is rendered left-to-right inside the Copilot chat input field instead of right-to-left, which makes typing and reading difficult.

comments: In addition, the effective state does not change when checking and unchecking the box

Thanks for bringing to our attention a confusing aspect of the Settings editor.
"Enabled" is part of the name of the setting. For example, "Unified Agents Bar: Enabled" corresponds to the `chat.unifiedAgentsBar.enabled` setting. If the checkbox is checked, then the setting is enabled. Otherwise, the setting is not enabled. The checkbox shows the effective state (unless there's a [setting override](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/settings#_settings-precedence)).
Adding Hawk for additional triage and thoughts.

title: Agents: Update session target picker for remote repos
description: - `+` new session - Select a remote repo - I see cloud is the default option - I click on the dropdown with cloud - ❓ CLI is still clickable, even though it doesn't work
comments: Based on @stevencl's recent findings, I also wonder if there's more information we could provide to users here about what's expected and what will happen (in case they don't know exactly what a cloud session entails, why they're defaulted to it, etc). In VS Code, we have a docs link to learn about agent handoffs - would we consider something like that?

title: VS Code WSL Server download fails with 502/503/504 from update.code.visualstudio.com (Azure Front Door origin unavailable)
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.99.x (commit: 560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679)
- OS Version: Windows 11 (Build 26200)
- WSL: WSL2 (Ubuntu, kernel 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2)
- VS Code Remote - WSL Extension Version: 0.104.3

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Install VS Code stable on Windows 11
2. Ensure WSL2 (Ubuntu) is installed and working (`wsl` opens normally)
3. Open VS Code
4. Run "Remote-WSL: Reopen Folder in WSL" or execute `code .` from WSL
5. VS Code attempts to download and install VS Code Server inside WSL

Expected Result:
VS Code downloads the server tarball and starts the WSL remote session successfully.

Actual Result:
VS Code fails to download the server on both client and WSL sides.

Logs show:
- Client-side download failure:
  `Unable to download server on client side: Error: Request downloadRequest failed unexpectedly without providing any details.`
- WSL-side attempt hangs/fails during download
- Direct requests to the server endpoint return gateway errors:
  - 502 (PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest)
  - 503 / 504 (browser)

Failing endpoint:
https://update.code.visualstudio.com/commit:560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679/server-linux-x64/stable

Browser result:
504 Gateway Timeout
"The service behind this page isn't responding to Azure Front Door."

Additional Diagnostics:

- WSL is fully functional independently
- Network connectivity is working for other domains
- Issue reproduces:
  - in VS Code
  - via PowerShell (Invoke-WebRequest)
  - via browser (Chrome)
- Indicates failure at Azure Front Door / upstream origin for this specific commit

Impact:

- Blocks all WSL usage in VS Code for affected commit
- Cannot bootstrap `.vscode-server`
- Requires workaround (VPN, different VS Code build, or waiting for backend recovery)

Workarounds Identified:

- Using VPN (different region) allows download
- Installing VS Code Insiders (different commit) avoids the issue
- Manual download + extraction works when endpoint is reachable

Notes:

This appears to be a CDN/origin availability issue for specific VS Code server commits rather than a local environment or configuration problem.
comments: Probably the same as https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310744

Team is actively investigating. We apologize for the issue. Consolidating to tracking issue #310757.

*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*
comments:
## Stable Release Anomaly Details

- **Anomaly type**: `new`
- **Previous stable release**: [1.115.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/d819bd63-3c52-10d4-5532-1260564a39e5?product=VSCode&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- **Current stable release**: [1.116.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/d819bd63-3c52-10d4-5532-1260564a39e5?product=VSCode&commit=560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679)
- **Users**: 3,470
- **Hits**: 4,802
- **Commit range**: [41dd792b...560a9dba](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b...560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679)

> ⚠️ Insider build data was unavailable for commit range narrowing — the range above spans the full stable-to-stable delta.

### Deminified Stack Summary

The error occurs when `AbstractContextKeyService` is used after disposal. The call path is:

1. `contextKeyService.ts` → `contextMatchesRules` — evaluates context key rules on a disposed service
2. `menuService.ts` → `createActionGroups` / `getActions` — menu service resolves action groups
3. `toolbar.ts` → `_updateToolbar` — toolbar refresh triggers the evaluation

This indicates a disposed context key service is still being referenced during toolbar updates, likely due to a lifecycle mismatch where a toolbar component outlives its owning context key service scope.

### Related Issues

- #246652 — Same error (`AbstractContextKeyService has been disposed`) through `contextMatchesRules` → `menuService`, but triggered via `gutterFeature.ts` (diff editor) instead of `toolbar.ts`. Assigned to `@hediet` on "On Deck" milestone.
- #260086, #210947 — Older instances of the same error pattern.
- #310756 — Same error message but different call path (`createOverlay` → `aiCustomizationListWidget`), Agents product.

> Generated by [errors-stable-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24519474162/agentic_workflow) · ● 8.7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-stable-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-stable-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24519474162, workflow_id: errors-stable-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24519474162 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-stable-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `AbstractContextKeyService has been disposed` is thrown when a `MenuWorkbenchToolBar`'s debounced `onDidChange` event fires **after** the scoped `IContextKeyService` it references has been disposed. This is a race condition between the `DebounceEmitter`'s `setTimeout`-based delivery and view lifecycle disposal. The Agents/Sessions feature added in 1.116.0 (particularly `ChangesViewPane`) creates many rapidly-changing scoped context keys, significantly increasing the probability of this race. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit was identified. The error is a **pre-existing race condition** in the `DebounceEmitter` → toolbar update path that became triggerable at scale due to the many new Agents/Sessions features added across multiple PRs in 1.116.0. The `ChangesViewPane` introduced by `@lszomoru` creates 10+ scoped context keys that update via `bufferChangeEvents()` on every session state change, widening the timing window for the race. > The commit range spans full stable-to-stable (1.115.0→1.116.0). Insider data shows the error first appeared in build [`1dfb3b9d`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681) (Apr 13). The regression window (~Apr 12–13) contains ~30 commits, none of which individually modify `toolbar.ts` or `menuService.ts`. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant CKS as ScopedContextKeyService participant Menu as Menu (DebounceEmitter) participant Toolbar as MenuWorkbenchToolBar participant MenuInfo as MenuInfo CKS->>Menu: onDidChangeContext (context key changes) Note over Menu: DebounceEmitter.fire():
pauses, queues event,
starts setTimeout(delay) Note over CKS: ⚠️ View disposed →
ScopedContextKeyService
._isDisposed = true Menu->>Menu: setTimeout fires → resume() Menu->>Toolbar: onDidChange callback Toolbar->>MenuInfo: _updateToolbar() → getActions() MenuInfo->>CKS: createActionGroups() → contextMatchesRules() Note over CKS: 💥 _isDisposed === true
throws "AbstractContextKeyService
has been disposed" ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/platform/contextkey/browser/contextKeyService.ts:322` | **Crash site** — `contextMatchesRules()` throws when `_isDisposed` is true | | `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts:285` | **Data flow** — `createActionGroups()` calls `contextMatchesRules()` on potentially disposed CKS | | `src/vs/platform/actions/browser/toolbar.ts:392` | **Trigger** — `_updateToolbar()` invoked by debounced `onDidChange` callback | | `src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1569-1578` | **Root cause** — `DebounceEmitter` doesn't clear `_handle` timeout on disposal | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts:675` | **Trigger source** — `bufferChangeEvents()` batch-updates 10+ context keys on the scoped CKS | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code with the Agents/Sessions feature active (1.116.0+) 2. Start or open an agent session so the Changes view (`ChangesViewPane`) is visible 3. Perform an action that causes rapid session state changes (e.g., switching sessions, committing, or closing a session) 4. While context keys are being updated, close the view or dispose the session quickly 5. The debounced `onDidChange` timer fires after the scoped CKS is disposed → error > This is a timing-sensitive race condition. It's more likely when many context keys change in rapid succession (which `bufferChangeEvents()` at line 675 ensures) and the view is closed within the debounce delay window (~100ms default). ### Fix Proposal Three possible approaches, in order of preference: 1. **Fix in `DebounceEmitter.dispose()`** (best — prevents the root cause): - **Fix location**: `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` → `DebounceEmitter` class - **Approach**: Override `dispose()` to call `clearTimeout(this._handle)` before calling `super.dispose()`. This ensures no debounced events fire after disposal. This is the most defensive fix since it prevents the entire class of debounce-after-dispose errors. 2. **Guard in `MenuInfo.createActionGroups()`** (defense in depth): - **Fix location**: `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` → `createActionGroups()` - **Approach**: Before calling `this._contextKeyService.contextMatchesRules()` at line 285, check if the CKS is disposed. If disposed, skip the item (or return empty results). This mirrors the pattern already used in `getContextKeyValue()` at `contextKeyService.ts:333` which returns `undefined` when disposed instead of throwing. 3. **Guard in `contextMatchesRules()`** (most conservative): - **Fix location**: `src/vs/platform/contextkey/browser/contextKeyService.ts` → `contextMatchesRules()` - **Approach**: Change line 321-322 from throwing to returning `false` when disposed, matching the graceful degradation pattern in `getContextKeyValue()`. This would prevent all callers from hitting this error. ### Recommended Owner `@jrieken` — platform actions owner (`toolbar.ts`, `menuService.ts`, `event.ts`). The fix belongs in the platform event/menu infrastructure. Related issue #246652 follows the same error pattern through `contextMatchesRules → menuService`. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24520153137/agentic_workflow) · ● 34.4M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

title: Why is Claude Opus 4.7 priced at 7.5x even after the 50% promotion?
description: Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open VSCode. 2. Open the model picker. 3. Find Claude Opus 4.7. It is priced at 7.5x, which is insane compared to Opus 4.5/4.6 being priced at 3x. --- And is the 7.5x price already the result of the 50% promotion, meaning its original price is 15x? I have checked its API price on the official webpage and OpenRouter; the prices for input and output remain the same: So, is this just a simple typo where "1.5" was written as "7.5", or is it actually that expensive?
comments:
And, I have tried this model to write some code tonight.
Include some hammerspoon configurations (Lua), raycast extension (Typescript).
It wasn't worth the 7.5 x price, sincerely.

title: Terminal Shell session tab selection should auto-focus terminal input
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
When multiple shell sessions are open in the terminal, the current interaction requires two clicks to begin using a session: first selecting the session tab, and then clicking inside the terminal pane to focus it for input.

This introduces unnecessary friction in workflows that involve frequent switching between sessions.

Proposed Improvement:
Selecting a session tab should automatically focus the corresponding terminal pane, allowing immediate interaction with the shell using a single click.

This change would streamline the user experience and make session switching faster and more intuitive, especially for users managing multiple concurrent terminal sessions.
comments: you can do this with `terminal.integrated.tabs.focusMode`

Probably the same as https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310744, it seems to have been up and down all day. It was broken this morning and was then fixed for some hours, but now it's all over the place again.

We're still working on our investigation and monitoring traffic. Sorry for the inconvenience you are facing.

cc: @lszomoru @rzhao271 @alexdima

Closing this issue now that we have mitigated it.
The Southeast Asia instances were overloaded and failed, and that pattern then spread across the other regions. We have mitigated the issue by adding more instances to each region.
We are still confirming the exact root cause, and we will add more server-side diagnostics so that we can monitor and resolve these issues more quickly in the future.

*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*
comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary `AbstractContextKeyService has been disposed` is thrown when the `AICustomizationListWidget` performs an async `refresh()` → `loadItems()` cycle and the scoped `contextKeyService` is disposed before the list finishes rendering. During `commitDisplayEntries()` → `list.splice()`, the list framework calls the renderer's `renderElement()`, which invokes `this.contextKeyService.createOverlay()` on the now-disposed service. This is a use-after-dispose async race condition — the same class of bug as #309919. ### Culprit Commit This is **not a single-commit regression** — it is a latent async race condition present since the `AICustomizationListWidget` was first introduced. The error surfaces during session teardown or editor disposal when an in-flight `refresh()` completes after the context key service scope is torn down. The error was reported on commit [`98b4d3e2`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62) (1.117.0-insider, April 14), which is **before** the fix in PR #310205 was merged (April 15). | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Fix PR | #310205 | | Fix Author | `@joshspicer` | | Fix Commit | `951fb7c6` (merged April 15) | | Status | **Likely already fixed** — the fix adds `_store.isDisposed` guards in `loadItems()` that prevent this exact code path | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Autorun as autorun (observable change) participant Widget as AICustomizationListWidget participant LoadItems as loadItems() participant FilterItems as filterItems() participant List as WorkbenchList.splice() participant Renderer as AICustomizationItemRenderer participant CKS as ContextKeyService Autorun->>Widget: refresh() Widget->>LoadItems: await loadItems() Note over LoadItems: ⏳ Fetching items async... Note over CKS: ⚠️ Session teardown
disposes contextKeyService LoadItems->>LoadItems: Items fetched, check _store.isDisposed Note over LoadItems: ❌ OLD CODE: no isDisposed check
✅ NEW CODE (PR #310205): returns early LoadItems->>FilterItems: filterItems() FilterItems->>List: commitDisplayEntries() → splice() List->>Renderer: renderElement() Renderer->>CKS: createOverlay() Note over CKS: 💥 Error thrown:
AbstractContextKeyService
has been disposed ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` | Root cause — async `loadItems()` lacked disposal guard (line 1133 before fix) | | `src/vs/platform/contextkey/browser/contextKeyService.ts` | Crash site — `createOverlay()` throws on disposed service (line 315) | ### Repro Steps 1. Open the AI Customization editor in the Agents product 2. Trigger a session teardown / editor switch while items are loading (e.g., close the editor tab rapidly while it's populating) 3. The async `refresh()` → `loadItems()` completes after the scoped contextKeyService is disposed 4. The list splice triggers rendering which calls `createOverlay()` on the disposed service This is timing-dependent and more likely to reproduce with slow item sources or rapid editor lifecycle changes. ### Fix Proposal **Already fixed in PR #310205** (merged into `main` on April 15, milestone 1.117.0). The fix added `this._store.isDisposed` guards: - **`loadItems()`** at line 1133: prevents `filterItems()` → `list.splice()` → render from executing after disposal (covers this exact stack trace) - **`refresh()`**: guard after `await loadItems()` before `updateAddButton()` - **`setSection()`** and **`generateDebugReport()`**: same pattern This error should stop appearing in Insiders builds newer than the merge commit. If it persists on builds after the fix, there may be an additional gap where the `contextKeyService` is disposed independently of the widget's `_store` (e.g., parent scope disposal ordering). In that case, a defensive `try-catch` around `createOverlay` in the renderer's `renderElement` would be needed. ### Recommended Owner `@joshspicer` — authored the fix PR #310205 for the same class of bug (#309919) and owns the AI customization list widget code. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24521247256/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.3M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## Related Error Buckets

These additional error buckets are tracked by this issue (v1.115.0, Agents product):

| Bucket | Error Message | Version |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| [`59317032`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/59317032-9d5f-b309-386a-a15af1883b87?product=Agents&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) | AbstractContextKeyService has been disposed | 1.115.0 |

This is a different call path through the same `aiCustomizationListWidget` — `refresh()` → `updateAddButton()` → `buildCreateActions()` → `menuService.getMenuActions()` → `contextKeyService.contextMatchesRules()` (vs. the `renderElement()` → `createOverlay()` path in bucket `22a01c00`). Same root cause: disposed context key service used during widget operations. 11 users, 11 hits on 1.115.0. Likely also addressed by PR #310205.

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The `openSearch()` method in `extensionsWorkbenchService.ts` crashes with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'search')` when `viewPaneContainer` is `undefined`. In the **Agents** product, the Extensions viewlet may not be available, so `openViewContainer(VIEWLET_ID)?.getViewPaneContainer()` returns `undefined` — but the code calls `.search()` on it without a null check. **This is a duplicate of #309920**, which was already investigated and fixed by PR #309950 (merged Apr 14). The error persists at commit `98b4d3e2` because that build predates the fix. It should self-resolve in the next Insiders build. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`e9c8a5c7`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/e9c8a5c77057ec4fcc123855f8cf9170004ba393) | | Author | `@sandy081` | | PR | #308478 | | Message | disable copilot chat extension by default and adopt accordingly | | Why | This commit removed `ChatContextKeys.Setup.disabled` guards from multiple code paths, enabling chat/plugin features in the Agents window where the Extensions viewlet is not available. This triggered calls to `openSearch()` in a context where `openViewContainer()` returns `undefined`. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Caller as Chat/Plugin Action participant EWS as ExtensionsWorkbenchService participant VS as ViewsService participant Sidebar as SidebarPart Caller->>EWS: openSearch(searchValue) EWS->>VS: openViewContainer(VIEWLET_ID, true) VS->>Sidebar: openPaneComposite(id) Note over Sidebar: ⚠️ Root cause:
Extensions viewlet unavailable
in Agents window Sidebar-->>VS: undefined VS-->>EWS: undefined EWS->>EWS: ?.getViewPaneContainer() → undefined Note over EWS: 💥 viewPaneContainer.search(searchValue)
TypeError: Cannot read 'search' of undefined ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/extensionsWorkbenchService.ts` (L1614-1620) | **Crash site** — `openSearch()` lacked null guard on `viewPaneContainer` | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatSetupContributions.ts` | Modified by culprit — removed `disabled` checks enabling more code paths | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatParticipant.contribution.ts` | Modified by culprit — chat view shown in more scenarios | ### Repro Steps 1. Open the **Agents** window (sessions desktop product) 2. Trigger an action that calls `openSearch()` — e.g., open a workspace with recommended agent plugins, click "Show Plugins", or run the Plugins command 3. The Extensions viewlet fails to open → crash ### Fix Proposal **Already fixed** in PR #309950 (merged 2026-04-14). The fix adds a null guard on `viewPaneContainer` before calling `.search()` and `.focus()`, and widens the type cast to `IExtensionsViewPaneContainer | undefined`. **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/extensionsWorkbenchService.ts` → `openSearch()` The fix is on `main` but was not included in the build at commit `98b4d3e2`. The error should stop appearing in subsequent Insiders builds. ### Recommended Owner `@bryanchen-d` — authored the fix (PR #309950) and is already assigned. This is a duplicate of #309920. ### Duplicate Notice This issue (bucket `42fe0e93`, version 1.117.0-insider) is a duplicate of #309920 (bucket `22108fd1`, version 1.116.0-insider). Same root cause, same crash site, different bucket ID due to different build. The fix in PR #309950 addresses both. **88 hits / 57 users** on the pre-fix build; expect zero hits on post-fix builds. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24521260114/agentic_workflow) · ● 3.2M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The `AbstractContextKeyService has been disposed` error is a use-after-dispose race condition in `AICustomizationListWidget`. When `refresh()` triggers an async `loadItems()` → `fetchItemsForSection()` and the widget's scoped context key service is disposed during the await (e.g., during session teardown), the post-await `filterItems()` → `commitDisplayEntries()` → `list.splice()` chain triggers synchronous list rendering, which calls `AICustomizationItemRenderer.renderElement()` → `contextKeyService.createOverlay()` on the disposed service. This error affects all platforms (Windows, Mac, Linux) in the Agents product. ### Culprit Commit This is **not a single-commit regression** — the race condition has been present since at least v1.115.0-insider (April 2), growing in frequency as AI customization usage increased. The bucket history shows a steady rise from 1 hit (v1.115.0) to 88 hits / 71 users (v1.117.0). > **This error is already fixed** by PR #310205 (`Fix use-after-dispose in AICustomizationListWidget`), merged April 15, 2026 by `@joshspicer`. The error data in this issue is entirely from commit `98b4d3e2` (April 14) — before the fix was merged. No hits have been recorded on post-fix builds. | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Fix PR | [#310205](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/310205) | | Author | `@joshspicer` | | Related Issue | [#309919](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309919) (same root cause, different stack trace) | | Message | Fix use-after-dispose in AICustomizationListWidget | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Autorun as autorun callback participant Widget as AICustomizationListWidget participant LoadItems as loadItems() participant FilterItems as filterItems() participant ListView as List.splice() participant Renderer as AICustomizationItemRenderer participant CKS as ContextKeyService Autorun->>Widget: refresh() Widget->>LoadItems: await loadItems() LoadItems->>LoadItems: await fetchItemsForSection() Note over CKS: ⚠️ Service disposed
during await (session teardown) LoadItems->>FilterItems: filterItems() [no isDisposed check pre-fix] FilterItems->>FilterItems: groupMatchedItems() → commitDisplayEntries() FilterItems->>ListView: list.splice(0, length, entries) ListView->>Renderer: renderElement(entry) Renderer->>CKS: createOverlay(overlayPairs) Note over CKS: 💥 Error thrown:
AbstractContextKeyService
has been disposed ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` | Root cause — `loadItems()` lacked `isDisposed` guard before calling `filterItems()` post-await; `renderElement()` calls `createOverlay()` | | `src/vs/platform/contextkey/browser/contextKeyService.ts` | Crash site — `createOverlay()` (line 315) throws when `_isDisposed` is true | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) on a build before April 15 fix 2. Open the AI Customizations editor (triggers `refresh()` → `loadItems()`) 3. While items are loading asynchronously, close the session or switch contexts (causes the scoped context key service to be disposed) 4. The post-await `filterItems()` triggers list rendering on the disposed service This is a race condition — not deterministic. Likelihood increases with slower network/disk I/O during `fetchItemsForSection()`, concurrent `autorun` callbacks from observable changes, and rapid session transitions. ### Fix Proposal **Already fixed** in PR #310205 (merged April 15). The fix adds `this._store.isDisposed` guards: - `loadItems()` → early return before `filterItems()` if disposed during await - `refresh()` → early return before `updateAddButton()` if disposed - `setSection()` and `generateDebugReport()` → same pattern **Remaining hardening opportunity**: The `Delayer` at line 532 (`delayedFilter`) is not registered with `this._register()`, so it's not cancelled on dispose. A delayed search filter callback could theoretically fire after disposal, calling `filterItems()` without a guard. Consider: `this._register(this.delayedFilter)` and/or adding an `isDisposed` check in `toggleGroup()` and the search handler. ### Recommended Owner `@joshspicer` — author of the fix PR #310205 and owner of the AI customization list widget. Should verify this bucket's hit count drops to zero on post-fix builds. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24529674218/agentic_workflow) · ● 7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path: unknown:` occurs when the Agents product opens a session without repository metadata. `_buildWorkspace()` creates a fallback `URI.parse('unknown:')` which has no path component. This URI flows into workspace folders, and when the snippets service calls `folder.toResource('.vscode')` → `joinPath()`, it throws because `joinPath` requires a non-empty path. **680 hits / 352 users** since April 14. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`198fa82c`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/198fa82c74df1f9a96ac29274ea3c134f67c90cb) | | Author | `@aeschli` | | PR | #309777 | | Message | Empty sessions view: cannot call joinPath | | Why | Changed `URI.parse('unknown://')` → `URI.parse('unknown:')` and added the URI to the error message, creating this new bucket. Both URIs lack a path. The underlying root cause (path-less URI used as workspace folder) predates this commit, but this PR created the specific error signature tracked by this bucket. `@aeschli` explicitly noted in the PR: *"Maybe you want to revisit `unknown:///`"*, tagging `@sandy081` and `@bpasero`. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant SM as SessionsManagement participant BW as _buildWorkspace() participant WFM as WorkspaceFolderManagement participant WCS as WorkspaceContextService participant SS as SnippetsService participant URI as URI.joinPath() SM->>BW: Active session (no repo metadata) Note over BW: ⚠️ Root cause:
repoUri is undefined →
fallback URI.parse('unknown:')
(no path component) BW->>WFM: ISessionWorkspace with unknown: URI WFM->>WCS: addFolders([{uri: unknown:}]) WCS->>SS: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders SS->>URI: folder.toResource('.vscode')
→ joinPath(unknown:, '.vscode') Note over URI: 💥 Error thrown:
[UriError]: cannot call joinPath
on URI without path: unknown: ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` line 978 creates `URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | **Missing guard** — `getActiveSessionFolderData()` only filters `AGENT_HOST_SCHEME`, not path-less URIs | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/snippets/browser/snippetsService.ts` | **Crash site** — `_initWorkspaceFolderSnippets()` calls `folder.toResource('.vscode')` | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Throw site** — `URI.joinPath()` throws for URIs without path | | `src/vs/sessions/services/workspace/browser/workspaceContextService.ts` | Data flow — `_doUpdateFolders()` fires workspace change event | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) 2. Start a session that has **no repository metadata** (e.g., a new session before any repo is associated) 3. The snippets service listener fires on workspace folder change 4. Error is thrown in the developer console: `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path: unknown:` This affects any consumer that calls `joinPath` / `toResource` on workspace folder URIs (snippets, extensions config, etc.). ### Fix Proposal Two complementary fixes are needed: **Fix 1 (root cause)**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()` **Approach**: Either use `URI.parse('unknown:///')` (which has path `/`) instead of `URI.parse('unknown:')`, or better — return `undefined` for `workspace` when `repoUri` is undefined, so no workspace folder is created for sessions without repository info. **Fix 2 (defense in depth)**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` → `getActiveSessionFolderData()` **Approach**: Add a guard `if (!repository.path)` to return `undefined`, preventing path-less URIs from ever becoming workspace folders. This is consistent with how `AGENT_HOST_SCHEME` is already filtered. ### Recommended Owner `@bpasero` — code owner of the sessions workspace infrastructure (`copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts`, `workspaceFolderManagement.ts`), and explicitly tagged by `@aeschli` in PR #309777 to revisit the `unknown:` URI handling. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24521230267/agentic_workflow) · ● 7.9M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## Details
| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Bucket ID | [`7e39eb88-7112-0b84-dccb-e437ba590e33`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/7e39eb88-7112-0b84-dccb-e437ba590e33?product=Agents&commit=98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62) |
| Version | 1.117.0-insider |
| Commit | [`98b4d3e2`](https://insiders.vscode.dev/github/microsoft/vscode/tree/98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62) |
| Last Seen | Wed Apr 15 2026 23:52:50 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) |
| Platforms | Windows, Linux, Mac |
| Product | Agents |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*
comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary When a Copilot agent session has no associated repository, the session workspace provider creates a workspace folder with a pathless URI (`unknown:`). When the preferences contribution registers editor menu items for workspace folders, it calls `joinPath` on this pathless URI, which throws `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path: unknown:`. This affects 352+ users on the Agents product across all platforms. **Note**: This is a **pre-existing issue** (60k+ hits across versions 1.114–1.116 under [old bucket `152c86ba`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/152c86ba-75c8-5abd-c702-a9ea3c617a66?product=Agents)). It appears as a "new" regression because PR #309777 changed the error message (appending the URI string), which created a new error bucket. The underlying bug has existed since at least March 2026. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`198fa82c`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/198fa82c74df1f9a96ac29274ea3c134f67c90cb) | | Author | `@aeschli` | | PR | #309777 | | Message | Empty sessions view: cannot call joinPath | | Why | Changed fallback URI from `unknown://` to `unknown:` and added the URI to the error message — both URIs have empty paths so the error persists, but the new message created a fresh error bucket that was flagged as a regression. The PR author noted this was a debugging improvement and tagged `@sandy081` / `@bpasero` to revisit the `unknown:` URI design. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant WCS as SessionsWorkspaceContextService participant PC as PreferencesContribution participant PS as PreferencesService participant WF as WorkspaceFolder participant URI as URI.joinPath WCS->>PC: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders event PC->>PC: updatePreferencesEditorMenuItemForWorkspaceFolders() Note over PC: Iterates all workspace folders PC->>PS: getFolderSettingsResource(folder.uri) PS->>WF: folder.toResource(".vscode/settings.json") WF->>URI: joinPath(this.uri, relativePath) Note over URI: ⚠️ Root cause:
folder.uri = unknown: (no path)
from copilotChatSessionsProvider
_buildWorkspace fallback Note over URI: 💥 Error thrown:
[UriError]: cannot call joinPath
on URI without path: unknown: ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` line 978 uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as fallback, producing a pathless URI | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/preferences/browser/preferences.contribution.ts` | **Crash site** — `updatePreferencesEditorMenuItemForWorkspaceFolders()` line 1297 calls `getFolderSettingsResource` without guarding against pathless URIs | | `src/vs/workbench/services/preferences/browser/preferencesService.ts` | **Data flow** — `getFolderSettingsResource()` line 138 calls `folder.toResource()` which calls `joinPath` | | `src/vs/platform/workspace/common/workspace.ts` | **Data flow** — `WorkspaceFolder.toResource()` line 435 delegates to `joinPath` | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Throw site** — `URI.joinPath()` line 362 throws when `!uri.path` | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) 2. Start a Copilot agent session that has **no associated repository** (e.g., a session where `_extractRepositoryFromMetadata` returns no `repoUri`) 3. The session workspace gets a folder with `unknown:` as its URI 4. The preferences contribution's `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` handler fires and crashes when iterating this folder This error occurs automatically when sessions without repositories are opened — no special user action beyond opening a session is required. ### Fix Proposal Two complementary fixes are recommended: **Fix 1 (root cause)**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()` **Approach**: Change `URI.parse('unknown:')` to `URI.from({ scheme: 'unknown', path: '/' })` so the fallback URI always has a valid path. This is what aeschli hinted at in PR #309777 when suggesting to revisit `unknown:///`. **Fix 2 (defensive)**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/preferences/browser/preferences.contribution.ts` → `updatePreferencesEditorMenuItemForWorkspaceFolders()` **Approach**: At line 1297, wrap the `getFolderSettingsResource` call in a guard — either check `folder.uri.path` before calling, or use a try-catch, or check the return value for null before calling `.toString()`. Skip menu item registration for folders where settings resources cannot be resolved. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Tagged by the PR author (`@aeschli`) in #309777 as the person to revisit the `unknown:` URI pattern; also the area owner for workspace/settings services where the defensive fix should go. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24521212182/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.4M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

Thanks for creating this issue! We figured it's covering the same as another one we already have. Thus, we closed this one as a duplicate. You can search for [similar existing issues](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22bug%22%20label%3A%22error-telemetry%22%20label%3A%22recent-regression%22%20label%3A%22agents%22). See also our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting).

Happy Coding!

Additional context:  It eventually *syncs* correctly, but is not correct each time that sub-applet appears (see screenshots in other issues).

I open this a lot to get a gauge of how many daily requests I have made, so that I can budget myself for the month.

If this is not accurate, it's misleading.

title: [outage] Failed updates, test runs, ssh sessions: "Failed to parse response from https://update.code.visualstudio.com/api/releases/stable?released=true as JSON"
description:
CI tests on GitHub are failing because vscode-test is unable to download VS Code:

```
Starting "dart" tests folder in workspace "hello_world"...
Failed to parse response from https://update.code.visualstudio.com/api/releases/stable?released=true as JSON
Failed to download VS Code, will retry: Failed to get JSON
Failed to parse response from https://update.code.visualstudio.com/api/releases/stable?released=true as JSON

Failed to download VS Code, will retry: Failed to get JSON
