Failed to download VS Code, will retry: Failed to get JSON
Failed to parse response from https://update.code.visualstudio.com/api/releases/stable?released=true as JSON
Failed to get JSON
Failed with exit code: 1
```

This happened a bunch this morning and it seemed like it was fixed, but now it's back again. It doesn't happen 100% of the time (around 50% of bots in a run seem to work, and around 50% fail with this).

I presume this is an infrastructure issue, but it's not clear there's anywhere better for us to report this.

If I access the URL manually, I see "429.608 TooManyRequests" which is odd, because I'm definitely not making any requests to that domain from my local machine except for clicking that link to test it.
comments: I'm also failing to connect to remote machines, which I suspect might be related, because my local version of VS Code is newer, and it's trying to download the server. Edit: logs: ``` [15:50:44.398] Got request to download on client for {"artifact":"cli-alpine-arm64","destPath":"/home/danny/.vscode-server/vscode-cli-560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679.tar.gz"} [15:50:44.398] server download URL: https://update.code.visualstudio.com/commit:560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679/cli-alpine-arm64/stable [15:50:44.398] Downloading VS Code server locally... [15:51:18.130] Resolver error: Error: Failed to download VS Code Server (Failed to fetch) at y.Create (c:\Users\danny\.vscode\extensions\ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0\out\extension.js:2:745163) ```
Same here @DanTup , also the updater fails with either error 502, 503 or 504.
Same issue here. Unable to download locally (502, 503, or 504 error) or connect to remote server due to failed remote download. Tried connecting with university VPN and home internet.
We're actively taking a look. Using #310757 for tracking.

title: Included premium requests tracker dark pattern? (attempt two)
description: This is in response to the previous message that I filed that was replied with: > Please look at the following meta issue: [#252230](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/252230). Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem. My response: > No it is not the same issue. This is a new issue that I just started experiencing. I wasn't even using Copilot when that other issue was filed. If you please read my message more carefully, I'm saying that *the included premium requests tracker is NOT accurate*. - Copilot Chat Extension Version: - VS Code Version: 1.116.0 (Universal) - OS Version: macOS Tahoe 26.4.1 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Included premium requests tracker - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): any (mostly been using Claude Sonnet 4.6) - Logs: N/A Steps to Reproduce: The reported usage information does not match what GitHub reports online, even across two different machines. This was never an issue before. I use this to track a daily budget of how many requests to send, and it feels like a dark pattern if it's not accurately tracking this, leading me to use it more.
comments: The previous issue that I filed was: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310742

Please look at the following meta issue: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/252230. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

title: Included premium requests tracker dark pattern?
description: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: - VS Code Version: 1.116.0 (Universal) - OS Version: macOS Tahoe 26.4.1 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Included premium requests tracker - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): any (mostly been using Claude Sonnet 4.6) - Logs: N/A Steps to Reproduce: The reported usage information does not match what GitHub reports online, even across two different machines. This was never an issue before. I use this to track a daily budget of how many requests to send, and it feels like a dark pattern if it's not accurately tracking this, leading me to use it more.
comments: Please look at the following meta issue: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/252230. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

title: Custom added model will still show in model picker

comments: What do you see when you open the model configurations file - there is a command Open Language Models (JSON) to see that

title: Built-in media player: AAC audio does not play in MP4 videos
description:

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

Version: 1.115.0 (Universal)
Commit: 41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b
Date: 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z (1 wk ago)
Electron: 39.8.5
ElectronBuildId: 13703022
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Darwin arm64 25.3.0

**Built-in media player: AAC audio does not play in MP4 videos**

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a `.mp4` file that contains an AAC audio track directly in VS Code (e.g. drag into explorer or `code video.mp4`)
2. Press play in the built-in media player
3. Video plays but there is no audio

**Expected:** Audio plays alongside video

**Actual:** Video plays silently. No audio output at all.

**Audio track confirmed present via ffprobe:**

Stream #0:1(und): Audio: aac (LC) (mp4a / 0x6134706D), 44100 Hz, mono, fltp, 129 kb/s (default)


The same file plays with audio in QuickTime Player, VLC, and in a browser (`<video>` tag). The issue is isolated to the VS Code built-in media player.

AAC LC is a baseline codec fully supported by Chromium 142, so this appears to be a regression or a missing audio output configuration in the Electron media player webview.
comments: /duplicate of #167685

Thanks for creating this issue! We figured it's covering the same as another one we already have. Thus, we closed this one as a duplicate. You can search for [similar existing issues](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22triage-needed%22). See also our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting).

Happy Coding!

title: The issue I’m facing is not a normal rate limit.
description: The issue I’m facing is not a normal rate limit. The system gives me a reset time, but when that time arrives, it keeps extending it again and again. Every time I click “Try Again,” the system: Shows a new reset time Pushes the limit forward And never actually unlocks my access This means the problem is not with my usage, my payment, or my card. It’s a system bug in how the weekly usage counter is being calculated. In other words, the service is putting me in a loop: It says “wait until this time” I wait Then it changes the time again And repeats the same message This behavior indicates a technical issue on their side, not a legitimate weekly limit. Summary: Not a payment issue Not a Visa issue Not a real weekly limit It’s a system error where the reset time keeps refreshing instead of unlocking access

description:

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.116
- OS Version: Windows Server 2022

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Download the CLI
2. Authenticate against GitHub using a PAT (old or new) with full permissions
3. Validate that the login has worked
4. Try to create a tunnel

Here are the scripts and the outputs. First with an old PAT, all scopes / permissions accepted:

```
PS C:\Users\sshuser\Downloads> code tunnel user login --provider github --access-token ghp_...
PS C:\Users\sshuser\Downloads> code tunnel user show
logged in with provider GitHub Account
PS C:\Users\sshuser\Downloads> code tunnel --accept-server-license-terms --name whatever
*

* the Microsoft Privacy Statement (https://privacy.microsoft.com/en-US/privacystatement).

```

The same for a new PAT with all repo and account scopes
```
PS C:\Users\sshuser\Downloads> code tunnel user login --provider github --access-token github_pat_...

logged in with provider GitHub Account

```

title: Integrated browser "edit mode": use inline text edits as a signal for Copilot edits
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

When performing marketing copy edits, it is often faster to just change text copy directly in the integrated browser than to describe the change in chat and wait for Copilot to make the change and the dev server to update the integrated browser (especially true with slower dev servers).

In the integrated browser, introduce a mode where DOM elements/nodes containing text are temporarily editable (eg. the behavior of `contenteditable` attribute). The user can then edit text inline and click on a "Save" button, upon which Copilot should use the diff of the edited text (plus nearby DOM context and `innerHTML`, as necessary) as a signal to propose source edits in the backing `tsx` / `jsx` / `html` / `svelte` / `vue` / etc file.

This proposal is NOT about deterministically mapping + persisting DOM edits to files. The browser would only be the interaction surface, while Copilot still generates normal source edits for review, based on the diff.

This could be extended to other non-text edits in future, such as image paths.

## Alternatives considered

1. Full WYSIWYG editor, mapping changes to source files deterministically

   A WYSIWYG-style visual page editing experience in the integrated browser, where users select elements and edit them visually, and changes are mapped back to source files deterministically by the editor. Maybe  similar to the issue proposed here:

   - #282975

   Upsides:
   
   - more performant
   - probably more feature-complete

   Downsides:

   - very complex to implement across different frameworks and rendering patterns

cc @meganrogge @jruales @kycutler
comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->
This feature request is now a candidate for our backlog. The community has 60 days to [upvote](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging#up-voting-a-feature-request) the issue. If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

title: VS Code freezes and crashes specifically when using GitHub Copilot
description:
### Summary
The entire VS Code application freezes and subsequently crashes while using GitHub Copilot. This happens randomly during code generation or while interacting with the Copilot chat/suggestions.

### Details
- **Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** No (It specifically happens when GitHub Copilot is enabled).
- **VS Code Version:** 1.117.0-insider (and also occurs on 1.116.0 stable).
- **OS Version:** Windows 11 (Running on NVIDIA RTX 5090).

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a multi-language project (Go/TypeScript).
2. Use GitHub Copilot for code suggestions or chat.
3. The editor UI becomes completely unresponsive (freezes) and then the process terminates/closes.

### Additional Context
- I have already ruled out network/TUN driver issues.
- I am using high-end hardware (ASUS ROG Strix SCAR 9).
- Even after upgrading to the latest Insiders build, the crash persists.
- The crash seems to be linked to the Extension Host or Renderer process being overloaded during AI generation.

### Logs
(I will attach logs from `Developer: Open Logs Folder` if requested by the team.)
comments:
[main.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26782341/main.log)

[renderer.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26782347/renderer.log)

[main.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26782355/main.log)

I thought the bug where Copilot causes VSCode to freeze had been resolved. To my surprise, version 1.116 has brought it back. This is severely disrupting my workflow; every time it freezes, I have to use the system's process manager to force-quit the application.

Currently, I suspect the issue might be caused by the approval tool.

> 我原以为 Copilot 导致 VSCode 卡死的 bug 已经修复了。没想到 1.116 版本又把它带回来了。这严重影响了我的工作流程；每次卡死，我都得用系统进程管理器强制关闭程序。

title: Add "Max" thinking effort level for Claude Opus 4.6 in model picker
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Anthropic's API supports effort: "max" for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 ("Absolute maximum capability with no constraints on thinking depth"). The VS Code model picker currently only offers Low/Medium/High. Please add "Max" as a fourth option for models that support it. https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/effort
comments: We do support "max" thinking effort with [BYOK.](https://code.visualstudio.com/blogs/2025/10/22/bring-your-own-key)

title: UI Bug: Clicking "Codex" agent selector in Copilot Chat does not respond
description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:Built-in
- VS Code Version:1.116.0
- OS Version:Windows 10 22H2
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode):Agent Selection (Codex)
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet):Codex
- Logs:Noting in Output view

Steps to Reproduce:

1.Open the Copilot Chat panel in VS Code.
2.Click on the agent selector icon at the bottom left (usually showing "Local" or "Cloud").
3.In the dropdown menu, click on the "Codex" option.
4.Actual Result: The menu closes, but the agent does not switch to Codex; the selection remains unchanged and no error message is shown.
5.Expected Result: The chat agent should switch to Codex, and the icon/text in the bottom bar should update accordingly.

Additional Context:
I have confirmed that other agents like "Claude" or "Cloud" are working correctly, and I have an active Copilot subscription. Restarting VS Code does not resolve the issue.

title: Infected by JS.Siggen5.44590
description: Version 1.116.0-1 in [AUR](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/visual-studio-code-bin) is infected with JS.Siggen5.44590.
comments: I have the same problem. It's preventing the update from installing because the JS file is blocked by antivirus. I can't manage it because of policies. Fix it please.
I can confirm, Version: 1.116.0 Commit: 560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679 contains infected es5-ext module. (I've extracted it from app bundle and sent to online scanner)

While [it looks harmless](https://github.com/medikoo/es5-ext/blob/main/_postinstall.js) at first sight, it is a registered threat in major antivirus scanners and might make troubles to end users.

It might be introduced by https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/62a503dc25486befdac4c02f43dfb691f8362c3b ([package.json](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/62a503dc25486befdac4c02f43dfb691f8362c3b#diff-7ae45ad102eab3b6d7e7896acd08c427a9b25b346470d7bc6507b6481575d519R90)) as `@microsoft/dev-tunnels-connections@1.3.41` depends on `es5-ext@0.10.64`

```
├─┬ @microsoft/dev-tunnels-connections@1.3.41
│ ├─┬ es5-ext@0.10.64
│ │ ├─┬ es6-iterator@2.0.3
│ │ │ ├─┬ d@1.0.1
│ │ │ │ └── es5-ext@0.10.64 deduped
│ │ │ └── es5-ext@0.10.64 deduped
│ │ └─┬ esniff@2.0.1
│ │   ├── es5-ext@0.10.64 deduped
│ │   └─┬ event-emitter@0.3.5
│ │     └── es5-ext@0.10.64 deduped
│ └─┬ websocket@1.0.35
│   └── es5-ext@0.10.64 deduped
```
@aol-nnov Its unexpected behavior what can lead to bigger threats. There should be zero-patience politics against such kinds of things.

@alersrt you might better file an upstream bug and link it here rather than debating on the threat size...

Let's make it clear: it's not a supply chain attack or anything. It' a well-known, ahem, "feature" of es5-ext. And it should be addressed upstream first-hand.

@aol-nnov We're exactly here already. And again — it's considered as malicious because it has undocumented behavior. Such kind of things are unacceptable for production use, that's the main reason why this issue was opened at all.

Similar issue was closed [#160012](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/160012).

It doesn't seem like anything special for now, but it's also not a serious approach for a company of this level.

The risk that data on my computer could be destroyed or stolen after such antics after some autoupdate is funny.
While multiple developers are dumping es5-ext or pinning to older versions, others create maintainable forks with no b-shit attached: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@unes/es5-ext
@aol-nnov Fork is okay, but it's using should be managed on the Microsoft side.
@alersrt keep calm and wait for the official response. I'm just ~~мимокрокодил~~ random guy from the Internet and throwing infos I've found along the way.
@connor4312 Should this issue closing be considered as not planned to fix?

There is no exposure in product. The issue refers to a postinstall nag script that the module has. Just as in the linked issue, that is never shipped in VS Code, and the module is otherwise benign/useful (though technically extraneous as it's a polyfill for older browsers we don't support anyway, [usage here](https://github.com/theturtle32/WebSocket-Node/blob/47067c97d656f0a5449a7360d7afecba94ab24ce/lib/browser.js#L6)) so no immediate plans to fix. But if someone wants to make a PR to stub it out feel free.

>  that is never shipped in VS Code

@connor4312 it is, actually. And it triggers anti-virus software that is not always under end-user's control. That's why this issue exists.

```
stat /Applications/Visual\ Studio\ Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/node_modules/es5-ext/_postinstall.js
16777223 312417991 -rwxr-xr-x 1 aol staff 0 2553 "Apr 15 15:44:59 2026" "Apr 12 21:09:10 2026" "Apr 15 15:44:56 2026" "Apr 12 21:09:10 2026" 4096 8 0 /Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/node_modules/es5-ext/_postinstall.js
```

Same on Windows, I suppose.

title: Explore subagent behavior lost after merge into search_subagent
description:
## Problem

The Explore subagent's deep search behavior is no longer accessible. PR #309935 merged Explore into `search_subagent` with a `thoroughness` parameter (`normal` | `deep`), but the `thoroughness` schema is gated behind `SearchSubagentThoroughnessEnabled` experiment flag. When the flag is off, the model only sees `normal` mode — the Explore behavior is effectively gone and Plan mode's `Explore` subagent reference is broken.

## Root cause

1. `thoroughness` parameter is only added to the tool schema when the experiment is enabled
2. Without it, the prompt lacks the deep exploration guidance ("Go broad to narrow…")
3. The relaxed prompt wording ("Once you have searched" vs "Once you have thoroughly searched") further reduces exploration depth

## Key files

- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/tools/node/searchSubagentTool.ts` — experiment gate for thoroughness
- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompts/node/agent/searchSubagentPrompt.tsx` — thoroughness-dependent prompt guidance
- `extensions/copilot/src/platform/configuration/common/configurationService.ts` — `ConfigKey.SearchSubagentThoroughnessEnabled`

## Suggestion

Fix plan agent to use the right agent.

Consider enabling `thoroughness` by default or restoring the deeper search prompt when the experiment is off.
comments: Looks like user-error on my side as original cuase and me misreading the debugging by my agent.

- VS Code Version: 1.116.0 (Universal)
- OS Version: macos 26.0.1

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a Widget/Page named SamplePage inside Sample.dart file
2. Now inside main.dart file, try to return the SamplePage() as homepage or whatever importing by typing "Samp" it will auto complete until yesterday night in v1.115, but once I updated vscode now the Auto suggestion only shows the default Widgets which is Available in the SDK (Only if Available).

Eg: In the attached video, I try to use my custom widget named `Iconbutton()`, but it only suggests the default widget from the sdk named `IconButton()` only.
Once I manually right click and Imported the file, then if I type Iconbu, it comes in auto suggestion.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c4169a62-6f0e-4b8a-8456-385a26ecfbaa
comments: No Changes made in Flutter sdk ✅ Flutter extension ✅ Dart extension ✅ My Project code ✅ Just Reverting back to [1.115](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_115) solved the problem ✅ Note: This tiny button stimulated me to UPDATE to the latest version. ⚠️
@DanTup I don't think the VS Code update can influence this, right?

I can't think of a reason why a VS Code update would affect this, but it does sound odd if downgrading fixed it again.

@irajeshh are you able to reproduce this issue in any open source project that I can test/debug with?

@irajeshh it looks like the code completion popup has additional items and a scrollbar - could the issue just be related to the size of the completion popup? I'm not sure why the size would be smaller after the upgrade, but if you upgrade and manually resize it again (or just move down through the list), is it in there? Edit: I presume it's not the sorting has changed? I tried to repro, but it shows up at the top: If you have a repro that reproduces this that I can test with, that would help. If not, you could try: - typing `Iconbut` and then closing completion with `esc` - run the **Dart: Capture Analysis Server Logs** command from the command palette - press Ctrl+Space/Cmd+Space to trigger code completion (reproducing the issue) - click **Cancel** on the logging notification to stop logging and open the log file In the log file, there should be LSP protocol traffic for the completion request which might be useful. You only need to share the line that contains the `completion` request and then subsequent responses with the same `id`. Be sure there's nothing sensitive in anything you share.

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: 

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 
- OS Version: 

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a 330MB file in VS Code
2. VS Code crashes immediately or shortly after the file is opened

**Expected:** VS Code should handle large files gracefully without crashing.
**Actual:** VS Code crashes upon opening a 330MB file.
comments:
You can solve this by increasing your starting memory allocation using the flag --max-memory=4096mb.  also disable all your extensions. ik you mentioned you aren't using any

extension but it's still best to launch with the --disable-extensions flag just to be sure.  check your  code settings to verify if large file optimization is enabled or not and code uses electron under the hood so it naturally takes a lot of RAM to open and maintain 200mb+ large files

description:

<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->

<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.106.1 (user setup)
- OS Version: Windows 11

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Connect to a remote server using Remote-SSH.
2.Directly open a relatively large image file (approximately 20MB) from the remote folder in VS Code.
3.Repeat the above steps multiple times. The CacheStorage folder size grows rapidly.

Expected Behavior
The WebView cache should have a reasonable automatic cleanup mechanism, or provide a setting option to limit the maximum cache size.
Actual Behavior
The cache is never automatically cleaned up. The only solution is to manually delete the entire folder. Every time a large image is opened, a large amount of new cache data is generated.
Additional Information

Related existing issues: #166632 #152519
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.116.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary `CursorWorkspaceMcpDiscoveryAdapter.watchFolder()` calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.cursor', 'mcp.json')` without checking if the folder's URI has a `path` component. In the Agents/Sessions environment, workspace folders can be added with URIs that lack a path (e.g., virtual workspace schemes or certain remote URIs), causing `URI.joinPath()` to throw `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path`. This error has been consistently present since at least v1.114.0-insider (March 27, 2026) across all platforms and affects ~276 users on the latest build alone. ### Culprit Commit No specific culprit commit was identified. The error has been present since the file was first created — the file content at every commit in the error bucket history (spanning v1.114.0 through v1.116.0) is byte-identical. No commit range was provided in the issue body or comments by the regression scan workflow. The bug is a missing guard that has existed since the `CursorWorkspaceMcpDiscoveryAdapter` class was authored. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant WCS as SessionsWorkspaceContextService participant Adapter as CursorWorkspaceMcpDiscoveryAdapter participant Resources as resources.ts (joinPath) participant URI as URI.joinPath WCS->>WCS: _doUpdateFolders() — adds folder with path-less URI WCS->>Adapter: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders (e.added) Adapter->>Adapter: watchFolder(folder) Note over Adapter: ⚠️ Root cause:
No guard for folder.uri.path Adapter->>Resources: joinPath(folder.uri, '.cursor', 'mcp.json') Resources->>URI: URI.joinPath(uri, ...pathFragment) Note over URI: 💥 !uri.path → throws
[UriError]: cannot call
joinPath on URI without path ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/discovery/workspaceMcpDiscoveryAdapter.ts` | **Root cause** — `watchFolder()` (lines 50-76) calls `joinPath` without guarding `folder.uri.path` | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Crash site** — `URI.joinPath()` (line 361) throws when `!uri.path` | | `src/vs/base/common/resources.ts` | Data flow — delegates to `URI.joinPath` | | `src/vs/sessions/services/workspace/browser/workspaceContextService.ts` | Trigger — `_doUpdateFolders()` fires the workspace change event | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) 2. Add a workspace folder that uses a URI scheme without a path component (e.g., a virtual filesystem scheme or a remote URI with only an authority) 3. The `CursorWorkspaceMcpDiscoveryAdapter` listens to `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` and calls `watchFolder()` on the new folder 4. `joinPath(folder.uri, '.cursor', 'mcp.json')` throws because `folder.uri.path` is empty/undefined This is non-deterministic in that it depends on what workspace folder URIs are used — it only triggers for URIs without a path component. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/discovery/workspaceMcpDiscoveryAdapter.ts` → `watchFolder()` **Approach**: Add an early return guard at the top of `watchFolder()` to skip folders whose URI has no path. The `.cursor/mcp.json` config file only makes sense for filesystem-based workspace folders, so folders with path-less URIs should be silently skipped: ```typescript private watchFolder(folder: IWorkspaceFolder) { if (!folder.uri.path) { return; } // ...existing code unchanged... } ``` This follows the same defensive pattern used by `NativeFilesystemMcpDiscovery.setDetails()` (line 142 of `nativeMcpDiscoveryAbstract.ts`), which checks `if (!file) { continue; }` before calling `watchFile`. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — MCP area owner per `triage/working-areas.md` (owns MCP UI, MCP Management, MCP Gallery) > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24487462087/agentic_workflow) · ● 10.5M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path` is thrown in `debugConfigurationManager.ts` when the debug `Launch` class's `uri` getter calls `resources.joinPath(this.workspace.uri, '/.vscode/launch.json')` on a workspace folder whose URI (`unknown:`) has no path component. This occurs in the Agents (sessions) product when an active session has no repository metadata — the `AgentSessionAdapter._buildWorkspace()` method uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as a fallback, producing a pathless URI that gets added as a workspace folder. On any workspace folder change event, the debug configuration manager creates `Launch` objects from all folders and accesses their `uri` getter, which throws. This is the **same root cause** as #308827 and its related bucket family (8+ error buckets, 760+ hits). ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit was identified for this specific bucket. The error first appeared on 1.115.0-insider commit `6c8fcf18` (April 6) and has been present across all subsequent 1.115.0-insider builds. The root cause code (`URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback in `_buildWorkspace()`) pre-dates the 1.115.0 release branch, suggesting this is a latent issue that was either introduced with the sessions workspace infrastructure or became triggerable when sessions without repository metadata started becoming active more frequently. The broader error class was already identified in #308827, which covers the same root cause through different call paths (including `workspaceExtensionsConfig`, `promptFilesLocator`, `snippetsService`, etc.). This issue (#310370) is specifically the `debugConfigurationManager` call path variant. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant WCS as WorkspaceContextService participant CM as ConfigurationManager participant Launch as Launch participant URI as URI.joinPath WCS->>CM: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders / onDidChangeWorkbenchState CM->>CM: initLaunches() — creates Launch per folder CM->>CM: selectConfiguration(undefined) CM->>Launch: this.selectedLaunch.uri (line 433) Launch->>URI: joinPath(workspace.uri, '/.vscode/launch.json') Note over URI: workspace.uri = URI.parse('unknown:')
— no path component Note over URI: 💥 [UriError]: cannot call
joinPath on URI without path ``` **Root cause origin:** ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Session as AgentSession (no repo metadata) participant Builder as _buildWorkspace() participant FolderMgmt as WorkspaceFolderManagement participant WCS as WorkspaceContextService Session->>Builder: _extractRepositoryFromMetadata() → [undefined, ...] Note over Builder: ⚠️ Root cause: repoUri is undefined
→ falls back to URI.parse('unknown:') Builder->>FolderMgmt: workspace = { repositories: [{ uri: unknown: }] } FolderMgmt->>FolderMgmt: getActiveSessionFolderData() → { uri: unknown: } Note over FolderMgmt: No path validation on repository URI FolderMgmt->>WCS: addFolders / updateFolders Note over WCS: Folder with pathless URI is now
in the workspace ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` (line 978) uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as fallback when session has no repository metadata, producing a pathless URI | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | **Data flow** — `getActiveSessionFolderData()` (line 82) passes through the `unknown:` URI as a workspace folder without validating it has a path | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/debug/browser/debugConfigurationManager.ts` | **Crash site** — `Launch.uri` getter (line 646) calls `joinPath(this.workspace.uri, ...)` and `selectConfiguration` (line 433) accesses the `uri` getter | | `src/vs/sessions/services/workspace/browser/workspaceContextService.ts` | **Event source** — `_doUpdateFolders` (line 163) fires `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` that triggers the debug configuration manager | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Throw site** — `URI.joinPath()` (line 362) throws when URI has no path | ### Repro Steps 1. Open the VS Code Agents (sessions) application 2. Have an active session whose metadata does not include repository information (no `repositoryPath`, `workingDirectoryPath`, or cloud repo metadata) — the `_extractRepositoryFromMetadata()` method returns `[undefined, ...]` 3. The session's workspace gets a folder with `URI.parse('unknown:')` as the URI 4. Any workspace folder change event triggers `ConfigurationManager.registerListeners()` → `initLaunches()` → `selectConfiguration(undefined)` 5. `selectConfiguration` accesses `this.selectedLaunch.uri` (line 433), which triggers the `Launch.uri` getter that calls `resources.joinPath(this.workspace.uri, '/.vscode/launch.json')` 6. `URI.joinPath` throws because `unknown:` has no path component The error is deterministic once a session without repo metadata becomes active and the debug configuration manager initializes. ### Fix Proposal This is the same root cause as #308827. The fix should be applied at the source of the pathless URI rather than in each consumer: **Primary fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()` **Approach**: When `repoUri` is `undefined`, either: 1. Return `undefined` from `_buildWorkspace()` instead of constructing a workspace with a pathless URI. The method signature already allows `ISessionWorkspace | undefined`. 2. Or change the fallback URI to include a root path: `URI.from({ scheme: 'unknown', path: '/' })` — this ensures `joinPath` can operate without throwing. **Secondary defense**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` → `getActiveSessionFolderData()` (line 82) — add a guard to reject URIs without a path (`if (!repository.path) return undefined`), preventing any pathless URI from becoming a workspace folder regardless of how it was produced. **Note**: A fix in `debugConfigurationManager.ts` alone (e.g., try/catch around the `uri` getter) would only address this specific call path; the same error would persist through the 7+ other code paths identified in #308827. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Already assigned to #308827 which tracks the same root cause. Primary maintainer of `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` and the sessions workspace infrastructure where the fix should be applied. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24487510022/agentic_workflow) · ● 4.5M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The sessions `ConfigurationService` throws `"Unknown folder"` when a workspace folder is removed before its configuration has been loaded into the internal `folderConfigurations` map. This is a race condition inherent to the sessions configuration service design — the `onWorkspaceFoldersChanged` handler iterates `e.removed` folders and calls `compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration()`, which throws if the folder config was never loaded. The error affects the Agents product across all platforms (Linux, Windows, Mac) and has been present since version 1.114.0-insider (March 27, 2026), with increasing hit counts as adoption grows — currently **~600+ hits / 268 users** on the latest 1.116 insider builds. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit — this is an inherent design bug in the sessions `ConfigurationService` introduced when the service was originally created. PR #299302 by `@sandy081` ("fix reading configuration from workspace folders", merged March 5, 2026) is the closest related PR. The error has been present across versions 1.114 through 1.117, hitting consistently on every insider build. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Caller as Workspace Consumer participant WCS as SessionsWorkspaceContextService participant CS as Sessions ConfigurationService participant Cfg as Configuration (platform) Caller->>WCS: addFolders([A]) / updateFolders(...) WCS->>CS: onWillChangeWorkspaceFolders (added: [A]) CS->>CS: loadFolderConfigurations([A]) (async) Note over CS: ⏳ Awaiting folder config load... WCS->>WCS: workspace.update(newWorkspace) WCS->>CS: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders (removed: []) Note over CS: ✅ No removals, OK Caller->>WCS: removeFolders([A]) WCS->>CS: onWillChangeWorkspaceFolders (added: []) Note over CS: Nothing to load WCS->>WCS: workspace.update(newWorkspace) WCS->>CS: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders (removed: [A]) CS->>Cfg: compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration(A) Note over Cfg: ⚠️ folderConfigurations.get(A)
returns undefined if config
never loaded (race/init timing) Note over Cfg: 💥 throw new Error('Unknown folder') ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/platform/configuration/common/configurationModels.ts` | 💥 Crash site — `compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration()` throws at line 946 | | `src/vs/workbench/services/configuration/common/configurationModels.ts` | Data flow — override delegates to super at line 140 | | `src/vs/sessions/services/configuration/browser/configurationService.ts` | ⚠️ Root cause — `onWorkspaceFoldersChanged()` at line 262 iterates `e.removed` unsafely | | `src/vs/sessions/services/workspace/browser/workspaceContextService.ts` | Trigger — `_doUpdateFolders()` fires events at line 163 | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders with the Agents product (sessions enabled) 2. Create or enter a multi-folder workspace in a session 3. Rapidly add and remove workspace folders (or trigger a workspace update that removes a folder before its configuration has been fully loaded) 4. The error fires when a removed folder's configuration was never loaded into the `folderConfigurations` map — this can happen if: - `initialize()` hasn't completed when a folder removal event arrives - `loadFolderConfigurations()` fails or is still in-flight when a removal occurs - The `_configuration` object is replaced by `initialize()` (line 102-114) while folder changes are in progress This is non-deterministic and depends on timing between async config loading and folder removal events. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/services/configuration/browser/configurationService.ts` → `onWorkspaceFoldersChanged()` **Approach**: Follow the pattern used by the workbench `ConfigurationService.onFoldersChanged()` (line 944-962 of `src/vs/workbench/services/configuration/browser/configurationService.ts`). Instead of iterating `e.removed` and calling `compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration()` directly, iterate over `this.cachedFolderConfigs.keys()` and only delete folders that are no longer in the workspace. This ensures only folders whose configs were actually loaded are deleted: ``` // Current (unsafe) pattern in sessions ConfigurationService: for (const folder of e.removed) { const change = this._configuration.compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration(folder.uri); // throws! } // Suggested fix (safe pattern from workbench ConfigurationService): for (const key of this.cachedFolderConfigs.keys()) { if (!this.workspace.folders.some(f => f.uri.toString() === key.toString())) { this.cachedFolderConfigs.deleteAndDispose(key); changes.push(this._configuration.compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration(key)); } } ```` This guarantees `compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration` is only called for folders that have entries in the `folderConfigurations` map, eliminating the race condition. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Original author of the sessions `ConfigurationService` and configuration infrastructure (PR #299302), and primary contributor to sessions workspace/configuration services. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24487432059/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.6M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary `getSessionTasks` in `sessionsConfigurationService.ts` calls `_ensureFileWatch(folder)` with a URI that has no path component. This happens when a session's repository has no metadata, causing `_buildWorkspace` in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` to fall back to `URI.parse('unknown:')` — a URI with scheme `unknown` and an empty path. The `joinPath` call inside `_ensureFileWatch` then throws because it cannot join paths on a URI without a path. The error affects 101+ users across all platforms. ### Culprit Commit No single regression commit was identified. This is a **latent interaction bug** between two code paths: 1. `_buildWorkspace()` in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` (line 978) uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as a fallback when a session has no repository metadata — creating a URI with no path. 2. `getSessionTasks()` in `sessionsConfigurationService.ts` (line 148–153) checks `if (folder)` but a pathless URI object is truthy, so it passes through to `_ensureFileWatch` which calls `joinPath` and crashes. The `_ensureFileWatch` and `getSessionTasks` code was introduced by `@benibenj` in PRs #297027 / #297371 (Feb 2026). The `URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback was introduced later. The error first appeared in telemetry on April 7 with commit `d09c516f` (1 hit) and grew to 147 hits / 101 users by `41dd792b`. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Provider as copilotChatSessionsProvider participant Model as agentSessionsModel participant RunScript as runScriptAction participant Config as sessionsConfigurationService participant URI as URI.joinPath Provider->>Model: _refreshSessionCache → fire onDidChangeSessions Model->>RunScript: observable recompute (_computeFn) RunScript->>Config: getSessionTasks(activeSession) Note over Config: _getSessionRepo(session)
→ repo.uri = URI.parse('unknown:') Config->>Config: folder = repo.workingDirectory ?? repo.uri Note over Config: ⚠️ Root cause:
folder is truthy but has no path
(if (folder) passes) Config->>Config: _ensureFileWatch(folder) Config->>URI: joinPath(folder, '.vscode', 'tasks.json') Note over URI: 💥 Error thrown:
[UriError]: cannot call joinPath
on URI without path ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionsConfigurationService.ts` | **Crash site** — `_ensureFileWatch` (L378) calls `joinPath` on pathless URI | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause producer** — `_buildWorkspace` (L978) creates `URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/runScriptAction.ts` | Data flow — observable chain triggers `getSessionTasks` | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | Guard — `joinPath` correctly validates URI has a path | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) 2. Create or open a session that has **no repository metadata** (e.g., a session where `_extractRepositoryFromMetadata` returns all `undefined` values — this can happen with sessions that haven't fully resolved or have incomplete metadata) 3. The observable chain from `runScriptAction.ts` triggers `getSessionTasks` on the active session 4. `_buildWorkspace` returns a workspace with `uri: URI.parse('unknown:')` (no path) 5. `getSessionTasks` passes this URI to `_ensureFileWatch`, which crashes on `joinPath` This is non-deterministic in the sense that it depends on session metadata availability. It triggers most often when sessions are being resolved or refreshed with incomplete data. ### Fix Proposal **Two complementary fixes are recommended:** **Fix 1 (primary)** — `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionsConfigurationService.ts` → `getSessionTasks()` **Approach**: Add a path check before calling `_ensureFileWatch`. Change the guard from `if (folder)` to `if (folder?.path)` to ensure the URI actually has a path before attempting to join paths on it. This mirrors the defensive pattern already used in `_getTasksJsonUri` (line 360) which also calls `joinPath` but only after confirming `folder` is defined. **Fix 2 (defensive)** — `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()` **Approach**: When `repoUri` is undefined, either return `undefined` from `_buildWorkspace` instead of creating a workspace with a fake `unknown:` URI, or give the fallback URI a path (e.g., `URI.parse('unknown:/')`) so downstream consumers don't crash. The current `URI.parse('unknown:')` creates a URI that is technically invalid for any file-system-like operations. ### Recommended Owner `@benibenj` — author of `sessionsConfigurationService.ts` (`getSessionTasks`, `_ensureFileWatch`) where the primary fix should go (PRs #297027, #297371, #302478) > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24487423759/agentic_workflow) · ● 15.4M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The `WorkspaceSettingsReader` in `workspacePluginSettingsService.ts` calls `joinPath(f.uri, configFolder)` on every workspace folder URI whenever workspace folders change, but does not guard against folders whose URIs have no path component. When a session's workspace contains a repository or worktree URI without a path (e.g., a URI with only a scheme and authority), `URI.joinPath` throws `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path`. This affects the Agents product across all platforms, with **3,470 total hits / 1,443 users** spanning versions 1.114 through 1.116. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`342a781`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/300585/commits/342a78145fe05163a37faf8f17a19ac78f702e86) | | Author | `@connor4312` | | PR | #300585 | | Message | plugins: initial support for workspace plugins | | Why | Introduced `WorkspaceSettingsReader` which unconditionally calls `joinPath` on all workspace folder URIs without validating that the URI has a path. The error has been present since this file was first added (merged March 11, milestone 1.112.0). | ### Code Flow ````mermaid sequenceDiagram participant WFM as WorkspaceFolderManagement participant WCS as SessionsWorkspaceContextService participant OBS as observableFromEvent participant WSR as WorkspaceSettingsReader participant URI as URI.joinPath WFM->>WCS: addFolders([{uri: pathlessUri}]) WCS->>WCS: _doUpdateFolders() Note over WCS: Creates WorkspaceFolder
with URI lacking path WCS->>OBS: fire(onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders) OBS->>WSR: getValue() callback WSR->>URI: joinPath(f.uri, ".claude") Note over URI: ⚠️ Root cause:
f.uri.path is empty string URI->>URI: 💥 throw Error("[UriError]:
cannot call joinPath on
URI without path") ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/plugins/workspacePluginSettingsService.ts` | **Root cause** — line 190: unguarded `joinPath` in observable callback | | `src/vs/sessions/services/workspace/browser/workspaceContextService.ts` | **Trigger** — line 163: fires `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` without validating folder URIs have paths | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | **Data producer** — provides workspace folder URIs from session data | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Crash site** — line 362: `joinPath` throws when `uri.path` is falsy | ### Repro Steps 1. Open the VS Code Agents product (vscode.dev with agents enabled) 2. Start or switch to a session whose workspace includes a repository URI without a path component (e.g., a URI with only scheme + authority, or a session that hasn't fully resolved its workspace folder path) 3. The `WorkspaceFolderManagement` contribution adds this folder to the workspace context 4. The `WorkspaceSettingsReader` observable reacts and attempts to `joinPath` on the pathless URI 5. The error is thrown as an unhandled error from the observable callback > **Note**: The exact workspace configuration that produces a pathless URI depends on the session provider and its data. The error is cross-platform (Windows, Linux, Mac) and affects ~1,400 unique users, suggesting it occurs under relatively common workspace configurations. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/plugins/workspacePluginSettingsService.ts` → `WorkspaceSettingsReader` constructor (line 190) **Approach**: Filter out workspace folders whose URIs have no path before calling `joinPath`. Change the `observableFromEvent` callback from: ``` folders.map(f => joinPath(f.uri, configFolder)) ``` to: ``` folders.filter(f => f.uri.path).map(f => joinPath(f.uri, configFolder)) ```` Alternatively (defense in depth), also add a guard in `SessionsWorkspaceContextService._doUpdateFolders` to skip or normalize workspace folders whose URIs lack a path, preventing the invalid state from propagating to all downstream observers. ### Recommended Owner `@connor4312` — author of `workspacePluginSettingsService.ts` (PR #300585) and the workspace plugin system. This is a missing guard in the code they introduced. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24487445951/agentic_workflow) · ● 3.9M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

**Root cause**: Workspace folder URIs in the Agents product lack a `path` component. The same root cause affects multiple components — see also #310366, #310364, #310367, #310368, #310371, #310370, and #308827.

<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-triage -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path` is thrown when `workspaceRecommendations.ts` handles a file change event and iterates workspace folders, calling `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')` on a folder whose URI (`unknown:`) has no path component. This is the **same root cause** as the family of errors tracked in #308827 — workspace folder URIs in the Agents product lack a `path` component because `AgentSessionAdapter._buildWorkspace()` uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as a fallback when a session has no repository metadata. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit was identified. The error bucket history shows this error has been present since at least **1.114.0-insider** (first seen 2026-03-27) across all insider versions through 1.116.0. This is a systemic issue in the Agents workspace model, not a single-commit regression. The `URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback at `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:978` has existed since the sessions workspace infrastructure was introduced. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant IPC as File Watcher (IPC) participant FS as FileService participant WR as WorkspaceRecommendations participant ExtUri as extUri.joinPath participant URI as URI.joinPath IPC->>FS: onDidFilesChange FS->>WR: onDidFilesChange(e) WR->>WR: getWorkspace().folders.some(folder => ...) Note over WR: Iterates ALL workspace folders
including folder with URI 'unknown:' WR->>ExtUri: joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode/extensions') ExtUri->>URI: URI.joinPath(URI.parse('unknown:'), ...) Note over URI: 💥 [UriError]: cannot call
joinPath on URI without path Note over WR: ⚠️ Root cause: _buildWorkspace()
uses URI.parse('unknown:') fallback
when session has no repo metadata ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` (line 978) uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` (no path) as fallback when session has no repository metadata | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | **Data flow** — `getActiveSessionFolderData()` passes the `unknown:` URI through as a workspace folder URI without validating it has a path | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/workspaceRecommendations.ts` | **Crash site** — line 61-62: `onDidFilesChange` handler calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')` on all workspace folders | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/workspaceRecommendations.ts` | **Crash site (2nd bucket `f6bd80ca`)** — line 80: `fetchWorkspaceExtensions()` calls `joinPath(workspaceFolder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')` | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Throw site** — `URI.joinPath()` line 362 throws when URI has no path | ### Repro Steps 1. Open the VS Code Agents (sessions) application 2. Have an active session whose metadata does not include repository information (no `repositoryPath`, `workingDirectoryPath`, or cloud repo metadata) 3. The session's workspace folder is created with a `unknown:` URI (no path component) 4. Any file change event fires (e.g., from MCP tool registration, schema association changes, or actual filesystem activity) 5. `WorkspaceRecommendations.onDidFilesChange` iterates all workspace folders and calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')`, which throws on the pathless URI The error is deterministic once a session without repo metadata becomes active — it triggers on every file change event. Telemetry shows **16,600 hits from 104 users** on version 1.115.0 alone (commit `41dd792b`). ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()` **Approach**: This is the same root cause as #308827 and should be addressed together. The fix should either: 1. **Preferred**: Change the fallback URI to include a root path: `URI.from({ scheme: 'unknown', path: '/' })` — this ensures `joinPath` can operate without throwing, fixing all 8+ downstream call paths at once. 2. **Alternative**: Return `undefined` from `_buildWorkspace()` when `repoUri` is undefined, preventing a workspace folder from being created at all for sessions without repository metadata. The method signature already allows `undefined`. 3. **Defense in depth**: Add a guard in `workspaceFolderManagement.ts` → `getActiveSessionFolderData()` to reject URIs without a path (`if (!repository.path) return undefined`). ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Already assigned to the parent issue #308827 with the same root cause. Primary maintainer of the sessions workspace management layer and `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts`. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24487475155/agentic_workflow) · ● 6M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path` occurs in the Agents product when a session has no repository metadata. The `_buildWorkspace()` method in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` creates a fallback URI `URI.parse('unknown:')` which has **no path component**. When workspace folders change, `textFileService.ts` calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode')` on every folder URI, and this path-less URI causes the throw. This is a persistent issue across versions 1.114–1.116 affecting ~1,600+ users cumulatively. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`f62d4324`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/f62d4324c5cbb0a5188f117f5a5c2344be2ae790) (via PR #309777) | | Author | `@aeschli` | | PR | #309777 | | Message | Empty sessions view: cannot call joinPath | | Why | This PR changed `URI.parse('unknown://')` to `URI.parse('unknown:')` and improved the error message, but **both URIs have empty paths** — the fix was incomplete. The original `unknown://` fallback was introduced earlier when `_buildWorkspace` was created. The PR author noted: "Maybe you want to revisit `unknown:///`" — suggesting the proper fix was not applied. | > **Note**: The error existed *before* PR #309777 as well (since at least March 27, 2026). The original `URI.parse('unknown://')` also has no path. PR #309777 was an incomplete fix attempt. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant WFM as WorkspaceFolderManagement participant WCS as SessionsWorkspaceContextService participant TFS as TextFileService participant URI as URI.joinPath WFM->>WFM: updateWorkspaceFoldersForSession() Note over WFM: Session has no repo metadata
→ getActiveSessionFolderData()
returns uri: URI.parse('unknown:') WFM->>WCS: addFolders([{uri: unknown:}]) WCS->>WCS: _doUpdateFolders() WCS->>TFS: fire onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders TFS->>TFS: getDefaultEncodingOverrides() loop Each workspace folder TFS->>URI: joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode') Note over URI: ⚠️ folder.uri.path is ''
(empty = falsy) URI-->>TFS: 💥 throw [UriError]:
cannot call joinPath on
URI without path end ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` line 978 creates `URI.parse('unknown:')` with no path | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | Data flow — passes the path-less URI as a workspace folder | | `src/vs/workbench/services/textfile/browser/textFileService.ts` | **Crash site** — `getDefaultEncodingOverrides()` line 813 calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode')` | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | Error thrown at `URI.joinPath()` line 362 when `uri.path` is falsy | ### Related Issues - **#308827** — Same root cause (`unknown:` URI with no path), different crash site (`workspaceExtensionsConfig.ts` / `mcpToolRegistration`). Already assigned to `@sandy081`. ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Agents (sessions desktop) 2. Create or switch to a session that has **no repository metadata** (e.g., an empty/new session without a configured repository) 3. The workspace folder management adds a folder with URI `unknown:` (no path) 4. Any code listening to `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` that calls `joinPath` on the folder URI will throw The error is triggered automatically when workspace folders update — no explicit user action beyond having a session without repo metadata is required. ### Fix Proposal **Primary fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()` **Option A (preferred)**: When `repoUri` is `undefined`, do not create a workspace at all — return `undefined` from `_buildWorkspace()` instead of using a meaningless `unknown:` fallback. The downstream code in `workspaceFolderManagement.ts` already handles `undefined` workspace data (lines 45-49). **Option B (minimal)**: Change `URI.parse('unknown:')` to `URI.parse('unknown:///')` which includes a root path `/`. This prevents the `joinPath` error but leaves a non-resolvable URI as a workspace folder, which may cause other issues (as `@aeschli` noted in PR #309777). **Option C (defensive)**: Add a guard in `textFileService.ts` → `getDefaultEncodingOverrides()` to skip folders whose URIs have no path: `if (folder.uri.path) { ... }`. This protects against *any* path-less URI but only addresses this one call site — other listeners (like #308827's `workspaceExtensionsConfig.ts`) would still crash. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Already assigned to the sibling issue #308827 (same root cause, different crash site) and is the primary author of the sessions workspace management code (PR #298395, multiple `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` changes). > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24487406138/agentic_workflow) · ● 10.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary `BannerPart.show()` calls `clearNode(this.element)` but `this.element` is only assigned during `createContentArea()`, which runs when the workbench layout initializes the part. If `WorkspaceTrustManagementService` fires `onDidChangeTrust` before the BannerPart's DOM element is created (a startup timing race), `this.element` is `undefined` and `clearNode()` throws `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'firstChild')`. This is a pre-existing race condition that affects the **Agents** (sessions) product especially, likely due to its different startup sequence. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit was identified. The bucket history shows this error spanning from version 1.113 through 1.117 with increasing hit counts (from 1 hit/version to 76 hits on commit `47add6df` in 1.116). This is a pre-existing race condition between BannerPart layout initialization and workspace trust event firing, not a regression from a specific commit. The growing hit count likely reflects increasing usage of the Agents product. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant WS as WorkspaceTrustMgmt
(workspaceTrust.ts) participant Handler as WorkspaceTrustUXHandler
(workspace.contribution.ts) participant Banner as BannerPart
(bannerPart.ts) participant DOM as clearNode
(dom.ts) Note over Banner: createContentArea() NOT yet called
⚠️ this.element = undefined WS->>WS: updateWorkspaceTrust() WS->>Handler: onDidChangeTrust(trusted=false) Handler->>Handler: updateWorkbenchIndicators(false) Handler->>Handler: getBannerItem(true) → item Handler->>Banner: show(bannerItem) Banner->>DOM: clearNode(this.element) Note over DOM: 💥 TypeError:
Cannot read properties of
undefined (reading 'firstChild')
(this.element is undefined) ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/browser/parts/banner/bannerPart.ts` | **Crash site** — `show()` (line 188) calls `clearNode(this.element)` without checking if element exists | | `src/vs/workbench/browser/part.ts` | **Root cause** — `element!: HTMLElement` (line 181) uses definite assignment assertion but element is actually `undefined` until `create()` is called | | `src/vs/base/browser/dom.ts` | **Throw site** — `clearNode(node)` (line 219) accesses `node.firstChild` on `undefined` | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/workspace/browser/workspace.contribution.ts` | **Trigger** — `updateWorkbenchIndicators()` (line 403) calls `bannerService.show()` during trust change | | `src/vs/workbench/services/workspaces/common/workspaceTrust.ts` | **Event source** — `updateWorkspaceTrust()` (line 348) fires `onDidChangeTrust` | ### Repro Steps 1. Use the **Agents** (sessions) product variant of VS Code Insiders 2. Open a workspace that is **not trusted** (or where trust state changes on startup) 3. The workspace trust service resolves trust state and fires `onDidChangeTrust` before the workbench layout has fully initialized the BannerPart DOM element 4. This is a timing-dependent race condition — it's more likely to occur when workspace trust resolution is fast (e.g., cached trust state) and UI layout is slower 5. The error is non-deterministic; it depends on startup timing ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/browser/parts/banner/bannerPart.ts` → `show()`, `close()`, `focus()` **Approach**: Add a guard clause in `show()` to check if `this.element` is initialized before operating on it. If the element doesn't exist yet, the method should either: 1. **Defer the banner**: Store the pending banner item and render it when `createContentArea()` is called (preferred — ensures the banner is shown once layout is ready), or 2. **Early return**: Simply bail out with a no-op if `this.element` is undefined (simpler but may drop the banner) Option 1 (deferred rendering) is recommended. The `createContentArea()` method should check for a pending item: ``` // In show(): if (!this.element) { this.pendingItem = item; return; } // In createContentArea(): if (this.pendingItem) { this.show(this.pendingItem); } ```` The same guard should be applied to `close()` (line 99) and `focus()` (line 156), which also access `this.element` without null checks. ### Recommended Owner `@lszomoru` — Area owner for Workspace Trust and BannerPart per working-areas.md > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24486158115/agentic_workflow) · ● 4.3M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'element')` occurs in `listView.ts:845` when `TraitSpliceable.splice` (listWidget.ts:240) tries to look up an element at a stale/out-of-bounds trait index during a full list replace in the AI Customization list widget. The crash is triggered when `commitDisplayEntries()` replaces all list items via `splice(0, list.length, newEntries)` and a focus/selection/anchor trait holds an index that exceeds the current view items length. This affects the Agents product across all platforms. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`ec038bfa`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/ec038bfa43a98172fcaf92bc8868ce8ae5fd3517) | | Author | `@joshspicer` | | PR | #307745 | | Message | fix: customization provider API rendering bugs and sessions window isolation | | Why | This PR added multiple new refresh triggers (`onDidChangeInstructions`, `availableHarnesses` in autorun) and changed the group layout for instructions. The increased refresh frequency creates more opportunities for trait indices to become stale relative to the view's items array during async load–splice cycles. The error first appeared in commit `6c8fcf18` (first build after this PR merged). | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Trigger as refresh() trigger
(autorun / event) participant Load as loadItems() participant Filter as filterItems() participant Commit as commitDisplayEntries() participant ListSplice as List.splice() participant Trait as TraitSpliceable participant View as ListView Trigger->>Load: refresh() → loadItems() Load->>Filter: filterItems() Filter->>Commit: groupMatchedItems() → commitDisplayEntries() Commit->>ListSplice: list.splice(0, list.length, displayEntries) ListSplice->>Trait: CombinedSpliceable iterates traits first Note over Trait: trait.get() returns stale index i
where i >= view.items.length Trait->>View: view.element(i) Note over View: 💥 this.items[i] is undefined
Cannot read 'element' ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listView.ts:845` | Crash site — `this.items[index].element` where `items[index]` is undefined | | `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listWidget.ts:240` | `TraitSpliceable.splice` — reads trait indices and accesses `view.element(i)` without bounds checking | | `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listWidget.ts:1621` | `List.splice` — orchestrates via `CombinedSpliceable` (traits first, then view) | | `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/splice.ts:17` | `CombinedSpliceable` — iterates spliceables in order | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` | `commitDisplayEntries()` — calls `list.splice(0, list.length, displayEntries)` to replace all items | ### Repro Steps 1. Open the Agents app (VS Code Insiders 1.115.x) 2. Navigate to the AI Customizations editor (Instructions section) 3. Focus or select an item in the list 4. Trigger rapid list refreshes — e.g., by switching workspaces, enabling/disabling extensions that register customization providers, or having file system changes fire `onDidChangeInstructions` 5. The list refresh cycle (`loadItems` → `filterItems` → `commitDisplayEntries`) replaces all items, and the `TraitSpliceable` reads a stale focus/selection index that exceeds the new items count The error is non-deterministic and depends on timing between user interaction (which sets trait indices) and async refresh triggers. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listWidget.ts` → `TraitSpliceable.splice()` **Approach**: Add a bounds check before accessing `this.view.element(i)` in the `TraitSpliceable.splice` method. When `i >= this.view.length`, skip that trait index (treat it as if the element no longer exists, so it won't be preserved across the splice). This is a defensive guard in the base list infrastructure that prevents the crash regardless of how trait indices become stale: ``` // In TraitSpliceable.splice, line 240: const pastElementsWithTrait = this.trait.get() .filter(i => i >= 0 && i < this.view.length) // ← guard .map(i => this.identityProvider!.getId(this.view.element(i)).toString()); ```` Alternatively, the `aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` could clear the list's focus/selection before calling `commitDisplayEntries()` to prevent stale indices from being read during the splice. ### Recommended Owner `@joshspicer` — author of the culprit commit (PR #307745) and primary contributor to the AI Customization list widget > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24486217728/agentic_workflow) · ● 17.5M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` accesses `this.input.currentModeKind` (line 1006 of `chatWidget.ts`) without a null guard. The `input` getter returns `this.inputPartDisposable.value!` — a non-null assertion that fails at runtime when the `ChatInputPart` hasn't been created yet (widget not rendered) or has been disposed. This is triggered through the async `showModel()` → `updateWidgetLockState()` → `lockToCodingAgent()`/`unlockFromCodingAgent()` call chain, where the widget lifecycle can change during awaited session loading. 205 hits across 135 users spanning versions 1.113–1.117 indicate a persistent async lifecycle race. ### Culprit Commit No specific culprit commit was identified. The issue body has no commit range analysis, and the error telemetry shows hits across 5+ insider versions since v1.113 (2026-03-31), indicating a pre-existing race condition in the widget lifecycle rather than a single regression commit. The `lockToCodingAgent` / `unlockFromCodingAgent` methods have always called `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` without guarding for an uninitialized input part. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant VP as ChatViewPane participant SM as showModel() participant CS as ChatService participant CW as ChatWidget participant RW as renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded() VP->>SM: showModel(token, modelRef) SM->>CS: await acquireOrLoadSession() Note over CS: ⚠️ Async gap: widget
may be disposed or
not yet rendered CS-->>SM: session reference SM->>VP: updateWidgetLockState(sessionType) VP->>CW: lockToCodingAgent(name, displayName, agentId) CW->>RW: renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded() RW->>CW: this.input.currentModeKind Note over CW: 💥 this.input is undefined
(inputPartDisposable.value is null) ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` | Crash site (line 1006) and root cause — `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` accesses `this.input` without null guard; `lockToCodingAgent()` (line 2161) and `unlockFromCodingAgent()` (line 2181) call it unsafely | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts` | Trigger — `showModel()` (line 745) awaits async session loading then calls `updateWidgetLockState()` which invokes lock/unlock on the widget | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) with a chat session type that resolves to a coding agent (e.g., a contributed session) 2. Rapidly open/close the chat view pane, or trigger a session commit event while the pane is being disposed 3. The async `showModel()` starts loading a session, the widget gets disposed during the await, and `lockToCodingAgent` runs on the stale widget 4. The error is non-deterministic — it depends on the timing of widget disposal vs. async session resolution ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` → `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` **Approach**: Add an early return guard at the top of `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` to check whether the input part has been initialized: ```typescript if (!this.inputPartDisposable.value) { return; } ``` This is consistent with the existing defensive pattern already used elsewhere in the widget — for example, `unlockFromCodingAgent()` uses `this.inputEditor?.updateOptions(...)` (line 2187) with optional chaining. The same safety should apply to `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()`. Additionally, `lockToCodingAgent()` and `unlockFromCodingAgent()` could check `this._isDisposed` before proceeding, since these methods may be called from an async context after the widget has been disposed. ### Recommended Owner `@roblourens` — already assigned; active maintainer of the chat widget and sessions infrastructure (most recent commits to both affected files) > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24485184189/agentic_workflow) · ● 9.7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
We have a temporary workaround on our end by updating the launch.json file to use `"console": "integratedTerminal"`

Here is what Opus thinks caused it, unsure if the recent VSCode update did actually change how those processes are ran on windows or if it was spitting out garbage: 

> Why the default stopped working: By default (without the console setting), VS Code launches dlv as an invisible background child process and communicates with it over a pipe/socket. Recent VS Code updates changed how these internal child processes are spawned on Windows — specifically how stdio handles and process groups are managed. This broke the timing of the DAP (Debug Adapter Protocol) handshake: VS Code starts dlv, then tries to connect to its DAP port, but the internal process spawning is now slightly slower, so the connection attempt happens before dlv is ready to accept it, and it times out.

For more context, this also affected 3 of my coworkers **DIRECTLY** after they updated to todays release
- Related Issue: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310218

The error message you're getting is coming from the Go extension, those strings aren't in vscode.

We also have not made any changes in this area afaik.

This issue is caused by an extension, please file it with the repository (or contact) the extension has linked in its overview in VS Code or the [marketplace](https://aka.ms/vscodemarketplace) for VS Code. See also our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting). If you don't know which extension is causing the problem, you can run `Help: Start extension bisect` from the command palette (F1) to help identify the problem extension.

Happy Coding!

Hello @TotalLife-Micheal 
My colleagues and I have the same problem, but it doesn't seem tied to the vscode extension version (last one was on 27th of January), nor VSCode version, By installing 1.112 I still have the timeout.
From the tests we made, it seems to be the new Windows 10 and 11 update that broke it. No idea why though! Uninstalling the updates are broken on my machine so can't even test it correctly.
If some colleagues of yours don't have the problem, list the last Windows updates you all did, it might come from this, it is the only coherent common denominator we noticed.
Thanks for the workaround by the way

title: user_weekly_rate_limited
description:
This at first I thought was fine, then as time went on and I would wait the amount of time needed in hopes of a "weekly reset" I did. Time cam around about a day 1/2 later and then I went to test, gave Copilot a task and let it run, I waited for it to work and a few minutes later I get the same issue but this time after it iterated twice it states I needed to wait 57 seconds. I did.

Now it says after I input "Try Again" it then stated for me to wait 4 minutes, this all consumes my rates.

At this point it has to be a bug or problem, I can't find anywhere to show a weekly limit and on top of that it keeps spitting these messages out.

I laugh and joke but in all seriousness this has to be some sort of issue. Right?
comments: This issue is a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/253124. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.
Microsoft sold you a car and then told you you’re only allowed to drive 50 miles a day.

title: ERROR: Protected fields have been made PUBLIC.

1. Clone the repository anew, run `npm install`, run `npm run compile-build`
