comments:
## Regression Details

- **Product**: Agents
- **Regression type**: New error
- **Last release without this error**: [1.116.0-insider (ae130017)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/60483725-f071-1372-ccc2-51712e734964?product=Agents&commit=ae130017f8afe532557dbb8539a6ef3bdaec6389)
- **First release with this error**: [1.117.0-insider (98b4d3e2)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/60483725-f071-1372-ccc2-51712e734964?product=Agents&commit=98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62)
- **Commit range**: [ae130017...98b4d3e2](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/ae130017f8afe532557dbb8539a6ef3bdaec6389...98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62)

### Deminified Stack

```
at (anonymous) (copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:1168:0) → createNewSession
at (anonymous) (sessionsManagementService.ts:261:27) → createNewSession
at (anonymous) (newChatViewPane.ts:250:0) → _createNewSession
at (anonymous) (newChatViewPane.ts:710:0) → _onWorkspaceSelected
at (anonymous) (newChatViewPane.ts:153:0)
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1355:12)
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1386:8)
at (anonymous) (sessionTypePicker.ts:126:0) → onSelect
at (anonymous) (actionList.ts:1161:0) → onListSelection
```

### Analysis

The error is thrown in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:createNewSession` (line 1168) when a user selects a GitHub repository workspace but the session type is not a Copilot Cloud session. The flow is: user selects a workspace via `sessionTypePicker.ts` → `newChatViewPane._onWorkspaceSelected` → `_createNewSession` → `sessionsManagementService.createNewSession` → `copilotChatSessionsProvider.createNewSession` which throws the validation error.

This is a new error in 1.117.0-insider (18 users, 36 hits) that did not exist in any prior insider build. The validation error is not being caught/handled and propagates as an unhandled error.

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24461922297/agentic_workflow) · ● 5.7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24461922297, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24461922297 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary When a user has a GitHub repository workspace selected and attempts to switch session types via the session type picker, the picker incorrectly determines which session types are supported. It passes `session.resource` (the session's own URI, e.g. `agentsessions-cloud://...`) to `getSessionTypes()` instead of the workspace's repository URI. Since the session resource URI never has the `GITHUB_REMOTE_FILE_SCHEME`, the method always returns `[CopilotCLISessionType]` as "supported" — the opposite of what's correct for GitHub repos. This allows the user to select CLI, which throws the unhandled error. 36 hits, 18 users affected. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`852f0ef7`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/852f0ef7ce04426b5750737c00f57ac62dda8d71) | | Author | `@sandy081` | | PR | #309563 | | Message | fix #309128 and add docs to the sessions provider | | Why | This PR refactored `getSessionTypes()` from accepting a `sessionId` (and looking up the session instance to check its type) to accepting a `workspaceUri` (and checking the URI scheme). However, the call site in `SessionTypePicker.refresh()` was changed to pass `session.resource` (the session URI) instead of the workspace repository URI. Since the session URI scheme is never `GITHUB_REMOTE_FILE_SCHEME`, the supported types are always computed incorrectly for GitHub workspaces. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User as User participant Picker as SessionTypePicker participant NewChat as NewChatWidget participant MgmtSvc as SessionsManagementService participant Provider as CopilotChatSessionsProvider User->>Picker: Selects session type (e.g. CLI) Note over Picker: ⚠️ Root cause:
refresh() called getSessionTypes(session.resource)
instead of getSessionTypes(workspaceUri).
CLI shown as enabled for GitHub repos. Picker->>NewChat: onDidSelectSessionType(CopilotCLISessionType.id) NewChat->>NewChat: _onWorkspaceSelected(githubWorkspace, 'copilot-cli') NewChat->>MgmtSvc: createNewSession(providerId, githubRepoUri, 'copilot-cli') MgmtSvc->>Provider: createNewSession(githubRepoUri, 'copilot-cli') Note over Provider: 💥 Error thrown:
"Only Copilot Cloud sessions can
be created for GitHub repositories" ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts` | **Root cause** — `refresh()` passes `session.resource` instead of workspace URI to `getSessionTypes()` | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Crash site** — `createNewSession()` throws when session type mismatches workspace | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/newChatViewPane.ts` | **Data flow** — `_onWorkspaceSelected()` passes unchecked session type to management service | | `src/vs/sessions/services/sessions/browser/sessionsManagementService.ts` | **Data flow** — `createNewSession()` forwards session type to provider | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product, version 1.117.0) 2. Open or connect to a **GitHub repository** workspace (a repo accessed via the `GITHUB_REMOTE_FILE_SCHEME`, e.g. `vscode.dev` on a GitHub repo) 3. In the new chat view, the session type picker will incorrectly show "CLI" as the enabled/selected type 4. Click the session type picker and select "CLI" (or it may already be defaulted to CLI) 5. The unhandled error "Only Copilot Cloud sessions can be created for GitHub repositories" is thrown ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts` → `refresh()` function (line ~41) **Approach**: Change `provider?.getSessionTypes(session.resource)` to pass the workspace's repository URI instead. The session's workspace is available via `session.workspace` (an `IObservable`). Read the workspace, get the first repository's URI, and pass that to `getSessionTypes()`. For example: ``` const workspace = session.workspace.get(); const repoUri = workspace?.repositories[0]?.uri ?? session.resource; this._supportedSessionTypes = provider?.getSessionTypes(repoUri) ?? []; ```` This ensures `getSessionTypes()` receives a URI with the correct scheme (`GITHUB_REMOTE_FILE_SCHEME` for GitHub repos), so it correctly returns `[CopilotCloudSessionType]` and disables CLI in the picker. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Author of the culprit commit (PR #309563) and primary contributor to the sessions management system. MEMBER of the VS Code team. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24466296252/agentic_workflow) · ● 10M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

title: Copilot Chat not working – "Language models unavailable" + "Codebase index not available"
description:
I’m unable to use GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code. The chat shows errors like:

* "Language model unavailable"
* "Codebase index not available"

Copilot appears to be authenticated correctly, but no language models are loaded 


## Environment

* VS Code version: 1.116
* OS: [Windows 11 education


## Copilot Status Log

[ExtensionState] ===============================================================
[ExtensionState] INCLUDE THIS INFORMATION IF YOU ARE OPENING AN ISSUE
[ExtensionState] ===============================================================
  Auth: anyGitHubSession=true, repoGitHubSession=true, copilotToken=true
  Username: elnuevojorge-alt
  Proxy: http.proxySupport=override, http.proxy=(not configured)
  Language models loaded: false (count: 0)
  Copilot chat provider registered: false (models: 0)
  Copilot embeddings model registered: true (models: [copilot.text-embedding-3-small])
  Tools loaded: true (count: 89)
[ExtensionState] ===============================================================

## Actual Behavior

* Chat does not respond
* "Language model unavailable" error appears
* "Codebase index not available" is shown
* No models are loaded according to logs

## Troubleshooting Performed

* Reloaded VS Code window
* Restarted extension host
* Signed out and back into GitHub
* Reset Copilot connection
* Reinstalled Copilot and Copilot Chat extensions
* Verified Copilot Pro subscription is active
* Tried different workspace/folder

## Additional Notes

* Authentication appears successful (`copilotToken=true`)
* Issue seems related to models not loading or chat provider not initializing
* Embeddings model is available, but no chat models are registered



Any guidance on additional debugging steps would be appreciated.

title: "run bash command" ui truncates the folder it will be running the command in, making me uneasy it might be in the wrong sub-directory
description: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: - Version: 1.117.0-insider (user setup) Commit: 98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62 Date: 2026-04-14T16:52:34Z Electron: 39.8.7 ElectronBuildId: 13797146 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19045 - VS Code Version: - OS Version: - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): auto - Logs: Steps to Reproduce: 1. Have the agent do something where it needs to run a shell command, in a monorepo if that is more clear 2. Notice the prompt where you are approving the command which changed in the last few days. It shows "Run bash command within start of pat" 3. but that path is truncated and there is no way I could see to get it to 'tell me' what folder it was going to run the command in. It used to be clear (but sometimes only because it would do a 'cd' first and you could see it doing the cd etc. I appropriate that the issues with it remembering what directory it was last in beteween commands etc was a problem -- but just this UI isn't comfortable to me. Approving a command when I'm not 100% sure it is in the right place when it is running it makes me uncomfortable.

title: [security] v1.116 lowers security by breaking GitHub Copilot AI per-workspace opt-in
description:
Users may have some workspaces used with GitHub Copilot, but also have other sensitive workspaces for which under no circumstances must any local information be sent to an LLM. **VSCode v.116 has broken the ability to opt-in to GitHub Copilot AI, and instead forced sensitive workspaces to _opt-out_ of GitHub Copilot AI.** This is not workable and against security best practices. **Now VS Code exposes a system to inadvertently leaking information in arbitrary directories.**

**Expected:** Before v1.116 I could disable GitHub Copilot globally, and only enable it on a per-workspace basis. I would do this by disabling the plugin globally, and then selecting "enable (workspace)" only in each workspace for which I wanted to use GitHub Copilot. This is referred to as "opt-in". This is security best practices: I should not be required to hunt down all my sensitive workspaces and opt them out. Instead, I should be allowed to only opt-in workspaces that I want to use with AI.

**Actual:** In v1.116 [GitHub Copilot is built-in](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_116#_github-copilot-is-now-builtin). There is now only a single `chat.disableAIFeatures` setting which allows the user to turn off AI either globally, or on a per-workspace basis. The plugin no longer has an option to enable it on a per-workspace basis, because the plugin is now built in. Although it appears I can _disable_ AI, for one particular workspace, this is an "opt-out" paradigm which is extremely insecure.

### Discussion

Imagine if a user has 10 sensitive directories (e.g. bank statements, love letters, the formulas for secret sauces). Some of them may not even have VS Code workspace yet, as VS Code automatically creates workspaces when a directory is opened. It is unacceptable, intrusive, and unreasonable that the user must go into each of these 10 sensitive directories, create VS Code workspaces for them, and then opt them out. What if the user forgets a directory? Plus the user has to open VS Code in that directory opt them out!

### Implications: Abandoning VS Code

The lack of an opt-in mechanism is simply too great of a security risk. I cannot expose all my system to the possibility of inadvertently sending information to LLMS from sensitive directories. If VS Code is removing its AI opt-in feature, I will be removing VS Code as my default editor.

**VS Code is not secure unless it brings back an opt-in feature for AI.** I will not expose my entire system to an information-leaking security risk.
comments: To add insult to injury, if you _disable_ AI for a particular workspace using `"chat.disableAIFeatures": true`, if the workspace is restricted (the secure mode) then every time you try to open that workspace, VS Code **nags you trust the folder** (i.e. to make the workspace _less_ secure) in order to disable AI! > ### Do you trust the authors of the files in this folder? > A feature you are trying to use may be a security risk if you do not trust the source of the files or folders you currently have open. > > If you don't trust the authors of these files, we do not recommend continuing as the files may be malicious. … Apparently it sees a feature related to AI and warns of a security risk, _even though the AI "feature" is the option to turn off AI_! **This is a secondary issue.** However it illustrates two things: * The default use of AI is a security issue, as the warning basically admits. * The disconnect that VS Code thinks you must _lower_ security of a workspace in order to _disable AI_ to make it more secure shows how forcing an opt-out paradigm is against security best practices and was ill-thought-out to begin with. I reiterate: **VS Code must have an AI opt-in mechanism**, not an opt-out mechanism.

@garretwilson the intent was not to break this scenario but preserve the ability to allow to globally disable the extension and then enable it on a per-workspace. However it turns out that the action appears disabled, which is clearly a bug: You can still configure `chat.disableAIFeatures` as a workspace setting and that will work, but its not the ideal solution. Thanks for the report, we will fix this asap.

Verification Steps:

- Open Extensions view in VS Code on Folder 1
- Type `@builtin chat` 
- Right click on Copilot Chat extension and run **Disable AI Features** action
- Make sure AI features are disabled
- Close and Open VS Code on Folder 2
- Make sure AI features are disabled
- Go to Extensions View and type `@builtin chat` 
- Right click on Copilot Chat extension and run **Enable AI Features (Workspace)** action
- Make sure AI features are enabled in this Folder 2
- Open VS Code on Folder 1 and make sure AI features are still disabled in Folder 1 and also on any other folders

title: Allow sub-agents to be invoked with a different model
description:
Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0
VS Code Version: Version: 1.115.0 (Universal)
OS Version: macOS 26.4.1
Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode launching a subagent
Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 4.6 (probably is affected for all MS CoPilot models)
Logs: na

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Ask to do something with a sub agent. Tell the orchestrator to use a specific model from Microstoft CoPilot (no third party involved here)
2. Verify that the sub agent did not run with that model, it has only run with the same model launching the sub agent



___________ AI writeup and suggestion ____________
AI WriteUp for better understanding:

## Summary

When the agent invokes a sub-agent via `runSubagent`, there is no way to specify which model that sub-agent should use. The sub-agent always inherits the model selected in the VS Code UI by the user. This prevents the agent from delegating tasks to a more suitable model (e.g. a cheaper/faster model for simple lookups, or a more capable model for complex reasoning steps).

## Current Behaviour

Sub-agents are spawned using the same model that was selected by the user for the top-level conversation. There is no `model` parameter on the `runSubagent` tool. Prompting the agent to "use Claude Opus for this sub-agent" has no effect — the model is fixed at the session level.

## Expected Behaviour

The `runSubagent` tool should accept an optional `model` parameter:

```json
{
  "prompt": "Analyse this codebase for security vulnerabilities...",
  "agentName": "Explore",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-5"
}
```

When omitted, the sub-agent inherits the session model (preserving current behaviour). When specified, the sub-agent runs with the requested model.

## Constraints / Scope

- This should **only be supported for official GitHub Copilot models** (i.e. models available in the Copilot model picker). Third-party or custom model endpoints should not be injectable through this path.
- The available model identifiers should be validated against the user's current Copilot subscription/model access, and the call should fail gracefully (with a clear error) if the requested model is not available.
- This is an opt-in capability — existing agent definitions that don't specify a model are unaffected.

## Use Cases

- Use a fast/cheap model (e.g. GPT-4o mini) for high-volume read-only exploration sub-agents.
- Use a high-capability model (e.g. Claude Opus) for complex reasoning or security analysis sub-agents, independent of what the user has selected for the main conversation.
- Agent definitions (`.agent.md`) could declare a preferred model for their specific task domain.

## Impact

Without this capability, the agent cannot optimise cost/quality trade-offs across sub-tasks, and power users cannot build multi-model agentic workflows within VS Code Copilot.

title: `run_in_terminal` tool response should include the exit code
description:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0
- VS Code Version: Version: 1.115.0 (Universal)
- OS Version: macOS 26.4.1
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): All models
- Logs: na

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Let CoPilot Chat Execute a Terminal command that does not have stderr or stdout - for example just a "touch test.file"
2. Review the output window. There will be shown in the chat window, that no output occured. Even though those basic commands and anything else should always output an exit code which the AI interprets also.

####
___________ AI writeup and suggestion ____________
AI WriteUp for better understanding:

## Summary

When the agent uses the `run_in_terminal` tool, the tool response only contains stdout/stderr. The exit code of the executed command is not included. This means the agent cannot detect silent failures within the same turn without resorting to the workaround of appending `; echo $?` to every command.

## Current Behaviour

A command like `chmod 444 /tmp/test.txt` that succeeds silently returns an empty tool response. A failing command that produces no stderr (exit code ≠ 0, no output) is indistinguishable from a successful one within the same reasoning turn.

The exit code *is* surfaced in the next turn's injected context block (`Exit Code: <n>`), but only for the *last* command and only after the current turn has already completed — too late for the agent to react within the same step.

## Expected Behaviour

The `run_in_terminal` tool response should include the exit code alongside stdout/stderr, for example:

```json
{
  "stdout": "",
  "stderr": "",
  "exitCode": 0
}
```

This allows the agent to branch on failure immediately, retry the command, or surface a clear error to the user — all within the same turn.

## Workaround

Appending `; echo "Exit: $?"` to every command provides the exit code in stdout, but is fragile:
- It pollutes stdout and can be confused with actual command output.
- It requires every command invocation to be authored defensively.
- It doesn't work cleanly for multi-command pipelines.

## Impact

Without inline exit codes, the agent may silently proceed after a failed command, leading to confusing downstream errors that are difficult to trace back to the root cause. Reliable exit code access is a basic requirement for robust agent command execution.

title: Lost of Chat Sessions in Code UI - JSONL persist in Folders - Chat: Import Chat only works JSON (v1.115 -> v1.116 transition (?))
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: `v1.115` and as it upgraded to `v1.116`
- OS Version:  `Windows 11 Pro Insider Preview, Build 26100 ge release 240331-1435`

Steps to Reproduce:

1. I lost my chat sessions in the UI and the contextual flow that comes from it - I have the `jsonl` and all the `chatSessions` (per hash) under `%APPDATA%\Roaming\Code\User\workspaceStorage\{{HASH}}\chatSessions`
2. A loss of contextual reuse and flow of work. The chats were the ground truth, and research material.
3. However `Chat: Import Chat` (`F1`, `Ctrl+P`) only imports `.json` and not `.jsonl` from file type picker.

Note I built a ps1 script to check for importable `json`, and only 2 were found under `%APPDATA%\Roaming\Code\User\workspaceStorage` - and it skipped any folders without a `chatSession`.

So the valuable data is in the wrong format: `JSONL` not `JSON` for chat import. 
Plus, this should be the first principal feature, not obscure and difficult to use.

Chats Portability and the inherent contextual intelligence is a valuable thing to me as I design processed and how AI works out what to do, as I then capture the process, and other proprietary workflows. 

I do have a `report-ChatProcess-process` prompt that captures the chat context from the UI and that works.

Just lost the last few days/last weeks and the contextual cues UI dervived chats have.

The outcomes deliverables are good. 

**<ins>My project is knowledge-based, markdown project with no precursor to exemplify or  be based on, so the development chat flow (per chat sessions) is the valuable ground truth. All gone.</ins>**

What am I missing or is Import Chat under specified?
comments: A custom script's results to investigate

Okay, bit of a false positive, - the chats came back (but not all of them, only in a certain timeframe) ... but it leads me in a loop for all morning ... but it does show how brittle the chats infra / UI feature set is.

So room to improve. And by product, I found specStory and copilot-chat-to-markdown to help preserve at least the textual context and not the AI flow.

and the Import / Export Chats (flow, process) need to be first class feature / enhanced like archive and not hidden as the point is that the chats themselves are ground truth occasionally. Particularly in the emphasis of the new companion Agents app. 

Plus a bulk import/export (or sync, as a configuration option) between Code and Code Insiders would allow for greater resilience in the Chat sessions if both are installed by side by side.

title: Extension host proxy support does not cover http2.connect() paths; patched tls.connect still allows direct target DNS/connect instead of proxy tunnel
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->

# VS Code Extension Host Proxy Patching Does Not Cover Raw HTTP/2 (`http2.connect()`) Traffic

## Summary

VS Code's extension host proxy patching appears not to transparently cover Node.js `http2.connect()` traffic. On proxy-required networks, HTTP/1.1, `fetch`, and `undici` paths work through the configured proxy, but raw HTTP/2 clients can still fall back to direct DNS and direct target socket connection behavior. This causes failures such as `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND <host>` when external name resolution is only possible through the proxy.

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** N/A

## System Information

- VS Code Version: 1.117.0-insider (user setup), Commit `98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62`
- OS Version:
  - Host: Windows 11 Enterprise 10.0.26100
  - Remote: WSL2 Ubuntu 25.04 (`Linux 6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2`)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure VS Code / extension host to use an HTTP proxy on a network where direct DNS resolution for external hosts fails and only the proxy can reach external destinations.
2. Run an extension in the Node extension host that uses Node's `http2.connect()` directly (for example, a gRPC or HTTP/2 client that does not use `fetch`, `undici`, `http.request`, or `https.request`).
3. Attempt to connect to an external HTTPS endpoint over HTTP/2.
4. Observe that the connection path does not resolve and tunnel through the configured proxy. Instead, it falls back to direct target DNS/socket behavior and fails with errors such as `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND <host>` on proxy-mandatory networks.

## Expected behavior

All outbound extension host traffic should honor the configured proxy consistently, including HTTP/2 clients using `http2.connect()`. If proxy support is enabled, the extension host should establish a proxy tunnel before TLS/HTTP2 session setup, rather than attempting direct DNS/connect to the target host.

## Actual behavior

`http.request`, `https.request`, `fetch`, and `undici`-based traffic are proxy-aware, but `http2.connect()`-based traffic is not transparently proxied. In practice, `tls.connect` is patched for certificate handling, but if it is invoked without a preconnected socket, the path still allows direct target DNS/socket connection behavior. On networks where external DNS only works through the proxy, this results in persistent `ENOTFOUND` / direct-connect failures.

## Why I believe this belongs in VS Code

VS Code explicitly patches extension host networking through `src/vs/workbench/api/node/proxyResolver.ts`, including `http`, `https`, `net`, `tls`, and `undici` via `@vscode/proxy-agent`.

## Relevant code

- <https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62/src/vs/workbench/api/node/proxyResolver.ts#L366-L370>

- <https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62/src/vs/workbench/api/node/proxyResolver.ts#L388-L417>

This suggests the extension host owns the proxy-patching model, but there is a gap for raw HTTP/2 connection establishment.

## Likely root cause

There appears to be no transparent proxy-aware handling for `http2.connect()` itself. If `http2.connect()` eventually reaches patched `tls.connect()` without a pre-established proxy tunnel socket, the "no socket" branch still allows creation of a new direct socket connection to the target host instead of first resolving and connecting via proxy.

## Impact

Any extension relying on raw Node HTTP/2 / gRPC transports may fail in enterprise environments where:

- proxy usage is mandatory
- direct outbound 443 is blocked
- external DNS resolution is unavailable except through the proxy

## Possible fixes

1. Make the `tls.connect` patch proxy-aware even when called without a preconnected socket, so it can resolve the proxy and establish a CONNECT tunnel first.
2. Alternatively, provide/document a supported proxy-aware path for extension host `http2.connect()` usage.
3. Consider adding test coverage for extension-host HTTP/2 connectivity behind a mandatory proxy.

## Notes

- Confirmed in current VS Code Insiders WSL2 remote session (`.vscode-server-insiders`, commit `98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62`) with `--use-host-proxy` on extension host.
- Current WSL2 extension host environment includes `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` / `NO_PROXY`, but HTTP/2 paths can still fail via direct target DNS/connect behavior.
- Observed in a VS Code-derived environment and consistent with behavior in the shared extension-host proxy patching model.

title: VSCode asked me to upgrade while I'm already Pro+ (might be cross-device issue)
description: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0 - VS Code Version: 1.115.0 (Universal) - OS Version: macOS 15.7.5 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): all - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude 4.6 Sonnet ``` Version: 1.115.0 (Universal) Commit: 41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b Date: 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z Electron: 39.8.5 ElectronBuildId: 13703022 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Darwin arm64 24.6.0 ``` Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open a VSCode window, `Connect to` a server, and open remote project. 2. Open another window, and open a local workspace. Chat function on remote `server` works fine, while `local` project showed `Claude Opus 4.6` and `Claude Sonnet 4.6` unavailable. I can understand logging on multiple devices shall be limited. But I'm actually using one notebook and trying to work on different projects.
comments: Fixed after reboot.

Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.116.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

title: Getting "Retried with Autopilot" messages with autopilot disabled
description: On a flaky internet connection, I got a "Retried with Autopilot" message in a chat session with auto approvals enabled, but autopilot disabled. I think the plugin retried on a network timeout, witch might be a normal and expected behavior, but at a minimum **the action label is confusing** (and/or wrong). - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0 - VS Code Version: - Version: 1.115.0 - Commit: 41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b - Date: 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z - Electron: 39.8.5 - ElectronBuildId: 13703022 - Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 - Node.js: 22.22.1 - V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 - OS: Linux x64 6.17.0-20-generic - OS Version: Linux Mint 22.3 - Xfce 64-bit - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Auto - Logs: See attached [GitHub Copilot Chat.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26743407/GitHub.Copilot.Chat.log) Steps to Reproduce: 1. Send a prompt on an intermittent internet connection. Let the plugin retry on timeouts. The chat messages should not mention Autopilot.
comments: Same issue when the auth token expires

title: Copilot Chat shows "Language model unavailable" for all models
description:
Type: Bug I am using GitHub Copilot Pro, and my subscription is active. However, in VS Code: - All models (GPT-5.4, Claude, Auto) return "Language model unavailable" - Both Agent mode and Ask mode do not work - I have already tried: - Reload Window - Sign out / Sign in GitHub - Reinstall Copilot and Copilot Chat extensions This issue started recently. Previously, models worked normally. Please help check if my account has lost access to Copilot Chat models or if this is a backend issue. Extension version: 0.43.0 VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19045 Modes:

Viewing output of GitHub Copilot Chat, I am seeing this error: You can check my account as well; I have an active Pro License. **This is a paid feature, disabling it for a large portion of the user base needs to be escalated.**

title: Global rate limit timer behaves incorrectly in VS Code
description:
Type: Bug Bug: global rate limit timer behaves incorrectly in VS Code What happens: The Claude Hikaru 4.5 in VS Code shows “You’ve hit your global rate limit,” but after pressing Try Again the reset time is inconsistent and can increase instead of steadily counting down. Changing the model shows the same message and issues. Examples seen in chronological order: * 2 seconds * 1 minute * 43 seconds Additional detail: I also waited about 1 real minute, but the timer in VS Code only decreased by around 10 seconds instead of counting down normally. Expected: A stable countdown that matches real elapsed time, or a clearer explanation that the wait is based on a rolling/account-wide limit rather than a true timer. Please investigate whether this is: * expected account-level throttling * multiple-session quota behavior Environment: VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

If you already have a folder open and you want to create a new subfolder, do it here: If instead you want to create a folder anywhere on your workstation, either use other tools (e.g. Windows Explorer) or use Open Folder... from VS Code's File menu, then use the "New folder" facility in the workstation's native dialog.
@Mehbub-Ara If you have WSL or (I believe) Git Bash installed, you could also use the `mkdir` command. Not sure how this works without a Unix-like system or subsystem installed...

title: Cli version mismatch
description: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.116.0 - OS Version: Windows NT x64 10.26220 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Update the VSCode to the latest version 2. Download the VSCode CLI from https://code.visualstudio.com/download 3. The latest VSCode version is 1.116.0 and the CLI version is 1.115.0 4. Since we use `./code command-shell --cli-data-dir /home/azureuser/vscode-agent/cli --on-port=8884 --on-host=127.0.0.1` command to create VSCode server in the remote. When the version is mismatch, this error will show, and the only way to workaround is downgrade the version of VSC. Please consider synchronize the publishing process of VSCode and the VSCode CLI so that their versions align more closely, as the VSCode version has been updated frequently recently.
comments: `command-shell` does not do compatibility assurances and is designed for internal use only. If you use it it's up to you to make sure versions match. Consider `code serve-web` or `code tunnel` instead.

title: opilot Chat "Language model unavailable" error in VS Code despite active Copilot Pro
description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: Latest
- VS Code Version: Latest
- OS Version: Windows 11
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Chat (Ask mode)
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Default (auto-selected, not manually chosen)
- Logs:
Error message displayed in Copilot Chat:
"Language model unavailable"

Additional context:
- Copilot Pro subscription is active
- Issue persists after sign out / sign in
- Extensions are up to date
- VS Code window reloaded
- Tested with and without VPN

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open Visual Studio Code
2. Open Copilot Chat panel
3. Enter any prompt (e.g., "Explain this code")
4. Submit the request
5. Observe the error: "Language model unavailable"
comments: me too all model agent is die eg claude gpt all model
It has been down for two days straight—from yesterday through today—and the computing power issue still hasn't been fixed.

This issue is a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/253137. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

title: "Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free."
description: I have an active paid GitHub Copilot Pro subscription on my account, and the billing page confirms: “GitHub Copilot Pro is active for your account”. However, in VS Code Copilot Chat fails to load any models and returns HTTP 403 with this message: “Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free.” This appears to be a backend entitlement/provisioning mismatch, because: * authentication works * Copilot token is successfully issued * model fetch fails with 403 * the error incorrectly treats my paid account as a paused trial Please check and refresh/reset the Copilot entitlement state for my account. Relevant log excerpt: * Logged in successfully * Got Copilot token * Failed to fetch models: 403 * “Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused...” What can I do?
comments:
2026-04-15 09:18:26.452 [warning] Invalid remote agent response: Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free.
 (SyntaxError: Unexpected token 'C', "Copilot Pr"... is not valid JSON)
2026-04-15 09:18:29.012 [error] Error: Failed to fetch models (915c4b24-dddb-4961-84fc-c9f82c9a5c2c): Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free.

Please look at the following meta issue: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/252230. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

Sorry, but this meta ticket does not seem relevant to my issue.

My problem does not appear to be related to premium request counters or the consumptive billing model. It seems that my previous free trial may have been blocked, while my account is nevertheless shown as “Copilot Pro”.

My billing page shows that GitHub Copilot Pro is already active. However:

* github.com/copilot returns “Access denied”
* VS Code cannot load **ANY** models
* VS Code logs return 403

This suggests an account entitlement/provisioning mismatch: the billing page shows an active paid Copilot Pro subscription (as in screenshot), but the Copilot service appears to still treat my account as a paused trial.

Could you please escalate this as an account entitlement/provisioning issue and refresh or reset the Copilot entitlement on my account?

If this is not the correct support channel, could you please tell me exactly where I should open a ticket for a manual check of this mismatch and, if confirmed, to have the account unblocked?
ok i solved

> ok i solved

Can you share what you did to resolve this issue?  I'm encountering the same problem and could use some help.
Same issue

**If you believe this was closed in error:**

Thank you for helping us maintain a welcoming and productive community.

Dear Support Team,

I am writing to report a persistent issue with GitHub Copilot that is
currently affecting my workflow.

Despite having an active paid subscription, I am unable to access Copilot
services. I consistently receive an “Access Denied” error, and within
GitHub Codespaces, Copilot Chat fails with a message indicating that the
language model is not valid.

I initially raised this issue on GitHub, but it was closed without
resolution, flagged as not meeting community guidelines. However, this is a
legitimate technical issue that continues to persist.

For clarity, here are the details:

Issue: Copilot access denied / Copilot Chat not working
Error message: “Language model is not valid” (in Codespaces)
Environment: GitHub Codespaces + VS Code
Subscription status: Active paid account
Impact: Unable to use Copilot Chat or related AI features

Steps to reproduce:

1. Open a Codespace
2. Attempt to use Copilot Chat
3. Error appears indicating invalid language model

Expected behavior:
Copilot Chat should function normally with access to the supported language
models.

Actual behavior:
Access is denied, and Copilot Chat does not function.

I would appreciate your assistance in:

Verifying my account and Copilot entitlements
Identifying whether this is a configuration, permissions, or backend issue
Providing steps to resolve the “invalid language model” error

Please let me know if you need logs, screenshots, or account identifiers to
assist with troubleshooting.

Thank you for your time and support.

Kind regards,
Kunda Oliver

title: Support sandbox modes for chat agents
description:
## Summary

Create explicit sandbox modes for chat agents.

## Discussion points

- Sandboxing should have modes similar to Claude Code, including a YOLO-style mode.
- YOLO mode should effectively be represented by auto-approval enabled together with sandboxing enabled.
- There should also be a regular confirmation mode, similar to the current non-sandboxed behavior, where commands still require confirmation as appropriate.

## Expected outcome

Define and expose sandbox modes that make the relationship between sandboxing and command confirmation behavior clear to users.

## Regression Details

- **Product**: Agents
- **Regression type**: `new`
- **Last release without this error**: [1.116.0-insider (fc6d18c3)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/422a1210-9a5a-e865-fe80-14fbb05c3836?product=Agents&commit=fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)
- **First release with this error**: [1.116.0-insider (e4f6e114)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/422a1210-9a5a-e865-fe80-14fbb05c3836?product=Agents&commit=e4f6e1148528f4e6fba4bf58e4a5f28539135156)
- **Commit range**: [fc6d18c3...e4f6e114](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a...e4f6e1148528f4e6fba4bf58e4a5f28539135156)

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24426425462/agentic_workflow) · ● 4.6M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24426425462, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24426425462 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `potential listener LEAK detected, popular` is triggered when the `onDidColorThemeChange` event emitter on the theme service accumulates too many listeners (exceeding the leak detection threshold). Each `XtermTerminal` instance created via `createDetachedTerminal` registers a listener on this global service. In the Agents product, each terminal tool invocation creates a `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` (or `DetachedTerminalCommandMirror`), each instantiating a full `XtermTerminal`. When an agent session runs many terminal commands, the cumulative listener count exceeds the threshold. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit was identified. This is a design-level issue: the `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` constructor eagerly creates a full `XtermTerminal` (with all its global service listeners) for each terminal command output snapshot. The error first appeared in telemetry on April 10, 2026, likely due to growing Agents product usage pushing the listener count over the threshold. The error persists across all commits from `3d70aab9` (1.115.0) through `1dfb3b9d` (1.116.0). Related issue: #308254 (same root cause, different stack frame — listener leak from detached terminal links contribution). ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Chat as ChatTerminalToolProgressPart participant Section as ChatTerminalToolOutputSection participant Snapshot as DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror participant Service as TerminalService participant Xterm as XtermTerminal Chat->>Section: _renderSnapshotOutput(snapshot) Section->>Snapshot: new DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror() Note over Snapshot: Constructor eagerly creates
a full XtermTerminal Snapshot->>Service: createDetachedTerminal() Service->>Xterm: new XtermTerminal() Note over Xterm: Registers listeners on:
• onDidColorThemeChange
• onDidChangeConfiguration
• onDidChangeLogLevel Note over Xterm: ⚠️ Root cause: Each instance
adds listeners to global singletons Xterm-->>Service: xterm instance Note over Chat: After N tool invocations... Note over Xterm: 💥 ListenerLeakError thrown:
"potential listener LEAK
detected, popular" ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1062` | Crash site — `Stacktrace.create()` in leak detection | | `src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1262` | Emitter subscription check triggers leak detection | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/xterm/xtermTerminal.ts:289` | Root cause — registers `onDidColorThemeChange` listener per instance | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/chatTerminalCommandMirror.ts:611` | Data producer — `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` constructor eagerly creates XtermTerminal | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/chatTerminalCommandMirror.ts:413` | Data producer — `DetachedTerminalCommandMirror._getOrCreateTerminal()` also creates detached terminals | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/terminalService.ts:1098` | `createDetachedTerminal` entry point | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts:1396` | Creates `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` per tool result | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product / sessions window) 2. Start an agent session that runs many terminal commands (e.g., a coding agent running build/test/lint commands) 3. As the agent runs 50+ terminal commands, each creates a `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` to display the output 4. Each mirror creates a full `XtermTerminal` that registers listeners on global services 5. When the listener count on `onDidColorThemeChange` exceeds the threshold, the leak error is thrown 6. The error is more likely to trigger with longer agent sessions that produce more terminal outputs ### Fix Proposal Two complementary approaches: **1. Lazy terminal creation in `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror`** (primary fix) **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/chatTerminalCommandMirror.ts` → `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` constructor **Approach**: Move the `createDetachedTerminal` call out of the constructor and into the `attach()` or `render()` method. The terminal should only be created when the snapshot is actually being displayed, not when the mirror object is constructed. This would also allow the terminal to be disposed when scrolled out of view in virtualized lists. **2. Lightweight rendering for snapshot output** (longer-term) **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/chatTerminalCommandMirror.ts` → `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` **Approach**: For read-only snapshot terminals that display static output, consider using a lightweight rendering approach (e.g., a canvas or pre-rendered HTML) instead of a full `XtermTerminal` instance with all its service listeners. Snapshots don't need live theme change handling, configuration watching, or log level monitoring. **3. Increase listener threshold for detached terminals** (quick mitigation) **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/xterm/xtermTerminal.ts` constructor **Approach**: When creating XtermTerminal for detached/snapshot use, consider not registering on global service events, or unsubscribing when not attached to the DOM. ### Recommended Owner `@meganrogge` — area owner for terminal chat integration, authored the `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror`/`DetachedTerminalCommandMirror` classes and the `createDetachedTerminal` infrastructure (PRs #285985, #283906, #280442) > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24430402125/agentic_workflow) · ● 7.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24426425462, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24426425462 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `ListError [AgentSessionsView] Invalid index 0` is thrown when `AgentSessionsControl.reveal()` calls `getRelativeTop()` on the sessions list tree while the tree model and list widget are out of sync. The tree model reports a node exists at index 0 but the list widget has 0 rendered items. This is a race condition triggered during orientation transitions (switching to side-by-side view) in the Chat View Pane. ### Culprit Commit No narrowed commit range was provided by the regression scan. Based on telemetry bucket history, the error first appeared at [`3d70aab9`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/3d70aab9a4ed407832e43584ac7a661be08e2d74) (1.115.0-insider, Apr 3) with 1 hit, then increased across 1.116.0-insider builds (up to 12 hits, 8 users). The `reveal()` method with the `getRelativeTop` pattern was originally introduced in PR #290233 (merged Jan 25), but the error only started appearing later — likely exposed by subsequent changes to session list layout, grouping, or visibility logic. > The root cause is a missing guard in the `reveal()` method itself, rather than a specific commit introducing a regression. ### Code Flow ````mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Layout as ChatViewPane.layoutSessionsControl participant Control as AgentSessionsControl participant AsyncTree as AsyncDataTree participant AbstractTree as AbstractTree participant ListView as ListWidget Layout->>Control: update() [via Throttler] Control->>AsyncTree: updateChildren() Note over AsyncTree: Tree model updated,
nodes map populated AsyncTree-->>Control: Promise resolves Layout->>Control: reveal(sessionResource) Control->>Control: hasNode(session) → true ✅ Control->>AsyncTree: getRelativeTop(session) AsyncTree->>AbstractTree: getRelativeTop(dataNode) AbstractTree->>AbstractTree: model.getListIndex() → 0 AbstractTree->>ListView: getRelativeTop(index=0) Note over ListView: 💥 this.length === 0
0 >= 0 → throws ListError ```` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsControl.ts` | **Root cause** — `reveal()` method lacks guard before `getRelativeTop()` call (line 836) | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts` | **Trigger** — orientation switch to side-by-side calls `reveal()` after async update (line 987–992) | | `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listWidget.ts` | **Crash site** — `getRelativeTop()` throws `ListError` when index ≥ length (line 2003–2004) | | `src/vs/base/browser/ui/tree/abstractTree.ts` | **Data flow** — `getListIndex()` returns valid index from tree model, passes to list widget (line 3103–3110) | | `src/vs/base/browser/ui/tree/asyncDataTree.ts` | **Data flow** — `hasNode()` checks `nodes` Map (different structure than list widget length) | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) with the Chat View Panel visible 2. Have at least one agent session in the sessions list 3. Resize the Chat View pane width rapidly across the `SESSIONS_SIDEBAR_VIEW_MIN_WIDTH` threshold — this forces repeated orientation switches between stacked ↔ side-by-side 4. The race window exists between `update()` completing (tree model updated) and the list widget fully rendering its items 5. Alternatively, this can trigger during initial layout when the chat view pane is first opened in a width that selects side-by-side orientation > This is a race condition and is non-deterministic. Rapid resizing or slow data source responses increase the likelihood. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsControl.ts` → `reveal()` **Approach**: Wrap the `getRelativeTop()` call in a try-catch to gracefully handle the case where the tree model and list widget are out of sync. If `getRelativeTop()` throws, fall through to `reveal(session, 0.5)` unconditionally (which is the desired behavior when the element isn't visible anyway): ```typescript reveal(sessionResource: URI): boolean { // ... existing checks ... try { if (this.sessionsList.getRelativeTop(session) === null) { this.sessionsList.reveal(session, 0.5); } } catch { // Tree model and list widget can be temporarily out of sync // during orientation transitions — fall through to focus/select } this.sessionsList.setFocus([session]); this.sessionsList.setSelection([session]); return true; } ``` Alternatively, a more targeted fix would check the list widget's length before calling `getRelativeTop`, but the try-catch is more resilient against other edge cases in the tree widget lifecycle. ### Recommended Owner `@bpasero` — primary author of the chat sessions integration code in `chatViewPane.ts` and the agent sessions layout logic (PRs #289325, #305555, #284396). The `reveal()` call site in the orientation switch code falls within his ownership area. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24430402660/agentic_workflow) · ● 14.5M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

title: msal authentication suddenly stopped working from VS code
description:
Type: Bug Was using MCP servers in my VS code and they suddenly stopped working. Further troubleshooting isolated this to authentication issue. [Cannot Sign in to Azure using new 'Azure Resources' from Visual Studio Code. · Issue #1039 · micros…](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-azureresourcegroups/issues/1039) which in turn pointed me to [January 2025 (version 1.97)](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_97#_microsoft-account-now-uses-msal-with-wam-support-on-windows) - changing the Microsoft account setting to msal-no-broker fixed the issue with one extension (powerbi-remote) but other internal extensions are not working. I am raising this issue to understand why msal authtentication setting is not working from VS code and if there is a way to fix this. I already tried manually cleaning up vscode and reinstalling the same a couple of times. I would either get 404 or 401 errors - would get prompted for my enterprise credentials 3 or 4 times and then it just fails. Account information only shows my Github accoun and not my corp account. title: Language model unavailable in GitHub Copilot Chat
description:
Type: Bug Even with an active GitHub Copilot subscription, correct login to my GitHub account, and the latest VS Code version (1.115.0), Copilot Chat consistently returns the message “Language model unavailable”. - Extension installed: only GitHub Copilot Chat. - Login confirmed via terminal (gh auth login) and inside VS Code. - Subscription confirmed at github.com/settings/copilot. - Tested without VPN, firewall, or proxy. - /troubleshoot command only returns Language model unavailable. Steps to reproduce: - Install VS Code 1.115.0 (Windows 11). - Install only the GitHub Copilot Chat extension. - Log in with a GitHub account that has an active Copilot subscription. - Open any code file and attempt to use Copilot Chat or inline suggestions. Expected result: Inline suggestions and Copilot Chat responses should work normally. Actual result: Persistent error message: “Language model unavailable”. title: FR: Update integrated browser
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
The integrated browser seems to be Chromium 142.0.7444.265, which was released about 6 months ago. 

It would be fantastic if it could be updated to at least v147, the current stable version. 

Even better would be if we could somehow install beta, dev or canary. Obviously it is fine if that's not practical/possible (probably needs to build vscode from source...)

This would allow us to test our apps with the latest browser functionality - currently I have to open a separate browser for that.
comments:
I see now that it depends on what version of electron VS Code uses. Currently 1.117.0-insider uses electron 39.8.7. The latest stable electron is v41.2, which uses chromium 146.0.7680.179

useful links:
* https://github.com/ewanharris/vscode-versions
* https://releases.electronjs.org/release?channel=stable

Hi @nickchomey, you are correct that it depends on the version of Electron. While we stay up-to-date with Electron's security patches, the major version can lag behind for various reasons.

I'll close this issue as there's nothing additional that can be done for the integrated browser, but you can follow https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/292445 to track the progress of upgrading VS Code to Electron v41.
Thanks! I looked for such an issue but couldnt find it. i'll follow there

same error

But it still didn't work, when I canceled the trial and tried to go back to the free plan...

title: Allow selection of code changed by Copilot
description: When using Copilot in Agent mode, I often want to keep the change that was made, but there is some part of the replaced code that I want to use for a manual improvement to Copilot's edit, that I want to ask Copilot about, or that I want to do something else with. E.g.: If is frustrating that I cannot copy the red text. Because I can't, I often find myself copying the green instead, clicking Undo, pasting, and then copying the original. Please allow selection and copying of the replaced text.
comments: I'd like it too but this isn't really practical for us to do, since this isn't a "real" diff editor. The easiest way is to either go through git/scm or to use these buttons to open the changes in a diff editor

title: Agents: Review pass on configurations that should be hidden or modified
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
We've already done an initial pass on commands: we should consider another pass on config (commands, settings, any UI elements or entry points) that doesn't make sense (at all, or at least not as-is) in Agents.

Example: Various debug commands.
comments: ## Commands ### 1. Debug Commands in Command Palette While the Run/Debug top-level menu is hidden, all debug commands (Start Debugging, Step Over, Toggle Breakpoint, Add Configuration, Open Debug Console, etc.) still appear in the Command Palette. The breakpoint editor contributions add gutter functionality that might be useful when viewing code, but stepping/starting debug sessions doesn't seem as relevant. **Proposed action**: Hide debug commands from the Command Palette. ### 2. Problems commands These currently no-op, and given the output-oriented nature of the app, they don't feel as relevant. **Proposed action**: Hide problems commands from the Command Palette. ## UI ### 1. Top-level menus I'm testing on Windows, so considering https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309894, I don't have the top-level menus. I'll explore through the lens of assuming the top-level menus will be available in Windows/Linux too at some point (so consider which of the current VS Code top-level menu items do or don't make sense in Agents). Also worth noting that some of these may have already been removed (I can't verify since the top-level menus aren't available at all on Windows). **Proposed action:** Remove the following items from each menu area: - `File` - `Close Folder` (folders aren't the sole focus / particularly "opened" in Agents) - `Edit` - `Find in Files` and `Replace in Files` (again since folders aren't the sole focus - which files would it be searching?) - `Selection` - Should we generally remove these, as the navigation concepts are different in Agents vs VS Code? - `View` - `Problems` - `Output` - `Debug Console` - `Chat` (should we remove this, since chat is basically always visible in Agents?) - `Go` - Should we generally remove these, as the navigation concepts are different in Agents vs VS Code? - `Run` - Already removed entirely (already available through separate button on top right) - `Terminal` - Remove entirely (already available through separate button on top right) - `Help` - Should we remove the entire first section (`Welcome` through `Ask vscode`), given they're welcome experiences specific to VS Code? ## Settings ### 1. Near term Given the continued evolution of features and settings in Agents, I think we could take a couple passes. **Proposed action:** - Rename `vscode` to `Agents` - ### 2. Mid to long term **Proposed action:** - Curate an updated list of `Commonly Used` settings - Since this is one of the more common user-facing entry points - Perform an even deeper analysis of settings to modify or remove ## Notifications **Proposed action:** Update user-facing strings that hardcode `VS Code`. - Account / auth entries, including about invalid GH token and then being prompted to install using VS Code account menu - Entries below ### MCP - [`mcpListWidget.ts#L170`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/mcpListWidget.ts#L170) — `"This server is managed by VS Code and forwarded to all compatible agent sessions."` - [`mcpListWidget.ts#L813`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/mcpListWidget.ts#L813) — `"MCP servers contributed by installed VS Code extensions."` - [`mcpListWidget.ts#L840`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/mcpListWidget.ts#L840) — `"MCP servers built into VS Code. These are available automatically."` ### Prompt Validation These are reachable but only when a user opens a prompt file in the editor and that file has an unsupported YAML frontmatter attribute. It's a fairly specific scenario — editing customization files with invalid attributes. They're low-frequency compared to the MCP ones. - [`promptValidator.ts#L264`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptValidator.ts#L264) — `"Attribute '{0}' is ignored when running locally in VS Code."` - [`promptValidator.ts#L266`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptValidator.ts#L266) — `"Attribute '{0}' is not supported in VS Code agent files."` - [`promptValidator.ts#L272`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptValidator.ts#L272) — `"Attribute '{0}' is not supported in rules files by VS Code agents."` - [`promptValidator.ts#L278`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptValidator.ts#L278) — `"Attribute '{0}' is not supported by VS Code agents."` ### Notifications / Misc - [`terminalView.ts#L211`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/terminalView.ts#L211) — `"Be sure to restart VS Code if this is a newly installed font."`

title: Language model unavailable in GitHub Copilot Chat (models not loading)
description:
Type: Bug 1. Open Visual Studio Code 2. Install GitHub Copilot Chat extension 3. Sign in to GitHub account 4. Open Copilot Chat panel 5. Try to start a new chat session Actual Result: The chat shows "Language model unavailable" and does not respond. Expected Result: Copilot Chat should load normally and respond to prompts. - GitHub account is successfully logged in - Copilot subscription is active (premium requests visible) - VS Code version: 1.115.0 - Copilot Chat version: 0.43.0 Error logs show: "Failed to fetch models" "Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused" "Language models loaded: false" The issue seems related to Copilot not loading language models despite having an active subscription. The logs indicate a 403 error and mention that Copilot Pro trials are paused. This prevents the chat feature from working. title: Add policy metadata for agent sandbox setting
description:
Add product policy metadata for the `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` setting.

The change registers the setting under the `ChatAgentSandboxEnabled` policy name and uses the existing agent sandbox enabled setting description for policy localization.

Related PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/309981
Cherry-pick commit: `6342cc3434b`
Original commit: `8e2f29b41aa`
comments: Hi @dileepyavan. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.
Closing as this appears to have made it into the release branch

When trying to open it, I get the following message:

Chat took too long to get ready. Please ensure you are signed in to GitHub and that the extension GitHub.copilot-chat is installed and enabled. Click restart to try again if this issue persists.

✅ Current status
I am properly signed in to GitHub
Copilot appears as active
The github.copilot-chat extension is installed
The extension is enabled
Privacy settings and permissions are allowed
❌ Issue

The chat does not load and stays stuck in a loading state without starting.

🔍 Expected behavior

Copilot Chat should start normally inside Visual Studio Code and allow me to use the chat feature.

📌 Additional information
There are no visible errors in the UI, only a timeout message.
GitHub session is active.
The issue persists even after restarting the editor.

🧪 Steps already tried
Verified GitHub login ✔
Confirmed Copilot Chat extension is installed ✔
Ensured Copilot Chat is enabled ✔
Restarted Visual Studio Code ✔

## AI Translation: ### Title: Lost credits due to faulty AI ### Body: Type: Bug Hello, I lost several hours and numerous credits because of a poor analysis by the Claude Sonnet 4.6 agent, which failed to identify a trivial Laravel model binding issue. I am requesting a refund for the credits consumed during this session.

Thanks for creating this issue! We figured it's missing some basic information or in some other way doesn't follow our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting). Please take the time to review these and update the issue.

For Copilot Issues, be sure to visit our [Copilot-specific guidelines](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Copilot-Issues) page for details on the necessary information.

Happy Coding!

Node.js : 22.22.1
V8 : 14.2.231.22-electron.0
Système d’exploitation : Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

Dépôt : PASROD/OrgaProf

Agent IA : Claude Sonnet 4.6 puis GPT 4.1

Pendant au moins 3 heures, j'ai cherché à résoudre un bug dans la vue ElevesView.vue de mon projet créé en VueJS. J'ai indiqué à l'agent les logs, les informations de la console de debug -> Network de Chrome et donné des directives car je pensais qu'il ne cherchait pas dans la bonne direction. Pendant tout ce temps, il est resté campé sur ses positions à me proposer des tests et des manipulations dont le résultat était stérile. Quand je n'ai plus eu de crédits à cause des prompts répétés pour résoudre une erreur qui s'est révélée triviale, l'agent GPT 4.1 a pris la suite et a trouvé directement d'où venait le souci avec les mêmes informations que je donnais à Claude Sonnet 4.6. A savoir sur une méthode PUT la réponse suivante : {"user":null,"inscriptions":[],"inscription_courante":null,"parents":[]} dans l'onglet Network de Chrome. En faisant 2 petites requêtes sur la table incriminée, GPT à trouvé la solution : 
Rempalcer Route::apiResource('eleves', EleveController::class); par Route::apiResource('eleves', EleveController::class)->parameters(['eleves' => 'eleve']);

Claude Sonnet 4.6 malgré mes directives et interrogations, est resté campé sur des tests et des solutions hors contexte ce qui m'a fait consommer un grand nombre de crédit de requêtes Premium pour rien !!! Ce problème étant dû à l'agent, j'aimerai que l'on me recrédite mes requêtes Premium perdues lors de ce dysfonctionnement.

[Bug Claude Sonnet 4.6.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26731201/Bug.Claude.Sonnet.4.6.txt)

title: [Bug] `chat.disableAIFeatures` is removed from user profile without user approval, re-enabling AI features
description: ## Steps to Reproduce ## 1. Open the latest VSCode Insider build. 2. Observe the `chat.disableAIFeatures` setting was automatically removed from my settings profile without my approval. ### System Information ### Version: 1.117.0-insider (user setup) Commit: 98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62 Date: 2026-04-14T16:52:34Z Electron: 39.8.7 ElectronBuildId: 13797146 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
comments: Looks like #310143

@gjsjohnmurray While possibly related this issue is distinct. This issue is that the setting was completely removed from my user-level settings with no action on my part. This is not related to needing to set a workspace-level setting to enable AI per workspace.
I am also running the Insiders build, worth noting.

- **Anomaly type**: `new`
- **Previous stable release**: [1.114.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/3c9a3a4c-6e52-bc43-b2a8-5145e896176a?product=VSCode&commit=e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036)
- **Current stable release**: [1.115.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/3c9a3a4c-6e52-bc43-b2a8-5145e896176a?product=VSCode&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- **Last release without this error**: [1.114.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/3c9a3a4c-6e52-bc43-b2a8-5145e896176a?product=VSCode&commit=e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036)
- **First release with this error**: [1.115.0-insider](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/3c9a3a4c-6e52-bc43-b2a8-5145e896176a?product=VSCode&commit=dcd017c96b0f0d515131b0478847f8cec911570e)
- **Commit range**: [compare link](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036...dcd017c96b0f0d515131b0478847f8cec911570e)

**Impact**: 19.0K users, 74.8K hits on 1.115.0 stable across Linux, Mac, Windows.

**Deminified call path**: `event.ts` → `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts:371` → `instantiationService.ts` → `chatToolInvocationPart.ts:196` → `chatToolInvocationPart.ts:120` → `chatToolInvocationPart.ts:98` (render) → `autorunImpl.ts` → `transaction.ts` → `observableValue.ts` → `chatToolInvocation.ts:241` → `languageModelToolsService.ts:582` → `mainThreadLanguageModelTools.ts:63`

The listener leak originates in the chat tool invocation UI rendering path. When `chatTerminalToolProgressPart` is constructed, it creates an event listener chain through the observable/transaction system that exceeds the threshold during tool invocation state transitions.

> Generated by [errors-stable-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411677600/agentic_workflow) · ● 10.4M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-stable-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-stable-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24411677600, workflow_id: errors-stable-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411677600 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-stable-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary Each `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` instance (one per terminal tool invocation) subscribes to global service emitters like `keybindingService.onDidUpdateKeybindings()` in its constructor. In agentic chat sessions with many concurrent terminal tool invocations, the accumulated listener count on these emitters exceeds the leak detection threshold (line 371 at commit `41dd792b`), throwing the "potential listener LEAK detected, popular" error. A secondary race condition in `_initializeTerminalActions()` — called both directly and via `whenConnected.then()` — compounds the issue by registering duplicate `onDidRegisterTerminalInstanceWithToolSession` listeners. ### Culprit Commit This is a **pre-existing latent bug** — the problematic code was identical in both 1.114.0 (`e7fb5e96`) and 1.115.0 (`41dd792b`). The error became detectable in 1.115.0 due to increased agentic feature adoption, which pushed the number of concurrent `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` instances above the leak detection threshold. No single commit in the regression window introduced the bug. **Fix PRs** (both merged to `main`, milestone **1.116.0** — not in 1.115.0 stable): | PR | Author | What it fixes | |----|--------|---------------| | [#308000](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308000) | `@meganrogge` | Removes the per-instance `onDidUpdateKeybindings` subscription (the source of this stack trace) and refactors the action bar to a scoped `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` | | [#308819](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308819) | `@Copilot` | Fixes the `_initializeTerminalActions()` race condition (double-call → single guarded call) | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant ToolSvc as languageModelToolsService participant Invoc as chatToolInvocation participant InvPart as chatToolInvocationPart participant TermPart as chatTerminalToolProgressPart participant KBSvc as keybindingService (global) ToolSvc->>Invoc: transitionFromStreaming() Invoc->>Invoc: _state.set({Executing}) Note over Invoc: Observable triggers autorun Invoc->>InvPart: autorun → render() InvPart->>InvPart: partStore.clear() (dispose old) InvPart->>TermPart: createToolInvocationSubPart() → new ChatTerminalToolProgressPart Note over TermPart: Constructor subscribes to
keybindingService.onDidUpdateKeybindings TermPart->>KBSvc: _register(onDidUpdateKeybindings) Note over KBSvc: ⚠️ With 50+ tool invocations,
listener count > threshold KBSvc-->>KBSvc: LeakageMonitor.check() Note over KBSvc: 💥 "potential listener LEAK
detected, popular" ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` | **Root cause + crash site** — constructor subscribes to global emitter per instance (line 371) | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatToolInvocationPart.ts` | Rendering loop — creates `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` via `createToolInvocationSubPart()` | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatProgressTypes/chatToolInvocation.ts` | State transitions trigger re-renders via observable | | `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | Leak detection infrastructure — `LeakageMonitor` throws when threshold exceeded | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code 1.115.0 stable 2. Start an agentic chat session (e.g., Copilot agent mode) 3. Let the agent execute many terminal tool invocations (30+) in a single session 4. Each tool invocation creates a `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` that subscribes to `keybindingService.onDidUpdateKeybindings()` 5. When the total listener count on the keybinding service emitter exceeds the threshold (~50+), the leak error is thrown The error is non-deterministic in the sense that it depends on the number of concurrent terminal tool invocations. Sessions with fewer invocations won't hit the threshold. ### Fix Proposal **Already fixed** in PRs [#308000](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308000) and [#308819](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308819), both targeting **1.116.0**: 1. **Fix location**: `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` → constructor **Approach (PR #308000)**: Remove the per-instance `onDidUpdateKeybindings` subscription entirely. Replace the manual `ActionBar` with a scoped `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` that manages keybinding tooltips internally, eliminating the need for per-instance keybinding change listeners. 2. **Fix location**: `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` → constructor / `_initializeTerminalActions()` **Approach (PR #308819)**: Guard the double `_initializeTerminalActions()` call with a `didInitializeTerminalActions` flag to prevent the async race condition that registers duplicate `onDidRegisterTerminalInstanceWithToolSession` listeners. **For 1.115.0 stable**: Consider cherry-picking PR #308000 and #308819 if a patch release is planned. ### Recommended Owner `@meganrogge` — terminal chat area owner, authored the primary fix PR (#308000) and reviewed/merged the secondary fix (#308819) > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24420844171/agentic_workflow) · ● 17.9M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)
