title: GitHub Copilot Chat: Error "Language model unavailable" during service degradation
description: Description: I am unable to use GitHub Copilot Chat. Every time I try to send a message, I receive the error message: "Language model unavailable". Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open Visual Studio Code. 2. Open GitHub Copilot Chat panel. 3. Type any query and press Enter. 4. The error "Language model unavailable" appears. Additional Context: 1. I have tried signing out and signing back in to GitHub. 2. I have restarted VS Code multiple times. 3. I am aware that GitHub Status is currently "Yellow" with reported degradation in Copilot services (as of April 14, 2026). However, the chat remains unusable even after these attempts.
comments: Duplicate? https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309766

title: Agent: "No auto mode endpoints provided"
description: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No - VS Code Version: - OS Version: Steps to Reproduce: 1. 2. log:
comments:
If you have the following message on the GitHub Copilot Chat output:
Error: Failed to fetch models (...): Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free.
You have to switch to a paid subscription, and pay now if you want to continue using the service as free trials have been completely paused. The switch is on https://github.com/settings/billing/licensing > Manage subscription.

> If you have the following message on the GitHub Copilot Chat output: Error: Failed to fetch models (...): Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free. You have to switch to a paid subscription, and pay now if you want to continue using the service as free trials have been completely paused. The switch is on https://github.com/settings/billing/licensing > Manage subscription.

My Pro subscription does not expire until May 1. Currently, I can only upgrade to Pro+. Canceling the Pro subscription will also only take effect on May 1. I am still within the 30-day period. If I stop the trial, there should be a payment option for Pro.

title: Language model unavailable

Actual Result The error message "Language model unavailable" appears, and AI features cannot be used. They paused all GitHub Copilot Pro trials, and my request to downgrade subscription into Copilot Free will remain pending until the day my trial expires. I don't even know how to use copilot now
Same issue and reported similar last month with zero response and nothing done
Hmm, I have paid for the Pro version, but I think technically I am still in the 'trial' period, I start paying in about 2 weeks. I think I have been affected by this too

FYI changing my subscription settings fixed this for me. Either downgrade to free or skip the trial and begin payment early. See update at top of this post: https://github.blog/changelog/2026-04-10-pausing-new-github-copilot-pro-trials/
Doesn't seem to allow me to manage the subscription and simultaneously both says I am on a free trial, which I can't cancel, and that I have an active subscription? Meanwhile access is still blocked.
Thanks - fixed it. Under Billing/Licensing > Licensing> clicking 'Start Paid subscription'. https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/manage-your-account/view-and-change-your-copilot-plan
I don't seem to have that option?
@nervoustestpilot  same...
@nervoustestpilot same

title: GitHub Copilot model access blocked despite active Pro subscription
description:
Type: Performance Issue Hello VS Code Support Team, I am experiencing an issue with GitHub Copilot model access inside Visual Studio Code despite having an active GitHub Pro subscription. Details: - I have already paid for GitHub Pro and confirmed my subscription is active. - My billing overview shows GitHub Pro at $0.00 per month (with $4.00 off for 2 years remaining) and Copilot Free. - I worked one month with paid usage successfully. - After the second month renewal, Copilot inside VS Code does not enable Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.4 models. They still show the “Upgrade” option. - I am logged into my GitHub account from VS Code. I tried logging out and back in, but the issue persists. Request: Please investigate why these models remain inaccessible despite my subscription and payment. Also add functionality for attache document or screenshot in your issue reporter. Thank you, Mathews Extension version: 0.43.0 VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Copilot Chat shows "Language model unavailable" in VS Code Remote SSH after update
description:
Hi,

I am using Copilot Chat in VS Code with a remote SSH server. My bot is running normally, but Copilot always shows:

"Language model unavailable"

My Copilot Pro subscription is active, and this was working before.

Is this a known issue with Remote SSH or recent updates?

Thanks.
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! We figured it's missing some basic information or in some other way doesn't follow our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting). Please take the time to review these and update the issue.

For Copilot Issues, be sure to visit our [Copilot-specific guidelines](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Copilot-Issues) page for details on the necessary information.

Happy Coding!

title: Access denied issue
description: - Copilot Chat Extension Version:0.43.0 - VS Code Version:1.115.0 - OS Version:windows 11 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode):all feature - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): all models - Logs: Access is denied and I'm a paied user. I can't use any advanced model in my vs code. When i tried to chat with copilot on website, it's said access is denied.
comments: Does it related with 30 trials?

title: Access denied issue
description: Attached you can find the issue in more detailed. I can't even access it on the website.
comments:
If you have the following message on the GitHub Copilot Chat output:
Error: Failed to fetch models (...): Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free.
You have to switch to a paid subscription, and pay now if you want to continue using the service as free trials have been completely paused, or switch to Copilot Free.

Hi @Dubeymohit0201, sorry to hear that you're having issues still. Have you also tried signing out of your GitHub account and logging back in? You can sign out from here. Regarding the steps to obtain logs: 1. Run Cmd+Shift+P to open the control palette and type "Set Log Level" 2. Select "Trace" 3. Run Cmd+Shift+P again and type "Reload VS Code" 4. Try sending a chat message and wait for it to fail 5. Open the Output view 6. Select "GitHub Copilot Chat", click into the text, press Cmd+A and then Cmd+C to copy the content and share here

[image: image.png]

Hello GitHub Support Team,

I am currently experiencing an issue with GitHub Copilot across multiple
IDEs (Android Studio, Visual Studio, and VS Code).

Although my account shows that GitHub Copilot Pro trial is still active
(with remaining days), I am unable to use Copilot features. I consistently
receive the following error:

"Error: Failed to fetch models (403) - Copilot Pro trials have been
temporarily paused."

Details:

   - Account: NChienMS
   - Copilot status: Pro trial (still active)
   - Issue occurs on:
      - Android Studio
      - Visual Studio Code
      - Visual Studio
   - Network tested:
      - WiFi
      - Mobile hotspot (4G)
   - Already tried:
      - Logging out and logging back in
      - Reinstalling Copilot extensions
      - Restarting IDEs
      - Switching networks

It appears that my Copilot trial may have been temporarily paused or
restricted on the server side.

Could you please check my account status and help resolve this issue?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
NChienMS

comments: I don't think it's just you. Happening to me too.
Is your device also showing "Language model unavailable"?
Exactly the same here. I have no idea how to reconnect the models right now, and it's seriously slowing down my workflow. Feels like I'm coding completely solo in the age of AI agents — rough times.
Yes, my work just can't get any progress, lol
I have the same issue.
me too...any solution??
I also noted this.

If you have the following message on the GitHub Copilot Chat output:
Error: Failed to fetch models (...): Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free.
You have to switch to a paid subscription, and pay now if you want to continue using the service as free trials have been completely paused, or switch to Copilot Free.
(Edited to delete UUID)
@ppedrotti Thank you for reminding me, but then what's the point of my Copilot Pro subscription of $10.00 per month?

Current i using Copilot Pro trials, i have 3 days left on my GitHub Copilot trial. But i receved error "Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free."

> Current i using Copilot Pro trials, i have 3 days left on my GitHub Copilot trial. But i receved error "Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free."

Same with me.
Oh, i find [this one ](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-04-10-pausing-new-github-copilot-pro-trials/)
Duplicate: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309766

- **Commit range**: [0271b4d2...1ec46de1](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/0271b4d2fe910341fa33a2a4748835168c2a7f92...1ec46de152cab65d0304db62e2aa0fcb6e1a5b1f)

| Bucket ID | Error Message | Users |

| [`75c4c8b5`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/75c4c8b5-9361-7286-930e-ddf8a1fc29a9?product=Agents) | Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString') | 6 |
| [`f6615ef4`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/f6615ef4-5aad-e261-9453-96ff9eb86bdf?product=Agents) | Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString') | 6 |

## Analysis

The error occurs in the Agents code review service when `getCodeReviewVersion` tries to call `toString` on an undefined value. The deminified stack shows:

```
at (anonymous) (codeReviewService.ts:66:34)
at (anonymous) (codeReviewService.ts:66:3)
at getCodeReviewVersion (codeReview.contributions.ts:150:0)
at (anonymous) (autorunImpl.ts:110:10)
at (anonymous) (lazyObservableValue.ts:21:9)
at (anonymous) (autorunImpl.ts:103:11)
```

This is an Agents-specific regression in the code review feature, likely caused by a nullable value in the code review service not being guarded before calling `toString()`.

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24373578551) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24373578551, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24373578551 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString')` occurs when opening a session in the Agents app that contains file changes where `modifiedUri` is `undefined` (e.g., deleted files represented as `IChatSessionFileChange2`). The `getCodeReviewVersion()` function calls `file.currentUri.toString()` but `currentUri` can be `undefined` when the v2 type guard (`isIChatSessionFileChange2`) fails — most notably after session cache deserialization strips the `uri` field. ### Culprit Commit No single commit in the regression range (`0271b4d2...1ec46de1`) directly modifies the crash-site files. This is a **latent bug** in the v1/v2 file-change handling code that was exposed by a data-flow change in the range — likely a commit in `extensions/copilot/` that changed how session file changes are produced, causing `IChatSessionFileChange2` objects (with optional `modifiedUri`) to appear where only v1 objects were seen before. Most likely triggers from the range: - `c7fd9716` (`@DonJayamanne`) — refactored `clearWorkspaceChanges` handling - `f6d4cad1` (`@lszomoru`) — changed `git add -A` → `git add` for GVFS compatibility ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User as User clicks session participant SessMgmt as sessionsManagementService participant Autorun as codeReview.contributions autorun participant Extract as getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges participant Version as getCodeReviewVersion User->>SessMgmt: openSession() → setActiveSession() SessMgmt->>Autorun: activeSession observable triggers Autorun->>Autorun: reads activeSession.changes Autorun->>Extract: getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges(changes) Note over Extract: isIChatSessionFileChange2 returns false
(uri field missing after deserialization)
⚠️ Falls to v1 path: currentUri = change.modifiedUri Note over Extract: modifiedUri is undefined (deleted file)
→ currentUri = undefined Extract->>Version: getCodeReviewVersion(files) Note over Version: 💥 file.currentUri.toString()
Cannot read properties of undefined ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/codeReview/browser/codeReviewService.ts:66` | **Crash site** — `getCodeReviewVersion()` calls `file.currentUri.toString()` without null guard | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/codeReview/browser/codeReviewService.ts:48-61` | **Root cause** — `getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges()` v1 path uses `change.modifiedUri` directly without fallback | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/codeReview/browser/codeReview.contributions.ts:149-150` | **Trigger** — autorun reads changes and calls both functions | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsModel.ts:882-887` | **Contributing** — cache deserialization only handles v1, strips `uri` field from v2 changes | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:958-959` | **Contributing** — `_extractChanges()` unsafely casts any changes array to `IChatSessionFileChange[]` | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatSessionsService.ts:471-474` | **Type guard** — `isIChatSessionFileChange2` requires `uri instanceof URI`, fails on deserialized data | ### Repro Steps 1. Open the VS Code Agents app (Insiders 1.116.0+) 2. Create a session that produces file changes including a **deleted file** (so `modifiedUri` is `undefined` in the v2 change object) 3. Close and reopen the app (session gets cached and deserialized) 4. Click on the session in the sessions list to open it 5. The code review autorun triggers → `getCodeReviewVersion()` crashes The error may also occur without cache if the session provider produces v2 changes where `uri` is not a proper `URI` instance. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/codeReview/browser/codeReviewService.ts` → `getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges()` **Approach**: Add a guard in the v1 fallback path to handle `modifiedUri` being `undefined`. The simplest fix mirrors the v2 path's fallback pattern: ```typescript // In getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges, the else branch (v1 path) should guard: // Before: currentUri: change.modifiedUri, // After: currentUri: change.modifiedUri ?? (change as any).uri, // Or filter out entries with undefined currentUri ``` **Secondary fix** (recommended): Update `AgentSessionsCache.loadCachedSessions()` in `agentSessionsModel.ts:882-887` to properly handle `IChatSessionFileChange2` during deserialization — preserve the `uri` field and use `isIChatSessionFileChange2` to apply the correct revive logic. **Tertiary fix**: Update `_extractChanges()` in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:958-959` to avoid the unsafe cast — use `isIChatSessionFileChange2` to properly distinguish v1 and v2 changes. ### Recommended Owner `@bryanchen-d` — already assigned to this issue. `@benvillalobos` is the `chat-review` area owner and may want to review the fix given it touches the code review service. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24374294580/agentic_workflow) · ● 25.9M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'isLoading')` occurs in `ChangesButtonBarWidget._getButtonConfiguration()` when the `reviewState` parameter is `undefined`. The `reviewState` value is captured from a `derivedOpts` observable (`reviewStateObs`) inside an autorun closure. Although the observable's computation function always returns an object, the VS Code observable system's `Derived._recompute()` silently catches computation errors via `onBugIndicatingError` — if the computation throws (e.g., a nested service call fails), the internal `_value` remains `undefined` (its initial state), and this `undefined` propagates through the closure to the button config callback. A subsequent menu change event (delivered via `DebounceEmitter.resume()`) triggers the callback, causing the crash. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`a9d50bd7`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/a9d50bd72636ba403a7ee65204d27b03224ef411) | | Author | `@lszomoru` | | PR | #308781 | | Message | Sessions - fix title menu context when switching between views | | Why | This is the only commit in the regression range (`0271b4d2...1ec46de1`) that modifies `changesView.ts`. It moves the "Title actions" autorun from the `ChangesViewPane` constructor to `onVisible()`, changing the lifecycle of `renderDisposables`. This alters when `ChangesButtonBarWidget` is created/destroyed and when `updateActions()` fires, exposing a latent timing issue where `reviewStateObs` computation can fail during view initialization. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant V as ChangesViewPane.onVisible() participant W as ChangesButtonBarWidget participant D as reviewStateObs (derivedOpts) participant M as MenuWorkbenchButtonBar participant E as DebounceEmitter V->>V: renderDisposables.clear() V->>V: Title actions autorun → updateActions() V->>W: createInstance(ChangesButtonBarWidget) W->>D: reviewStateObs.read(reader) Note over D: ⚠️ If computation throws
(e.g. service not ready),
error caught by onBugIndicatingError,
_value stays undefined D-->>W: returns undefined (or valid object) W->>M: new MenuWorkbenchButtonBar(..., buttonConfigProvider closure) M->>M: menu.onDidChange(update) + update() Note over E: Menu change queued
(debounced) E->>M: resume() → fire() → update() M->>W: buttonConfigProvider(action) W->>W: _getButtonConfiguration(action, outgoingChanges, undefined) Note over W: 💥 reviewState.isLoading
TypeError: Cannot read properties
of undefined ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts` | Crash site (`_getButtonConfiguration` L173) and root cause (`reviewStateObs` derivation L104-139, autorun closure L141-159) | | `src/vs/platform/actions/browser/buttonbar.ts` | `MenuWorkbenchButtonBar` — calls `buttonConfigProvider` during `update()` (L120, L238) | | `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/derivedImpl.ts` | `Derived._recompute()` silently catches computation errors, leaving `_value` as `undefined` (L195-228) | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) with a session active 2. Switch between views or trigger the Changes view to re-render (causing `onVisible()` → `renderDisposables.clear()` cycle) 3. The error is non-deterministic — it occurs when the `reviewStateObs` derived observable's computation fails during initialization (depends on service readiness) 4. The `RUN_SESSION_CODE_REVIEW_ACTION_ID` action must be present in the `ChatEditingSessionChangesToolbar` menu for the code path to reach line 173 ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts` → `_getButtonConfiguration()` (line 172) **Approach**: Add a defensive null guard on `reviewState` before accessing `.isLoading`. This handles the case where `reviewStateObs` returns `undefined` due to a silently failed computation: ```typescript if (action.id === RUN_SESSION_CODE_REVIEW_ACTION_ID) { if (!reviewState) { return { showIcon: true, showLabel: false, isSecondary: true }; } // existing logic... } ``` Additionally, the autorun at line 141 should provide a fallback value when reading `reviewStateObs`: ```typescript const reviewState = reviewStateObs.read(reader) ?? { isLoading: false, commentCount: undefined }; ``` ### Recommended Owner `@lszomoru` — culprit commit author and primary contributor to the Sessions changes view code in this regression range > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24374283230/agentic_workflow) · ● 17.3M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## Analysis

This is a `popular`-type listener leak originating from `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` (toolbar/menu service path). The deminified stack shows:

```
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1062:14) — LeakageMonitor.create
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1262:33) — Emitter._event
at Object.u (menuService.ts:437:0) — onWillAddFirstListener
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1271:19) — Emitter._event
at new mn (toolbar.ts:377:0) — WorkbenchToolBar constructor
at (anonymous) (instantiationService.ts) — _createInstance/createInstance
at new YFe (chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts:448:0)
at hU.createToolInvocationSubPart (chatToolInvocationPart.ts:199:36)
at (anonymous) (chatToolInvocationPart.ts:123:37)
at hU render (chatToolInvocationPart.ts:167:2)
```

The leak occurs through toolbar menu service subscriptions created when rendering terminal tool progress parts in chat. A similar leak in this component was previously fixed in #304828 (v1.112.0), but this is a different leak path going through the toolbar/menuService subscription chain.

**Note**: Both bucket IDs (`9ab4bed4` and `7ebc94dc`) represent different emitters leaking via the same component — they share the same `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts → chatToolInvocationPart.ts` creation path but differ in which specific emitter triggers the leak warning.

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary Each `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` creates a `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` instance that subscribes to the shared `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` singleton emitter. In long Agents sessions with many terminal tool calls, the number of concurrent toolbar instances exceeds the global leak warning threshold of **175** (configured in `sessions/browser/workbench.ts`), triggering the "potential listener LEAK detected, popular" error. The "popular" classification means no single call site dominates — many independent toolbar instances each contribute one listener. ### Culprit Commit This is an **accumulation/architecture issue** rather than a single-commit regression. The error first appeared at low volume in a 1.115.0-insider build (commit `fc23f2d2`, March 31 — 1 user, 4 hits) and grew as the Agents user base expanded through 1.116.0-insider builds (41 users, 165 hits by commit `47add6df`). The per-instance `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` pattern has existed since `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` was introduced. Growth in Agents usage (more users, longer sessions with more terminal tool calls) pushed the concurrent listener count past the threshold. No single commit in the regression window (`41dd792b...fc6d18c3`) introduced the leak pattern. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Renderer as ChatListItemRenderer participant ThinkingPart as ChatThinkingContentPart participant ToolPart as ChatToolInvocationPart participant TerminalPart as ChatTerminalToolProgressPart participant Toolbar as MenuWorkbenchToolBar participant Menu as MenuImpl participant HiddenStates as PersistedMenuHideState Renderer->>ThinkingPart: finalizeTitleIfDefault() Note over ThinkingPart: Materializes lazy tool item
if single tool, no thinking text ThinkingPart->>ToolPart: Lazy.value → new ChatToolInvocationPart ToolPart->>TerminalPart: createToolInvocationSubPart() → new ChatTerminalToolProgressPart TerminalPart->>Toolbar: new MenuWorkbenchToolBar(MENU_CHAT_TERMINAL_TOOL_PROGRESS) Toolbar->>Menu: menuService.createMenu() → new MenuImpl Menu->>HiddenStates: onDidChange subscription (via onWillAddFirstListener) Note over HiddenStates: ⚠️ Root cause:
175+ subscriptions from
concurrent toolbar instances HiddenStates-->>Menu: LeakageMonitor.check() Note over HiddenStates: 💥 ListenerLeakError thrown:
"potential listener LEAK detected, popular" ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` | **Root cause** — creates a `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` per instance (line 448) | | `src/vs/platform/actions/browser/toolbar.ts` | Toolbar subscribes to `menu.onDidChange` (line 377) | | `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` | Menu subscribes to shared `hiddenStates.onDidChange` via `onWillAddFirstListener` (line 437) | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatToolInvocationPart.ts` | Creates `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` as sub-part (line 199) | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/chatThinkingContentPart.ts` | Lazy materialization of tool parts triggers creation (line 985) | | `src/vs/sessions/browser/workbench.ts` | Sets global leak threshold to 175 (line 325) | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Agents (sessions workbench) 2. Start a long chat session that makes many terminal tool calls (e.g., ask the agent to perform a complex multi-step task that involves running many shell commands) 3. Continue the session until 175+ terminal tool invocations have been rendered 4. The error will fire when the next `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` is created, as the shared `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` emitter exceeds its listener threshold The error is more likely in sessions with many sequential tool calls (agentic coding tasks) rather than simple Q&A sessions. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` → constructor (line 448) **Approach**: The toolbar subscription chain (`MenuWorkbenchToolBar` → `MenuImpl` → `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange`) creates a per-instance listener on a global singleton emitter. Options to fix: 1. **Lazy toolbar creation** (recommended): Defer creating the `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` until the tool part is visible/interactive. Completed terminal tool invocations don't need an active toolbar with menu subscriptions. Only create the toolbar when the part is in view and the user might interact with it. 2. **Dispose completed toolbars**: After a terminal tool call completes and has been rendered in its final state, dispose the `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` to release the menu subscription. Re-create it on demand if the user interacts. 3. **Share menu instance**: Use a shared `MenuImpl` across multiple toolbar instances for the same `MENU_CHAT_TERMINAL_TOOL_PROGRESS` menu ID, rather than creating a new menu per toolbar. This would require changes in the `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` API. Note: A previous similar leak in this component was fixed in #304828 (v1.112.0) via a different path. ### Recommended Owner `@meganrogge` — area owner for `chat-terminal` and terminal tool progress parts (co-owned with `@tyriar`). The fix involves the `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` constructor's toolbar creation pattern. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24374122692/agentic_workflow) · ● 20.2M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

title: Agent mode create_file produces corrupted Go files due to race between empty file creation and gopls workspace/didCreateFiles
description:
## Description

When Copilot agent mode creates a new `.go` file using the `create_file` tool, the resulting file is frequently corrupted with duplicate `package` declarations, misplaced comments, and large blocks of empty lines. This affects **only Go files** — all other file types (`.ts`, `.js`, `.json`, `.yaml`, `.md`, etc.) work correctly.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/37fb7358-602e-4a66-8958-bc9bcdcf86ee

## Previously reported (closed without fix)

- #296856 — "Go code generation with gopilot very difficult"
- #287987 — "create_file Tool Produces Corrupted Output (Intermittent)"

Both were closed as `info-needed` / `not-planned`, but the root cause has now been identified.

## Root Cause Analysis

This is a **race condition** between VS Code's chat editing session and **gopls** (the Go language server).

### The sequence

1. `_acceptEdits()` in `chatEditingSession.ts` calls `_getOrCreateModifiedFileEntry()` which calls `_createModifiedFileEntry()`.

2. Since the file doesn't exist, `createModelReference()` fails. The catch block runs:
   ```typescript
   // chatEditingSession.ts, _createModifiedFileEntry
   await this._bulkEditService.apply({ edits: [{ newResource: resource }] });
   ```
   This creates the file **empty on disk** (no `contents` property is set on the edit).

3. `WorkingCopyFileService.doCreateFileOrFolder()` creates the empty file via `fileService.createFile()`, then fires `onDidRunWorkingCopyFileOperation`.

4. `MainThreadFileSystemEventService` forwards this to the extension host via `$onDidRunFileOperation()` → `_onDidCreateFile.fire()` → the VS Code API `workspace.onDidCreateFiles` event is emitted.

5. The **vscode-languageclient** library forwards this to **gopls** as the LSP `workspace/didCreateFiles` notification.

6. **gopls** handles `DidCreateFiles` in `server/workspace.go`. It calls `completion.NewFile()` in `golang/completion/newfile.go`, which:
   - Checks if `ui.newGoFileHeader` is enabled (default: `true`)
   - Checks if the file is empty (`len(content) != 0`) — **it IS empty at this point**
   - Generates a `package <name>\n` declaration (and optionally a copyright header)
   - Sends `workspace/applyEdit` back to VS Code to insert this content

7. VS Code applies gopls's edit to the text model — the model now contains `package foo\n`.

8. **Meanwhile**, the chat editing session continues: `_createModifiedFileEntry` finishes, and `_acceptEdits` calls `entry.acceptAgentEdits()` which applies Copilot's content (which also contains `package foo`) via `modifiedModel.pushEditOperations()`.

9. **Both edits land in the same text model** → duplicate `package` line, misplaced comments, and corruption.

### Why only Go?

gopls is one of very few language servers that auto-populate new files via the `workspace/didCreateFiles` notification. This behavior is controlled by the gopls setting `ui.newGoFileHeader` (default: `true`), registered with the file operation filter `**/*.go`.

## Corruption Pattern

The resulting file typically looks like:
```go
package foo
// Package foo does something...
package foo

<many empty lines>

<truncated or missing code>
```

Instead of the expected:
```go
// Package foo does something...
package foo

import (...)

// actual code...
```

## Reproduction Steps

1. Open a Go project in VS Code with the Go extension and Copilot
2. Use agent mode to ask Copilot to create a new `.go` file
3. Observe the file has duplicate `package` declarations and corrupted content
4. Retry — sometimes it works if the file already exists from the previous attempt

## Workaround

Add to VS Code settings:
```json
"gopls": {
    "ui.newGoFileHeader": false
}
```

## Suggested Fix

In `_createModifiedFileEntry` (`chatEditingSession.ts`), when creating a new file for agent edits via `bulkEditService.apply({ edits: [{ newResource: resource }] })`, the `workspace/didCreateFiles` notification should either be suppressed or the file should not be created empty. Options:

1. **Suppress `didCreateFiles`** for files created as part of an agent editing session (e.g., by adding metadata to the file operation event)
2. **Create the file with initial placeholder content** so that gopls's `len(content) != 0` guard rejects its auto-insertion
3. **Defer the `didCreateFiles` notification** until after the agent's initial content has been applied

## Environment

Affects all OS platforms, all VS Code versions with agent mode, when the Go extension with gopls is active.

## Key Code Locations

- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts` — `_createModifiedFileEntry`, line where empty file is created
- `src/vs/workbench/services/workingCopy/common/workingCopyFileService.ts` — `doCreateFileOrFolder`, fires `onDidRunWorkingCopyFileOperation`
- `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadFileSystemEventService.ts` — forwards event to extension host
- gopls `gopls/internal/server/workspace.go` — `DidCreateFiles` handler
- gopls `gopls/internal/golang/completion/newfile.go` — `NewFile()` auto-inserts package declaration into empty files
comments: Hi @zhichli. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

title: YAML support for VSCode tasks
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
The ability to define VSCode tasks in YAML instead of JSON would be super useful, especially since task configurations tend to be repetitive by nature, and we could use YAML anchors in order to DRY away the repetitions. Often, it's only the `command` that needs to change.

In projects with dozens of tasks, this could cut down the size of the tasks file by well over 50%.

It's 2026. YAML is a pretty universal standard by now.
comments:
Just found this very old issue:

- https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/4851

Hopefully that decision can be revised 10+ years later.

VS Code should already have the ability to parse YAML by now (because of the AI stuff that uses YAML configs).

YAML can simply be a fallback for JSON, so that it has no impact whatsoever on current users. Only if `tasks.json` is not found would `tasks.yml` be parsed (if it exists). People who do not use YAML configs would not be affected at all.

Just parse the YAML and send the resulting object into your current JSON code path (the final result/structure will be the same for both formats).

This could be considered an advanced feature, so no need for Intellisense in `tasks.yml` either.
Side note: Any JSON is a valid YAML file

not JSONC tho
`// comments` are not valid YAML

title: Agents: Expect CLI's plan to be rendered and saved somewhere in the Changes panel
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
In testing @DonJayamanne's work on planning support in the Agents app (powered by the Copilot CLI), I like that an artifact is generated with the plan, and I think I expect it to be rendered within the Changes panel (additionally or alternatively).

The save button is valuable (gives me the option to make this plan as persistent or fleeting as I'd like), though I think I initially expected to be guided to save the plan in either a new file in my `Files` tab, or a new `Artifacts` tab (rather than guided to pick a location on my machine overall).
comments: @bamurtaugh  Updated title to indicate that the artifact is what needs to be rendered, as I'm working on the Plan widget, and my understanding is that it will still say the same (i.e. displayed above the chat input), note I am working on updating the plan widget based on feedback received.,
Might as well not show the Aritifacts, currently CLI makes a tool call to display the artifacts.

Offline discussions with Brigit
* No need of artifacts view
* Display the plan widget above chat input
* After plan is approved, we display the plan information in the changes view.

title: Workspace picker becomes less obvious after selecting a workspace
description: Testing #309100 Expected: After selecting a workspace in the Agents App, the ARIA label of the workspace picker mentions that the menu allows a user to pick another workspace. Actual: After selecting a workspace in the Agents App, the ARIA label of the workspace picker only mentions that there is a collapsed menu button.
comments: That's ok. We want what's present visually to be read by the screen reader, as it is here. And the button collapsed opens list indicates a user can choose.

title: Screenreader context is lost after navigating away for a while
description: Testing #309100 Expected: NVDA can read out the elements of the Agents App after navigating to another window for several minutes. Actual: NVDA reports "unknown" for various elements of the Agents App after navigating to another window for several minutes.
comments: The issue seemed to disappear after restarting NVDA.
sounds like a screen reader issue.

title: New button should be disabled if it is a no-op

title: AI Customization: Unify item discovery through provider abstraction & cut over sessions
description:
> NOTE: AI Generated work log


## Summary

The AI Customization list widget (`aiCustomizationListWidget.ts`, ~2000 lines) currently maintains two completely separate code paths for item discovery — one for the core `promptsService` and one for external `IExternalCustomizationItemProvider` harnesses. This duplication makes it difficult to evolve the UI, blocks the sessions (Agents) window from using the same provider-based approach as core VS Code, and creates maintenance burden across ~650 lines of sessions-specific `PromptsService` overrides that exist solely because the widget can't consume items through a unified pipeline.

This issue tracks a two-phase refactor:
1. **Phase 1** — Route both code paths through a single provider-shaped abstraction (PR #308626)
2. **Phase 2** — Cut over the sessions window to use an extension-contributed `itemProvider`, deleting the hardcoded `AgenticPromptsService` overrides

---

## Phase 1: Route AI Customization List Through Item Sources

**PR:** #308626 (`josh/refactor-2`)

### Problem

`aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` is a monolith that handles:
- Item model definition (`IAICustomizationListItem`)
- Per-type data fetching from `promptsService` (agents, skills, prompts, hooks, instructions) via `fetchCoreItemsForSection()`
- Per-type data fetching from external providers via `fetchProviderItemsForSection()` / `fetchItemsFromProvider()`
- Storage inference (local/user/extension/plugin/builtin) via `_inferStorageAndGroup()`
- Grouping & filtering via separate `filterItemsForCore()` and `filterItemsForProvider()` methods
- Sync overlays via `fetchLocalSyncableItems()`
- Utility functions (`getFriendlyName()`, `storageToIcon()`, `findPluginUri()`, `isChatExtensionItem()`, `applyBuiltinGroupKeys()`)
- The actual list widget UI rendering

The branching happens in `fetchItemsForSection()` and `filterItems()`:
```typescript
// fetchItemsForSection — two completely different fetch pipelines
if (activeDescriptor.itemProvider && promptType) {
    return this.fetchProviderItemsForSection(activeDescriptor, promptType);
}
return this.fetchCoreItemsForSection(promptType);

// filterItems — two completely different grouping pipelines  
if (activeDescriptor.itemProvider) {
    this.filterItemsForProvider(matchedItems);
} else {
    this.filterItemsForCore(matchedItems);
}
```

### Solution

Introduce a pipeline architecture where both data sources converge on a single shape early:

```
promptsService ──→ PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider ──→ IExternalCustomizationItem[]
                                                                        │
External Provider ────────────────────────────────────────→ IExternalCustomizationItem[]
                                                                        │
                                                                        ▼
                                                  ProviderCustomizationItemSource
                                                  (merges remote + sync, expands hooks)
                                                                        │
                                                                        ▼
                                                    AICustomizationItemNormalizer
                                                  (IExternalCustomizationItem → IAICustomizationListItem)
                                                                        │
                                                                        ▼
                                                    IAICustomizationListItem[] → Widget
                                                    (single groupMatchedItems path)
```

### New Files

| File | Purpose | Lines |
|------|---------|-------|
| `aiCustomizationListItem.ts` | Extracted `IAICustomizationListItem` interface + `IAICustomizationItemSource` contract | 63 |
| `aiCustomizationItemSourceUtils.ts` | `getFriendlyName()` utility (was private widget method) | 23 |
| `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts` | `IExternalCustomizationItem` → `IAICustomizationListItem` mapper. Contains `storageToIcon()`, `resolveSource()`, `inferStorageAndGroup()`, `isChatExtensionItem()`, `findPluginUri()` | 155 |
| `promptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.ts` | Adapts `IPromptsService` to produce `IExternalCustomizationItem[]`. Contains all per-type fetch logic (agents, skills, instructions, hooks, prompts), builtin grouping, storage source filtering, workspace subpath filtering, instruction file filtering. Implements both `provideChatSessionCustomizations()` (all types) and `provideCustomizations(promptType)` (type-scoped) | 385 |
| `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts` | Unified `IAICustomizationItemSource` — merges remote provider items + local sync items, runs through normalizer. Composes `onDidChange` from provider + sync + promptsService events | 179 |

### Widget Changes (-782 lines)

The widget loses:
- `IAICustomizationListItem` interface (→ `aiCustomizationListItem.ts`)
- `storageToIcon()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts`)
- `isChatExtensionItem()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts`)
- `applyBuiltinGroupKeys()` (→ `promptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.ts`)
- `fetchCoreItemsForSection()` (→ `promptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.ts`)
- `fetchProviderItemsForSection()` (→ `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts`)
- `fetchItemsFromProvider()` (→ `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts`)
- `_expandProviderHookItems()` (→ `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts`)
- `_inferStorageAndGroup()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts`)
- `fetchLocalSyncableItems()` (→ `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts`)
- `getFriendlyName()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemSourceUtils.ts`)
- `findPluginUri()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts`)
- `filterItemsForCore()` — **deleted entirely**; the provider path's `filterItemsForProvider()` is renamed to `groupMatchedItems()` and used for all harnesses

The widget gains:
- `itemNormalizer: AICustomizationItemNormalizer` field
- `promptsServiceItemProvider: PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider` field
- `getItemSource(descriptor)` factory method — returns a `ProviderCustomizationItemSource` wired to either the harness's `itemProvider` or the built-in `promptsServiceItemProvider`

Direct `promptsService` event subscriptions (`onDidChangeCustomAgents`, `onDidChangeSlashCommands`, `onDidChangeSkills`, `onDidChangeInstructions`) are removed from the widget — they're now owned by `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.onDidChange` (which also adds `onDidChangeHooks`).

### Behavioral Analysis

The PR converges the core path onto the provider path's existing behavior. A multi-model council review initially flagged two "regressions" — but detailed analysis confirmed these are **intentional convergence**, not regressions:

**`extensionLabel` (hover tooltips):** The core path's `applyBuiltinGroupKeys` set `extensionLabel: extInfo.displayName || extInfo.id.value` for non-builtin extension items. The provider path **never set `extensionLabel`** — it showed URI-based tooltips instead. The PR converges to the provider path's behavior. This is correct because the provider API (`IExternalCustomizationItem`) has no `extensionLabel` field — the data simply isn't available through that contract.

**`'agents'` group header (hooks tab):** The core path's `filterItemsForCore` defined an explicit `{ groupKey: 'agents', label: "Agents", icon: agentIcon }` group. The provider path's `filterItemsForProvider` **never had this group** — it used the dynamic fallback (`formatDisplayName(key)`, `Codicon.folder`). The PR converges to the provider path's behavior. Agent-embedded hooks with `groupKey: 'agents'` are only emitted by the `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider` (and only when `!isSessionsWindow`), so this only affects the core VS Code workbench's hooks tab for agent-defined hooks — a narrow edge case.

Both of these are artifacts of the core-only path that's being intentionally retired.

### Known Follow-ups in Phase 1

These are code quality items worth addressing in the PR:

1. **`getItemSource()` creates a new `ProviderCustomizationItemSource` on every call** — the autorun subscription path and `fetchItems` path use different transient instances. Should cache per active harness.
2. **Hook expansion logic duplicated** — near-identical JSONC → `parseHooksFromFile` → expand parsing in `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.fetchPromptServiceHooks()` and `ProviderCustomizationItemSource.expandProviderHookItems()`. Should share a utility.
3. **`isChatExtensionItem()` duplicated** — character-identical private method in both `AICustomizationItemNormalizer` and `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider`. Should extract to `aiCustomizationItemSourceUtils.ts`.

---

## Phase 2: Cut Over Sessions to Provider-Based Approach

### Current Sessions Architecture

The sessions (Agents) window maintains ~650 lines of hardcoded overrides to make the customization list work:

**`AgenticPromptsService` (extends `PromptsService`)** — `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/promptsService.ts` (~330 lines):
| Override | What It Does |
|----------|-------------|
| `createPromptFilesLocator()` | Returns `AgenticPromptFilesLocator` (session-scoped) |
| `findAgentSkills(token)` | Discovers built-in skills from `vs/sessions/skills/{name}/SKILL.md`, appends with lowest priority |
| `listPromptFiles(type, token)` | Includes built-in skill paths, deduplicates by name |
| `listPromptFilesForStorage(type, storage, token)` | Special-cases `BUILTIN_STORAGE` to return built-in skills |
| `getSourceFolders(type)` | Uses `~/.copilot` subdirectories as user-level source folders |

**`AgenticPromptFilesLocator` (extends `PromptFilesLocator`)** — same file (~80 lines):
| Override | What It Does |
|----------|-------------|
| `getWorkspaceFolders()` | Returns only the active session's project folder |
| `getWorkspaceFolder(resource)` | Only matches resources under the active project root |
| `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders()` | Emits from `activeProjectRoot` observable |
| `getPromptSourceFolders(type)` | Filters out Claude-specific `.claude/` folders |
| `getDefaultSourceFolders(type)` | Filters out Claude defaults |
| `getHookSourceFolders()` | Falls back to `{activeRoot}/.github/hooks/` |

**`SessionsCustomizationHarnessService`** — `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/customizationHarnessService.ts` (33 lines):
- Registers a single CLI harness via `createCliHarnessDescriptor(getCliUserRoots(userHome), [BUILTIN_STORAGE])`
- No `itemProvider`, no `syncProvider` — falls through to the core `promptsService` path

**Built-in skills** — `src/vs/sessions/skills/` (10 bundled SKILL.md files):
- `act-on-feedback`, `commit`, `create-draft-pr`, `create-pr`, `generate-run-commands`, `merge`, `sync`, `sync-upstream`, `update-pr`, `update-skills`
- Each discovered at runtime by scanning the directory, parsing YAML frontmatter, validating name/description

### How After Phase 1 the Cutover Becomes Simple

After Phase 1, **the widget doesn't care where items come from** — it always goes through `getItemSource(descriptor) → fetchItems(promptType) → IAICustomizationListItem[]`. The current sessions flow is:

```
CLI Harness (no itemProvider)
    ↓ getItemSource() sees no itemProvider
    ↓ falls back to PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider
    ↓ reads AgenticPromptsService (which overrides findAgentSkills, listPromptFiles, etc.)
    ↓ returns IExternalCustomizationItem[]
    ↓ normalizer → widget
```

The cutover changes this to:

```
CLI Harness (WITH itemProvider from copilot-cli extension)
    ↓ getItemSource() sees itemProvider
    ↓ calls itemProvider.provideChatSessionCustomizations()
    ↓ extension returns IExternalCustomizationItem[] directly
    ↓ normalizer → widget
```

### What Changes

**Copilot CLI extension (or agent-host) registers its harness with an `itemProvider`:**

The extension already knows what customizations exist (it discovers them for its own runtime). Instead of letting VS Code rediscover them through `AgenticPromptsService`, it supplies them directly via the provider API:

```typescript
const cliProvider: IExternalCustomizationItemProvider = {
    onDidChange: cliCustomizationsChangedEvent,
    async provideChatSessionCustomizations(token) {
        return [
            // Built-in skills — no more runtime SKILL.md discovery
            { uri: builtinSkillUri('commit'), type: 'skill', name: 'commit', 
              description: 'Commit staged or unstaged changes...', groupKey: 'builtin', enabled: true },
            // ... all built-in skills statically listed
            
            // Workspace agents, skills, instructions — already discovered by the CLI runtime
            ...workspaceItems,
            
            // User-level items from ~/.copilot/
            ...userItems,
        ];
    }
};

harnessService.registerExternalHarness({
    ...createCliHarnessDescriptor(cliUserRoots, extras),
    itemProvider: cliProvider,
});
```

**The provider API already supports everything sessions needs:**

| Sessions Capability | Provider API Field | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Built-in skills with "Built-in" grouping | `groupKey: 'builtin'` | ✅ Supported |
| Skill UI integration badges ("Used by Run button") | `badge` + `badgeTooltip` | ✅ Supported |
| Instruction categories (agent/context/on-demand) | `groupKey: 'agent-instructions'` etc. | ✅ Supported |
| Instruction pattern badges ("always added", glob) | `badge` + `badgeTooltip` | ✅ Supported |
| Enabled/disabled state | `enabled: false` | ✅ Supported |
| Per-type fetching | `provideCustomizations(promptType)` | ✅ Supported (type-scoped) |
| Loading/error status | `status` + `statusMessage` | ✅ Supported |
| Hook expansion | Items returned pre-expanded (one per hook) | ✅ Supported |
| Session-scoped workspace filtering | Provider only returns items for active session | ✅ Provider controls scope |

### What Gets Deleted

Once the extension supplies an `itemProvider`, these become dead code **for the UI listing**:

| File / Override | Lines | Why It's Dead |
|---|---|---|
| `AgenticPromptsService.findAgentSkills()` override | ~40 | Extension lists skills directly |
| `AgenticPromptsService.listPromptFiles()` override | ~30 | Extension lists all files directly |
| `AgenticPromptsService.listPromptFilesForStorage()` override | ~15 | Extension uses `groupKey` for storage grouping |
| `AgenticPromptsService.getSourceFolders()` override | ~10 | Extension knows its own source folders |
| `discoverBuiltinSkills()` + `getBuiltinSkills()` | ~60 | Extension ships the skill list statically |
| `AgenticPromptFilesLocator` (most overrides) | ~80 | Extension scopes to session's project |
| Built-in skills YAML parsing loop | ~45 | No runtime discovery needed |

**Total: ~280 lines of discovery/listing code deleted from sessions.**

### What Survives

- **`AgenticPromptsService` base class** — still needed for **runtime prompt resolution** (actually executing prompts, resolving instructions at chat time). The UI listing is decoupled from runtime resolution.
- **`SessionsAICustomizationWorkspaceService`** — `activeProjectRoot`, `commitFiles()`, `deleteFiles()`, `skillUIIntegrations`. These are write operations and session management, not item discovery.
- **`SessionsCustomizationHarnessService`** — still registers the CLI harness, but now with an `itemProvider` attached.

### Migration Path

1. **Phase 1 lands** (PR #308626) — widget uses single pipeline, sessions still works via `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider` fallback
2. **Copilot CLI extension adds `itemProvider`** — extension registers harness with provider that supplies items directly
3. **Delete sessions discovery overrides** — `findAgentSkills`, `listPromptFiles`, `listPromptFilesForStorage`, `getSourceFolders` overrides, `discoverBuiltinSkills`, most of `AgenticPromptFilesLocator`
4. **Optional: delete `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider`** — once all harnesses use providers, the adapter becomes dead code (core VS Code workbench would also need a provider)

---

## Validation

- [ ] Phase 1: `npm run compile-check-ts-native` clean
- [ ] Phase 1: `npm run precommit` passes
- [ ] Phase 1: Static harness (core VS Code) — agents, skills, instructions, hooks, prompts all render correctly
- [ ] Phase 1: Provider harness — items from external provider render with correct grouping
- [ ] Phase 1: Sync harness — remote items + local sync checkboxes work
- [ ] Phase 2: Sessions window — built-in skills appear under "Built-in" group
- [ ] Phase 2: Sessions window — workspace items scoped to active session
- [ ] Phase 2: Sessions window — skill UI integration badges ("Used by Run button") display correctly
comments:
## Phase 1 update — merged as #309666

PR #309666 landed into `main` as `32f144f24ab`. Here's what we shipped and what's left.

### What we did

**Unified interfaces** — All three customization item producers now implement a single `ICustomizationItemProvider` / `ICustomizationItem` interface (common layer in `customizationHarnessService.ts`):

1. **`PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider`** — adapts `IPromptsService` for the default/static harness (core VS Code local path). This stays permanently.
2. **Extension API** (`mainThreadChatAgents2.ts`) — builds an `ICustomizationItemProvider` that proxies `$provideChatSessionCustomizations` back to the extension host (this is how Copilot CLI registers its harness).
3. **AHP remote** (`remoteAgentHostCustomizationHarness.ts`) — implements `ICustomizationItemProvider` for remote agent host sessions.

**Consolidated pipeline** — Four intermediate files merged into one `aiCustomizationItemSource.ts` (~400 lines):
- `IAICustomizationListItem` (view model)
- `IAICustomizationItemSource` (widget contract)
- `AICustomizationItemNormalizer` (`ICustomizationItem` → `IAICustomizationListItem`)
- `ProviderCustomizationItemSource` (merges provider + sync overlays)
- Shared utilities (`expandHookFileItems`, `getFriendlyName`, `isChatExtensionItem`)

**Eliminated lossy round-trip** — `ICustomizationItem` now carries `storage?: PromptsStorage` end-to-end. Previously the adapter stripped storage via `toProviderItems()`, then the normalizer re-inferred it from URI heuristics.

**Removed duck-typed interfaces** — All providers implement `provideChatSessionCustomizations()` (the one method). No more optional `provideCustomizations?` pattern.

**Widget simplified** — `aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` lost ~800 lines of dual-path fetch/normalize/group logic. It now consumes `IAICustomizationItemSource.fetchItems(promptType)` regardless of harness type.

### Known issues from council review (to fix in follow-up)

A 3-model council review (GPT, Opus, Gemini) identified these issues in the landed code:

| # | Severity | Issue | Detail |
|---|----------|-------|--------|
| 1 | 🔴 Blocking | **Double hook expansion → N² entries** | `fetchPromptServiceHooks()` already expands hooks, then `fetchItemsFromProvider()` calls `expandHookFileItems()` again. A file with N hooks → N² entries. Also strips the `storage` field during re-expansion. Only affects the default harness (most common path). (`promptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.ts:159-179`, `aiCustomizationItemSource.ts:348-352`) |
| 2 | 🟡 | **Missing "Agents" group** | Old `filterItemsForCore` had a dedicated `agents` group with `agentIcon` + localized label for hooks defined in agent files. Now falls through to dynamic group → generic folder icon + lowercase unlocalized "agents". |
| 3 | 🟡 | **Extension display name regression** | Normalizer uses raw extension ID from URI path instead of `extInfo.displayName`. Tooltips show `"github.copilot-chat"` instead of `"GitHub Copilot Chat"`. |
| 4 | 🟡 | **No unit tests** | 745 lines of new pipeline logic without test coverage. The N² bug would have been caught. |
| 5 | 🟡 | **Over-fetching** | `provideChatSessionCustomizations()` fires 5 parallel async calls (all types), then the caller filters to 1 type and discards 4/5. |

### Phase 2 status

Phase 2 (sessions cutover) is now unblocked. The widget no longer cares where items come from — it always routes through `getItemSource(descriptor) → fetchItems(promptType)`. Currently sessions uses the `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider` fallback (no `itemProvider` on the CLI harness descriptor). Once the Copilot CLI extension registers a harness with its own `itemProvider`, ~280 lines of `AgenticPromptsService` discovery overrides become dead code.

### Next steps

1. Fix the council review findings above (especially the N² hook expansion)
2. Sessions cutover — CLI extension supplies `itemProvider`, delete `AgenticPromptsService` listing overrides

title: `send_to_terminal` progress message repeats first question when accepting all defaults in interactive commands
description:
## Bug

When an interactive command like `npm init` is run and the user accepts all defaults via the `askQuestions` carousel, every `send_to_terminal` progress message displays the first question's text (e.g. "Package name?") instead of the correct question for each prompt.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a chat session in default approvals mode
2. Ask the agent to run `npm init` in a terminal
3. The agent detects the interactive prompts and shows an `askQuestions` carousel with multiple questions (package name, version, description, entry point, etc.)
4. Accept all defaults by leaving every field empty and submitting the carousel
5. The agent sends each default answer via `send_to_terminal` (pressing Enter for each prompt)

## Expected Behavior

Each `send_to_terminal` progress message should show the corresponding question:

- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: Package name?)
- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: version?)
- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: description?)
- …

## Actual Behavior

Every progress message repeats the first question:

- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: Package name? (default: @xterm/xterm))
- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: Package name? (default: @xterm/xterm))
- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: Package name? (default: @xterm/xterm))
- …

## Cause

`_getQuestionContextForTerminal` matches the sent command text against carousel answer values to find the corresponding question. When all answers are identical (empty string for defaults), the loop iterates questions in order and always matches the first one.

## Fix

Use positional matching: count prior `send_to_terminal` tool invocations after the carousel in the response parts to determine the question index, rather than relying solely on answer-value matching.

description: Testing #309111 Git Diff stats mismatch between Changes panel and chat UI

that was the only session; and reload doesn't help
It looks like the wrong one is the Changes Panel one (+39 -18) since the following diff shows -16 lines.

I am not really sure what we can do here as this boils down the differences in diff algorithms. The Changes panel shows the diff statistics as returned by `git diff` while the chat content part shows the diff statistics as it is being returned by the edit session service that uses the VS Code diff algorithm.

title: Not clear what the branch does wrt the prompt box + other customizations.
description: Testing #309304 If I want to edit the customizations, and I see a branch/worktree/workspace name in the corner, I'm going to typically operate on the assumption that the edits are only in that workspace, so it seems strange that the prompt box would always send to a chat rather than doing a behind the scenes distillation from prompt -> instruction file (more akin to how we run an agentic codereview in a "background" thread. Additionally, I continue to just have issue with the fact that the agent is incredibly bad at editing my user instructions. I more often want to write general agent guidelines that control the behavior of my agent as a whole, rather than repo specific tweaks. I always have to paste an absolute path into the chat to get that to work.
comments:
Clicking the branch name in the bottom also opens the OS file explorer which feels a bit jarring since it never opens quickly, and then I'm not entirely sure what I'm switching because the agents app will always show me all workspaces.

Perhaps since we already have the data on the sessions, we can just show a picker of the available workspaces with customizations via an app styled flyout?
Yea, this is just the active folder you're looking at (the worktree in this case). I am planning on removing this altogether as it's a bit confusing

title: OS toast notification shows wrong terminal command when multiple tool invocations in response
description:
## Bug

When a chat response contains multiple terminal tool invocations, the OS notification toast shows the command from the **first** terminal invocation (which may have already completed) rather than the one actually **waiting for confirmation**.

### Repro

1. Start a session where the agent runs multiple terminal commands sequentially
2. The first command (e.g. `git status --short`) completes and the agent moves on
3. A later command (e.g. a complex `npx playwright test ...` invocation) requires user confirmation
4. The OS toast notification fires but displays the first command (`git status --short`) instead of the pending one

### Cause

`_getPendingTerminalCommand` in `chatWindowNotifier.ts` iterates over all response parts and returns the first `toolInvocation` with `toolSpecificData.kind === 'terminal'` — it does not check whether that invocation is actually in `WaitingForConfirmation` or `WaitingForPostApproval` state.

### Fix

Filter to only match tool invocations whose state is `WaitingForConfirmation` or `WaitingForPostApproval`, consistent with what `_confirmAllow` already does.
comments: From @karthiknadig

title: Requests for many things
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

# Many things...

## Custom Themes
A feature that allows you to create custom themes by choosing hexadecimal colors using a selector and saving them (perhaps in a `.vscode-theme` file extension) for sharing or saving.

## Custom Languages
I'd also like a feature that allows you to create custom languages, where you can decide:

- What file extension they will have
- Their rules
- The icon (recommended with a transparent background)
- (Not mandatory) a required extension
- etc.

You can save these languages ​​(perhaps in a `.vscode-lang` file extension) for sharing or saving.

## VS Code Languages
I'd also like VS Code to be available in more languages ​​than just English. (Possibly include Italian as well.)
comments:
See our documentation. this can all be done with settings and extensions to VS Code
- custom themes: https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/themes
- languages https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/languages/overview
- translations https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/locales

title: Agents app on Linux prevents returning to classic VS Code experience
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** Yes

**VS Code Version:** latest insiders
**OS Version:** Ubuntu (Linux)

---

### Steps to Reproduce:

1. On Ubuntu Linux, open VS Code Insiders.
2. Use `Chat: Open Agents Application` command.
3. Another "VS Code Insiders" instance opens with the Agents UI.
4. Close out of all "VS Code Insiders" windows.
5. Reopen "VS Code Insiders".

**Expected:** Should return to classic VS Code experience.
**Actual:** Get Agents UI again, and VS Code logo in the title bar does not fix the problem.

Additional observations:
- Agents does not show in Ubuntu's "spotlight search" (as it does on macOS), suggesting it's not treated as a separate app on Linux.
- This prevents easily returning to the standard VS Code environment and is highly disruptive for Linux users.


[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C09MVCZKLP4/p1776098451267679?thread_ts=1776098451.267679&cid=C09MVCZKLP4)
comments: Note that we don't currently expect Linux to support a "spotlight search" experience, since we haven't done the Electron support for "sub apps" on Linux yet. But in the meantime, we should prevent and disruption to VS Code + Agents app usage on Linux.
I can go back to classic VS Code by clicking the VS Code button in the title toolbar.
An idea is to never disable the button even if no workspace is selected to allow to open an empty window.

title: [Unhandled Error] Cannot resolve canonical URI
description:
## Error Message
````
Cannot resolve canonical URI
````

## Stack Trace
    at o1.getCanonicalURI ([./src/vs/common/extensionHostManager.ts:420:0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b/src/vs/common/extensionHostManager.ts#L420))  
    at async pmt._getCanonicalURI ([./src/vs/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts:308:0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b/src/vs/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts#L308))

<details>
<summary>Raw Stack Trace (minified)</summary>

````
Error: Cannot resolve canonical URI     at o1.getCanonicalURI (out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:4962:64441)     at async Promise.all (index 0)     at async pmt._getCanonicalURI (out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:4978:3491)
````

</details>

## Details
| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Bucket ID | [`44704a8c-b683-12a7-7589-730efee7c7fe`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/44704a8c-b683-12a7-7589-730efee7c7fe?product=VSCode&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) |
| Version | 1.115.0 |
| Commit | [`41dd792b`](https://insiders.vscode.dev/github/microsoft/vscode/tree/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) |
| Last Seen | Sun Apr 12 2026 23:57:23 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) |
| Platforms | Windows, Mac, Linux |
| Product | VSCode |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*
comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error "Cannot resolve canonical URI" is thrown in `extensionHostManager.getCanonicalURI()` when the extension host proxy is `null` — meaning the extension host either failed to start or was disposed. This affects users with remote connections (SSH, WSL, containers, etc.) when any LocalProcess extension host has a null proxy. The error has existed at low levels since v1.110.0 but spiked in v1.115.0 stable due to the wider user base (2,681 hits / 1,395 users), with the per-user hit rate remaining roughly constant (~1.9 hits/user) across versions. ### Culprit Commit No specific culprit commit was identified. The issue body contained no commit range, and no regression scan comments were posted. The bucket history shows the error existed in v1.110.0-insider (1 hit), v1.114.0 (2 hits), and v1.115.0-insider (7 hits) before spiking in v1.115.0 stable (2,657 hits). The per-user rate is consistent across versions, indicating the spike is proportional to the larger stable user base rather than a new code change. The affected files (`extensionHostManager.ts`, `nativeExtensionService.ts`) had no relevant changes in the v1.115.0 cycle. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant WS as WorkspaceTrust / Terminal participant RAS as RemoteAuthorityResolverService participant NES as NativeExtensionService participant EHM as ExtensionHostManager WS->>RAS: getCanonicalURI(uri) RAS->>NES: _getCanonicalURI(authority, uri) NES->>NES: _getExtensionHostManagers(LocalProcess) NES->>EHM: Promise.all([extHost.getCanonicalURI()]) EHM->>EHM: await this._proxy Note over EHM: ⚠️ Root cause:
proxy is null (extension host
failed to start or was disposed) EHM-->>NES: 💥 throw Error("Cannot resolve canonical URI") Note over NES: Promise.all propagates
the rejection — even if
other hosts could resolve NES-->>RAS: Error propagated RAS-->>WS: Unhandled error ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/extensionHostManager.ts` (L417-422) | **Crash site** — throws when `this._proxy` resolves to null | | `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts` (L294-318) | **Error propagation** — `Promise.all` at L308 propagates the throw from any failed extension host | | `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/lazyCreateExtensionHostManager.ts` (L180-185) | **Alternate crash site** — same error thrown when lazy host was never created | | `src/vs/platform/remote/electron-browser/remoteAuthorityResolverService.ts` (L45-55) | **Entry point** — `getCanonicalURI` invoked by callers like workspace trust | | `src/vs/workbench/services/workspaces/common/workspaceTrust.ts` (L202-205) | **Trigger** — calls `getCanonicalURI` during trust evaluation for remote workspace folders | ### Repro Steps 1. Connect VS Code to a remote host (SSH, WSL, or Dev Container) 2. Have an extension host that fails to start (e.g., due to a crashing extension, resource exhaustion, or a timing issue) 3. Trigger a canonical URI resolution — this happens automatically during workspace trust evaluation on startup, or when opening an external terminal on a remote workspace 4. The `_getCanonicalURI` path queries all LocalProcess extension hosts via `Promise.all`; if any one has a null proxy, the error is thrown This is a race condition / resilience issue: the error occurs when at least one extension host is in a failed state while a canonical URI resolution is attempted. ### Fix Proposal The core issue is that `extensionHostManager.getCanonicalURI()` **throws** when the proxy is null, even though its return type is `Promise` (null is already a valid return value). The caller in `_getCanonicalURI` already handles null results gracefully (iterates results looking for non-null values). Two fixes should be applied: **Fix location 1**: `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/extensionHostManager.ts` → `getCanonicalURI()` **Approach**: Return `null` instead of throwing when `proxy` is null. This aligns with the method's return type `Promise` and with how other methods (e.g., `measure()` at L186-188) handle a null proxy. **Fix location 2**: `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/lazyCreateExtensionHostManager.ts` → `getCanonicalURI()` **Approach**: Same change — return `null` instead of throwing when `this._actual` is null. **Fix location 3 (defensive)**: `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts` → `_getCanonicalURI()` **Approach**: Wrap each `extHost.getCanonicalURI()` call in a catch that returns `null`, so `Promise.all` doesn't short-circuit on a single failure. This makes the code resilient even if individual extension host managers throw for other reasons. ### Recommended Owner `@alexdima` — area owner for extension host process, communication protocol, resolvers, and extension activation (`triage/working-areas.md`) > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24425015638/agentic_workflow) · ● 12.5M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)
