title: Add buttons for suggested tasks
description: Type: Feature Request Hi, Copilot is always saying after do something "Do you want me to x?". Could you just have an ok button to click in these instances? Be more efficient than typing something. Extension version: 0.43.0 VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Linux x64 6.19.10-200.fc43.x86_64 Modes:
comments: pls provide an example.

This is a simple example, but the time taken to focus the input, type something in, press enter. It could just be a button saying "Yes". Small improvement, but given it asks me this repeatedly, it will save time, context switching, and cognitive effort.
I think that in `Autopilot` and with certain models it would do better. Maybe we could add some prompting here, but this is really up to the agent.
Can you hover the response to see which auto model you're getting? Or grab the log? https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Copilot-Issues#language-model-requests-and-responses

It happens on most models, but I think I was using Claude Opus 4.6.  Actually. I did have something like what I am talking about when I asked the model to ask me for more information.  It then gave me a list of questions, with clickable boxes beneath them to choose different options.  If these simple "Yes" prompts could do the same thing, would be a time saver.

title: test: new confirmation carousel v1
description:
ref https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/306186

- [x] anyOS @mjbvz 
- [x] anyOS @Giuspepe 
- [x] anyOS @hawkticehurst 

Complexity: 2


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309330%0A%0A&assignees=justschen)

---

this iteration, we improved on the UI on batching tool calls in an effort to avoid having to click in a ton of different places when we get tool calls that require approval in parallel.

this now works on all confirmations, including single confirmations and subagent confirmations.

## Testing:
1. ensure that `chat.tools.confirmationCarousel.enabled` is enabled
2. trigger tool calls that require confirmation in parallel - this can be done by prompting the model in the user request. i've had success testing with something along the lines of the folloiwng:
```
use the list dir to search for vscode and vscode-copiolot chat folders outside of this workspace in parallel.
```
3. alternatively, you can prompt via extension:

```ts
const commands = [
{ command: 'echo "Terminal 1: Hello from first terminal"', explanation: 'First parallel terminal command', goal: 'Test parallel terminal 1' },
{ command: 'echo "Terminal 2: Hello from second terminal"', explanation: 'Second parallel terminal command', goal: 'Test parallel terminal 2' },
{ command: 'echo "Terminal 3: Hello from third terminal"', explanation: 'Third parallel terminal command', goal: 'Test parallel terminal 3' },
];

const invocations = commands.map(cmd =>
	vscode.lm.invokeTool('run_in_terminal', {
		input: {
			command: cmd.command,
			explanation: cmd.explanation,
			goal: cmd.goal,
			isBackground: false,
			timeout: 15000,
		},
		toolInvocationToken,
	})
);
```

4. test in both CLI in chat, the agents app, and local chat.
5. test accept all, skip, and allowing once for each tool!

title: Test: Artifact View — Rules, Subagent Sources, and Artifact Tools
description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309325

- [x] anyOS @isidorn
- [x] anyOS @dileepyavan

Complexity: 4

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309325%0A%0A&assignees=hediet)


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309326%0A%0A&assignees=hediet)

---

The artifacts view in chat surfaces important outputs (screenshots, plans, files) in a collapsible tree widget. Artifacts can come from multiple sources simultaneously:

- **Rules**: deterministic extraction from conversation content based on MIME-type patterns, file-path globs, and memory-file-path globs
- **`artifacts` tool**: the agent or subagent explicitly sets artifacts via tool call
- **`artifactRules` tool**: overrides the rules-based extraction config for the current session

All sources are merged and shown together, grouped by origin (rules, agent, each subagent).

## Setup

- Enable: `"chat.artifacts.enabled": true`
- Default rules are already configured — no extra settings changes needed for basic tests

---

## A. Rules-based artifact extraction

### 1. Screenshots from tool results (byMimeType)

- Default rule: `"image/*": { "groupName": "Screenshots", "onlyShowGroup": true }`
- In agent mode, ask the agent to take a screenshot using the integrated browser (e.g. "open https://example.com in the browser and take a screenshot")
- Verify the artifact widget appears with a "Screenshots" group
- Verify `onlyShowGroup: true` means only the group header is shown, not individual screenshot items
- Click the group — verify it opens the screenshot carousel

### 2. Change onlyShowGroup to false

- In settings, change the rule to `"image/*": { "groupName": "Screenshots", "onlyShowGroup": false }`
- Verify the artifact widget updates automatically — individual screenshot files should now be listed under the group

### 3. Plans from file writes (byFilePath)

- Default rule: `"**/*plan*.md": { "groupName": "Plans" }`
- Ask the agent to create a plan file on disk (e.g. "create a plan.md for refactoring the auth module")
- Verify the artifact widget shows a "Plans" group with the file listed
- Click the plan — verify it opens in the editor

### 4. Plans from memory writes (byMemoryFilePath)

- Default rule: `"**/*plan*.md": { "groupName": "Plans" }`
- Ask the agent to write a plan to memory (e.g. "save a plan.md to session memory with the refactoring steps")
- Verify the memory-written plan appears in the "Plans" group in the artifact widget
- Click the plan — verify it opens (resolves the memory file URI)

### 5. No artifacts if no rules match

- Clear all artifact rules in settings (set `byMimeType`, `byFilePath`, and `byMemoryFilePath` to `{}`)
- Verify the artifact widget disappears for existing conversation content (no rules match)

---

## B. `artifacts` tool (main agent)

### 6. Agent sets artifacts via tool

- Ask the agent to set artifacts explicitly (it should call the `artifacts` tool)
- Verify artifacts appear in the widget
- Ask the agent to update the artifact list — verify the list is overwritten (not appended) on each tool call

### 7. URI validation

- Have the agent set an artifact with a `file://` URI that doesn't exist
- Verify an error is thrown: "Artifact URI does not exist"
- Set an artifact with an `https://` URI — verify it succeeds (no existence check for HTTP)

---

## C. Subagent artifacts via `artifacts` tool

### 8. Two subagents set artifacts in parallel

- Ask the agent: "Run two subagents in parallel — each should research a different topic and save their findings to `results.md`, then set it as an artifact"
- Verify each subagent's artifacts appear in a separate group in the artifact widget, labeled with the subagent's name
- Verify both groups are visible simultaneously

### 9. Clear subagent artifacts

- With subagent artifacts showing in the widget, look for a clear/dismiss action on a subagent group
- Click it — verify only that subagent's artifacts are removed, not the other sources

---

## D. `artifactRules` tool

### 10. Override rules via tool call

- The agent calls the `artifactRules` tool with custom rules, e.g.:
  ```json
  { "byMimeType": { "text/html": { "groupName": "Web Pages" } }, "byFilePath": { "**/*.css": { "groupName": "Stylesheets" } } }
  ```
- Verify the session now uses these overridden rules instead of the settings-based ones
- Verify previously produced and newly produced artifacts follow the overridden rules

title: Test: Model Parameter in Run Subagent Tool
description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/298380

- [x] anyOS @federicobrancasi
- [x] anyOS @kycutler

Complexity: 3

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23298380%0A%0A&assignees=hediet)


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309322%0A%0A&assignees=hediet)

---

The `runSubagent` tool now accepts an optional `model` parameter that lets the LLM (or a user via system prompt) request a specific model for the subagent invocation. The model is specified as a qualified name like `"Model Name (Vendor)"`.

## Setup

- Use VS Code Insiders with Copilot
- Have multiple models available in the model picker (e.g. GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet 4.6)
- Agent mode in chat

## 1. Subagents use different models

- Open a chat in agent mode
- Ask the agent: "Run two subagents — one with GPT-4o (copilot) and one with Claude Sonnet 4.6 (copilot). Each subagent should report which model it is running on."
- Verify each subagent runs successfully
- Verify the two subagents report different models corresponding to what was requested

## 2. Explicit model overrides agent-configured model

- Create a custom agent `.md` file with a specific model configured (e.g. `model: GPT-4o (copilot)`)
- Ask the LLM to invoke that agent via subagent but explicitly set `model` to a different model (e.g. `"Claude Sonnet 4.6 (copilot)"`)
- Verify the explicit `model` parameter takes precedence over the agent's configured model

## 3. Multiplier constraint — model not more expensive than main model

- Select a cheaper model as your main chat model (e.g. GPT-4o Mini)
- Ask the agent to "use a subagent with model GPT-4o (copilot)" or another model with a higher multiplier
- Verify the tool returns an error message explaining the model exceeds the current model's cost tier

## 4. Unknown model name error

- Ask the agent to use a subagent with a model name that doesn't exist (e.g. `"NonExistent Model (copilot)"`)
- Verify a clear error message: `Requested model 'NonExistent Model (copilot)' not found.`

## 5. No model specified — falls back to main model

- Run a subagent without specifying any model parameter
- Verify the subagent uses the same model as the parent conversation
comments: Verified.
Overall works well!

title: Independent Rendering Setting for Tabs and Spaces
description: Provide the ability to independently toggle the rendering of tabs and spaces. Currently, `editor.renderWhitespace` treats all whitespace characters as a single group, which can lead to excessive visual noise in specific formatting styles. As shown in the attached screenshot, I use tabs for logical indentation and spaces for character-precise alignment (e.g., aligning function arguments across multiple lines). When `editor.renderWhitespace` is set to `all`, the alignment spaces create a dense field of dots that are uncomfortable to look at. An option to selectively render tabs and spaces would solve this. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/558b05d1-9b38-4ad0-a252-97afa13bee6b)
comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->

If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

title: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd
description:
﻿### Problem

VS Code injects `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at an early position in the integrated terminal's process PATH. This causes `code` to resolve to `Code.exe` (the GUI binary) instead of `code.cmd` in the `bin\` subfolder.

Every `code` CLI invocation (`code --version`, `code --list-extensions`, etc.) opens a **new VS Code window** instead of executing in the terminal.

### Version

- VS Code: 1.115.0 (system setup)
- OS: Windows 11 (10.0.26100)
- Not running as Administrator
- Compatibility Run as admin is NOT set on Code.exe

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install VS Code system-wide (default: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code`)
2. System PATH correctly contains only `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin`
3. Open VS Code integrated terminal (PowerShell)
4. Run: `env:PATH -split ';' | Where-Object { _ -match 'VS Code' }`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at position 2
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin` at position 33
5. Run: `Get-Command code | Select-Object Source`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe` (wrong!)
6. Run: `code --version` - opens a new VS Code window instead of printing the version

### Expected Behavior

`code` should resolve to `code.cmd` in `bin\`, which sets ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 and runs cli.js correctly.

### Root Cause

VS Code prepends its installation directory (containing Code.exe) to the terminal's process PATH, shadowing the correct `bin\code.cmd`.

### Impact on Copilot Agent Mode

GitHub Copilot agent mode uses run_in_terminal to execute `code --list-extensions` and similar CLI commands. Each invocation spawns a new VS Code window without the user's consent. Especially disruptive during automated multi-step tasks.

### Workaround

In settings.json:
`terminal.integrated.env.windows`: { `PATH`: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin;env:PATH` }

This prepends bin\ so code.cmd takes priority over the injected Code.exe.

### Note

This is NOT the admin/elevated terminal issue (#91613, fixed in 1.110). This occurs in a normal non-elevated terminal session.

title: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd
description:

Every `code` CLI invocation (`code --version`, `code --list-extensions`, etc.) opens a **new VS Code window** instead of executing in the terminal.

### Version

- VS Code: 1.115.0 (system setup)
- OS: Windows 11 (10.0.26100)
- Not running as Administrator
- Compatibility Run as admin is NOT set on Code.exe

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install VS Code system-wide (default: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code`)

4. Run: `env:PATH -split ';' | Where-Object { _ -match 'VS Code' }`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at position 2
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin` at position 33
5. Run: `Get-Command code | Select-Object Source`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe` (wrong!)
6. Run: `code --version` - opens a new VS Code window instead of printing the version

### Expected Behavior

`code` should resolve to `code.cmd` in `bin\`, which sets ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 and runs cli.js correctly.

### Root Cause

comments: @scheilch Could not repro on my windows machine with windows powershell set as default and delegating to agent to run the commmand. Do you have this under Program Files instead of App Data because of company policy? Potentially related to: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/300231 https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/303021 https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/302026 /cc @dmitrivMS

@anthonykim1 Thanks for looking into this. Let me provide more detail:

**Yes, this is a system-wide installation** under `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` — standard enterprise deployment (DATEV company policy, installed via system setup, not user setup).

**The issue is specifically about PATH injection, not admin/elevation.** Here's what the integrated terminal's PATH looks like right now:

```
[2] C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin      ← my workaround (settings.json)
[3] C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code           ← INJECTED by VS Code (the problem)
[34] C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin      ← system PATH (correct)
```

**Without my workaround**, position `[3]` (the injected installation root containing `Code.exe`) comes before position `[34]` (`bin\` with `code.cmd`). Result: `Get-Command code` resolves to `Code.exe` → every `code --version` opens a new window.

**To reproduce:**
1. Install VS Code via **system setup** (not user setup) → installs to `Program Files`
2. Do NOT add the workaround to settings.json
3. Open integrated terminal
4. `$env:PATH -split ';' | Select-String 'VS Code'` — you'll see the installation root injected before `bin\`
5. `Get-Command code` → resolves to `Code.exe`

**The linked issues are different:**
- #300231, #303021, #302026 are about elevated/admin terminals — I'm running as a normal user, no elevation
- This is purely about VS Code prepending the installation **root** directory (which contains `Code.exe`) to the terminal's process PATH, shadowing the correct `bin\code.cmd` from the system PATH

**Key question:** Why does VS Code inject `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` (the root, containing the GUI binary) into the terminal PATH at all? Only `bin\` should be there.

This particularly impacts **Copilot Agent Mode**, which uses `run_in_terminal` to execute `code --list-extensions` and similar commands — each invocation spawns a new VS Code window.

@meganrogge Thanks for the thorough initial triage and for adding the correct labels (chat-terminal, windows). Really appreciated that you recognized this as a legitimate terminal PATH issue affecting Copilot agent mode.

IMHO it is duplicate of #300231 (it have nothing about elevated/admin terminals as far as I can see). Did you try workaround mentioned here and it not work for you?

```
rem For System install
reg delete "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\App Paths\code.exe" /v Path
rem For User install
reg delete "HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\App Paths\code.exe" /v Path
```

> IMHO it is duplicate of [#300231](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/300231) (it have nothing about elevated/admin terminals as far as I can see). Did you try workaround mentioned here and it not work for you?
> 
> ```
> rem For System install
> reg delete "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\App Paths\code.exe" /v Path
> rem For User install
> reg delete "HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\App Paths\code.exe" /v Path
> ```

I don’t know if this issue is a duplicate but as the creator of [#300231] I can confirm that it does not involve any elevation. The issue happened as a normal user.

This issue affects per-user installation as well. In my case, `AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code` has precedence over `AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin` in PATH, making `code` invocation resolve to `Code.exe` isntead of `code.cmd`.

title: Automatically update in Administrator in Windows
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

1. I always working on Administrator.
2. I want user-scope installation, I don't want system wide installation because I don't need to install vscode for other users.
3. I want VSCode automatically update.

Now it pops up 
```
Updates are disabled because you are running the user-scope installation of Visual Studio Code as Administrator.
```
Every time it stats up. And auto-update doesn't work, I have to download the user-scope installer and manually install it on each new version. 

Please add a option or something can bypass this restriction.
comments: @liyafe1997 Do you have UAC disabled on your machine?

> @liyafe1997 Do you have UAC disabled on your machine?

No UAC for Administrator

I am working on enabling automated updates for both system and user installs when UAC is disabled - that seems doable since everything is elevated already in those scenarios.

The problem with running user install as administrator with UAC enabled is we would be potentially applying admin ACLs in files which would then become unusable from a regular user account/process.

If you always work as administrator, why not disable UAC?

> The problem with running user install as administrator with UAC enabled is we would be potentially applying admin ACLs in files which would then become unusable from a regular user account/process.

I understood what you mean. I think you are considering about if a `User under Administrators`, with UAC enabled, in this case something called `Admin Approval Mode` takes effect, when a user run a program it will has no admin permissions unless click `Run As Administrator`

But when you login directly with the built-in Administrator account, you will always have admin permissions and UAC takes no effect in this case I think, equal to `UAC disabled`.

In this case, no permission/ACL problem because it always has admin permissions. And as you said, since everything ls elevated and also it is a `user install` so it will not impact other users. In this case, the auto-update should works, but VSCode disabled it and no way to enable it.
I see your point, let me think more about it.

title: Undo history for repository search (Ctrl Shift F)
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Repository-wide search (accessed via Control + Shift + F, or H for replace) does not track its own undo/redo history. Code repositories are huge and crawling them using search is an essential part of any programmer's flow. I often have to track more than one possible lead in the code, and right now I do that with a separate document open in notepad++ for my queries, since search does not try to remember any past searches I've made.

I think it would be good to record unique searches that a user makes. Some ideas of when: interacting with search results, resizing the pane (to see search results), on focus change, etc. The history should be retained at least as long as the search page is continuously open.
comments:
you can use the up/down arrows
to cycle through previous searches

same for ctrl+f find
Oh. I was trying ctrl+z, not terminal keybinds. Thanks! closing

title: Automatically update in Administrator in Windows
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

1. I always working on Administrator.
2. I want user-scope installation, I don't want system wide installation because I don't need to install vscode for other users.
3. I want VSCode automatically update.
comments: We offer a user installation of VS Code on our download page: https://code.visualstudio.com/Download

Yes, but automatically doesn't work on Administrator 
Even it pops up 
```
Updates are disabled because you are running the user-scope installation of Visual Studio Code as Administrator.
```
Everytime it starts up

title: Updating changes
description:
Type: Bug Hello VSCode team, I'd like to report a problem. I've been using the "Kilo Code" extension for a while and never had any issues. But today, when I asked it to explain some code, the code file didn't update. I tried again and again, and when I explained the problem to the artificial intelligence, I received a response that "the file had indeed updated." I don't know if the problem is with the AI ​​or with VSCode, which updated the information for the AI ​​but not for me. I await a response and, if possible, a solution to my problem. Thank you. title: Agents: thread can disappear immediately while background agent keeps running and editing the workspace
description:
## Summary

In **VS Code Insiders** with **GitHub Copilot Chat / Agents**, a newly-created agent thread can disappear from the visible Agents/Sessions UI almost immediately, while the background worker keeps running and continues making real workspace changes.

This is not a "task stopped" problem. The background work continues; the **UI-visible thread is what disappears**.

## Expected

- The thread should stay visible while the agent is running.
- If persistence partially fails, the thread should remain **recoverable** in the Sessions list instead of silently vanishing.
- A persistence/index failure should either block background launch or surface a visible broken/recoverable session state.

## Actual

- The visible agent thread disappears almost immediately after creation.
- The agent still appears to continue in the background and makes file edits / workspace changes.
- The user loses the visible conversation/thread even though the task is still executing.

## Repro pattern

This has been intermittent, but the pattern is:

1. Open a normal workspace in **Agents - Insiders**.
2. Start an agent task that performs actual file edits / multi-step work.
3. Very soon after creation, observe that the visible thread disappears from the Agents/Sessions UI.
4. Despite that, the workspace continues changing, indicating the background worker is still alive.

## Sanitized local evidence

I am intentionally **not** attaching raw agent transcripts, prompts, repository contents, or personal file paths because they contain private working context.

What I can share safely:

- The background worker clearly continued after the UI thread disappeared, because workspace files changed after the visible thread was gone.
- On the affected machine, lightweight session/debug records for the visible session path were missing or nearly empty, while a separate background/session-state event stream continued to show activity.
- This strongly suggests a divergence between **runtime/background agent execution** and the **persisted UI session record / index**.

## Likely area

This looks related to chat session persistence / restore / index handling, likely in or around:

- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatSessionStore.ts`
- possibly related session creation / visibility wiring in chat service / controller code

## Why I think this belongs with existing chat-session persistence bugs

This symptom looks closely related to other cases where chat sessions are lost, malformed, or missing due to persistence/index issues.

Related issues / PRs:

- #308369 — Copilot Chat session history intermittently disappears
- #308730 — malformed `chatSessions` JSONL makes sessions unloadable
- #307348 — incomplete in-flight session data makes sessions unloadable
- #296890 — parallel agent writes can corrupt session JSONL
- #305109 — PR fixing session index loss during workspace transitions
- #308921 — PR fixing another `ChatSessionStore` flush/persistence bug

## Suggested fix direction

I suspect the product needs one or more of the following:

1. **Durably persist the session base record + index entry before background agent work begins**
   - If that write fails, do not silently launch a background worker that the UI cannot recover.

2. **Do not drop sessions entirely when persistence is incomplete or malformed**
   - Keep a visible/recoverable placeholder item in Sessions instead of making the thread disappear.

3. **Harden malformed/incomplete session recovery**
   - If a session log is incomplete or partially malformed, salvage what is valid instead of treating the session as missing.

4. **Regression tests**
   - disappearing session while background work continues
   - incomplete/malformed session data not removing the session from the visible list
   - any index/flush gap involved in agent-session visibility

## Environment

- VS Code: `1.116.0-insider`
- GitHub Copilot Chat extension/runtime observed version: `0.44.2026041005`
- OS: macOS
- Feature area: Agents / agent mode / session persistence

I can provide **redacted** logs if needed, but I do not want to upload raw prompts, task transcripts, or project-specific details.
comments:
Draft PR opened: #309286

This PR is a small, targeted hardening change in `ChatSessionStore`: if incremental session serialization fails, VS Code now falls back to writing a full snapshot instead of aborting the write and leaving the session non-durable.

I kept the report sanitized and did not attach raw agent transcripts, prompt contents, project names, or personal file paths.

comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

This likely root cause of the error is overpopulating of chat request with images in BASE64 format, which has been fixed in this PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308901.

The fix is available in latest VS Code **Insiders** and **Stable** version **1.116** and beyond.
Please update to this (or later) version and verify if the issue is resolved.

If you still can reproduce the error with **413 response** code in chat, please ping me with the following information:
- VS Code version your are running
- What language model/version are you using?
- What was the chat doing immediately before it responded with the error?
- If there were images the chat was inspecting, how many were there and what were their sizes?

title: VS Code Bug Report: Renderer Crash (SIGSEGV 11) when rendering certain emojis in Shell Scripts
description:
Type: Bug # VS Code Bug Report: Renderer Crash (SIGSEGV 11) when rendering certain emojis in Shell Scripts ## Description VS Code window terminates unexpectedly (crashed, code 11) when opening a file containing specific emojis (e.g., 🔊, 🔇). The crash is reproducible even with extensions disabled and appears to be related to the `PNGReadPlugin` in the renderer process on macOS. ## Environment - **VS Code Version:** 1.115.0 - **OS Version:** macOS 14.1 (23B74) - **Architecture:** x64 (Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-6300U) ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Create a shell script (e.g., `test.sh`) with the following content: ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash echo "🔊 Unmuted" echo "🔇 Muted" ``` 2. Open the file in VS Code. 3. The window crashes immediately with the message: `The window terminated unexpectedly (reason: 'crashed', code: '11')`. ## Expected Behavior The file should open normally, and the emojis should be rendered correctly. ## Actual Behavior The renderer process crashes with a segmentation fault (SIGSEGV 11). ## Diagnostic Information A macOS diagnostic report (`.ips`) identifies the crash in the `CrRendererMain` thread. ### Crash Summary - **Exception Type:** `EXC_BAD_ACCESS (SIGSEGV)` - **Exception Subtype:** `KERN_INVALID_ADDRESS` - **Termination Signal:** `Segmentation fault: 11` - **Faulting Thread:** `CrRendererMain` ### Relevant Stack Trace (from `Code Helper (Renderer)`) ``` 0 com.apple.ImageIO 0x7ff817886c67 PNGReadPlugin::InitializePluginData(IIOImageReadSession*, IIODictionary*, IIODictionary*, CGImageMetadata*, CGColorSpace**, ReadPluginData&, PNGPluginData&, __CFDictionary*) + 869 1 com.apple.ImageIO 0x7ff8178858fc PNGReadPlugin::initialize(IIODictionary*) + 62 2 com.apple.ImageIO 0x7ff81787db8f IIOReadPlugin::callInitialize() + 407 3 com.apple.ImageIO 0x7ff81787d66a IIO_Reader::initImageAtOffset(CGImagePlugin*, unsigned long, unsigned long, unsigned long) + 78 4 com.apple.ImageIO 0x7ff817872e16 IIOImageSource::makeImagePlus(unsigned long, IIODictionary*) + 704 5 com.apple.ImageIO 0x7ff81787fe7f IIOImageSource::createImageAtIndex(unsigned long, IIODictionary*) + 81 6 com.apple.ImageIO 0x7ff81787fb55 CGImageSourceCreateImageAtIndex + 325 7 com.apple.CoreText 0x7ff81432f693 (anonymous namespace)::TCGImageData::TCGImageData(TFont const*, unsigned short, TsbixContext const&, double, CGPoint) + 1735 8 com.apple.CoreText 0x7ff8143340e2 DrawGlyphsAtPositions(TFont const*, unsigned short const*, CGPoint const*, unsigned long, CGContext*, (anonymous namespace)::DrawGlyphsConfiguration const&)::$_11::operator()(CGAffineTransform, CGAffineTransform) const + 230 9 com.apple.CoreText 0x7ff8143309bb DrawGlyphsAtPositions(TFont const*, unsigned short const*, CGPoint const*, unsigned long, CGContext*, (anonymous namespace)::DrawGlyphsConfiguration const&) + 3583 10 com.apple.CoreText 0x7ff8142d9f80 CTFontDrawGlyphs + 315 ``` ## Workaround Removing the emojis from the file prevents the crash. VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Darwin x64 23.1.0 Modes:

title: Test: AI Generated branch/worktree names for Copilot CLI
description: Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/306191 - [x] anyOS @bamurtaugh - [x] anyOS @hediet Complexity: 2 [Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309280%0A%0A&assignees=DonJayamanne) --- ## AI gnerated branch names for Copilot CLI **Enable this setting `github.copilot.chat.cli.aiGenerateBranchNames.enabled` by setting it to `true`** Note: This feature is `off` by default (release vscode after changing) 1. Open a VS Code folder (such as VS code repo or any other) 2. Open a new Chat Session 3. Select `Copilot CLI` chat agent 4. Ensure you select the `worktree` option (see screenshot) 5. Send a prompt such as `explain the contents of this repo`, `explain the contents of xyz.ts`, `fix the tests of xyz.test.ts` Verify the branch (worktree) thats created is not a random name, but something thats meaningful and aligned with the prompt See below screenshot for sample name thats generated based on the prompt (previously we'd generate branches named after some animals)
comments: Works! Just have to reload the window after setting it
Thanks , yes didn’t want this to be responsive to config changes as it’s only used to control feature for now

title: Constanly failed to create/edit long file
description:
Type: Bug Constantly failed with this error: Sorry, there was a network error. Please try again later. title: Test: Reasoning in Copilot CLI
description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/304641

- [x] anyOS @joshspicer
- [x] anyOS @aeschli

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309276%0A%0A&assignees=DonJayamanne)

---

## Thinking Effort Level in Model Picker for Copilot CLI

(same as https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/303685, but this is specific to Copilot CLI)

**Enable/Disable this setting `github.copilot.chat.cli.thinkingEffort.enabled` by setting it to `true/false`**
Note: This feature is `on` by default

A new **Thinking Effort** submenu has been added to the model picker for models in Copilot CLI

1. **Picker Works:**
   - Ensure you are using Copilot CLI agent (not Local)
   - Select a reasoning model (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4); verify a ">" arrow appears on the picker item
   - Click the arrow; verify a "Thinking Effort" submenu shows effort levels for that model
   - Select an effort level; verify the picker label updates (e.g., "Claude Sonnet 4.6 · High")
   - Select a non-reasoning model (e.g., GPT-4.1, GPT-4o); verify no submenu appears
   - Try a few different Anthropic and OpenAI models
   - Available levels vary per model (e.g., some have None, some skip Medium)

2. **Effort Reaches the Backend:** 
- First locate the `<home>/.copilot/session-state` folder
- Rename this folder to something else e.g. `session-state-backup`
This will temporarily remove all of your Copilot CLI sessions (you can just restore this folder to get all of your sessions back)
- Now start a session with a specific thinking/reasoning level
- Identify the new session created in this folder
- Look for the file `events.jsonl` and look for a row in this file that contains an entry similar to the following
```
"selectedModel":"claude-sonnet-4.6","reasoningEffort":"medium"
```
- As you send more requests with different model/reasoning levels, you'll get new entries in this file with different event, verify the latest entry contains the correct model and reasoning effort.
comments: I am seeing the requests sent with the same reasoning effort in the UI. I also see the `session.model_change` events in the log ✅

title: Remote SSH: extension host terminated on session switch kills background processes (regression)
description:
## Issue

After updating to the latest VSCode (server commit `41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b`), switching between extension panel sessions on Remote SSH causes the **entire extension host to be terminated**, killing all child processes (exit code 143 / SIGTERM).

Previously (server commit `e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036`, ~April 1), the extension host survived session switches and background processes continued running.

## Environment

- **Client**: VSCode (latest stable, April 2026)
- **Remote**: macOS Tahoe 26.3.1 (Mac Mini, arm64) via Remote SSH over Tailscale
- **Extension affected**: Claude Code (`anthropic.claude-code`) — but the issue is at the extension host level, not extension-specific

## Steps to reproduce

1. Connect to a remote machine via Remote SSH
2. Open an extension panel that spawns a long-running child process (e.g., Claude Code session)
3. Start a task in that session
4. Switch to a different session/tab in the same panel
5. The previous session's process receives SIGTERM (exit code 143)

## Logs

Extension host log shows the termination is initiated by the renderer on every switch:

```
Extension host terminating: received terminate message from renderer
  at cP.terminate (extensionHostProcess.js:505:12344)
  at i.terminate (extensionHostProcess.js:507:1072)
Extension host with pid XXXXX exiting with code 0
```

Claude Code extension log shows the channel being forcefully closed:

```
Closing Claude on channel: hrzqndz3grv
Error spawning Claude (on channel hrzqndz3grv): Error: Query closed before response received
Failed to load config cache: Error: Query closed before response received
```

## Frequency

In a single day session: **6 extension hosts created** (exthost1 through exthost6), each terminated when a new one started. On the previous server version (same usage pattern), extension hosts survived and coexisted.

## Comparison

| Server commit | Date | Extension hosts/day | Background processes survive? |
|--------------|------|---------------------|-------------------------------|
| `e7fb5e96` | Apr 1 | 1-2 | Yes |
| `41dd792b` | Apr 12 | 6 | **No** |

## Possibly related

- #305240 — Extension host shutdown hang regression in 1.113.0 (fixed in 1.114.0)
- #306129 — Duplicate of above
- microsoft/vscode-remote-release#11546 — Extension host terminated with "renderer closed the MessagePort"

The 1.114.0 fix for #305240 worked correctly (server `e7fb5e96`). The new server (`41dd792b`) appears to have re-introduced aggressive extension host termination on Remote SSH.
comments:
**Update: root cause was on our side, not VSCode.**

After further investigation, the SIGTERM (exit code 143) was caused by a custom `SessionStart` hook script that killed all Claude Code processes except the newest one. When switching sessions, the new session's startup hook would terminate the previous session's process.

The VSCode Server update was a red herring — the behavior would have occurred on any version.

Closing this as not a VSCode bug. Apologies for the noise.

title: Feature request: New-install option for queue-first command processing
description:
# Change Ticket: Microsoft VS Code/Copilot Chat — Queue-First Command Processing Config For New Installs

**ID:** DEV-2026-0412-001
**Date:** 2026-04-12
**Priority:** High
**Type:** Enhancement / Default Configuration
**Status:** Open
**Requested by:** Tom B.
**Assigned to:** Microsoft VS Code + Copilot Chat Product Team

---

## Problem

Current command execution workflows are often stop-and-wait (steering style), which slows users who run repeatable operations in sequence. Teams need queue-first behavior where users can stack command inputs and keep processing without manual pauses between each command.

For new installs, this should be configurable as a default behavior so organizations can standardize operations from day one.

## Request

Add a product-level configuration option for new installs that allows administrators/users to choose:

- `queue-first` (recommended): users can stack command requests and execution continues by default.
- `steering-first`: one-by-one interactive stop-and-confirm behavior.

This should be available during first-run/onboarding and as a persistent setting in product configuration.

## Desired Behavior

1. New install includes an explicit setup choice for command processing mode.
2. Queue-first mode supports batching/stacking multiple commands in one run.
3. Queue-first mode continues processing after individual item failures by default.
4. Optional fail-fast mode is available for strict pipelines.
5. Setting is org-policy compatible (for managed environments) and user-overridable when policy allows.
6. UI clearly displays active mode (`Queue` vs `Steering`) and current queue status.

## Why This Matters

- Reduces operator wait time and context switching.
- Improves reliability for repeatable SDLC workflows.
- Aligns with automation-first usage patterns in enterprise environments.
- Prevents accidental workflow drift where some users run queueing and others run one-by-one.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] First-run install flow includes command processing mode selection.
- [ ] Settings page includes persistent toggle/enum for processing mode.
- [ ] Queue-first supports stacking multiple commands without stopping between items.
- [ ] Queue-first default behavior continues after failures; fail-fast is opt-in.
- [ ] Managed policy setting can enforce queue-first for enterprise tenants.
- [ ] Documentation includes migration guidance from steering-first to queue-first.

## Proposed Configuration Shape

Example (illustrative):

```json
{
  "copilot.commandExecution.mode": "queue",
  "copilot.commandExecution.continueOnError": true,
  "copilot.commandExecution.failFast": false
}
```

## Implementation Notes

- Preserve current behavior as backward-compatible fallback where needed.
- Add telemetry to compare completion rates/time-to-complete between modes.
- Provide clear warning text when switching modes for users with existing workflows.

## External Filing Targets

- VS Code repo issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues
- Copilot Chat extension repo (if feature belongs there): https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-release/issues

## Business Impact

Queue-first default for new installs can materially increase throughput for engineering and operations teams that run repeated command pipelines, and lowers training overhead by establishing a consistent baseline behavior.
comments: This already exists.

title: Test: Show historical sessions in Agent Debug Panel
description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309261

- [x] anyOS @anthonykim1

Complexity: 3




[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309262%0A%0A&assignees=vijayupadya)

---
## Agent Debug Log
To open the Agent Debug Logs panel:

- Enable the following setting:

       `github.copilot.chat.agentDebugLog.FileLogging.enabled`


- Create a chat session and send few messages in chat
- Select the ellipsis (...) menu in the Chat view and select **Show Agent Debug Logs**.
- Navigate to home page by clicking on the 'Agent Debug Logs' root item in the navigation link

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8fa56a8d-1b7e-49a4-a87c-6c844f5d6640)

- You should see old sessions listed upto 5 items with 'Show more' button
- Click on 'Show more', ensure it loads 5 more (display capped at 15 items currently)
- Select any of the old sessions
- Click "View Logs" → log list shows events with timestamps, names, details.
- Click "Agent Flow Chart" → flowchart renders with nodes for model turns, tool calls, subagents
- Send a message and verify events appear — verify new events, model turn, tool calls etc appear
- Navigate back to Debug panel home page
- Start a new chat session and send few messages in chat
- Check the new session title appears in the Debug panel, navigate to it and check events show up in logs view

title: Agent App session change/diff view should have branch changes option as well
description: Type: Feature Request In Copilot CLI I can `/diff` and then `b` to switch to a diff view of all changes in the branch. This is super useful for reviewing changes holistically. I would love if the Agents app diff view also had the ability to diff the whole branch and not just the session changes and leave comments on those. VS Code version: Agents - Insiders 1.116.0-insider (47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0, 2026-04-10T20:25:41+09:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26220 Modes:
comments:
> 🔍 **Updated understanding of the problem**

After investigating further, I think the original issue description was incomplete. The deeper problem is:

**Sessions started in the Copilot CLI don't have diff/changes tracking when viewed in the Agents app.**

When you start a session in the CLI (e.g. Copilot CLI), the CLI manages its own git operations — commits, branches, file edits. But when the Agents app later connects to that session (via "Open in Agents App" or by browsing sessions), the session doesn't have:
- `repositoryPath` or `baseBranchName` in its metadata
- Checkpoint refs (`firstCheckpointRef`, `lastCheckpointRef`)
- Tracked file changes in `session.changes`

This means the Changes view shows "Initialize Repository" and is empty — even though the branch has real changes.

### What we actually want

The Agents app should be able to show diffs for **any session on a branch**, regardless of whether the session was started in the CLI or the Agents app. Specifically:

1. **For CLI-originated sessions**: The Agents app should detect the workspace git context (current branch, base branch) and populate the session metadata so the Changes view can show diffs.
2. **"Full Branch Diff" mode**: Even without per-session checkpoint tracking, we should be able to show `git diff baseBranch...HEAD` — all changes on the branch vs its base. This doesn't require session-specific tracking at all, just git context from the workspace.

comments:
log
[0df6bd4f.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26648339/0df6bd4f.md)

I give them instructions to avoid making mistakes as much as possible, but they still make mistakes.
And it seems the agents don't understand why they made the mistakes.

Random tool selection by AI agents is a known and under-appreciated problem. The agent has access to multiple tools, and in some contexts it picks the wrong one — not because of a misunderstanding, but because the tool-selection heuristics have no deterministic guardrail.

What makes this particularly hard to debug:

1. **Non-deterministic by nature**: The same prompt can produce different tool selections across runs, making it impossible to reliably reproduce
2. **Instruction-following gap**: The agent "knows" it should use `askQuestions` last, but the tool-selection layer doesn't always respect that ordering constraint
3. **Cascade effects**: Once the wrong tool is selected, the subsequent execution path diverges from the intended workflow, and the user sees unexpected behavior

This connects to a broader pattern we see in AI-generated code: the agent selects the right general area but the wrong specific implementation. For example, calling a deprecated overload instead of the current API, or importing from a subpath that doesn't exist. The intent is correct, but the execution is off by one layer of specificity.

The most effective mitigation we've found is deterministic validation of agent outputs: regardless of which tool the agent selected, verify the result against the expected contract. This catches both wrong-tool and right-tool-wrong-usage scenarios.

For CI pipelines with AI-generated code: npx @opencodereview/cli scan . --sla L1 validates agent outputs against objective criteria, independent of tool selection.
It looks like the askQuestions tool was not enabled in this run

> It looks like the askQuestions tool was not enabled in this run

If the AI ​​saw this log, it would say the same thing as you.
VS Code features, including askQuestions, are randomly unloaded. This log has indeed been unloaded.
It is mandatory to call activate_vs_code_interaction before calling askQuestions.
However, if not called, the agent will either say "not found" or will always call vscode_renameSymbol.
The call is successful on lines 3262 and 3689, but otherwise it calls vscode_renameSymbol.
For now, I'll create a hook and see how it goes with ask and additional context.

I've discovered something new.
If renameSymbol is disabled, vscode_listCodeUsages is called.

### I had Copilot conduct the investigation.

---

I did a bit more testing and this may not be specific to `renameSymbol`.

### Additional observation

When I disable the rename tool, the agent starts calling `CodeUsages` instead.

Because of that, I’m not sure the issue is only that `renameSymbol` is being selected incorrectly. It may be that, in some cases, the agent is not choosing `askQuestions` when clarification is still needed, and is instead selecting one of the available code-related tools.

### Possible explanation

The custom agent instructions indicate that it should use `askQuestions` for clarification, but from this behavior it seems possible that this is not always reflected strongly enough in tool selection.

The behavior appears to be something like:

1. The request is still ambiguous
2. Instead of calling `askQuestions`, the agent selects a code-related tool
3. If `renameSymbol` is available, it may choose that
4. If `renameSymbol` is disabled, it may fall back to `CodeUsages`

If that interpretation is correct, the issue might be broader than a single tool and could be related to tool selection / prioritization rather than only the rename tool itself.

### Why this might matter

This could mean:
- `askQuestions` is available, but not always preferred when clarification is needed
- code-navigation/refactoring tools may sometimes be considered too early
- disabling one incorrectly selected tool may just cause another similar tool to be selected instead

### Possible direction to investigate

It might be worth checking whether ambiguous requests should more strongly prefer `askQuestions` before tools like `renameSymbol` or `CodeUsages` are considered.

From my testing, this may be a broader tool-selection behavior rather than only a `renameSymbol` problem.

title: Weekly updates are too frequent
description:
Type: Performance Issue I appreciate that the VS Code team is working at a breakneck pace, but so is my team. Updates take time. I just lost an hour because VS Code kept crashing because some components auto-updated to 1.115 before I was able to update the core. I haven't updated the core because it seems I constantly have an agent or compilation or test suite or such running in one of VS Code's windows. Finally I had to discard what I was working on to complete the upgrade so the crashes would stop. If I wanted weekly updates to my CORE development tool, I would sign up for insider. My time is valuable, my development environment is valuable, please limit the instability of releases to once a month. title: Git Hub Feature/Bug/ MCP server
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Great insight on both counts.

## Link to VS Code Feature Request

Open a new issue here:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new

You can use the template they provide and paste the feature request content we drafted.

---

## Git Hub Feature/Bug/ MCP server

**You're absolutely right.** This should be an MCP server capability.

Instead of:
1. Drafting the feature request
2. Opening GitHub manually
3. Copying/pasting content

You should have:
```
@MCP-GitHub-Liaison "Tell the VSCode team about this workspace-root-context feature"
```

And the MCP server would:
- ✅ Accept the feature request (structured or prose)
- ✅ Format it properly (GitHub issue template, markdown conventions)
- ✅ Authenticate to GitHub (via OAuth or PAT)
- ✅ Submit the issue on your behalf
- ✅ Return the issue URL
- ✅ Track status/responses

---

## Why This Pattern Matters

You've identified something fundamental: **Repetitive external communication should be abstracted as MCP tools.**

Right now you're doing:
- Manual GitHub opens
- Copy-paste drafting
- Manual submission

But that should be:
- One MCP tool call
- Automatic formatting
- Automatic submission
- Tracking built-in

**This is exactly the use case MCP servers were designed for** — offloading routine external operations to automated tools.

Do you want me to sketch out what a `vscode-feature-request-liaison` MCP server would look like? Or is this the kind of thing your Configuration Manager or ContentTraker MCP could handle?
comments:
VS Code already have Report issue (which also has an option to put feature request) built-in and doesn't rely on having subscription/burning tokens to request feature.

Please describe what functionality is missing there except tracking (you are automatically subscribed to your opened GitHub issues however)?

P.S. Did you intentionally just pasted LLM output with instructions for user(you)?

title: Open settings in the main window instead of a modal window by default
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Hello. Some time ago I updated my copy of Visual Studio Code (up to 1.114.0) and quickly noticed, that settings page appears to be a modal window now. Previously it was appearing as a tab in the main window. 

I use a 13'' laptop, and seeing all this space around the modal being wasted instead of used for displaying settings contents (which is very narrow now), is a frustrating user experience. There's also a button that reopens the modal as a tab in the main window, but I find it disturbing to use a button every time for a functionality that was a convenient default behavior previously.

Is there a way to make settings appear as a tab in the main window by default?
comments: try `workbench.editor.useModal`
It indeed worked. Thank you for the help.

title: Renaming opened folders
description: Continuation of #20071 (the workaround in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/20071#issuecomment-366490178 does not work anymore) and #171022. VSCode lets you rename files and subfolders, so the inability to rename folders seems very arbitrary and limiting. It means that once you've opened a folder in VSCode you're pretty much stuck with it.
comments:
@lramos15 so you're just gonna close it, no explanation? Don't Microsoft pay you guys crazy money to develop this program, for you to then just close it as a dupe and point *to the literal issue I linked in the issue description"?

You didn't even bother to check, did you?

title: Extension allowlist by publisher fails for remote extensions after version upgrade due to missing publisherDisplayName metadata
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.96
- OS Version: macOS (client), Linux (remote via SSH)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure GPO policy `extensions.allowed` with a publisher display name entry: `{ "microsoft": true }`
2. Connect to a remote (SSH/WSL/container) — extensions install and work correctly
3. Upgrade VS Code to a new version
4. Reconnect to remote — Microsoft-published extensions are now blocked

---

## Problem

When using the `extensions.allowed` GPO policy to allow extensions by publisher display name (e.g. `"microsoft": true`), remote extensions are blocked after a VS Code version upgrade with "not in the allowed list" errors. Local extensions remain unaffected.

## Root Cause

`AllowedExtensionsService.isAllowed()` resolves the publisher lookup key using `publisherDisplayName` from extension metadata:

```ts
// allowedExtensionsService.ts:126
const publisherKey = publisherDisplayName && this.publisherOrgs.includes(publisherDisplayName)
    ? publisherDisplayName : publisher;
```

- When `publisherDisplayName` is present (e.g. `"Microsoft"`), it matches the GPO entry `"microsoft"`.
- When `publisherDisplayName` is `undefined`, it falls back to the raw `publisher` from the extension ID (e.g. `"ms-vscode-remote"`), which does **not** match `"microsoft"`.

`publisherDisplayName` is only written to `package.json` `__metadata` during gallery installs (`extensionManagementService.ts:1087`). When a VS Code server upgrade migrates or reinstalls remote extensions without going through the gallery flow, this metadata is lost.

## Expected

Remote extensions should remain allowed after upgrade if they were allowed before.

## Suggested Fix

Any of:
- Preserve `__metadata` (including `publisherDisplayName`) during remote extension migration
- Re-fetch gallery metadata after migration to backfill the field
- Have the publisher fallback in `AllowedExtensionsService` also check `extensionPublisherOrgs` against the raw publisher ID prefix, not just the display name

## Workaround

Use full extension IDs in the allowlist (`"ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh": true`) instead of publisher display names. Extension ID matching doesn't depend on metadata.
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

Thank you for the detailed report. Could you please share the content of the `extensions.json` file on the remote side where the `publisherDisplayName` is read from? 

Also, could you clarify why you believe there is a migration happening? The `publisherDisplayName` should always be present if the extension was installed using VS Code or the VS Code CLI.

title: Save and Resume Copilot Chat Sessions
description: Type: Feature Request Problem: Copilot Chat conversations are lost when VS Code is closed or restarted. For complex, multi-session development work spanning days or requiring system restarts, users must re-explain context from scratch, wasting time and tokens. Proposed Solution: Add ability to save and resume chat sessions, similar to Gemini CLI's session management: Save current conversation state (full message history, context, file references) Resume saved sessions after restart Optional: Auto-save on close with prompt to resume on next launch Current Workarounds (Insufficient): /memories/ - Requires manual summarization, doesn't preserve conversation flow /memories/session/ - Wiped on restart Manual copy-paste - Loss of rich context (file links, tool outputs) Use Cases: Multi-day feature development System restarts during long debugging sessions Switching between projects without losing progress Pausing complex work to address urgent issues Restarting VSC to elevated permissions context Expected Behavior: User can checkpoint conversation at any point and restore full context later, maintaining continuity across sessions. VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:
comments: How is this different exactly from what we do today? You can quit vscode, reopen, resume your past sessions from the list, etc

I might be using it incorrectly, but it seems to capture the previous sessions at breakpoints of it's choosing.  I just reloaded and 45 mins of context was not captured when I did the reload from "Sessions."  If it is a knowledge gap on my part, it wouldn't be the first time.

title: Agent started accessing local files as web urls file:///
description:
Type: Bug Randomly, the agent stopped using local file access tools and started accessing files by its web tools, such as `file:///x/y/z/`. This asked me for permission ofcourse, but if the user accidentally allows all web urls such as `file:///*` this could mean that a malicious AI may circumvent protections that are in place for normal tool access, such as accessing files outside the workspace or accessing sensitive environment files. title: Frequent “Compacting conversation” loops causing extreme delays with reasoning models (Claude Sonnet 4.6)
description:
Type: Bug When using reasoning models such as Claude Sonnet 4.6, the system repeatedly enters a “Compacting conversation” state multiple times during a single task. This significantly increases response time, even for small or simple tasks. The issue results in unnecessary delays and disrupts workflow efficiency. Steps to Reproduce Open VS Code with AI agent enabled Select Claude Sonnet 4.6 (or similar reasoning model) Provide a small or medium-sized coding task Observe the execution flow in the agent panel Expected Behavior Task should complete in a reasonable time Minimal or no repeated “compacting conversation” steps Smooth, continuous execution Actual Behavior Frequent repeated messages: “Compacting conversation” Multiple unnecessary iterations before actual progress Significant delay in completing even small tasks Sometimes feels stuck in a loop before proceeding Impact Slows down development workflow Makes reasoning models inefficient for quick tasks Increases latency unnecessarily Additional Context Happens consistently with reasoning models (not observed as frequently with faster models) Occurs even when the conversation context is not large Seen during file reads, multi-step reasoning, and task execution VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Terminal: Missing devicePixelRatio listener causes misalignment on zoom
description:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.115.0
- OS Version: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code and open the integrated terminal in the panel.
2. Change the editor's zoom level using the keyboard shortcuts (`Ctrl` + `+` or `Ctrl` + `-`).
3. **Observe the bug:** The terminal canvas fails to repaint and align correctly with the new zoom dimensions. Depending on the zoom direction, the prompt text gets severely clipped at the top, disappears entirely out of the viewport bounds, or renders with excessive blank space above it. The inline suggestion ghost text also detaches and floats in the wrong position.
4. Switch to a different panel tab (e.g., "PORTS" or "OUTPUT") and switch back to "TERMINAL".
5. **Observe the workaround:** Forcing this tab switch triggers a re-render, causing the terminal canvas to properly recalculate its dimensions and fix the alignment.

### Testing on `main` build
I also tested existence of this issue on my fork by building on `main` , issue still persists.

**Media:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65040f0f-a5e0-48d2-a6b4-102141965f5c


### PR Ready
**Note to maintainers:** I have already investigated the root cause of this issue (the terminal instance is missing a listener for `window.devicePixelRatio` / zoom level changes, causing it to use stale dimensions for the canvas renderer until a manual layout refresh is triggered). **I have a Pull Request ready to go that implements this fix and will link it to this issue immediately after opening.** I would love to contribute to vscode given the opportunity!
comments:
Hey @pwang347 I have submitted a PR which resolves this issue through adding :

Two event listeners in the `TerminalInstance` constructor:

- **`PixelRatio.onDidChange`** — fires when `devicePixelRatio` changes (e.g. browser zoom, display scale)
- **`onDidChangeZoomLevel`** — fires from Electron's zoom API (e.g. `Ctrl++`/`Ctrl+-`)

title: ⁠[Copilot CLI][macOS][v1.0.24] Copilot not opened — “Apple could not verify ‘copilot’ is free of malware”
description: ## Summary On macOS, the Copilot CLI fails to open and shows an Apple security verification error: > “Copilot Not Opened. Apple could not verify ‘copilot’ is free of malware… [Move to Bin]” This appears to be related to code signing / notarization / malware verification for the Copilot CLI binary, not a VS Code issue. ## Environment - Copilot CLI Version: 1.0.24 - OS Version (macOS): Tahoe 26.4 (25E246) - CPU architecture: Apple Silicon (M1 Pro) ## Expected behavior Copilot CLI should open successfully (no macOS verification / malware warning) and Copilot features should work normally. ## Actual behavior Copilot CLI does not open. macOS blocks the binary with an Apple verification / malware-free check failure. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Install / update Copilot Chat extension to version: `1.0.24` via `brew upgrade copilot-cli` 2. Execute `copilot` is terminal(iTerm2 or Terminal) 3. Observe the Apple dialog: “Copilot Not Opened… Apple could not verify ‘copilot’ is free of malware…” ## Logs / Evidence * Screenshot (already attached) of the Apple dialog Add the following: - Full error text as shown in the dialog (exact wording): "Apple could not verify “copilot” is free of malware that may harm your Mac or compromise your privacy." - File path of the Copilot CLI binary: `/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/copilot-cli/1.0.24/copilot` ## Suspected cause - [ ] Code signing missing or invalid for this Copilot CLI binary version - [ ] Notarization missing/expired - [ ] macOS Gatekeeper/quarantine attribute issue - [ ] Binary mismatch for architecture (Intel vs Apple Silicon) - [ ] Other (describe): ## Workarounds tried - [X] Allow the binary in macOS Security settings (if attempted) `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /opt/homebrew/bin/copilot` worked for now.
comments: This is not a VS Code issue, thus closing

## Regression Details

- **Product**: Agents
- **Regression type**: New error
- **Last release without this error**: [1.116.0-insider (fc6d18c3)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/f84bb2a1-2eb8-e678-164d-6f7e0a4da293?product=Agents&commit=fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)

- **Commit range**: [fc6d18c3...e4f6e114](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a...e4f6e1148528f4e6fba4bf58e4a5f28539135156)

### Deminified Stack (top frames)

1. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts:176`
2. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/reactions/autorunImpl.ts:110`
3. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/reactions/autorunImpl.ts:167`
4. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/transaction.ts:102`
5. `acceptAgentEdits` — `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts:245`
6. `_acceptEdits` — `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts:1032`

The error occurs during agent edit acceptance in `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts` when `getRequests` is called on an undefined object. 16 users affected across Windows and Mac.

### Related Bucket

Bucket `5e789596` (11 users) has the same `getRequests` error but through a different code path (`chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts` → `_notifyAction` → `acceptStreamingEditsEnd`), suggesting a shared root cause where the chat model is disposed before edits complete.

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24272022562/agentic_workflow) · ● 5.9M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24272022562, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24272022562 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getRequests')` occurs when a `ChatResponseModel` is disposed (nullifying its `_session` back-reference) while the `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry` autorun still holds a reference to it. When the autorun fires (triggered by a transaction in `acceptAgentEdits`), it accesses `res.session.getRequests()` on the disposed response model where `session` is now `undefined`. This affects agent edit streaming scenarios where the chat session is torn down while edits are still in-flight. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`7c7259ff`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/7c7259ff50bc50689c167874b24fce9b5e698501) | | Author | `@roblourens` | | PR | #308638 | | Message | pref: Break references at disposal to limit the impact of leaks | | Why | This commit introduced `this._session = undefined!` in `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` to break retention cycles for GC. However, the observable autorun in `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts` and `_createUndoRedoElement` both access `res.session.getRequests()` without null-guarding `session`, which was never `undefined` before this change. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent as Agent Edit Stream participant Session as ChatEditingSession participant Entry as ModifiedDocumentEntry participant Response as ChatResponseModel participant ChatModel as ChatModel Agent->>Session: _acceptEdits(resource, edits, isLastEdits) Session->>Entry: acceptAgentEdits() Entry->>Entry: transaction: _waitsForLastEdits.set(true) Note over ChatModel: ⚠️ Session disposed concurrently ChatModel->>Response: dispose() Note over Response: _session = undefined! Entry->>Entry: autorun fires (transaction commit) Entry->>Response: _lastModifyingResponseObs.read(r) Response-->>Entry: res (disposed, session=undefined) Entry->>Response: res.session.getRequests() Note over Entry: 💥 Cannot read properties
of undefined (reading 'getRequests') ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | Root cause — `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` nullifies `_session` | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts:176` | Crash site — autorun reads `res.session.getRequests()` without null guard | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts:236` | Secondary crash site — `_createUndoRedoElement` also reads `response.session.getRequests()` unguarded (related bucket `5e789596`) | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts` | Owns `_lastModifyingResponseObs` observable that retains the stale response reference | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts` | Calls `acceptAgentEdits` which triggers the transaction/autorun | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (1.116.0+, commit `e4f6e114` or later) 2. Start a chat editing session with an agent (Copilot) that produces file edits 3. While the agent is actively streaming edits to a file, close the chat panel or dispose the chat session (e.g., by starting a new session, navigating away) 4. The race between the session disposal (nullifying `_session`) and the edit acceptance transaction committing (triggering the autorun) produces the error 5. The error is non-deterministic — it depends on the timing of session disposal relative to edit streaming ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts` → autorun at line 172 and `_createUndoRedoElement` at line 236 **Approach**: Add optional chaining when accessing `session` on the response model, since it can now be `undefined` after disposal: - **Line 176**: Change `res.session.getRequests()` → `res.session?.getRequests()` (with appropriate fallback when session is undefined — skip the `resourceFilter` installation) - **Line 236**: Change `response.session.getRequests()` → `response.session?.getRequests()` (use the fallback label when request can't be found) Additionally, consider checking `this._isDisposed` or the response model's disposed state in the autorun before accessing the session, to avoid any other potential null reference issues from the dispose-time cleanup. ### Recommended Owner `@roblourens` — culprit commit author (PR #308638) who introduced the `_session = undefined!` disposal pattern. The followup PR #308653 also by Rob extended the same pattern, confirming familiarity with this area. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24272264391/agentic_workflow) · ● 3.2M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)
Already fixed in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/309093

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error "Session type cannot be changed" is thrown when a user clicks on the session type picker in the Agents chat UI and selects the currently-active (enabled) session type. The `setSessionType` method on `CopilotChatSessionsProvider` unconditionally throws because session type switching is not implemented, but the `onSelect` handler in the picker calls it without guarding against the no-op case (selecting the already-active type). ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`ef3548d7`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/ef3548d7980a3e44c00505cf4214b4787d96f473) | | Author | `@sandy081` | | PR | #308770 | | Message | fix #308675 | | Why | This PR changed the session type picker to show **all** provider types (with unsupported ones disabled) instead of only supported ones. Before this change, the picker was hidden when only one type was supported, so users could never trigger `setSessionType`. After the change, the picker opens and the currently-active type is enabled/clickable, but clicking it still calls `setSessionType` which always throws. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User as User (click picker) participant Picker as SessionTypePicker participant Mgmt as SessionsManagementService participant Provider as CopilotChatSessionsProvider User->>Picker: Selects enabled session type Picker->>Picker: onSelect(type) Note over Picker: ⚠️ No guard: doesn't check
if type === current type Picker->>Mgmt: setSessionType(session, type) Mgmt->>Provider: setSessionType(sessionId, type) Note over Provider: 💥 throw new Error(
'Session type cannot be changed') ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts` | Root cause — `onSelect` handler calls `setSessionType` without checking if type is already active | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | Crash site — `setSessionType()` throws unconditionally (line 1188) | | `src/vs/sessions/services/sessions/browser/sessionsManagementService.ts` | Passthrough — routes `setSessionType` to provider (line 270) | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product, 1.116.0+) 2. Start a new session (Copilot CLI or Cloud) 3. Click the session type picker in the chat input area 4. Click the currently-active (enabled) session type in the dropdown 5. Error "Session type cannot be changed" is thrown as an unhandled error ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts` → `_showPicker()` → `onSelect` handler **Approach**: Add a guard in the `onSelect` callback to skip calling `setSessionType` when the selected type is already the current session type. The handler at line 101–103 should check `if (type.id !== this._sessionType)` before calling `sessionsManagementService.setSessionType(session, type)`. This prevents the no-op case from reaching the provider's throwing implementation. An alternative/complementary fix would be to make `CopilotChatSessionsProvider.setSessionType` return the existing session when the requested type matches the current type, rather than throwing unconditionally. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Author of the culprit commit (PR #308770) and primary maintainer of the sessions management and session type picker code. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24272231156/agentic_workflow) · ● 7.5M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)
