<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24272022562, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24272022562 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary `awaitStatsForSession()` in `chat.ts` crashes with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'entries')` due to a TOCTOU (time-of-check-to-time-of-use) race condition. The function checks that `model.editingSession` exists, then awaits `chatEditingSessionIsReady()`, but by the time the await resolves, the `ChatModel` has been disposed and `model.editingSession` has been set to `undefined`. This affects 30 users (56 hits) on the Agents product in Insiders 1.116.0. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`7c7259ff`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/7c7259ff50bc50689c167874b24fce9b5e698501) | | Author | `@roblourens` | | PR | #308638 | | Message | pref: Break references at disposal to limit the impact of leaks | | Why | This PR added `this._editingSession = undefined` in `ChatModel.dispose()` (line 2839 of `chatModel.ts`) to break GC retention chains. This causes `model.editingSession` to become `undefined` when a `ChatModel` is disposed while `awaitStatsForSession()` is mid-await, exposing a latent TOCTOU race condition. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Listener as addModelListeners() participant Refresh as refresh() participant Stats as awaitStatsForSession() participant Dispose as ChatModel.dispose() Listener->>Refresh: onDidCreateModel → refresh() Refresh->>Stats: chatModelToChatDetail() → awaitStatsForSession(model) Stats->>Stats: ✅ Check: model.editingSession !== undefined Stats->>Stats: await chatEditingSessionIsReady(session) Note over Dispose: ⚠️ Model disposed concurrently Dispose->>Dispose: this._editingSession = undefined Stats->>Stats: model.editingSession.entries.get() Note over Stats: 💥 TypeError: Cannot read properties
of undefined (reading 'entries') ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chat.ts` | **Crash site** — `awaitStatsForSession()` at line 47 accesses `model.editingSession.entries` after an await without re-checking | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | **Root cause** — `dispose()` sets `this._editingSession = undefined` (line 2839, added by PR #308638) | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts` | **Data flow** — `chatModelToChatDetail()` calls `awaitStatsForSession(model)`, `getLiveSessionItems()` iterates all session models | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/localAgentSessionsController.ts` | **Trigger** — `addModelListeners()` calls `refresh()` on model creation, which iterates all models including ones being disposed | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) with multiple chat sessions that have editing sessions 2. Create a new chat session (triggers `onDidCreateModel` → `addModelListeners` → `refresh`) 3. While `refresh()` is computing stats for existing sessions, one of those sessions gets disposed concurrently 4. The race window is between the `editingSession` null-check (line 42) and the post-await access (line 47) in `awaitStatsForSession()` > This is a race condition — it's non-deterministic and depends on the timing of model disposal relative to the `chatEditingSessionIsReady` await. Having multiple active agent sessions with editing sessions increases the likelihood. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chat.ts` → `awaitStatsForSession()` **Approach**: Capture `model.editingSession` in a local variable before the await, then use the local variable throughout the function. This eliminates the TOCTOU race because the local variable holds a strong reference to the editing session object regardless of whether `model.editingSession` is later set to `undefined`: ```typescript const editingSession = model.editingSession; if (!editingSession) { return undefined; } await chatEditingSessionIsReady(editingSession); await Promise.all(editingSession.entries.get().map(...)); const diffs = editingSession.entries.get(); ``` Alternatively, add a re-check after the await: `if (!model.editingSession) { return undefined; }` before line 47. However, the local variable approach is preferred as it's more robust against future changes. ### Recommended Owner `@roblourens` — culprit commit author (PR #308638) and original author of `awaitStatsForSession` (PR #281088) > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24272230990/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## Related Error Buckets

This additional error bucket was found in the Agents product for this issue (v1.116.0-insider, build 47add6d):

| Bucket | Error Message | Version |
|--------|--------------|--------|
| [`90fe2cc1`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/90fe2cc1-b95b-a50f-ad3a-a02b152cac53?product=Agents) | Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'entries') | 1.116.0-insider |

This bucket was observed on the Apr 10 insider build (`47add6d`), which predates this issue being closed (Apr 12). The error likely resolves on post-fix builds.

title: Long conversation jumps to beginning when scrolling down
description:
Type: Bug Ever since the "Show more" / "Show less" feature became active on VS-Code (for long user queries/prompts), I can no longer easily scroll up or down a (long) conversation well. When I try to scroll, it often jumps to the very beginning of the conversation. That makes it quite difficult to use VS-Code effectively. (Edit: Unsure whether something with the "Show more" / "Show less" feature is the root cause or not) -- A seemingly related issue that began at seemingly the same time is that the "up arrow" no longer allows me to scroll-up a bit at a time anymore, the only way I can go up to press below the "up arrow" which often jumps the conversation further up than desired. The "down arrow" on the scroll bar still works as it should (minus the "jumping back to the conversation" bug noted above often times when I scroll up/down now). --- Possibly relevant info: VS-Code Version: 1.114.0 Commit: e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036 Date: 2026-04-01T09:27:11Z Electron: 39.8.3 ElectronBuildId: 13658728 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Linux x64 6.17.0-19-generic snap / Ubuntu 24.04 VS Code version: Code 1.114.0 (e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036, 2026-04-01T09:27:11Z) OS version: Linux x64 6.17.0-19-generic snap Modes:
Extensions (1) Extension|Author (truncated)|Version ---|---|--- chatgpt|ope|26.406.40811
comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

Updated to VS-Code release 1.115.0 

The issue still exists (within a Codex conversation on VS-Code, in case that matters).

If I had to take a guess based on the behaviour I'm seeing, then it might be that there's some sort of issue when I scroll down and longer messages (prompts and/or responses) are loaded. The interaction of that with the "Show more / Show less" feature, perhaps.

> Generated by [errors-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24268154808/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24268154808, workflow_id: errors-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24268154808 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sessionResource')` occurs when a chat list template's event listener accesses `sessionResource` on a disposed chat element whose backing model's `session` reference has been nullified. This was introduced by a memory-leak mitigation commit that aggressively broke back-references during disposal, but downstream code (the chat list renderer) still held references to the old view model elements and accessed them during view model updates.

**A fix has already been merged** in PR #309093 (commit `6cf846ce`, Apr 10 2026), which reverts the session-nullifying behavior on `ChatResponseModel`.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`7c7259ff`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/7c7259ff) |
| Author | `@roblourens` |
| PR | #308638 |
| Message | pref: Break references at disposal to limit the impact of leaks |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant VP as ChatViewPane
    participant CW as ChatWidget
    participant LW as ChatListWidget
    participant LR as ChatListRenderer
    participant RVM as ChatResponseViewModel
    participant RM as ChatResponseModel

    Note over VP: User switches sessions
    VP->>CW: setModel(newModel)
    Note over CW: Old ChatModel disposed
    CW->>RM: dispose()
    Note over RM: _session = undefined! (PR #308638)
    CW->>LW: setViewModel(newVM)
    LW->>LR: updateViewModel(newVM)
    LR->>LR: fire _onDidUpdateViewModel
    Note over LR: Template listener checks currentElement.sessionResource
    LR->>RVM: get sessionResource
    RVM->>RM: get session.sessionResource
    Note over RM: session is undefined! Cannot read sessionResource
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | Root cause: `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` and `ChatModel.dispose()` null out `_session` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatViewModel.ts` | Crash site: `ChatResponseViewModel.sessionResource` getter (line 523) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatListRenderer.ts` | Trigger: template listener at line 611 accesses disposed element's `sessionResource` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` | Data flow: `setModel()` disposes old model before updating list widget |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatListWidget.ts` | Data flow: `setViewModel()` propagates to renderer |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (version 1.116.0, commit `1ec46de1` or similar)
2. Start a chat session and send at least one message (to create response elements)
3. Switch to a different chat session (e.g., open a new chat, or switch via the session picker)
4. The old `ChatModel` is disposed (nullifying `_session` on responses/requests)
5. The renderer fires `_onDidUpdateViewModel`, and a template still referencing an old element crashes when accessing `sessionResource`

> The error is timing-dependent: it occurs when template elements from the old session are still mounted when the view model update event fires.

### Fix Proposal

**Already fixed** in PR #309093 (commit [`6cf846ce`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/6cf846ce), merged Apr 10 2026).


**Remaining risk**: `ChatModel.dispose()` still sets `(r as any)._session = undefined` on **request** models (line 2826). If a template's `currentElement` is a `ChatRequestViewModel`, the same crash pattern could occur via `ChatRequestViewModel.sessionResource` (line 411). A similar guard or removal may be needed for request models.

**Additional hardening**: The listener in `chatListRenderer.ts:611` could defensively guard against disposed elements by adding a try-catch or checking element validity before accessing `sessionResource`.

### Recommended Owner

`@roblourens` - authored both the culprit commit (#308638) and the fix (#309093), and is the primary contributor to the chat model lifecycle code.

title: Agents App: Fetched web content empty
description: The tool call for fetched web content shows nothing when expanded
comments:
@sbatten I just noticed an interesting behavior, that appears to occur in both VSC and Agents app:

1. While the current turn is active, expanding a `Fetched web content` call will show nothing (as you called out)
2. Once the turn is over, then the tool call should become `Fetch Web Content`, and when you expand it, you should see the contents

I'd be curious to confirm if you see point 2 as well.

That said, @DonJayamanne: is this a known behavior of our Copilot CLI integration? Since this doesn't appear to be something that the Agents app is doing differently.
@lostintangent confirmed that after coming back to the session (turn was over) contents were present

title: CURRENT_MILLISECOND
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

I like how a snippet can insert [`CURRENT_SECOND`](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editing/userdefinedsnippets#_variables:~:text=CURRENT_SECOND%20The%20current%20second%20as%20two%20digits), I'd also like current millisecond. Can `CURRENT_MILLISECOND` be added as well please?

It would be defined as "the current millisecond as three digits".

Example use case: Entering current ISO 8601 formatted date-time:

```json
{
	"Insert DateTime": {
		"prefix": "dt",
		"body": [
			"${CURRENT_YEAR}-${CURRENT_MONTH}-${CURRENT_DATE}T${CURRENT_HOUR}:${CURRENT_MINUTE}:${CURRENT_SECOND}.${CURRENT_MILLISECOND}${CURRENT_TIMEZONE_OFFSET}"
		]
	}
}
```

---

A `CURRENT_MILLISECONDS_UNIX` could also be helpful defined as "the number of milliseconds since the Unix epoch" (similar to [`CURRENT_SECONDS_UNIX`](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editing/userdefinedsnippets#_variables:~:text=CURRENT_SECONDS_UNIX%20The%20number%20of%20seconds%20since%20the%20Unix%20epoch) but with millisecond precision).
comments: Adding support for `CURRENT_MILLISECOND` would remove a need for an extension for some use cases (e.g. https://github.com/jsynowiec/vscode-insertdatestring/issues/35).

<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->

If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

title: Bug Report: False "The terminal is awaiting input" message (but terminal is working fine or finished, no need to input anything)
description: Copilot Chat frequently and incorrectly shows the following message in the chat panel, even when the terminal is either still running a command or has already finished — with no user input required at all: > *"The terminal is awaiting input. Please provide the required input to the terminal."* A **"Focus terminal"** button is displayed alongside this message. This behavior is confusing and disrupts the workflow, as it suggests user action is needed when it is not. Sometimes when I don't do nothing, the message disappeares on its own and the chat continues. Sometimes I need to press on "Focus terminal", where I see a finished successful shell execution. In that terminal I press Enter out of confusion - and chat continues. ### Steps to Reproduce: I get this unexpectedly several times a day at several opportunities, but I couldn't figure out any guaranteed reproduction yet. I'll keep my eyes open to provide a reproduction. ### Screenshots: ### Expected Behavior Copilot should correctly detect when a terminal command is still running normally OR has completed - and proceed accordingly, without prompting the user for input when none is required. ### Actual Behavior Assumption: Copilot's terminal output monitoring (`run_in_terminal` / `get_terminal_output`) fails to recognize that the command has finished or is still working fine. It incorrectly assumes the terminal is waiting for user input and displays the misleading message. --- ### Workaround When command already finished, Pressing **Enter** in the terminal window "wakes up" Copilot and allows the chat to continue. When command is still running and I don't do anything, the chat continues sometimes without me need to click on Focus terminal or pressing enter in the terminal. --- ### Additional Context This appears to be related to the following known issues: - #284133 - microsoft/vscode-copilot-release#13592 - microsoft/vscode-copilot-release#12701 The issue seems to be in the `chat-terminal-output-monitor` component. It has been reproduced across multiple OS environments and shell types. - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0 - VS Code Version: Version: 1.115.0 (user setup) Commit: 41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b Date: 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z Electron: 39.8.5 ElectronBuildId: 13703022 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Local agent mode - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Sonnet 4.6

title: MCP: POST SSE handler doesn't filter empty priming events, causing "Failed to parse message" warning
description:
## Description

When connecting to an MCP server over Streamable HTTP, VS Code logs repeated warnings:

```
[warning] Failed to parse message: ""
```

This happens because the MCP spec (2025-11-25, SEP-1699) requires servers to send an SSE **priming event** with an empty `data` field before the actual JSON-RPC response:

```
data:
id: 0
retry: 3000

data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{...}}
```

The POST response SSE handler passes `event.data` (which is `""` for priming events) directly to `pushMessage()`, where `JSON.parse("")` throws.

## Root cause

In `src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostMcp.ts`, there are three SSE event handlers. Only one filters empty data:

**GET backchannel (has guard):**
```typescript
if (event.type === 'message' && event.data) {
    this._proxy.$onDidReceiveMessage(this._id, event.data);
}
```

**POST response (missing guard):**
```typescript
if (event.type === 'message') {
    this._proxy.$onDidReceiveMessage(this._id, event.data);
}
```

**Legacy SSE (missing guard):**
```typescript
if (event.type === 'message') {
    this._proxy.$onDidReceiveMessage(this._id, event.data);
}
```

## Suggested fix

Add `&& event.data` to the POST response and legacy SSE handlers, matching the GET backchannel handler:

```typescript
if (event.type === 'message' && event.data) {
```

## Reproduction

1. Configure an MCP server that uses Streamable HTTP transport and sends priming events per the spec (e.g. any server built with the `rmcp` Rust MCP SDK, which always sends priming events per SEP-1699)
2. Connect to it from VS Code
3. Open the MCP output log
4. Observe `[warning] Failed to parse message: ""` on each request/response cycle

The warnings are cosmetic -- the server works correctly despite them -- but they are noisy and confusing.

## Context

- The official MCP TypeScript and Python SDKs both send empty priming events per the spec (gated on protocol version >= 2025-11-25)
- The TypeScript and Python SDK *clients* already filter empty `event.data` before parsing
- VS Code's own GET backchannel handler already has the correct guard
- The `rmcp` Rust SDK is interesting because it sends priming events unconditionally, making the issue more visible
- VS Code version tested: 1.112.0

## References

- [MCP Spec 2025-11-25 - Transports (priming events)](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports)
- [SEP-1699 - Support SSE polling via server-side disconnect](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/1699)
- [Python SDK had the same issue on the client side (#1672)](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk/issues/1672)
comments: Duplicate of #303695 — same root cause (empty SSE priming events per SEP-1699 not filtered in the POST response handler). Our report adds the detail that the GET backchannel handler already has the `&& event.data` guard while the POST and legacy SSE handlers don't.

To figure out a bit more why this happens, please follow along:

#### Enable trace logging

* Open the Command Palette (<kbd>cmd</kbd>/<kbd>ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>shift</kbd>+<kbd>p</kbd>)
* Run the `Developer: Set Log Level...` command
* To the right of `Trace`, click the *double* check mark button. ✔️ ✔️ (enables trace logging as the default)

_Now wait for the issue to happen or trigger it..._

#### Getting the logs

* Open the Command Palette again (<kbd>cmd</kbd>/<kbd>ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>shift</kbd>+<kbd>p</kbd>)
* Run the `Output: Show Output Channels...` command
* Choose `GitHub Authentication`
* Copy and paste the contents of what opens in the panel in this issue (if the contents is too large, you may need to upload it as a file)

Do these steps again for these other output channels:
* `GitHub Copilot Chat`
* `GitHub Copilot`

#### Reverting trace logging (optional)

When we're done here, to revert the log level back to the default:
* Open the Command Palette
* Run the `Developer: Set Log Level...` command
* To the right of `Info`, click the double check mark button. ✔️ ✔️ (to set the default back to info)

title: [TPI] VS Code Walkthrough
description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/287621

- [x] windows @ntrogh
- [x] linux @eleanorjboyd
- [x] macOS @meganrogge

Complexity: 3

Authors: @eli-w-king , @cwebster-99 


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309099%0A%0A&assignees=cwebster-99,eli-w-king)

---

We are replacing/updating the traditional walkthrough shown after someone installs and opens VS Code for the first time. With Copilot Chat extension shipping with VS Code we wanted to put the sign in flow more upfront in the flow, and iterate on the steps so users are set up for success but not stuck in a long onboarding flow. 

The steps include:
- Sign in 
- Theme and keymaps (if we detect another tool installed on your machine)
- Extensions install
- AI feature overview 

Setup:

- Run `code-insiders --transient` 
- If you want to rerun the walkthrough in the same window you can do so with `Developer: Welcome Onboarding 2026`

Testing:
- Mimic a fresh install of VS Code. Ensure the new onboarding wizard shows by default. It shouldn't show if you refresh or open another VS Code window
- Closing/completing lands you in VS Code with the Welcome startup editor open 
- All buttons work / you are directed where you would expect
   - Signing in on step 1 actually signs you in 
   - Installing an extension actually installs the extension
   -  Testing themes should preview the theme
   -  If you don't sign in on the first page, you should see another sign in CTA on the last page. If you did sign in, there shouldn't be a second CTA 
- It is keyboard accessible 
- Keyboard shortcuts should match your OS
- Installed IDEs should be detected and suggested as Keybindings on the second step (if none installed, more themes should show instead). _Supported IDEs: Sublime Text, IntelliJ / JetBrains, Vim, Eclipse, Notepad++_
- Feedback on content / copy is welcome!
comments: Hi @cwebster-99. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

title: Test: terminal tool improvements - `send_to_terminal`, fg support, prompt for input revamping
description:
Refs: #309044, #308870, #308868, #308296, #308030, #288570

- [x] Windows @karthiknadig 
- [x] macOS @connor4312 
- [x] Linux @rzhao271 

Complexity: 5


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309095%0A%0A&assignees=meganrogge)

---

## What to Test

### Overview
This iteration overhauled how the agent handles terminal input. Previously, every terminal output chunk triggered an extra LLM call to classify whether the terminal was prompting for input — adding latency, burning tokens, and producing unreliable results across models. That entire mechanism has been removed. The agent now handles terminal input directly via `send_to_terminal` (which now works with foreground terminals), and uses the `ask_questions` carousel to defer to the user when needed.

**Key changes:**
- Removed LLM-based prompt-for-input detection (saves tokens + latency per command)
- `send_to_terminal` and `get_terminal_output` now work with foreground terminals, not just background
- `ask_questions` carousel gains a "Focus Terminal" button when triggered by terminal prompts
- Carousel auto-dismisses when user types directly in terminal
- `send_to_terminal` progress messages show the question being answered
- Confirmation dialog uses terminal title instead of raw UUID

> **Note:** Testers should run through sections 1–4 with both `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: true` (default) and `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false`.

---

### 1. Foreground terminal support (`send_to_terminal` / `get_terminal_output`)

- [ ] In agent mode, ask the agent to run a long-running foreground command (e.g. `npm run dev` or `python -m http.server`). Verify the agent can use `get_terminal_output` to read output from the foreground terminal.
- [ ] Ask the agent to send input to a foreground terminal (e.g. a running REPL). Verify `send_to_terminal` successfully writes to it.
- [ ] Run a command that prompts for input (e.g. `npm init`). Verify the agent can answer prompts via `send_to_terminal` without a separate LLM classification step.
- [ ] Verify that background terminals still work as before — `run_in_terminal`, `send_to_terminal`, and `get_terminal_output` all function for background terminals.

### 2. Question carousel with terminal context

- [ ] Run a command that prompts for user input (e.g. a password prompt, `ssh` host key confirmation, `npm init` questions). Verify the `ask_questions` carousel appears with a **terminal icon button** (Focus Terminal) in the header.
- [ ] Click the **Focus Terminal** button. Verify it focuses the correct terminal instance so you can type directly.
- [ ] While the carousel is showing for a terminal prompt, **type directly in the terminal** instead of using the carousel. Verify:
  - The carousel is **dismissed automatically**
  - The summary shows it was dismissed because the user typed in the terminal
  - The agent receives the message: _"The user is replying to the terminal prompts directly. Do not ask more questions or send input to the terminal."_
  - The agent **does not** try to send additional input via `send_to_terminal` after dismissal

### 3. Progress messages with question context

- [ ] When the agent uses `ask_questions` before `send_to_terminal` (e.g. asking "What is your project name?" then sending the answer), verify the `send_to_terminal` progress message shows context like: `Sending "my-project" to terminal (replying to: What is your project name?)`
- [ ] With multiple questions in a single carousel, verify each `send_to_terminal` call shows the **specific question** it's answering, not just the first one.

### 4. Confirmation UX improvements

- [ ] In default approvals mode, trigger `send_to_terminal`. Verify the confirmation dialog shows the **terminal title** (e.g. "node", "zsh") instead of a raw UUID.
- [ ] In **autopilot / bypass approvals** mode, verify `send_to_terminal` executes without showing a confirmation dialog.
- [ ] Verify no stale "awaiting input" confirmations appear from the old prompt detection system.

### 5. Autopilot mode

- [ ] In autopilot mode, run a command that triggers terminal prompts. Verify the agent auto-responds to `ask_questions` without showing a carousel, and the carousel summary is visible in the chat.
comments:
## Windows test results 

Ran the full matrix (sections 1–5 × `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications` both `true` and `false`) against [microsoft/python-environment-tools](https://github.com/microsoft/python-environment-tools) on Windows in agent mode.

### Pass / fail matrix

| Scenario | `bgNotif=true` + default approvals | `bgNotif=false` + default approvals | Autopilot / bypass |
|---|---|---|---|
| **S1.1** `get_terminal_output` on foreground long-running build | ✅ live output from sync `cargo build` (31/153 → 53/153) | ✅ live output from sync `cargo check` (60/155 → 99/155) | — |
| **S1.1-bonus** Auto-completion notification for sync terminal | ✅ fired on `cargo build` (exit 0 + full tail) | ⚠️ **still fired** for `cargo check` and `test-prompt.ps1` despite setting — see #309523 | — |
| **S1.2** `send_to_terminal` to foreground Python REPL | ✅ `PYVER 3 12 10`, `two plus two is 4` | ✅ `S1prime-b 42` | — |
| **S1.3** Answer 3 `Read-Host` prompts via `send_to_terminal` (no carousel) | ✅ all 3 accepted, script exit 0 | ✅ all 3 accepted, script exit 0 | — |
| **S1.4** Background/async terminal parity | ✅ async python REPL: `BG-TEST 42` | (covered by `bgNotif=true` pass) | — |
| **S2** Carousel Focus-Terminal button + auto-dismiss on direct typing | ✅ button present, focuses correct terminal, typing auto-dismisses, "user replying directly" message received, agent stopped sending | N/A — carousel path disabled by runtime instruction block (see #309524) | — |
| **S3** Per-answer progress message references the specific question | ✅ all three `send_to_terminal` progress messages referenced the correct question | N/A — no carousel under `bgNotif=false` | — |
| **S4** Confirmation dialog shows terminal title (not UUID); no stale "awaiting input" | ✅ Call B (`pwsh`) dialog showed **pwsh** title; Call A auto-approved from prior history (expected) | ✅ After clearing prior approval, dialog showed **pwsh** title, no stale prompts | ✅ Fresh-pattern calls executed silently (no dialogs), output produced |
| **S5** Autopilot auto-answers `ask_questions` without showing carousel | — | — | ⚠️ **carousel still appeared** and required manual input — see #309524. `send_to_terminal` under bypass: ✅ no dialogs, all 3 sends ran. |

### What worked as advertised
- Foreground `get_terminal_output` (live reads during running commands)
- Foreground `send_to_terminal` (REPL + interactive script prompts)
- Background terminal parity
- Carousel Focus-Terminal button + click-to-focus
- Carousel auto-dismiss when user types directly in terminal
- "User is replying directly" message reaching the agent
- Per-answer progress-message context
- Confirmation dialog showing terminal title instead of UUID
- No stale "awaiting input" dialogs anywhere in the session
- Bypass approvals suppressing all `send_to_terminal` dialogs

### Findings filed
- #309523 — `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false` still fires completion notifications for foreground terminals.
- #309524 — Section 5 unreachable: autopilot instruction block forbids `ask_questions`, and forced `ask_questions` still shows an interactive carousel.

title: Show "Deferring to user's input in the terminal" instead of "Skipped" when questions are dismissed by terminal input
description:
When the ask-questions carousel is dismissed because the user typed directly in the terminal, the UI currently shows a generic "Skipped" message. This is misleading — the questions weren't skipped by the user clicking skip; they were dismissed because the user is answering prompts directly in the terminal.

**Expected behavior:** When questions are auto-dismissed due to terminal input, the summary message should read **"Deferring to user's input in the terminal"** instead of "Skipped".

**Actual behavior:** The message says "Skipped" regardless of why the carousel was dismissed.

The fix is in `renderSkippedMessage()` in `chatQuestionCarouselPart.ts` — check `carousel.dismissedByTerminalInput` and use an appropriate message.

title: Terminal: Add 'Move Terminal Tab Up/Down' commands to reorder tabs
description:
## Summary

Currently there is no built-in command to reorder terminal tab groups without drag-and-drop, and no way to bulk-close terminals below a given tab. This makes keyboard-driven terminal management cumbersome.

## Feature Request

Add three commands:

- **`Terminal: Move Terminal Tab Up`** (`workbench.action.terminal.moveTabUp`)
- **`Terminal: Move Terminal Tab Down`** (`workbench.action.terminal.moveTabDown`)
- **`Terminal: Kill Terminals Below`** (`workbench.action.terminal.killGroupsBelow`)

**Move Up / Move Down:**
- Accessible via the Command Palette
- Bindable to custom keybindings
- Active when at least 2 terminal groups are visible

**Kill Terminals Below:**
- Accessible via the terminal tab right-click context menu and Command Palette
- Kills all terminal groups below the selected one (analogous to "Close to the Right" in editor tabs)
- Active when at least 2 terminal groups are visible

## Use Case

Users who rely on keyboard navigation or prefer not to use drag-and-drop have no way to reorder terminal tabs or quickly close a set of terminals below a given point. These commands close that gap and enable full keyboard-driven terminal management.
comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->
This feature request is now a candidate for our backlog. The community has 60 days to [upvote](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging#up-voting-a-feature-request) the issue. If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

Implemented in #309084.

- **Move Terminal Tab Up** / **Move Terminal Tab Down** — keyboard/Command Palette commands to reorder terminal groups (active when ≥2 groups exist)
- **Kill Terminals Below** — right-click context menu + Command Palette command to close all terminal groups below the selected one

Move Up/Down follow the editor tabs convention and are not exposed in the context menu.

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

I'd like to suggest a feature that makes it easier to access educational and entertainment resources inside VS Code. For example, integrating a simple panel, link manager, or launcher where users can browse, download, and launch unlimited games from resources like this Google Doc:

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OK_JLhYxK7jIm0JjfJlaSG-4N5W7BzF6Ewnx0gHbw1I/edit?tab=t.s7gmsb1cd3yf

Just download the game you want, use Ctrl+O to open it, and have fun! This would be especially valuable for educators, students, or users seeking sample games to learn from or enjoy in VS Code.

title: MCP client: Extension host crashes when authenticating with /common authority on multi-account/multi-tenants machines (tag_7cisv)
description:
Type: Bug When an MCP server uses `/common` as the OAuth authority, and the machine has multiple accounts in WAM (e.g., a personal MSA + a work account), the VS Code extension host crashes with `tag_7cisv, StatusInternal::Unexpected`. ###steps to reprpduce 1. Windows 11 machine has both personal (e.g., outlook.com) and working account (e.g. microsoft.com) signed in. Each account also has accociated with multiple tenants. 2. Configure an MCP server (type: http) that returns OAuth metadata pointing to `https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/v2.0` 3. VS Code prompts "MCP Server wants to authenticate to Microsoft" and prompts use to select account 4. Accept the prompt → log into working account --> extension host crashes - MSAL logs: `No HomeAccountId provided to normalize the realm` - MSAL logs: `Attempted to read cache with a non-normalized realm` - WAM throws: `tag_7cisv, StatusInternal::Unexpected` (multiple times) - Extension host terminates ### Workarounds Found 1. MCP server uses `/organizations` instead of `/common` (excludes personal MSA) 2. MCP server uses `/` (pins to specific tenant) ### Environment - VS Code: 1.1.15 - OS: Windows 11 - @azure/msal-node-extensions: 1.5.25 - Native Broker: enabled (true) - Accounts in WAM: 2 (1 personal MSA with 1 tenants + 1 work/school with 2 tenants) VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

comments:
Can you please try with this VS Code setting:

```
"microsoft-authentication.implementation": "msal-no-broker"
```

Thanks for the suggestion! — These resolves the issues:

No more extension host crash with /common authority on multi-account machines.
Token is cached properly — no repeated auth prompts on each MCP tool call
Everything works as expected now.

BTW, this change seems bypasses WAM entirely, which means losing seamless SSO with Windows and potentially device-compliance Conditional Access policies.

title: Ask-to-Workflow Handoff (not only Ask-to-Agent)
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Absolutely, and that makes the feature much stronger.

Add this extension to the spec:

**Capability Addition**
Ask-to-Workflow Handoff (not only Ask-to-Agent)

**User Story**
As a developer, I want to hand off my Ask output to a named workflow (for example, Report a bug workflow) instead of a generic coding agent, so execution follows a structured process and template.

**Examples**
- Hand off to Report a bug workflow
- Hand off to Security review workflow
- Hand off to Refactor planning workflow
- Hand off to Release readiness workflow

**Expected Behavior**
1. Ask mode supports: Send to Workflow.
2. User can pick a workflow from a registry/list.
3. Ask context is packaged into workflow inputs automatically.
4. Workflow runs in background while Ask remains interactive.
5. Ask receives workflow stage updates and final structured outputs.

**Workflow Contract Requirements**
1. Workflow metadata:
- name
- purpose
- required inputs
- optional inputs
- output schema
2. Workflow execution states:
- queued
- running
- waiting for input
- completed
- failed
3. Workflow output contract:
- summary
- artifacts generated
- actions taken
- unresolved items

**Acceptance Criteria**
1. User can invoke named workflow from Ask with one action.
2. Workflow can run concurrently with ongoing Ask conversation.
3. Ask can query workflow status and retrieve final artifacts.
4. If workflow requires more input, Ask can supply it without mode switching.
5. Same handoff model works for both Agent and Workflow targets.

**Suggested UX**
- Send to Agent
- Send to Workflow
- Target picker: Agent | Workflow
- Workflow picker: Report a bug workflow, etc.

This gives you a generalized handoff model:
Ask -> Execution Target, where Execution Target can be Agent or Workflow.
comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->

If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error "potential listener LEAK detected, dominated" is a **listener leak** in the language status → statusbar entry pipeline. Every time an extension updates a language status item (via `$setLanguageStatus`), the old registration is disposed **after** the new one is created, causing a transient window where duplicate entries with the same ID exist. The `_update` loop in `languageStatus.ts` fails to handle this duplicate, leaking a statusbar entry accessor and its listener on the `onDidOverrideEntry` emitter. Over time, leaked listeners accumulate until the emitter's threshold is exceeded and it refuses new listeners. This is **not a recent regression** — telemetry shows this error present across all versions from 1.113.0 through 1.117.0, with high hit counts on stable releases (e.g., 492K hits on 1.115.0, 580K on 1.114.0, 802K on 1.113.0). ### Culprit Commit No specific culprit commit was identified. This is a long-standing design issue in the interaction between `mainThreadLanguages.ts` and `languageStatus.ts`. The bug exists across all tracked versions (1.113.0–1.117.0) and is not tied to a recent change. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Ext as Extension (via RPC) participant MTL as mainThreadLanguages.$setLanguageStatus participant LSS as LanguageStatusService.addStatus participant LFR as LanguageFeatureRegistry.register participant LS as LanguageStatus._update participant SB as StatusbarPart.doAddEntry Ext->>MTL: $setLanguageStatus(handle, newStatus) Note over MTL: Evaluates addStatus() BEFORE
DisposableMap.set() disposes old MTL->>LSS: addStatus(newStatus) LSS->>LFR: register(selector, newStatus) Note over LFR: ⚠️ Root cause: old registration
still alive — both old + new coexist LFR->>LFR: onDidChange.fire() LFR->>LS: _update() [sync via event] Note over LS: model.dedicated has DUPLICATE
entries with same status.id LS->>LS: Loop iteration 1: finds old accessor
in _dedicatedEntries → update + delete LS->>SB: Loop iteration 2: not found →
addEntry() creates NEW accessor Note over SB: 💥 New listener added to
onDidOverrideEntry emitter LS->>LS: newDedicatedEntries.set(id, newAccessor)
overwrites old accessor — LEAKED! MTL->>MTL: DisposableMap.set() now disposes old Note over MTL: Old registration removed,
but old accessor already leaked ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadLanguages.ts` | **Root cause** — `$setLanguageStatus` evaluates `addStatus()` before `set()` disposes old value | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/languageStatus/browser/languageStatus.ts` | **Leak site** — `_update()` loop doesn't handle duplicate IDs; overwrites accessor in map without disposing | | `src/vs/workbench/browser/parts/statusbar/statusbarPart.ts` | **Crash site** — `doAddEntry()` subscribes to `onDidOverrideEntry.event`; accumulated leaked listeners trigger `ListenerRefusalError` | | `src/vs/workbench/services/languageStatus/common/languageStatusService.ts` | Data flow — `addStatus` delegates to `LanguageFeatureRegistry.register` | | `src/vs/editor/common/languageFeatureRegistry.ts` | Data flow — `register()` fires `onDidChange` synchronously | | `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | Detection — `ListenerRefusalError` thrown when listener count exceeds threshold2 | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code with an extension that frequently updates language status (e.g., TypeScript, Copilot, or any language server that calls `vscode.languages.createLanguageStatusItem` and updates it repeatedly) 2. Have a language status item marked as "dedicated" (pinned to statusbar) 3. The extension repeatedly calls `$setLanguageStatus` for the same handle — each call leaks one listener 4. After enough updates (threshold2 calls), the `onDidOverrideEntry` emitter refuses new listeners and throws `ListenerRefusalError` 5. The error is non-deterministic in timing — depends on how frequently extensions update their language status items. More active extensions (e.g., TypeScript language server during heavy editing) will trigger it sooner. ### Fix Proposal **Option A (preferred) — Fix in `mainThreadLanguages.ts`**: Dispose the old registration BEFORE creating the new one, eliminating the window where duplicates coexist. **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadLanguages.ts` → `$setLanguageStatus()` **Approach**: Call `this._status.deleteAndDispose(handle)` before `this._status.set(handle, this._languageStatusService.addStatus(status))`. This ensures the old registration is removed from `LanguageFeatureRegistry` before the new one is added, so `_update` never sees duplicate entries. **Option B (defense-in-depth) — Fix in `languageStatus.ts`**: In the `_update` loop over `model.dedicated`, deduplicate by `status.id` before iterating, or track and dispose overwritten accessors after the loop. **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/languageStatus/browser/languageStatus.ts` → `_update()` **Approach**: After the loop, check `newDedicatedEntries` for any accessor that was overwritten (not present in the final map but also not in `_dedicatedEntries` for cleanup). Alternatively, deduplicate `model.dedicated` by ID before the loop. ### Recommended Owner `@jrieken` — owns the API area (which includes `mainThreadLanguages.ts` and the language status API surface) and performance/leak investigations per working areas. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24530825899/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.2M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The `InlineEditsWordReplacementView` crashes with `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'getLineContent')` when an editor tab is closed while an inline edit suggestion is visible. The `_renderTextEffect` derived observable uses a non-null assertion (`!`) on `this._editor.model.get()`, but during editor closure the model is set to `null` and the observable recomputation chain attempts to read the now-null model. This is a high-impact, long-standing bug affecting **all platforms** across versions 1.110.0–1.117.0, with 195,859 hits from 130,689 users on 1.115.0 alone. ### Culprit Commit No single recent commit introduced this regression. The unsafe non-null assertion `this._editor.model.get()!` has been present since the `_renderTextEffect` derived observable was created as part of the inline edits word replacement view. The telemetry bucket shows consistent hits across all versions from 1.110.0 onward — this is a latent bug in the feature, not a sharp regression from a specific commit. ### Code Flow ````mermaid sequenceDiagram participant CloseEditors as EditorGroupView.closeEditors participant SetModel as CodeEditorWidget.setModel(null) participant Observable as Observable update chain participant RenderText as _renderTextEffect (derived) participant GetModel as _editor.model.get() CloseEditors->>SetModel: clearInput → setModel(null) SetModel->>Observable: endUpdate → propagate changes Observable->>RenderText: recompute derived value RenderText->>GetModel: this._editor.model.get()! Note over GetModel: ⚠️ Root cause:
model is null, but !
asserts non-null GetModel-->>RenderText: returns null RenderText->>RenderText: tm.getLineContent(...) Note over RenderText: 💥 TypeError: Cannot read
properties of null
(reading 'getLineContent') ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/inlineEditsViews/inlineEditsWordReplacementView.ts` | **Crash site & root cause** — line 98: `this._editor.model.get()!` with unsafe non-null assertion | | `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/utils/utils.ts` | Data flow — `mapOutFalsy` observable wrapper in the derived chain (line 377) | | `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/derivedImpl.ts` | Framework — derived observable recomputation engine (stable, not at fault) | ### Repro Steps 1. Open a file in VS Code that triggers inline edit / inline completion suggestions (e.g., type code that triggers Copilot inline suggestions) 2. While an inline edit suggestion is visible (the word replacement overlay is rendered), close the editor tab (Ctrl+W or click the X) 3. The editor model is set to null during the close sequence, but the observable update propagation triggers a recomputation of `_renderTextEffect` before the view is disposed 4. The error fires during the observable `endUpdate` chain **Note**: This is a race condition between editor disposal and observable recomputation. It occurs more frequently when inline edit suggestions are actively displayed during tab close. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/inlineEditsViews/inlineEditsWordReplacementView.ts` → `_renderTextEffect` derived (line 97–112) **Approach**: Replace the unsafe non-null assertion `this._editor.model.get()!` with a proper null guard. If the model is `null`, the derived should return early (the view is being disposed anyway, so no rendering is needed): ``` const tm = this._editor.model.get(); if (!tm) { return; } ```` This is the same pattern used in `_layout` (line 122: `if (!widgetStart || !widgetEnd ...) { return undefined; }`) — guard against transient null state during disposal. An existing community PR (#309155) proposes exactly this fix. ### Recommended Owner `@hediet` — feature area owner of inline edits/completions views (author of the `InlineEditsWordReplacementView` class, PRs #293509, #282719; MEMBER with `author_association: MEMBER`) > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24530888700/agentic_workflow) · ● 4.8M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `potential listener LEAK detected, popular` fires when the notebook outline panel renders many entries simultaneously. Each visible outline entry creates its own `IMenu` instance via `menuService.createMenu(MenuId.NotebookOutlineActionMenu, ...)`, and each menu's lazy listener subscribes to the **shared singleton** `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` emitter. With enough visible entries (large notebooks with many cells/headings), the listener count on this shared emitter exceeds the leak detection threshold, triggering the error. This affects all platforms (Windows, Mac, Linux) and has been present across versions 1.113–1.117. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit was identified. This is a **long-standing architectural pattern issue** — the per-element menu creation pattern in the notebook outline has existed for multiple release cycles. Telemetry shows hits starting from at least v1.113.0 (March 2026). There is no commit range narrowing available from the regression scan, and the error appears consistently across all recent versions. The issue was likely always latent and triggered whenever a user opens a notebook with enough cells to fill the outline panel. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Tree as Tree/ListView participant Renderer as NotebookOutlineRenderer participant MenuSvc as MenuService participant MenuImpl as MenuImpl (per entry) participant HiddenState as PersistedMenuHideState (singleton) Tree->>Renderer: renderElement() [×N entries] Renderer->>MenuSvc: createMenu(NotebookOutlineActionMenu) MenuSvc-->>MenuImpl: new MenuImpl(hiddenStates) Renderer->>MenuImpl: menu.onDidChange(...) Note over MenuImpl: onWillAddFirstListener fires MenuImpl->>HiddenState: hiddenStates.onDidChange(...) Note over HiddenState: ⚠️ Listener #N added
to shared singleton emitter HiddenState-->>HiddenState: 💥 Leak threshold exceeded
ListenerLeakError("popular") ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/contrib/outline/notebookOutline.ts` | **Root cause** — `renderElement()` (L184) creates a per-element `IMenu`; `setupToolbarListeners()` (L223) subscribes to `menu.onDidChange`, triggering the lazy listener chain | | `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` | `MenuImpl` constructor (L427-440) — `startLazyListener` subscribes to shared `hiddenStates.onDidChange` on first `menu.onDidChange` subscriber | | `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` | `PersistedMenuHideState` (L54-60) — singleton emitter that receives one listener per active menu instance | | `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | Leak detection infrastructure (L1261-1264) — monitors listener counts and fires `ListenerLeakError` | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code with a notebook file containing **30+ cells** (mix of markdown and code cells) 2. Open the **Outline** panel in the sidebar (View → Open View → Outline) 3. Expand the outline so many entries are visible simultaneously 4. The error fires in telemetry as the tree renders all visible entries, each creating a menu that subscribes to the shared `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` 5. The more cells visible in the outline, the more likely the threshold is exceeded ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/contrib/outline/notebookOutline.ts` → `renderElement()` / `setupToolbarListeners()` **Approach**: Instead of creating a **separate `IMenu` per outline entry**, share a single `IMenu` instance across all entries (scoped to the renderer or the outline pane lifecycle). The menu actions can be computed on-demand during `renderElement` by temporarily updating the scoped context keys, calling `menu.getActions()`, and setting the toolbar. The shared menu's `onDidChange` listener would trigger a refresh of all visible entries, but only one listener would exist on `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` instead of N. Alternatively, a lighter fix: create the menu in `renderElement` as today, but **do not subscribe to `menu.onDidChange`** — instead, rely on the tree's natural re-render cycle to update toolbar actions. Since the outline tree already refreshes when the notebook model changes, the `onDidChange` subscription may be redundant for this use case. This matches the pattern noted in the code comment at L218: `// same fix as in cellToolbars setupListeners re #103926`. ### Recommended Owner `@Yoyokrazy` — area owner for notebook toolbars (global toolbar, cell toolbar, context menu) per `triage/working-areas.md` > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24530962371/agentic_workflow) · ● 7.6M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString')` occurs in the inline edits line replacement view when calling `.toString()` on a `Color` value returned by `getEditorBlendedColor()` that is `undefined`. This happens because the observable framework's `_recompute` method catches errors thrown during color resolution (via `onBugIndicatingError`) and leaves the derived value as `undefined`, which then propagates to the `.map(c => c.toString())` callback. The error affects all platforms (Linux, Windows, Mac) and has been persistent across versions 1.110.0–1.117.0 with **millions of total hits** (~1.6M on 1.115.0 stable alone). ### Culprit Commit No specific culprit commit was identified — the vulnerable pattern (`getEditorBlendedColor(...).map(c => c.toString())` without null guard) has been present since the file was first created. The error was not introduced by a regression in a particular commit range; rather, it surfaces whenever the theme color resolution fails during observable recomputation (e.g., theme transitions, custom themes with incomplete color definitions). ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Theme as ThemeService participant ObsColor as observeColor() participant Derived as derivedImpl._recompute() participant Blend as getEditorBlendedColor() participant View as LineReplacementView L251 View->>Blend: getEditorBlendedColor(originalBorderColor, themeService) Blend->>ObsColor: observeColor(colorId, themeService) ObsColor->>Theme: getColorTheme().getColor(colorId) Note over Theme: Color not found (undefined) ObsColor-->>Derived: throw BugIndicatingError("Missing color") Note over Derived: catch (e) { onBugIndicatingError(e) }
_value remains undefined Derived-->>Blend: returns undefined (not Color) Blend-->>View: observable value = undefined View->>View: .map(c => `1px solid \$\{c.toString()}`) Note over View: 💥 TypeError: Cannot read properties
of undefined (reading 'toString') ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/inlineEditsViews/inlineEditsLineReplacementView.ts` | **Crash site** — line 251 calls `.toString()` on potentially `undefined` color | | `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/theme.ts` | **Root cause** — `getEditorBlendedColor()` and `observeColor()` throw `BugIndicatingError` when color missing, which is swallowed by observable framework | | `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/derivedImpl.ts` | **Error absorption** — `_recompute()` catches all errors (line 226), leaving `_value` as `undefined` | | `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/inlineEditsViews/inlineEditsSideBySideView.ts` | **Same pattern** — lines 453, 488 have identical unguarded `.toString()` calls | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code with a custom/non-default color theme (one that may not fully define `inlineEdit.originalBorder` or `inlineEdit.tabWillAcceptOriginalBorder` colors) 2. Open a file and trigger an inline edit suggestion (e.g., via Copilot inline completions) 3. The inline edit overlay renders, triggering the observable chain 4. If the theme color lookup returns `undefined` for one of the border colors (or during a theme transition), the error fires 5. The error is non-deterministic — it depends on theme configuration and timing of observable evaluation ### Fix Proposal **Option A (call-site guards — immediate fix):** Add null guards at each `.map(c => c.toString())` call site. **Fix location**: `inlineEditsLineReplacementView.ts` line 251, `inlineEditsSideBySideView.ts` lines 453/488, and similar patterns in `jumpToView.ts` and `inlineEditsCustomView.ts` **Approach**: Change `.map(c => \`1px solid \$\{c.toString()}\`)` to `.map(c => c ? \`1px solid \$\{c.toString()}\` : undefined)` (or use optional chaining). **Option B (root cause fix — preferred):** Make `getEditorBlendedColor` return a fallback `Color` instead of propagating `undefined` when `observeColor` fails. This would prevent undefined from ever reaching callers. **Fix location**: `theme.ts` → `getEditorBlendedColor()` or `observeColor()` **Approach**: Instead of throwing `BugIndicatingError` when a color is missing, return a sensible default (e.g., `Color.transparent` or `Color.black`), or add a fallback in `getEditorBlendedColor`'s `.map()` chain. > **Note:** Community PR #309156 implements Option A (null guards). It may need to be extended to cover `jumpToView.ts` and `inlineEditsCustomView.ts` which have the same unguarded pattern. ### Recommended Owner `@hediet` — inline completions feature area owner, authored the original code, and is already assigned as reviewer on the community fix PR #309156. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24529738235/agentic_workflow) · ● 9.7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

title: Integrated terminal on Windows can start with stale PATH and fail to resolve npm while standalone PowerShell works
description:
Type: Bug Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled? I have not fully verified that yet. VS Code Version VS Code Insiders OS Version Windows Repro Steps Install Node.js on Windows so that the installer adds C:\Program Files\nodejs to PATH. Open VS Code Insiders. Open an integrated terminal using PowerShell 7 (pwsh). Run: npm In some integrated terminal sessions, PowerShell reports: npm: The term 'npm' is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable program. Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again. Open standalone PowerShell outside VS Code and run: npm -v It works correctly there. Expected Behavior A newly created VS Code integrated terminal should inherit the current system/user PATH and resolve npm the same way standalone PowerShell does. Actual Behavior Some VS Code integrated terminal sessions start without C:\Program Files\nodejs in $env:Path, so npm cannot be resolved, even though: Node.js is correctly installed npm.cmd exists on disk system/user PATH already contains C:\Program Files\nodejs standalone PowerShell resolves npm correctly Verified Details On my machine: C:\Program Files\nodejs\node.exe exists C:\Program Files\nodejs\npm.cmd exists User PATH contains C:\Program Files\nodejs Machine PATH contains C:\Program Files\nodejs Standalone pwsh can resolve npm In the failing VS Code integrated terminal session, $env:Path did not contain C:\Program Files\nodejs I also checked my VS Code user settings and did not have any of these configured: terminal.integrated.env.windows terminal.integrated.inheritEnv terminal.integrated.profiles.windows terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows So this does not appear to be caused by a custom PATH override in settings. Workarounds These workarounds fixed it: Refresh PATH manually in the current terminal: $env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path','Machine') + ';' + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path','User') Disable terminal persistent sessions: "terminal.integrated.enablePersistentSessions": false, "terminal.integrated.persistentSessionReviveProcess": "never" Add a PowerShell profile workaround for VS Code terminals: if ($env:TERM_PROGRAM -eq 'vscode') { $machinePath = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path', 'Machine') $userPath = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path', 'User') $pathEntries = @($machinePath, $userPath) -split ';' | Where-Object { $_ } $env:Path = ($pathEntries | Select-Object -Unique) -join ';' } After this, newly created integrated terminals could resolve npm. Additional Notes This looks like the integrated terminal may be reusing or reviving a stale environment instead of always starting a truly fresh shell environment on Windows, especially after PATH changes from software installation. title: [TPI] Enable BYOK for business and enterprise users
description:
Refs https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/281199

- [x] anyOS @eleanorjboyd 
- [x] anyOS @cwebster-99

Complexity: 4


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309023%0A%0A&assignees=pierceboggan)

---

Enterprise and business users can now use BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) model providers in Copilot Chat, which was previously limited to individual users.

## Testing

### Enterprise/Business with BYOK enabled
1. Sign in with an enterprise or business Copilot account
2. Open Copilot Chat and go to the model picker
3. Select "Manage Models..." and verify BYOK providers are available (Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Ollama, etc.)
4. Add a BYOK model provider with a valid API key
5. Select the BYOK model and send a chat  verify it works end-to-endmessage 
6. Verify the model appears in the model picker and persists across restarts

### Enterprise/Business without BYOK setting enabled
1. Sign in with an enterprise or business account that does NOT have BYOK enabled via admin policy
2. Verify BYOK options are appropriately hidden or disabled
3. Ensure the standard Copilot-provided models still work normally

### Individual users (regression check)
1. Sign in with an individual Copilot account
2. Verify BYOK still works as  add a provider, select a model, chatbefore 
3. Ensure no regressions in the individual BYOK flow

### General checks
- Verify model picker shows correct labels for BYOK vs Copilot-provided models
- Verify error handling for invalid API keys or unreachable endpoints
- Test switching between BYOK and Copilot-provided models mid-conversation
comments: I can see BYO like Ollama Added Kimi 2.5
gave it a try and saw the same behavior as courtney - when I had my enterprise account from our test enterprise I couldn't add any models but my staff account I can

title: VS Code main process hits ~4GB V8 heap and crashes all windows; repeated document/editor lifecycle errors from Pylance before crash
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

### VS Code Version

  Version: 1.115.0
  Commit: 41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b
  Architecture: x64

  ### OS Version

  Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Noble) x64

  ### Relevant Extensions

  - ms-python.python 2026.4.0
  - ms-python.vscode-pylance 2026.2.1
  - ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh 0.122.0

  ### Problem

  VS Code frequently crashes without warning, and when it happens, **all open VS Code windows crash together**.

  From user-level logs, the direct crash reason is always the **VS Code main process** hitting the V8 heap limit at around 4 GB:

  `OOM error in V8: Ineffective mark-compacts near heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory`

  This does **not** look like a kernel OOM kill or GPU/driver reset. I checked kernel logs and did not see `oom-killer`, `Killed process`,
  `segfault`, `gpu hang`, `amdgpu reset`, etc.

  ### What I observed in the crashing session

  Latest crash:
  - Local time: `2026-04-10 21:57:02 +08:00`
  - Main process: `code.desktop[910430]`

  User journal shows:
  - heap near `3962.9 / 4000.8 MB`
  - then V8 OOM in the main process
  - then all VS Code windows exit together

  In the corresponding log session (`~/.config/Code/logs/20260410T190336`), I did **not** see extension host restart loops. Each window's extension
  host appears to start once and remain running.

  However, I repeatedly see document/editor lifecycle related errors from Pylance in multiple windows.

  Typical stack shape:
  - `DiagnosticRequestor.forgetDocument`
  - `DiagnosticFeatureProviderImpl.cleanUpDocument`
  - `updateVisibleDocuments`
  - `Bg.acceptDocumentsAndEditorsDelta`
  - `Bg.$acceptDocumentsAndEditorsDelta`

  I also see repeated warnings like:
  - `DiagnosticCollection with name 'Pylance' does already exist.`
  - `Error: illegal state - object is disposed`
  - `TextEditor is closed/disposed`

  These continue for hours and are still present close to the final crash.

  ### Why I am filing this in vscode and not only in Pylance

  The app-level symptom is a **shared main process OOM that takes down all windows**, not just a single extension host crash.

  The extension-side logs strongly suggest that Pylance is one trigger or reproducer, but I am not sure whether this is:
  1. a bug in Pylance itself,
  2. a VS Code lifecycle regression affecting extensions handling document/editor deltas,
  3. or an interaction between the two.

  I am filing here because the final failure is a VS Code main process crash, and there may be a core memory leak / retention issue after repeated
  document/editor lifecycle churn.

  ### Approximate repro pattern

  I do not yet have a minimal repro, but this is the rough pattern that seems to trigger it:

  1. Open multiple VS Code windows.
  2. At least one window uses Remote-SSH.
  3. Open/edit/close Python files in one or more windows so that Python + Pylance activate.
  4. Keep VS Code running for a while and continue normal editing/navigation.
  5. Eventually the shared VS Code process grows until it reaches ~4 GB heap and all windows crash together.

  ### Additional observations

  - In this specific session, Remote-SSH did log `PendingMigrationError: navigator is now a global in nodejs`, but the remote authority still
  resolved successfully, so this did not look like the immediate crash trigger.
  - I had previously removed GitLens from my profile, but in this exact crashing process it had already been loaded before uninstall, so I cannot
  use this session as a clean “without GitLens” control.
  - The strongest active errors close to the crash come from Pylance/document lifecycle handling, not from extension host restarts.
comments: Agree. The OOM error occurs frequently especially under SSH connection. I experience this multiple times every day.

comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

I asked the model why it switched to Spanish. It answered that it switched to Spanish because:
```
The Spanish text that appeared in your prompt context was:

`The current date is 10 de abril de 2026.`

That was the only instruction-like line in Spanish; the rest of the operational instructions were in English.
```

The date injection is using the system language, instead of Visual Studio Code language. Using ISO 8601 would be a better approach.

title: Not showing code in proper block and indent
description:
Type: Bug ### Problem Description When generating code outputs using **Codex (model 5.3, medium reasoning level)**, the responses frequently fail to properly format code blocks. Instead of wrapping code in Markdown-style triple backticks (` ``` `), the model often returns code mixed directly with plain text. This leads to several problems: * Code is **not enclosed in a code block**, making it harder to read or copy. * The output appears as **merged text and code**, without clear separation. * **Indentation is often broken or missing**, especially in Python, where indentation is critical. * Syntax highlighting is lost due to missing Markdown formatting. * The issue occurs **consistently across many different code outputs**, not just isolated cases. --- ### Expected Behavior * All generated code should be wrapped in proper Markdown code blocks: ```` ```python # example code ```` ``` ``` * Code should preserve **correct indentation and structure**. * Text explanations and code should be **clearly separated**. --- ### Actual Behavior * Code is embedded inline with normal text. * No Markdown code fences are used. * Indentation is flattened or inconsistent. * Output readability and usability are significantly reduced. --- ### Impact * Makes it difficult to copy and execute code. * Increases the likelihood of syntax errors (especially in Python). * Reduces developer productivity and trust in generated outputs. --- ### Additional Notes * The issue appears frequently even with a well-known, stable model configuration. * It does not seem tied to a specific prompt or language. * Occurs across multiple sessions and different types of coding tasks. VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z) OS version: Linux x64 6.19.11-200.fc43.x86_64 Modes:
