comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!
/gifPlease

Thanks for reporting this issue! Unfortunately, it's hard for us to understand what issue you're seeing. Please help us out by providing a screen recording showing exactly what isn't working as expected. While we can work with most standard formats, `.gif` files are preferred as they are displayed inline on GitHub. You may find https://gifcap.dev helpful as a browser-based gif recording tool.

If the issue depends on keyboard input, you can help us by enabling screencast mode for the recording (`Developer: Toggle Screencast Mode` in the command palette). Lastly, please attach this file via the GitHub web interface as emailed responses will strip files out from the issue.

Happy coding!

This issue has been closed as it has been labeled with `info-needed` and has not received the additional information that was requested from the issue author. It has been labeled as `info-needed` for 7 days.

title: VS Code repeatedly restores stale multi‑window session, causing freeze + exit code 137 loop
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open two separate folders in VS Code (example: `crystal-os` and `crystalclear-skills`) so they appear as two windows.
2. Close one of the windows, leaving the other open.
3. Quit VS Code completely.
4. Reopen VS Code by launching only one folder from Terminal (example: `code crystalclear-skills`).
5. VS Code attempts to restore the previous multi-window session.
6. One of the restored windows fails to load and terminates with exit code 137.
7. VS Code displays the popup: “The window terminated unexpectedly (reason: killed, code: 137)”.
8. Clicking “Reopen” causes the loop to repeat; clicking “Close” still results in VS Code attempting to restore the stale window on the next launch.

## Impact
- Lost multiple hours of productivity due to VS Code repeatedly attempting to restore a stale multi-window session.
- VS Code freezes on startup and requires manual terminal intervention to break the loop.
- Users working across multiple repos are disproportionately affected.
- The issue is not obvious to diagnose or fix without technical knowledge of VS Code’s restore behavior.
- The restore loop disrupts workflow momentum and significantly slows development.

## Additional Notes
- Running `code --disable-restore-windows .` breaks the loop, but this workaround is not discoverable for most users.
- Deleting `.code-workspace` files also resolves the issue, but users should not need to manually modify workspace files to recover from a failed restore.
- VS Code should detect stale or invalid windows and provide a clean “open only this folder” option when a window fails to restore.
- The popup message does not explain that the failure is caused by a stale multi-window session.
- A more robust session-restore mechanism or clearer recovery path would prevent this loop and avoid significant time loss.
comments: Can you please follow the steps in https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/wiki/Native-Crash-Issues to get at more details around the crash and attach the result here? Thanks!

title: Copilot diff is broken and unreadable on a single file.
description: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No - VS Code Version: Version: 1.115.0 - OS Version: Windows Copilot Extension Version 0.43.0 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Ask copilot to do something in agent mode. 2. Look at the diff. It looks like this all throughout, with random chunks of missing words and random solid red areas: The red sections are totally unreadable and the green sections are highlighted weirdly but you can still read them. This only happened to one file one time. I'll update this report if I can repeat it again. I simply accepted the changed and reviewed the code after that because I could not read the code while copilot was displaying the diff.
comments: Duplicate of #308821

> Duplicate of [#308821](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308821)

In what way is it a duplicate of that?...
Oh I see, you joined github today and you're just spamming...

title: Agents App: Changes are still in the panel even after running merge
description:
Steps to Reproduce:

1. Run a session in a worktree
2. Get some changes
3. Click the merge button

It will commit and merge, but the changes are still in the branch according to the UI. However, they are not.
comments: Just did this again with the `vscode-standup` repo. Repros 100%. Maybe relevant: I use `pwsh` on macOS.
This just happened on another repo using folder (not worktree) and using `/commit`

The short version is that this is by design at the moment, but I am open to suggestion on how to make this better. Based on user feedback, when you create a session that uses a worktree, we prefer to use the upstream branch as the source branch for the worktree. So if you have picked `main` as your source branch, we will create the worktree using `origin/main`. The "Branch Changes" view, shows you the changes by comparing the worktree branch with the base branch (ex: `origin/main`). When you perform the merge, the changes get merged from the worktree branch into `main` but they are not pushed to `origin/main`. This means that when we compare teh worktree branch with `origin/main` you will still see the changes. As soon as these changes are pushed to `origin/main` the changes will be gone. We also have a "Merge & Sync" action in the dropdown which will merge the changes and push to the remote. I am opened to suggestions on how to improve this flow.

@lszomoru ah yeah I wasn't reproing this, because my comment about was in response to folder sessions, not worktree sessions.

Let me do some more self-hosting on this flow over the weekend, and noodle on what options we could consider. Since I think this is worth acknowledging as a potential point of confusion for some users.

Even in the folder flow, you get this until you push the main branch up to the remote. In codex, the default changeset is "unstaged". I don't know if that is the right solution, but from a user perspective, I think clicking the big blue button should get me to a clean state and if it doesn't it should be clear there is an additional step to take.

title: Source Control Graph gets stuck on "All" and history reference picker becomes unclickable
description:
Type: Bug 1. Open a Git repository in VS Code. 2. Open the Source Control view. 3. In the built-in Graph pane, change the history reference from "Auto" to "All". 4. After switching to "All", try to change it back to "Auto". 5. The history reference control/icon becomes unclickable or unusable. 6. The Graph then shows: "The selected source control provider does not have any source control history items." Expected result: I should be able to click the Graph history reference control and switch from "All" back to "Auto", and the Graph should continue showing commit history for the current repository/branch. Actual result: The Graph gets stuck on "All". I can no longer switch it back to "Auto" because the control/icon is not clickable. The repository is still valid and detected, and commit history is still visible in GitLens, but the built-in Graph no longer shows history. Additional details: - Source Control still shows the repository and current branch correctly. - GitLens still displays commit history correctly. - I already tried: - Developer: Reload Window - View: Reset View Locations - This appears to be limited to the built-in Source Control Graph UI/state, not the repository itself. comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The terminal output monitor's `_createElicitationPart()` attempts to add an elicitation UI part (input prompt) to a chat response that has already been completed. This is a **race condition** between the async monitoring flow detecting terminal idle/input-needed states and the chat response being finalized. The error affects all platforms (Mac, Windows, Linux) and was introduced with the async prompt monitoring feature in PR #307199. ### Culprit Commit | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Commit | [`f540be8c`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/f540be8c1abf14ef594d42dd3a99c50f48732a7b) | | Author | `@meganrogge` | | PR | #307199 | | Message | Add completion notifications and async prompt monitoring for background terminals | | Why | This PR introduced the async monitoring mode and the system-initiated request mechanism in `_createElicitationPart`. Both the async and non-async code paths in this function lack a guard against the response already being complete before calling `acceptResponseProgress`. | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant SM as _startMonitoring participant WFI as _waitForIdle participant HIS as _handleIdleState participant RFFI as _requestFreeFormTerminalInput participant CEP as _createElicitationPart participant CM as ChatModel SM->>WFI: Poll for idle state WFI-->>SM: OutputMonitorState.Idle SM->>HIS: Handle idle state Note over HIS: Detects input-required pattern
or does async LLM analysis HIS->>RFFI: Request free-form terminal input RFFI->>CEP: Create elicitation part Note over CEP: ⚠️ Root cause:
Picks request whose response
is already complete (non-async),
or newly added request response
completed by concurrent event (async) CEP->>CM: acceptResponseProgress(request, part) Note over CM: 💥 Error thrown:
response.isComplete === true
Adding progress to a completed response ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts` | **Root cause** — `_createElicitationPart()` does not check `request.response.isComplete` before calling `acceptResponseProgress`. `_handleIdleState()` has async gaps where the response can be completed by another code path. | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | **Crash site** — `acceptResponseProgress()` throws when `request.response.isComplete` is `true` (line 2675-2676). | ### Repro Steps 1. Start a chat session with an agent that uses terminal tools (e.g., Copilot agent mode running a command) 2. The terminal command must produce output that triggers idle detection with an input-required pattern (e.g., a password prompt, `[Y/n]` confirmation, or "press any key" message) 3. The race occurs when the chat response completes (agent finishes processing) **while** the output monitor's `_handleIdleState` is mid-execution (performing async LLM analysis or awaiting free-form input creation) 4. The monitor then attempts to add an elicitation part to the already-completed response This is timing-dependent. It is more likely to occur when: - The terminal goes idle near the end of a chat response cycle - Background (async) terminal monitoring is active - The LLM analysis in `_determineUserInputOptions` takes time, creating a wider race window ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts` → `_createElicitationPart()` **Approach**: Add a guard before `chatModel.acceptResponseProgress(request, thePart)` to check if the response is already complete. Two options: 1. **Preferred**: Before calling `acceptResponseProgress`, check `request.response?.isComplete`. If true in non-async mode, create a new system-initiated request (matching what async mode already does) so there is always a fresh, non-complete response to attach the elicitation to. If in async mode and the freshly-added request's response is already complete (due to a synchronous listener), log a warning and return early. 2. **Simpler**: Wrap `chatModel.acceptResponseProgress(request, thePart)` in a try-catch, and if it throws due to a completed response, hide the part and resolve the promise with `undefined` (graceful degradation — the elicitation silently fails). Additionally, add `token.isCancellationRequested` checks at the entry of `_requestFreeFormTerminalInput` and `_createElicitationPart` to bail out early if the monitoring has been cancelled. ### Recommended Owner `@meganrogge` — author of the culprit commit (#307199), primary contributor to `outputMonitor.ts`, and owner of the `chat-terminal-output-monitor` area. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24218699390) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

- **First release with this error**: [1.116.0-insider](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/ac46a9b7-4716-9200-a4da-680445f225b8?product=Agents&commit=fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)
- **Commit range**: [41dd792b...fc6d18c3](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b...fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)

The error occurs in `agentFeedbackEditorUtils.ts` when reading `fsPath` of an undefined URI, triggered via `agentFeedbackEditorOverlay.ts` → `getSessionForResource` during editor theme changes.

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24202485243) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24202485243, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24202485243 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'fsPath')` occurs in `changeMatchesResource()` in `agentFeedbackEditorUtils.ts` when a session file change object has `modifiedUri` as `undefined`. The function assumes `modifiedUri` is always defined for `IChatSessionFileChange` objects (no optional chaining), but at runtime the data can arrive with `modifiedUri` missing due to an unsafe type cast in the session data pipeline. The crash is triggered during editor lifecycle events (theme initialization, model changes) via the `AgentFeedbackOverlayController` autorun. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit could be definitively identified. The root cause is a **pre-existing type-safety gap** in `_extractChanges()` in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` (line 959), which unsafely casts `session.changes as IChatSessionFileChange[]`. This cast masks `IChatSessionFileChange2` data (where `modifiedUri` is optional) as `IChatSessionFileChange` (where `modifiedUri` is required). The 10x regression spike from v1.115.0 → v1.116.0-insider likely occurred because the upstream data source (Copilot extension or session cache) started providing more `IChatSessionFileChange2`-shaped data with `modifiedUri` absent, exposing this latent bug. Candidates that may have increased the frequency: | PR | Author | Description | |-----|--------|-------------| | [#308188](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308188) | `@lszomoru` | Refactored changes/last-turn changes calculation in `changesViewModel.ts` | | [#308189](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308189) | `@sandy081` | Moved sessions management service to services layer | ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Theme as ThemeService.initialize participant EditorGroup as EditorGroupView participant Autorun as AgentFeedbackOverlayController participant Utils as agentFeedbackEditorUtils participant Provider as CopilotChatSessionsProvider Theme->>EditorGroup: onDidModelChange (theme init) EditorGroup->>Autorun: autorun fires Autorun->>Utils: getSessionForResource(candidate) Utils->>Provider: sessionsManagementService.getSessions() Provider-->>Utils: ISession[] (with changes observable) Utils->>Utils: session.changes.get() Note over Utils: ⚠️ changes may contain
IChatSessionFileChange2 objects
unsafely cast to IChatSessionFileChange Utils->>Utils: changeMatchesResource(change, resourceUri) Note over Utils: isIChatSessionFileChange2(change)
returns false (no uri property
or uri not instanceof URI) Utils->>Utils: change.modifiedUri.fsPath Note over Utils: 💥 TypeError: Cannot read
properties of undefined
(reading 'fsPath') ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/agentFeedback/browser/agentFeedbackEditorUtils.ts` (line ~57) | **Crash site** — `changeMatchesResource()` accesses `change.modifiedUri.fsPath` without null check | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` (line 959) | **Root cause** — `_extractChanges()` unsafely casts `session.changes as IChatSessionFileChange[]` | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/agentFeedback/browser/agentFeedbackEditorOverlay.ts` (line ~192) | **Trigger** — autorun in `AgentFeedbackOverlayController` calls `getSessionForResource()` | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatSessionsService.ts` | **Type definitions** — `IChatSessionFileChange` (required `modifiedUri`) vs `IChatSessionFileChange2` (optional `modifiedUri`) | | `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsModel.ts` (line ~882) | **Cache path** — `loadSessions()` calls `URI.revive(change.modifiedUri)` which returns `undefined` if input is `undefined` | ### Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product, v1.116.0-insider) 2. Have at least one Copilot Cloud or CLI session with file changes active 3. The session must have changes in `IChatSessionFileChange2` format where `modifiedUri` is absent (e.g., a change representing a new file with only `uri` set) 4. Open any editor — the `AgentFeedbackOverlayController` autorun fires on editor model/theme changes 5. The autorun calls `getSessionForResource()` which iterates all sessions and their changes 6. The error is thrown when `changeMatchesResource()` encounters a change with `modifiedUri === undefined` > Note: The error is triggered automatically by the editor lifecycle (theme initialization), not by explicit user action. It's most likely to occur on startup when sessions with cached `IChatSessionFileChange2` data are loaded. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location 1 (immediate)**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/agentFeedback/browser/agentFeedbackEditorUtils.ts` → `changeMatchesResource()` **Approach**: Add optional chaining for `modifiedUri` in the `IChatSessionFileChange` branch to handle the case where `modifiedUri` is `undefined` at runtime, even though the TypeScript type says it's required: ```typescript // Before (crashes): return change.modifiedUri.fsPath === resourceUri.fsPath // After (safe): return change.modifiedUri?.fsPath === resourceUri.fsPath ``` **Fix location 2 (proper)**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_extractChanges()` **Approach**: Replace the unsafe cast `session.changes as IChatSessionFileChange[]` with proper type discrimination. When the array contains `IChatSessionFileChange2` objects, either convert them to `IChatSessionFileChange` format (mapping `uri` → `modifiedUri`) or return them as `IChatSessionFileChange2[]` and update the session's `changes` observable type to accept both. **Fix location 3 (cache safety)**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsModel.ts` → `loadSessions()` **Approach**: Guard the `modifiedUri` revival in the cache loader: `modifiedUri: change.modifiedUri ? URI.revive(change.modifiedUri) : undefined`. Also handle `IChatSessionFileChange2` entries in cached data. ### Recommended Owner `@benibenj` — authored the `changeMatchesResource()` function (PR #302401) and the agent feedback editor utilities. For the deeper `_extractChanges()` fix, `@sandy081` or `@lszomoru` as session provider maintainers. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24202913104) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## 🔍 Error Investigation ### Summary The error `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path` is thrown when `URI.joinPath` is called on a workspace folder whose URI (`unknown://`) has no path component. This occurs in the Agents (sessions) product when an active session has no repository metadata — the `AgentSessionAdapter._buildWorkspace()` method uses `URI.parse('unknown://')` as a fallback, which is then added as a workspace folder. Any subsequent file-change event (e.g., triggered by MCP tool registration → JSON schema association) causes multiple listeners to call `folder.toResource()` → `joinPath()`, which throws. This affects **760 hits across 26 users** on all platforms through 8 different call paths. ### Culprit Commit No single culprit commit was identified. The commit range `41dd792b...fc6d18c3` spans a diverged branch comparison (1.115.0 release vs 1.116.0-insider) with ~4,108 commits, making bisection impractical. The root cause code (`URI.parse('unknown://')` fallback in `_buildWorkspace`) exists in both the last-good and first-bad commits, suggesting the regression may be caused by a change in session lifecycle (e.g., sessions without repos becoming active more frequently) or MCP tool registration timing. ### Code Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant MCP as MCP Tool Registration participant Schema as JSON Schema Registry participant Settings as Settings FS Provider participant FileService as File Service participant ExtConfig as Workspace Extensions Config participant Folder as WorkspaceFolder participant URI as URI.joinPath MCP->>Schema: registerSchemaAssociation() Schema->>Settings: onDidChangeSchemaAssociations Settings->>FileService: onDidChangeFile (synthetic) FileService->>ExtConfig: onDidFilesChange ExtConfig->>Folder: folder.toResource('.vscode/extensions.json') Folder->>URI: joinPath(URI.parse('unknown://'), ...) Note over URI: 💥 Error: cannot call
joinPath on URI without path Note over ExtConfig: ⚠️ Root cause: workspace folder
has URI with no path component ``` ### Affected Files | File | Role | |------|------| | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` (line ~977) uses `URI.parse('unknown://')` (no path) as fallback when session has no repository metadata | | `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | **Data flow** — `getActiveSessionFolderData()` passes the `unknown://` URI through as a workspace folder URI without validating it has a path | | `src/vs/workbench/services/extensionRecommendations/common/workspaceExtensionsConfig.ts` | **Crash site** — line 64 calls `folder.toResource(EXTENSIONS_CONFIG)` on all workspace folders in `onDidFilesChange` handler | | `src/vs/platform/workspace/common/workspace.ts` | **Crash site** — `WorkspaceFolder.toResource()` calls `joinPath(this.uri, ...)` which throws | | `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Throw site** — `URI.joinPath()` line 362 throws when URI has no path | ### Repro Steps 1. Open the VS Code Agents (sessions) application 2. Have an active session whose metadata does not include repository information (no `repositoryPath`, `workingDirectoryPath`, or cloud repo metadata) 3. Any MCP tool with an input schema gets registered (triggers JSON schema association event) 4. The file change event propagates to `workspaceExtensionsConfig.ts`, which iterates workspace folders and calls `toResource()` on the folder with `unknown://` URI 5. `URI.joinPath` throws because `unknown://` has no path component The error is deterministic once a session without repo metadata becomes the active session — it triggers on every schema association change. ### Fix Proposal **Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `AgentSessionAdapter._buildWorkspace()` **Approach**: When `repoUri` is `undefined` (no repository metadata available), `_buildWorkspace` should return `undefined` instead of creating a workspace with a pathless `URI.parse('unknown://')`. The method signature already allows returning `undefined` (`ISessionWorkspace | undefined`), but the current implementation never does. This will prevent `WorkspaceFolderManagementContribution` from adding a workspace folder without a valid URI. Alternatively, if sessions without repos still need a workspace representation, change the fallback URI to include a root path: `URI.from({ scheme: 'unknown', path: '/' })`. This ensures `joinPath` can operate on the URI without throwing. A defensive guard in `WorkspaceFolderManagementContribution.getActiveSessionFolderData()` could also be added to reject URIs without a path (`if (!repository.path) return undefined`), preventing any pathless URI from becoming a workspace folder. ### Recommended Owner `@sandy081` — Primary maintainer of `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` (8 of last 20 commits) and the sessions management layer. The root cause is in the `AgentSessionAdapter._buildWorkspace()` method which was likely introduced as part of the sessions decoupling work. > Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24202906982) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

## Agents Triage — Continuing Error Bucket This error bucket continues to appear in the latest Agents build (`98b4d3e2`): | Bucket | Error Message | Version | |--------|--------------|---------| | [`ea244cdc`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/ea244cdc-4eff-6b8a-8f98-8b301b983139?product=Agents) | [UriError] joinPath — workspace.ts:toResource / workspaceExtensionsConfig | `98b4d3e2` | 352 users, 12.9K hits. > [!WARNING] >
> ⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain > > The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `update.code.visualstudio.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "update.code.visualstudio.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > >
> Generated by [errors-agents-triage](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24516976927/agentic_workflow) · ● 19.2M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-triage%22&type=issues)

title: Copilot Chat: SSE stream inactivity timeout (60s) should be configurable — causing mid-stream failures under backend congestion
description:
## Type of feedback

Feature request / bug report hybrid.

## Summary

The Copilot Chat extension hardcodes the SSE stream **inactivity timeout** (the timer that runs *after* the first chunk has arrived) at `60 seconds`. Under backend congestion, the server starts a response, delivers the first chunk normally, then pauses for 60+ seconds. The idle timer fires and destroys the connection mid-stream. The user sees:

```
Sorry, there was a network error. Please try again later.
Error Code: SSE stream timed out after 60000ms of inactivity.
```

Retrying the **exact same request** succeeds, proving this is transient backend latency — not a client/network problem.

## Extension version

GitHub Copilot `0.44.2026040901` (VS Code Insiders)

## Relevant code (located via source inspection)

In `dist/extension.js`:

```js
Nwn = 2 * 60 * 1e3,  // 120s — waiting for FIRST chunk (reasonable)
Lwn = 60 * 1e3,      // 60s  — inactivity AFTER first chunk arrives (too aggressive)
```

The `jee()` async generator applies `Lwn` as the per-chunk inactivity window once streaming has started. Neither value is exposed as a VS Code setting.

## Observed behavior

1. Request is sent, first response chunk arrives in ~2–5s (TTFB normal)
2. Server pauses mid-stream - no chunks for approximately 60s (backend queue congestion)
3. `StreamIdleTimeoutError` fires — connection destroyed
4. Retry of identical request at same context size succeeds

This is clearly transient server-side behavior, not a network path issue (confirmed via TLS cert inspection showing direct connection, no proxy).

## Expected behavior

Option A — **Add a VS Code setting** to configure the inactivity timeout:

```json
"github.copilot.advanced.streamIdleTimeoutMs": {
  "type": "number",
  "default": 60000,
  "description": "Timeout in milliseconds for SSE stream inactivity after the first chunk is received."
}
```

Option B — **Increase the hardcoded default** from 60s to at least 120s to better tolerate transient backend pauses.

Option A is preferred because:
- Different deployment environments (enterprise proxied networks, slow VPNs, WSL remote) have different acceptable tolerances
- Large context requests (100K+ tokens) experience longer processing pauses that a single value cannot accommodate
- Users can opt in to a longer timeout without affecting others who prefer fast failure

## Workaround (requires re-applying after every update)

Manually patching the extension bundle:

```
# Change Lwn from 60s to 120s
Nwn=2*60*1e3,Lwn=60*1e3  →  Nwn=2*60*1e3,Lwn=120*1e3
```

Having to patch a bundled binary after every update is not a sustainable workaround.

## Environment

- OS: Linux (WSL2)
- VS Code: Insiders (Remote - WSL)
- Extension host: WSL
comments:
## Update — two failure modes found, both patched locally

After digging deeper with debug log analysis and extension source inspection, there are actually **two distinct failure modes** happening. Documenting both for completeness.

---

### Failure mode 1 (original): SSE inactivity timer (`Lwn`) fires

The `Lwn=60*1e3` patch from the original report wasn't sufficient — the backend now also produces pauses exceeding 120s, so the patched timer fires too:

```
Error Code: SSE stream timed out after 120000ms of inactivity
```

Log evidence (session `0ad77ad6`):
```
dur=140568ms  err=SSE stream timed out after 120000ms of inactivity  in=None  ttft=None
```

`in=None` / `ttft=None` every time — the server never delivers a first chunk within the window; the `Lwn` timer fires. Immediate retry succeeds at same context size. This rules out systematic throttling and confirms intermittent GitHub backend congestion.

**Local workaround**: increased `Lwn` to `5*60*1e3` (300s). Still asking for this to be a configurable setting.

---

### Failure mode 2 (new): HTTP/2 GOAWAY code 0 — retry path doesn't fire

```
Error Code: terminated: HTTP/2: "GOAWAY" frame received with code 0
```

The GOAWAY handler (`J0a` in `extension.js`) creates a custom error:
```js
new V2e(`HTTP/2: "GOAWAY" frame received with code ${t}`, ...)
```

The retry guard `sGt()` that triggers `disconnectAll().then(() => fetch())` checks:
```js
t.message === "ERR_HTTP2_GOAWAY_SESSION"  // Node built-in error string
```

These two strings never match. The `J0a` error path has a `.message` of `HTTP/2: "GOAWAY" frame received with code 0` but no `.code`. Meanwhile Node's built-in `ERR_HTTP2_GOAWAY_SESSION` (which `sGt` checks) is a different code path entirely.

**Result**: when `J0a`-originated GOAWAY fires on an in-flight request, `sGt()` returns false, the retry (`disconnectAll` + re-fetch) never runs, and the error surfaces to the user. The connection manager does reconnect in the background (separate path), so the *next* request usually works — but the current one fails visibly.

**Local workaround**: patched `sGt` to also match:
```js
(t.message === "ERR_HTTP2_GOAWAY_SESSION" || t.message.includes("GOAWAY"))
```

**Upstream fix asked for**: unify error handling so `J0a`-originated GOAWAY errors go through the same retry path as Node built-in `ERR_HTTP2_GOAWAY_SESSION` errors. The existing infrastructure (`disconnectAll` + re-fetch) is already there — it just doesn't get triggered.

---

### Summary of asks

| | Ask |
|---|---|
| `Lwn` | Expose as `github.copilot.advanced.streamIdleTimeoutMs` (or similar) |
| GOAWAY retry | Fix `sGt()` so `J0a` GOAWAY errors trigger `disconnectAll`+re-fetch |

Both local patches applied to `extension.js` at `byte 13430737` (`Lwn`) and `byte 16544266` (`sGt`). Happy to provide exact diffs if useful.

title: Worktrees that previously showed in the sidebar no longer visible after update to v1.115.0
description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.115.0 (also happens in Insiders)
- OS Version: macOS 26

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a new Git worktree of the current repo and place it in `~/.locki/worktrees`
2. Observe it does not show in the Source Control sidebar
3. Observe that downgrading to v1.114.0 fixes the issue

The same issue reported to Kilo Code: https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/8113 -- but despite the information there, the issue does not seem to be fixed.
comments:
I also had this issue, turns out there's a new setting, off by default. Setting it to `true` and reloading the window shows the worktrees again.
`"git.detectWorktrees": true`
@Vistyy Thanks! The default value has to be a mistake, I don't see why they would intentionally break existing behavior.
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/308671 - they mention they turned the setting off by default for performance reasons.

title: Persistent prompts about starting and trusting MCP servers
description: A user reported to me that in each new workspace they see the following message: And clicking "Start them now" leads to: We're not sure what's causing these. I've tried creating new profiles and running all of the "MCP: Reset xxx" commands, but I never see any prompts like this. The Dart MCP Server is registered via the `vs.lm.registerMcpServerDefinitionProvider` API (although it has an ID of "dart-sdk-mcp-servers", so I presume the extension id is being used here?) and we also register some additional tools from the extension using `vs.lm.registerTool`. It's not clear to me: - exactly what leads to the first prompt (what are the conditions causing "may have new tools" and "require interaction to start"?) - why the Dart MCP server is seemingly listed twice "dart, dart" - Why the user would be asked to trust the MCP server if they had already chosen to trust the extension during installation
comments: Hmm, can you confirm what version of VS Code they're on? There was I think one or two versions of VS Code that had that late last year right after the API came out, but it should not happen in the current version.

Thanks for raising this—this is helpful context.

On Fri, Apr 10, 2026, 10:26 AM Connor Peet ***@***.***> wrote:

> *connor4312* left a comment (microsoft/vscode#308818)
> <https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/308818#issuecomment-4224827426>
>
> Hmm, can you confirm what version of VS Code they're on? There was I think
> one or two versions of VS Code that had that late last year right after the
> API came out, but it should not happen in the current version.
>
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/308818#issuecomment-4224827426>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/B7VN24MZILXNYSXEHHIC2XL4VEHCTAVCNFSM6AAAAACXSZISH2VHI2DSMVQWIX3LMV43OSLTON2WKQ3PNVWWK3TUHM2DEMRUHAZDONBSGY>
> .
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.Message
> ID: ***@***.***>
>

@connor4312 they're on v1.115. However, we did discover that they had both a manual entry in `mcp.json`, and also the one from Cursor was being read (I've asked them to check the settings for this, because it seems like it's unticked for me, and I don't recall ever seeing this setting before).

So my guess is that the two "dart, dart" entries in the first screenshot related to those (although it's still not clear to me what the conditions are for that message to appear), and I presume the second screenshot (trust prompt) is also probably related to one of those.

I've asked if they can remove the global settings entry and (assuming it's enabled) disable the option to discover Cursor's MCP servers, and let me know if that changes anything.

I'd still be interested to better understand these prompts though (in case this comes up again). In what situations should each prompt appear (and should they be one-offs, or per-workspace, etc.)?

Thanks!

@connor4312 it sounds like removing the global config and unticking the Cursor discovery has stopped these prompts. But I'm wondering how many other users might have either of those (either they added the MCP manually in the past, or they also ticked the Cursor option).

Perhaps VS Code could do a better job of trying to de-dupe these? It's possible the two entries in VS Code + Cursor do not match exactly (in this case there were some extra flags passed in the Cursor config), and they might also not perfectly match the contributed server from Dart-Code (because we also pass some flags to disable some built-in tools that we provide our own version of from the extension).

nothing happens
